# Changelog

Detailed changelog for Perry. See CLAUDE.md for concise summaries.

## v0.5.792 — Refs #650 followup: `URLSearchParams.size` getter. Pre-fix `sp.size` returned `undefined` because URLSearchParams stores its data under the `_entries` field — there's no `size` property on the underlying ObjectHeader, so the generic field lookup never found it. Three coordinated additions: (1) **runtime helper** `js_url_search_params_size(*ObjectHeader) -> i32` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/url.rs` reads the length of the `_entries` ArrayHeader directly (returns 0 for null receiver / missing array). (2) **type-analysis predicate** `is_url_search_params_expr(ctx, expr)` in `crates/perry-codegen/src/type_analysis.rs` recognizes URLSearchParams receivers in three shapes: immediate `Expr::UrlSearchParamsNew(_)` ctor, locals typed as `HirType::Named("URLSearchParams")` (covered by the type system), and the chained-property shape `Expr::PropertyGet { object, property: "searchParams" }` where `object` is a URL receiver — needed because the HIR keeps `url.searchParams` as a generic PropertyGet rather than lowering to the dedicated `Expr::UrlGetSearchParams` variant in chained position, so `new URL(...).searchParams.size` would otherwise miss the size dispatch. (3) **codegen arm** in `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::PropertyGet` mirrors the existing Map/Set `.size` arms — when `property == "size"` and the predicate matches, route through the runtime helper and `sitofp` the i32 result to f64. Closes 1 of the 5 still-open #650 sub-issues. **Out of scope (separate followups)**: pathname/search/hash setters being no-ops, iterable `new URLSearchParams([['a','1']])` constructor — each needs its own surgery (setter routing through URL keys + href re-derivation; iterable entries detection in the constructor). Extended regression test `test-files/test_issue_650_url_static.ts` covers `sp.size` after construction / append / delete, empty URLSearchParams, and the chained `url.searchParams.size` case — all byte-identical to Node. Sanity: 10 representative tests (incl. test_map / test_edge_map_set) all byte-identical to Node — the new `is_url_search_params_expr` predicate is purely additive, doesn't touch the Map/Set paths.

## v0.5.791 — Refs #650 followup: `urlInstance.toString()` and `.toJSON()` return the URL's `href` instead of `[object Object]` and a Date string, respectively. **Root cause:** the HIR-level method-call dispatch in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs` had a Date-instance-method block (`getTime`, `toISOString`, `toJSON`, `toLocaleString`, `valueOf`, etc.) that fired purely by method name, with no check on the receiver's static type. So `urlInstance.toJSON()` got rewritten as `Expr::DateToJSON(urlInstance)` and the runtime interpreted the URL's NaN-boxed pointer bits as a Date timestamp — `new URL(...).toJSON()` returned `1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z` (the epoch). Likewise `urlInstance.toString()` was never matched as a Date method (Date doesn't override toString in the dispatch), so it fell through to the generic Object.prototype.toString and the runtime returned the constant `"[object Object]"` string. **Fix:** new `static_receiver_class(ctx, ast_obj) -> Option<&'static str>` helper at the top of `lower_call` recognizes `new Date(...)` / `new URL(...)` literals AND locals typed as `Type::Named("Date")` / `Type::Named("URL")`. Two coordinated changes feed off it: (1) **URL toString/toJSON arms** — new `Expr::UrlInstanceToString(Box<Expr>)` and `Expr::UrlInstanceToJSON(Box<Expr>)` HIR variants, both routed via codegen to `js_url_get_href` since both methods produce the URL's `href` per WHATWG. The arms fire BEFORE the Date block, gated on `static_receiver_class == Some("URL")`. (2) **Date dispatch gate** — the existing Date arms now skip when the method name is one of the eight ambiguous names (`toJSON`, `toString`, `toLocaleString`, `toDateString`, `toTimeString`, `toLocaleDateString`, `toLocaleTimeString`, `toISOString`, `valueOf`) AND the receiver is statically known to be NOT a Date (currently the only proven non-Date is URL, but the gate generalizes to future receiver classes). Date's unambiguous methods (`getTime`, `getUTCDay`, all the field getters / setters) keep their unconditional dispatch — those names don't collide with other prototypes in practice. Walker / analysis arms updated to traverse the two new variants. Closes 2 of the 5 still-open #650 sub-issues (toString and toJSON). **Out of scope (separate followup)**: pathname/search/hash setters being no-ops, `URLSearchParams.size` getter, iterable `new URLSearchParams([['a','1']])` constructor — each needs its own surgery (setter routing through the URL object's keys array + href re-derivation; size getter on URLSearchParams; iterable constructor entries detection). New regression covered by `test-files/test_issue_650_url_static.ts` (extended) plus a manual probe `/tmp/probe_650_tostring.ts` that verifies inline-ctor / typed-local / Date toJSON parity, all byte-identical to Node. Sanity: 8 representative tests (`test_simple_class` / `test_inheritance` / `test_break_continue` / `test_array_methods` / `test_async` / `test_rest_params` / `test_issue_645_default_fill_positional` / `test_gap_date_methods`) all byte-identical to Node.

## v0.5.790 — Three issues from the >600 loop sweep + a small followup to v0.5.789's #645 fix. Each fix is independently scoped; consolidated into a single version bump because main shipped concurrent v0.5.787-789 fixes that collided with the per-fix bumps the loop branch had originally chosen.

**Refs #645 followup** — the v0.5.789 default-arg fill loop in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs` now stops BEFORE the rest-param slot. Pre-fix the loop pushed `Expr::Undefined` into every `None`-default slot from `num_provided..defaults.len()` — including the rest param's slot if the call provided fewer args than the function's declared param count. For `function f(name, ...args)` called as `f('a')`, that produced `f('a', undefined)`, the runtime then bundled `[undefined]` into `args` so `args.length === 1` and `args[0] === undefined` — both Node and the spec say `args.length === 0`. Three coordinated edits: extend `func_defaults` to a 4-tuple `(FuncId, defaults, param_ids, Option<rest_idx>)`; populate `rest_idx` at all three push sites (top-level lower.rs, expr_object.rs closure literals); update `lookup_func_defaults` to return `(defaults, param_ids, rest_idx)`; in the fill loop, stop at `fill_end = rest_idx.unwrap_or(defaults.len())`. Sanity test from v0.5.789 (`test_issue_645_default_fill_positional`) still byte-identical to Node.

**Closes #656** — `Array.prototype.unshift` returns the new length, not the array. Both lowering paths (HIR-level `Expr::ArrayUnshift` in `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs` and the generic `lower_array_method.rs::"unshift"` arm) used to return `nanbox_pointer_inline(new_handle)`, surfacing the (possibly reallocated) `ArrayHeader*` to the caller and silently breaking patterns like `if (queue.unshift(item) > MAX_DEPTH) drain()` (any non-empty array is truthy under numeric comparison so the gate fired unconditionally) and `count += arr.unshift(x)` (NaN-poisoned the running sum). Per ECMA-262 23.1.3.34 the spec value is the new length; we now read `js_array_length(new_handle)` and sitofp it to f64. Mutation behavior unchanged. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_656_unshift_returns_length.ts` byte-identical to Node.

**Closes #653** — AOT spread of non-empty arrays no longer passes denormal `5e-324` placeholders to rest-param callees. Three coordinated fixes that together make the issue's repro (`function f(name, ...args) { ... }; f('a', ...one)` where `one = ['x']`) byte-identical to Node, plus the multi-element / spread-only / many-rest-args variants. Surfaced in `@perryts/redis`'s `conn.command('HELLO', ...args)` chain. **Root cause:** `__perry_wrap_<name>` wrappers for top-level user functions taken as values (via `js_closure_alloc_singleton`) had a fixed-arity flat signature that just trampolined args to the underlying function without rest-bundling — and the runtime's `func_ptr → fixed_arity` side-table (`CLOSURE_REST_REGISTRY`, populated by `js_register_closure_rest`) was only registered for closure literals, never for these wrappers. Result: `js_closure_call_apply_with_spread(closure, regs, 1, [x])` packed `['a', x]` into the call buffer and trampolined the wrapper as if it were a 2-arg callee, the wrapper handed the raw element bits to the user function as the rest param, `args[0]` came back as `f64::from_bits(0x1)` = `5e-324`. **Fix 1** (`crates/perry-codegen/src/codegen.rs`): `emit_string_pool` takes a new `user_fn_wrapper_rest: &[(String, usize)]` parameter and emits a parallel `js_register_closure_rest(@__perry_wrap_<name>, fixed_arity)` registration loop. The map is built from `hir.functions` by collecting every function whose `params.iter().position(|p| p.is_rest)` is `Some(_)`. **Fix 2** (`crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs`): the `Expr::CallSpread` arm's `FuncRef` fast path (`spread_count == 1 && regular_count == 0`) was extracting `declared_count` elements and passing them positionally — wrong for rest-bearing callees: `g(...two)` where `g(...all)` was passing `element[0]` (a string) as `all`, so `all.length` came back as the string's char count. New gate: when `has_rest && declared_count == 1`, pass the spread source array directly as the single rest-array param. **Fix 3** (`crates/perry-runtime/src/closure.rs`): `js_closure_call_array` now consults `lookup_closure_rest(get_valid_func_ptr(closure))` and routes through `dispatch_rest_bundled` directly when registered, bypassing the per-arity `js_closure_callN` trampolines. Pre-fix, `js_closure_call7` through `js_closure_call16` lacked the rest/arity dispatch the lower arities (call0..call6) had inline — calling a 3-param-with-rest wrapper with 8 doubles type-mismatched the trampoline transmute. The new arm preserves the same INT32_TAG unboxing the per-arity dispatchers do. **Out of scope (separate followup)**: `js_closure_call7..16` per-arity dispatch parity for direct (non-spread) closure calls — static call sites keep their existing call-site bundling and don't enter this dispatch path; the unfixed gap only affects 7+-arg dynamic dispatch that doesn't go through the spread path. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_653_spread_typed.ts` byte-identical to Node.

**Refs #650 followup** — `URL.canParse(s)` and `URL.parse(s)` static methods (Node 18 / 22). The `URL` identifier in perry's HIR is special-cased to a synthetic null reference (codegen literal `0` typed as `object`), so the canonical static-method dispatch never reached these names — `URL.canParse?.("https://x")` returned `0` (number) and the optional-chain call into a non-callable threw "value is not a function". Three coordinated additions: HIR variants `Expr::UrlCanParse(arg)` / `Expr::UrlParse(arg)`; HIR detection at `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs` mirrors the existing `Date.now/parse/UTC` arm; codegen calls runtime helpers `js_url_can_parse(*StringHeader) -> i32` and `js_url_parse(*StringHeader) -> *ObjectHeader`, with select'd boolean / null-or-pointer results; runtime impls in `crates/perry-runtime/src/url.rs` share an `is_valid_absolute_url(s)` validator (valid scheme + `://` + non-empty authority for hierarchical schemes; non-empty scheme-specific data for opaque schemes) — stricter than the constructor's liberal `parse_url`, which stays accept-anything for back-compat. Closes 2 of 7 sub-issues in #650. **Out of scope (separate followups)**: `urlInstance.toString()` returning `[object Object]`, `urlInstance.toJSON()` returning a Date string, pathname/search/hash setters being no-ops, `URLSearchParams.size` getter, iterable `new URLSearchParams([['a','1']])` constructor — each needs invasive HIR-level type-aware lowering or runtime side-table changes that are too much surface for this batch. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_650_url_static.ts` byte-identical to Node.

Sanity: 7 representative tests (`test_simple_class` / `test_inheritance` / `test_break_continue` / `test_edge_arrays` / `test_array_methods` / `test_async` / `test_gap_async_advanced` / `test_rest_params`) all pass byte-identical to Node.

## v0.5.789 — Refs #645 deeper followup / #488 drizzle-sqlite: HIR default-arg fill loop now pushes `Expr::Undefined` for slots whose param has no default, instead of skipping them — so a later `Some` default lands at the correct positional index.

Pre-fix, `tableBase(name, columns, extraConfig?, schema?, baseName=name)` called with 3 args (the load-bearing drizzle shape) had `defaults[3] = None` (`schema`) and `defaults[4] = Some(name)` (`baseName`); the loop's `if let Some(default_expr) = &defaults[i]` arm `continue`d past slot 3 and pushed baseName's default into the schema slot, so `schema` got the table name (`"users"`) and `baseName` was missing.

End-to-end symptom in drizzle-sqlite: rendered SQL became `INSERT INTO "users"."users" (...)` instead of `INSERT INTO "users" (...)` because the SQL builder's schema-prefix path saw a non-undefined `schema` value where Node sees `undefined`.

The fix in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs:5433-5460` synthesizes `Expr::Undefined` for None-default slots and pushes BOTH the param-map entry (so subsequent defaults can still reference earlier params via `substitute_param_refs_in_default`) AND the args vector (so positional arity matches the callee's declared param count).

Verified end-to-end: synthetic `tableBase("users", {id:1}, undefined)` returns `schema=undef baseName=users`; mid-call `f(a, b?, c="C")` with `f("A", "B")` correctly resolves to `c="C"`. Sanity: 11/11 class-related regression tests + new regression `test_issue_645_default_fill_positional.ts` byte-identical to Node, gap suite 27/28 (the 1 failure `test_gap_console_methods` is the pre-existing timing-sensitive `console.time` divergence unrelated to this fix).

## v0.5.788 — fix(ui-macos): closes #640 — geisterhand label decoding + SecureField `/type` onChange parity

Closes the residual surface around #640. v0.5.765's partial fix made `/value` distinguish empty-but-found from widget-not-found via the `\u{0}\u{0}NF` sentinel, but the user-reported "value cleared after swap" symptom didn't reproduce in a minimal repro and a fuller 3-route NavStack repro (signin → orders → addShop, exercising the Add Shop flow's load-bearing case) confirms the underlying NSTextField *does* persist across `setHidden` correctly — handles stay valid for both `/type` and `/value` after `screen.set(...)`, onChange fires, and the user-side captures (`shopName`, `shopBaseUrl`, …) get populated as expected. What this commit fixes is the two related papercuts that were *masking* triage of the original report:

**(1) Mangled labels in `/widgets`** — `perry_geisterhand_register` and `perry_geisterhand_register_with_shortcut` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/geisterhand_registry.rs` decoded the inbound `*const StringHeader` label using `mem::size_of::<[u64; 1]>()` (8 bytes) as the data offset. But `StringHeader` is 5 × `u32` = 20 bytes, so `data` actually pointed into the `capacity` / `refcount` / `flags` fields rather than the UTF-8 payload — every label in `/widgets` JSON came back as a few bytes of internal-bookkeeping noise (often whitespace because `capacity`'s lo-byte typically falls in the 0x10–0x40 range for short placeholder strings). Fix consolidates the inline decode at both call sites into a single `decode_label_from_string_header` helper that uses `(*header).byte_len` (offset 4) and `mem::size_of::<StringHeader>()` (offset 20) — matching the existing `widgets/textfield.rs::str_from_header` decoder. Labels now show the actual placeholder text (e.g. `"email"`, `"shop name"`, `"API key"`), not whitespace.

**(2) SecureField didn't register with geisterhand** — `widgets/securefield.rs::create` set up the AppKit text-change observer but never called `perry_geisterhand_register`, so `/type/<secure_handle>` would set the string value via `js_register_textfield_set_string` but the post-set REGISTRY-driven onChange dispatch in `geisterhand_registry.rs::PendingAction::SetText` would find no entry for the handle and silently skip the user's onChange callback. Real users typing into a password field (via AppKit's responder chain) hit `text_did_change` and got the callback, but the geisterhand smoke-test path bypassed it entirely. Added the cfg-gated `perry_geisterhand_register(handle, 1, 1, on_change, placeholder_ptr)` call in `securefield::create` (re-using `WIDGET_TEXTFIELD = 1` and `CB_ON_CHANGE = 1`; `NSSecureTextField` is-a `NSTextField` at AppKit, so the runtime's `perry_ui_textfield_set_string` works against secure fields with no further plumbing).

**Validation**: 3-route ad-hoc probe (signin → orders → addShop, 4 TextFields on addShop) — labels render correctly in `/widgets`, `/type` against any addShop TextField returns `ok:true`, `/value` returns the typed text, Save click logs all 4 user-side captures correctly. SecureField probe — `/type/<secure_handle>` now fires onChange and the user's `pw` capture gets populated. Existing `test-files/test_issue_640_navstack_textfield.ts` (added in v0.5.765) still mirrors the issue's exact 2-route reproducer; no changes there.

**Out of scope**: cross-platform parity (the SecureField fix lives in the macOS UI crate; iOS / GTK4 / Windows secure-input widgets have their own observer / FFI shapes — if `/type` smoke-driving secure inputs becomes a need on those targets, a parallel registration pass on each platform crate is the followup).

## v0.5.787 — feat(perry/ui): WebView v2 follow-ups (issue #658). Closes the four deferred items from the Phase 1-5 rollout: real Android navigation callbacks, persistent-cookie support on Windows + GTK4, web target postMessage docs, and resolution for #99. **v2-A (Android `WebViewClient` helper class).** Phase 3 (v0.5.786) shipped Android with stock `WebViewClient` and stored-but-not-delivered hooks. v2-A adds `crates/perry-ui-android/template/app/src/main/java/com/perry/app/PerryWebViewClient.kt` — a real `WebViewClient` subclass that proxies `shouldOverrideUrlLoading`, `onPageFinished`, and `onReceivedError` back through the JNI bridge to native Rust. Each Perry WebView gets its own `PerryWebViewClient(widgetHandle)` instance; the `widgetHandle` lets `nativeWebViewShouldNavigate` / `nativeWebViewLoaded` / `nativeWebViewError` look up the right `WebViewState` entry. Plus `PerryWebViewEvalCallback` — a `ValueCallback<String>` subclass that delivers `evaluateJavascript` results to Rust via per-call `callbackKey` (atomic counter, drained from `EVAL_CALLBACKS` on `nativeWebViewEvalResult`). The Rust side (`crates/perry-ui-android/src/widgets/webview.rs`) gains four `Java_com_perry_app_PerryBridge_native*` JNI exports that handle the routing — including the same allowlist-gate-before-user-closure pattern as Apple/Windows/GTK4, JSON-quoted-string unwrapping for plain `evaluateJavascript` results, and `js_is_truthy` boolean coercion for `onShouldNavigate` returns. Extends `PerryBridge.kt` with four new `external fun native*` declarations. End state: Android now has full callback parity with macOS / iOS / visionOS / Windows / GTK4 — `onShouldNavigate` (sync intercept), `onLoaded(url)`, `onError(code, msg)`, and async `evaluateJavascript` results all deliver. **v2-B (`set_ephemeral` deferred-init on Windows + GTK4 + macOS family).** The original v0.5.785 Windows backend documented `set_ephemeral` as a no-op because WebView2's `userDataFolder` is fixed at env-creation time; v0.5.786 GTK4 had the same constraint with `WebKitNetworkSession`. The fix changes `perry_ui_webview_create`'s FFI signature from `(url, w, h)` to `(url, w, h, ephemeral_hint)` so the data-store choice is made BEFORE any navigation kicks off. Codegen (`crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call/native.rs`) extracts `ephemeral` from the AnonShape and passes it through `sitofp` (i64 → f64) as the 4th create() arg, defaulting to `1.0` (ephemeral) when omitted. Dispatch table row updated to 4 args. **Backend changes**: Windows now branches at `userDataFolder` selection between `%TEMP%\PerryWebView\<pid>-<tag>` (ephemeral) and `%LOCALAPPDATA%\PerryWebView\persistent` (persistent — cookies survive across app launches, mirroring how Edge / Chrome's profile dir works). GTK4 branches at `WebKitNetworkSession::new_ephemeral()` vs `NetworkSession::new(data_dir, cache_dir)` with XDG-aware `~/.local/share/perry-webview` and `~/.cache/perry-webview` paths via the new `dirs = "5"` dep. macOS / iOS / visionOS apply the choice to `WKWebViewConfiguration.websiteDataStore` (`nonPersistentDataStore` vs `defaultDataStore`) at construction time so the very first navigation sees the right data store. Android calls `wipe_process_storage()` at create time when ephemeral is requested (Android WebView shares storage process-wide; this is the best-effort substitute). The post-create `set_ephemeral(handle, value)` FFI is preserved for backward-compat but is now redundant — documented in the runtime `set_ephemeral` docstrings as a no-op on backends with construction-time data-store choices. tvOS / watchOS stubs accept the 4th arg and ignore it. Web target accepts and ignores (iframe shares parent storage). **v2-C (web target cross-origin postMessage docs).** Resolves the design ambiguity in v0.5.786's web-target iframe stub. Cross-origin `postMessage` is a TS-side concern, not a cross-platform Perry API: user code calls `window.addEventListener("message", e => ...)` to receive frames from the iframe, and `webviewEvaluateJs(wv, "window.postMessage(...)")` to send into the iframe. The JSDoc on `types/perry/ui/index.d.ts::WebView` now includes the working pattern and explicitly notes that this is browser-only — native targets don't expose `postMessage` because that's the Tauri/Electron path Perry's WebView avoids. **v2-D (#99 Notifications WebView bridge — closed wontfix).** Closed per the #658 design discussion: a JS→native bridge for `Notification` API inside an embedded WebView is exactly the slippery slope toward Tauri/Electron-style native↔JS RPC. Workarounds documented in the close comment: (1) polling `webviewEvaluateJs` on `setInterval`, draining a JS-side queue; (2) web-target `postMessage` for browser-deployed Perry apps; (3) Tauri/Electron for apps whose primary surface is web content with native bridges. Reopenable in the future if a use case surfaces that genuinely needs the bridge and isn't served by the workarounds, but the surface would need to be a separate `webviewBridge()` opt-in API with documented Tauri-adjacent semantics. **End state**: WebView is now a complete primitive across all 8 Perry platforms with feature-parity callback delivery (where the OS APIs allow it), real persistent-cookie support, and clear documentation on the deliberate scope boundary between this and Tauri/Electron-style app shells.

## v0.5.786 — feat(perry/ui): WebView Phases 3, 4, 5 — Android / GTK4 / web target (issue #658, completing the cross-platform rollout). Three additional backends ship in this release; with v0.5.785's Windows phase already in, all 8 Perry-supported platforms now have a real or stub-with-state `WebView` implementation. **Phase 3 — Android** in `crates/perry-ui-android/src/widgets/webview.rs` (~330 LOC). Uses the existing `jni 0.21` bridge to instantiate `android.webkit.WebView` with the stock `WebViewClient` (so navigations stay inside the WebView instead of opening the system browser). Enables JavaScript and DOM storage on the `WebSettings`. Imperative ops route through JNI: `loadUrl(String)` / `reload()` / `goBack()` / `goForward()` / `canGoBack()` / `evaluateJavascript(String, ValueCallback<String>)` / `setUserAgentString`. Cookie isolation: `ephemeral: true` calls `CookieManager.removeAllCookies(null)` + `WebStorage.deleteAllData()` on init (best-effort — Android WebViews share storage process-wide; true per-WebView isolation needs a separate process). The `onShouldNavigate` / `onLoaded` / `onError` hooks are stored on the widget but not yet delivered — a custom `WebViewClient` subclass is required to override `shouldOverrideUrlLoading` / `onPageFinished` / `onReceivedError`, which needs a Perry-specific helper Java class deployed alongside the runtime APK. v1 ships the load-bearing surface that unblocks OAuth flows (open URL, user authenticates, the host page detects the callback URL via polling `evaluateJavascript("window.location.href")`); the helper-class iteration is tracked as a follow-up. Likewise `evaluate_js` fires the user callback synchronously with the empty string today (the Android `evaluateJavascript` API is async-callback-based; without a helper class we can't bridge `ValueCallback<String>` back to Rust). The Java-side script still runs — only the result delivery is stubbed. **Phase 4 — Linux/GTK4** in `crates/perry-ui-gtk4/src/widgets/webview.rs` (~280 LOC). Uses the new `webkit6 = "=0.4"` dep (last release pinned to `gtk4 0.9` matching the rest of perry-ui-gtk4; 0.5+ moved to gtk4 0.10 which would break the workspace gtk4 unification). Real implementations for everything: `decide-policy::navigation-action` is the sync intercept point matching WKWebView's `decidePolicyForNavigationAction` semantics — user closure returns falsy → `decision.ignore()` cancels the load. `load-changed::Finished` fires `onLoaded`; `load-failed` delivers `onError(code, message)` via `glib::Error`. `evaluate_javascript(...)` is async-callback-based and stringifies the result via `JSCValue::to_string`. Cookie isolation defaults ephemeral via `WebKitNetworkSession::new_ephemeral()`. `clear_cookies` walks `WebKitCookieManager::delete_all_cookies`. Build dep: `libwebkitgtk-6.0-dev` (Ubuntu 22.10+ / Debian 12+) — added to the documented Linux deps in a follow-up docs PR. **Phase 5 — Web target / WASM** in `crates/perry-codegen-wasm/src/wasm_runtime.js` (~70 LOC). Renders an `<iframe>` with `sandbox="allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-forms allow-popups"`. Cross-origin browser restrictions limit what we can hook: `onShouldNavigate` is best-effort (allowlist gate runs at the JS layer for any URL we set; cross-origin URLs that the iframe later navigates to are unreachable from JS for security reasons); `onLoaded` fires from the iframe's `load` event for any successful nav; `onError` from the `error` event (same-origin only). `evaluateJs` only works on same-origin frames (cross-origin `contentWindow.eval` throws). User-agent is browser-controlled. Cookie isolation: iframe shares parent storage; opening in a fresh tab is the only true isolation path on the web. Documented limitation in the FFI surface comments. **End state**: real backends on macOS / iOS / visionOS / Windows / Linux + best-effort iframe on web + JS-loadUrl-only on Android. The 14 FFIs are link-stable across all 8 platforms. tvOS / watchOS keep their stubs (no WebKit on those Apple platforms). **Out of scope (next iteration)**: Android `WebViewClient` helper class (perry-android-helpers JAR) for real callback delivery; cross-origin iframe message bridge on web; `set_ephemeral` deferred-init opt-out on Windows / GTK4 for explicit persistent-cookie use cases.

## v0.5.785 — feat(perry/ui): WebView Phase 2 — Windows backend via WebView2 (issue #658). Real Windows implementation in `crates/perry-ui-windows/src/widgets/webview.rs` (~640 LOC) replacing v0.5.784's stub. Uses `webview2-com = "=0.34"` (the last release pinned to `windows = "0.58"` matching perry-ui-windows). **Architecture**: a STATIC child HWND becomes the registered widget handle; an `ICoreWebView2Controller` is bound to that HWND with bounds set via `SetBounds`. WebView2's two-stage async init (`CreateCoreWebView2EnvironmentWithOptions` → `CreateCoreWebView2Controller`) is wrapped synchronously by pumping the message queue (`PeekMessageW` / `WaitMessage`) until both completion handlers fire — typical resolve time is tens of ms on a warm WebView2 cache, with a 10-second timeout to bail cleanly when the runtime is missing. **Navigation hooks** match the macOS / iOS / visionOS contract one-for-one: `add_NavigationStarting` for `onShouldNavigate` (sync intercept — calls `args.SetCancel(true)` when the user closure returns falsy or the host fails the allowlist gate); `add_NavigationCompleted` dispatches `onLoaded(url)` on success or `onError(status, message)` on failure. **Cookie isolation defaults ephemeral** via a per-handle temp `userDataFolder` under `%TEMP%\PerryWebView\<pid>-<tag>`. `clear_cookies` walks `ICoreWebView2_2.CookieManager.DeleteAllCookies`. `set_user_agent` casts to `ICoreWebView2Settings2` and calls `SetUserAgent`. **Layout integration**: a `WM_SIZE` subclass on the host HWND forwards bounds changes to `controller.SetBounds(rect)`. **`evaluate_js`** uses `webview.ExecuteScript` with an `ExecuteScriptCompletedHandler`; the v1 impl unwraps JSON-encoded plain string returns for ergonomic `document.cookie` reads. **Out of scope (next iteration)**: Phase 3 — Android `android.webkit.WebView` via JNI; Phase 4 — Linux/GTK4 via WebKitGTK 6.0; Phase 5 — web target via `<iframe>`.

## v0.5.784 — feat(perry/ui): cross-platform native `WebView` component (issue #658, Phase 1: macOS / iOS / visionOS). Closes the request from discussion #651 (auth flows, payment redirects, embedded admin/CMS pages) and unblocks issue #99 (Notifications WebView bridge) and the cheaper-path Windows-MapView discussion (#559) that all converge on the same primitive. **Scope discipline.** This is a "browser tab embedded in your native widget tree" primitive — explicit non-goals: Tauri/Electron-style native↔JS RPC bridge, custom protocol/scheme handlers, DevTools, file downloads, WebGL/camera/mic/clipboard permission negotiation, persistent shared cookie jars, service workers, WebRTC. If a user wants Tauri/Electron, they should use Tauri/Electron. **TypeScript surface** in `types/perry/ui/index.d.ts`: `WebView({ url, allowedDomains?, userAgent?, ephemeral?, onShouldNavigate?, onLoaded?, onError?, width?, height? }) → Widget` plus 7 imperative methods (`webviewLoadUrl` / `webviewReload` / `webviewGoBack` / `webviewGoForward` / `webviewCanGoBack` / `webviewEvaluateJs` / `webviewClearCookies`). The sync `onShouldNavigate(url) → boolean` intercept works because every backend's "should-load" hook is itself sync on the main thread (WKWebView `decidePolicyForNavigationAction`, WebView2 `NavigationStarting`, Android `shouldOverrideUrlLoading`, WebKitGTK `decide-policy`). User-side OAuth pattern: open the auth URL, intercept `https://myapp.com/oauth/callback?code=` in `onShouldNavigate`, extract the `code` query param, return `false` to block the actual nav. **Phase 1 implementation** in `crates/perry-ui-{macos,ios,visionos}/src/widgets/webview.rs` (~530 LOC × 3 — nearly identical files; iOS / visionOS share the UIKit-flavored `UIView` codepath, macOS is the AppKit `NSView` variant). Single Rust pattern across all three: a `PerryWebViewDelegate` (NSObject subclass conforming to `WKNavigationDelegate`) holds an ivar key into a thread-local `WEBVIEW_STATES: HashMap<usize, WebViewState>` registry. Per-handle state carries the user's TS closure pointers, the allowed-domains list, and a weak reference back to the WKWebView pointer for imperative ops. Delegate methods: `decidePolicyForNavigationAction:` (gates first on the static allowlist — a hard host match without TS round-trip — then falls through to user closure if registered), `didFinishNavigation:` (fires `onLoaded(url)`), `didFailNavigation:withError:` + `didFailProvisionalNavigation:withError:` (both route to the user's `onError(code, message)`). `evaluate_js` uses `block2::RcBlock` for the WKWebView `completionHandler:` block, stringifies the result via `description` (works for `NSString` / `NSNumber` / `NSDictionary` / `NSArray` / `NSNull`), and fires the user callback with the resulting string. `clear_cookies` walks `WKWebsiteDataStore.removeData(ofTypes:modifiedSince:completionHandler:)` with the full data-types set and epoch date. **Cookie isolation** defaults to `ephemeral: true` (`WKWebsiteDataStore.nonPersistent()`) — auth flows reusing a logged-in browser session is usually a footgun. Opt out via `ephemeral: false` (uses `defaultDataStore`). **Allowlist semantics**: empty/omitted = no host restriction; otherwise exact-match OR subdomain match (`["example.com"]` allows `example.com` and `*.example.com`). Disallowed hosts are blocked at the native delegate without the TS round-trip — defense-in-depth against hijacked OAuth pages redirecting to arbitrary origins. **FFI surface (14 functions)** in each Apple-platform `lib.rs`. **Dispatch entries** in `crates/perry-dispatch/src/lib.rs`: 14 `MethodRow`s. **Codegen** in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call/native.rs` adds a `WebView({...})` AnonShape destructure block (mirrors the `App({...})` and `Image({...})` patterns); the handle is returned NaN-boxed with POINTER_TAG. **Stub link-stability** for the 5 platforms not in Phase 1 (Windows, Android, GTK4, tvOS, watchOS): all 14 FFIs appended to each `lib.rs` returning `0` / no-op. Cross-platform programs that import `WebView` link cleanly on every target. **API manifest**: 8 new `method("perry/ui", ...)` entries. **Out of scope (deferred to next phases)**: Phase 2 — Windows via `webview2-com` (~400 LOC); Phase 3 — Android via JNI to `android.webkit.WebView` (~500 LOC); Phase 4 — Linux/GTK4 via WebKitGTK 6.0 (~300 LOC); Phase 5 — Web target via `<iframe>`. Also deferred: cookie sharing across multiple WebView instances; POST navigation / form submits; Notifications WebView bridge (#99).

## v0.5.783 — Closes #645 deeper followup (drizzle-sqlite acceptance, ref #488): cross-module IIFE-pattern static properties on `function` declarations now survive the import boundary. drizzle's `sql.raw` / `sql.identifier` / `sql.fromList` / `sql.empty` / `sql.placeholder` / `sql.param` / `sql.join` all use the canonical npm-package IIFE pattern in `drizzle-orm/sql/sql.js`:

```js
function sql(strings, ...params) { ... }
((sql2) => {
  sql2.raw = function raw(str) { return new SQL([new StringChunk(str)]); };
  sql2.identifier = function identifier(value) { return new Name(value); };
  // ...
})(sql || (sql = {}));
```

**Root cause** in `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::ExternFuncRef` value-read arm. Pre-fix each consumer module emitted its own `__perry_extern_closure_<src>__<name>` global via `llmod.add_internal_constant(...)` with LLVM `internal` linkage. The consumer-side closure pointer therefore differed from the source module's singleton (returned by `Expr::FuncRef(id)` via `js_closure_alloc_singleton(@__perry_wrap_<original_name>)`). `CLOSURE_DYNAMIC_PROPS` is pointer-keyed — the IIFE inside the source module writes `fn.X = Y` to `CLOSURE_DYNAMIC_PROPS[source_ptr]["X"]`, but the consumer reads `fn.X` from `CLOSURE_DYNAMIC_PROPS[consumer_ptr]` which is a different bucket. Every IIFE-set static property vanished at the import boundary. The const-bound closure pattern (`const fn = function fn() {}; export { fn };`) already worked correctly because the getter path returns the same address from every consumer.

**Fix** unifies the two cases: both `function fn()` and `const fn = function fn() {}` now resolve to the same `ClosureHeader` singleton from any module that imports them. Consumer's `Expr::ExternFuncRef` value-reads for function-declaration imports now call `js_closure_alloc_singleton(@__perry_wrap_perry_fn_<src>__<name>)` — exactly the same call the source module uses for its own `Expr::FuncRef(id)` value-reads. `js_closure_alloc_singleton` keys its memoization pool on the `func_ptr` argument, so calling it from any module with the source's wrapper symbol address returns the *same* `ClosureHeader`. The source's wrapper symbol has external linkage (emitted via `llmod.define_function` without `internal`), so the LLVM-IR `@__perry_wrap_perry_fn_<src>__<name>` reference resolves at link time to the source module's definition — one shared address, one shared singleton, one shared CLOSURE_DYNAMIC_PROPS bucket. The consumer-side wrapper signature is declared as `double(i64, double × min(imported_param_count, 5))` purely so LLVM accepts the symbol reference as well-formed; the runtime's closure-call machinery casts the func_ptr to its own ABI at dispatch time, so the signature is informational. The pre-fix consumer-side `__perry_extern_closure_<src>__<name>` global and `__perry_wrap_extern_<src>__<name>` wrapper are no longer referenced from the value-read path; link-time DCE strips them.

**Validation**: minimal repro `test-files/test_issue_645_iife_xmod_static_props.ts` exercises the IIFE-pattern static-property surface — output byte-identical to Node (`typeof fn.raw === "function"`, `fn.raw("a") === "raw:a"`). End-to-end on drizzle-sqlite (`tests/release/packages/drizzle-sqlite/`) the SQL produced by `db.insert(users).values([...]).run()` now has the correct columns + VALUES placeholders: `insert into "users"."users" ("id", "name", "age") values (?, ?, ?), (?, ?, ?), (?, ?, ?)` (was: `insert into "users"."users" (?, ?, ?) ?` — columns missing, VALUES collapsed). The remaining `"users"."users"` table-prefix is a separate downstream bug — drizzle's Table chunk reads `chunk[Table.Symbol.Schema]` and gets the table *name* back instead of `undefined`, suggesting a symbol-keyed instance-property dispatch issue. Tracked as the next iteration.

Sanity: 237/237 perry-runtime unit tests + 27/28 gap-suite tests byte-identical to Node (the 1 failure, `test_gap_console_methods`, is the pre-existing timing-sensitive `console.time` divergence). The fix doesn't change behavior for any path that doesn't go through `Expr::ExternFuncRef` value-read for function-declaration imports — direct cross-module calls (`Call { callee: ExternFuncRef }`), const-binding closures, class references, and namespace-import members all take unchanged paths. Refs #645, #488.

## v0.5.781 — fix(stream): closes #631 — `new Readable(opts)` / `Writable` / `Duplex` / `Transform` / `PassThrough` constructors and `Readable.from(iterable)` now return host objects whose method names are real callables (`typeof r.on === "function"` / `typeof r.pipe === "function"`), matching Node byte-for-byte instead of the pre-fix `undefined`. **Root cause**: pre-fix the `node:stream` module surfaced its classes through the QuickJS-only stub at `crates/perry-jsruntime/src/modules.rs:691` (`export class Readable { constructor() {} read() {} on() { return this; } pipe() { return this; } }`). That stub is consulted only on the V8/QuickJS interop path. Perry's AOT codegen pipeline imports the same names through `register_native_module(local, "stream", Some(imported))` and `new Readable(opts)` falls into `lower_new`'s generic Ident-callee arm in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_new.rs:646`, producing `Expr::New { class_name: "Readable", args, type_args: [] }`. With no user class registered for that name, the codegen for `Expr::New` allocated an empty placeholder `ObjectHeader` (no fields, no class_id) — so `r.on` / `r.pipe` reads landed on missing fields and returned `undefined`. Same shape collapsed `Readable.from([1, 2, 3])`: the unimplemented-API gate at `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs:1188` had no `from` entry under module `"stream"` and bailed with ``\`stream.from\` is not implemented in Perry``. **Fix**, four coordinated additions. **(1)** New runtime stub at `crates/perry-runtime/src/node_stream.rs` mirrors the closure-fields ObjectHeader pattern that `crates/perry-runtime/src/fs.rs::build_stream_object` uses for fs streams: allocate an `ObjectHeader` with one field per Node Readable method name (`on` / `once` / `off` / `addListener` / `removeListener` / `removeAllListeners` / `emit` / `listenerCount` / `listeners` / `read` / `pipe` / `unpipe` / `pause` / `resume` / `destroy` / `setEncoding` / `isPaused`), each value a NaN-boxed closure pointer to a stub function with the right arity for its Node call signature. Each closure captures the host object's NaN-boxed bits in slot 0 — the `this_value` helper reconstructs the f64 form so chain-returning stubs (`on` / `once` / `pause` / etc.) return `this`, preserving identity through `r.on(...).on(...).pipe(b)`. `pipe(dest)` returns `dest` so `r.pipe(a).pipe(b)` works per Node's spec. `read(n)` returns null (Node's "no data available"); `emit(...)` returns `false` ("no listeners fired"). Three sibling tables for Writable (16 methods incl. `write` / `end` / `cork` / `uncork`), Duplex (22 methods, union of Readable + Writable, deduped on the EventEmitter-side names), and Transform / PassThrough (delegated to Duplex's table since the `transform` / `flush` callbacks aren't wired through). Each table picks its own shape id band (READABLE_SHAPE_ID = 0x7FFF_FE60, WRITABLE = 0x7FFF_FE70, DUPLEX = 0x7FFF_FE80) so the runtime's shape cache doesn't alias them with fs streams (0x7FFF_FE40). Six entry points exposed: `js_node_stream_readable_new` / `_writable_new` / `_duplex_new` / `_transform_new` / `_passthrough_new` / `_readable_from` (the static `from(iterable)` accepts and discards its arg — same stub object). The closure-fields shape is intentionally chosen over the EventEmitter handle pattern (`new EventEmitter()` has the same `typeof handle.on === "undefined"` problem because handle property reads don't return prototype methods) because the host-object form makes property reads return real callables, satisfying `typeof r.on === "function"` directly. **(2)** Codegen wiring extended `lower_builtin_new` in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call/builtin.rs` with arms for `"Readable" | "Writable" | "Duplex" | "Transform" | "PassThrough"`, each lowering the optional `opts` arg to f64 (defaulting to `TAG_UNDEFINED`) and emitting a `call DOUBLE js_node_stream_*_new(opts)`. Sits next to the existing `EventEmitter` arm. Six matching extern declarations added to `crates/perry-codegen/src/runtime_decls.rs`. **(3)** `Readable.from` static — added `method("stream", "from", false, None)` to `crates/perry-api-manifest/src/entries.rs` so the unimplemented-API gate lets the call through, then a NATIVE_MODULE_TABLE row in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` mapping `(module="stream", has_receiver=false, method="from")` → `js_node_stream_readable_from` with `args: &[NA_F64], ret: NR_F64`. `Readable.from(...)` resolves through the existing `Readable.foo` → `stream.foo` route at expr_call.rs:1143 — the imported `Readable` is registered as a native module reference for `"stream"`, so `Readable.from(arg)` lowers as `Expr::NativeMethodCall { module: "stream", class_name: None, object: None, method: "from", args }`, dispatched through the table. **(4)** Pre-existing `withFileTypes` Dirent half — `js_fs_readdir_sync(path, options)` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/fs.rs:303` already honored `options.withFileTypes === true` and built proper Dirent objects with `name` / `isFile` / `isDirectory` / `isSymbolicLink` closure-based predicates as of v0.5.340; the issue's repro for that half passes unchanged. **Validation**: the issue's exact 18-line repro now produces output byte-identical to Node — `entry[0] typeof: object` / `entry[0] is string: false` / `entry[0].isFile type: function` / `Readable.from typeof: object` / `Readable.from .on type: function` / `Readable.from .pipe type: function` / `new Readable .on type: function` / `new Readable .pipe type: function`. `cargo build --release` clean (no new warnings). **Out of scope** (would belong in a dedicated streams runtime PR): real data flow through `read` / `write` / `pipe` (the stubs no-op and return `this` for chain compatibility); EventEmitter side-table for `emit('data', chunk)` to fan out to registered listeners; backpressure / highWaterMark; async-iteration protocol (`for await (const chunk of r)`); the full constructor option surface (`{ encoding, objectMode, autoDestroy, signal, ... }` — currently the opts arg is accepted and discarded). The acceptance criterion is a non-crashing surface that lets stream-using code compile and link; richer semantics will land when a workload surfaces them as the next blocker. **Issue #640** (NavStack TextField geisterhand probe) was investigated in tandem and remains open. The v0.5.765 fix (distinguishing empty-string from widget-not-found via the `\u0\u0NF` sentinel) clarified the diagnostic but the underlying widget-lookup-after-route-swap behavior needs interactive macOS UI tracing to confirm root cause: WIDGETS Vec is thread-local on the main thread, `set_hidden` doesn't remove from it, and `cleanup_widget_maps` only touches the auxiliary maps — none of the static-analysis hypotheses explain why `get_widget(handle)` would return None for a previously-active route's TextField after route swap. Deferred to a separate session with live `/value/:handle` instrumentation.

## v0.5.780 — Closes #645 deeper followup: three coordinated bugs blocking drizzle-sqlite acceptance (#488). Original #645 closed the mid-chain `(number).<method> is not a function` crash; running drizzle-sqlite end-to-end past that fix surfaced three further bugs along the same chain. **Bug 1 (compile-side rest-param propagation through `export *`)** in `crates/perry/src/commands/compile.rs::propagate exported_func_param_counts`: the propagation loop carried param counts through `ExportAll` / `ReExport` / `Named` chains but **not** the parallel `exported_func_has_rest` flag. So a `function f(...params)` declared in `driver.js` and re-exported via `export * from "./driver.js"` from the barrel `index.js` had `(driver.js, "f")` → `has_rest=true` registered, but `(index.js, "f")` was never written. Consumers importing `f` from the barrel module looked up `(index.js, "f")` in `exported_func_has_rest`, missed → `imported_has_rest` didn't include `f` → no rest bundling at the call site → `function f(...params)` ran with `params` as raw f64 args instead of a bundled array → `params[0]` indexed into a non-array and read undefined. Drizzle hits this exactly: `drizzle-orm/better-sqlite3/index.js` does `export * from "./driver.js"`; entry.ts does `import { drizzle } from "drizzle-orm/better-sqlite3"`. Symptom: `drizzle(sqlite)` inside drizzle's body had `params[0] === undefined`, took the `params[0] === void 0 || typeof === "string"` branch, allocated a fresh `new Client()` (heap-allocated wrapper, NOT the small-handle Database the user passed), and every downstream `this.client.prepare(...)` failed with `prepare is not a function` because the heap wrapper had no dispatch in `js_handle_method_dispatch`. Fix: extended the propagation tuple from `(key, param_count)` to `(key, param_count, has_rest)` and copy `has_rest` into the destination map alongside `param_count`. **Bug 2 (runtime constructor stub for small-handle native instances)** in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs` — added `"constructor"` intercept in BOTH small-handle property-read paths (the NaN-boxed branch in `js_object_get_field_by_name` and the IC-miss branch in `js_object_get_field_ic_miss`). Drizzle's `isConfig(data)` duck-types via `data.constructor.name !== "Object"`. Pre-fix `data.constructor` returned `undefined` for small-handle native instances (better-sqlite3 Database), then with #648's strict catch-all `(undefined).name` threw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'name')` and drizzle has no `try`/`catch` around isConfig so the whole call collapsed. Post-fix the intercept returns the existing `NULL_OBJECT_BYTES` stub — a real ObjectHeader-shape with no fields, so `(stub).name` returns undefined safely (no throw), and `undefined !== "Object"` makes isConfig return false at the first gate (correct: native-handle instances aren't config objects). The IC-miss path is the one the codegen actually reaches for class-field property reads on a small-handle stored in a typed `any` field — the runtime-fn path only fires when the receiver flows in as a NaN-boxed POINTER not stripped by the IC. Mirror the constructor stub there too for safety. **Bug 3 (runtime sqlite Database method dispatch missing)** in `crates/perry-stdlib/src/common/dispatch.rs` — added `dispatch_sqlite_db` mirroring the existing `dispatch_sqlite_stmt` (v0.5.764) for `db.prepare(sql)` / `.exec(sql)` / `.close()`. The Statement counterpart handled `raw|all|get|run` for stmt method calls reaching the dynamic-dispatch path (drizzle's `this.stmt.raw().all(...params)` chain); the Database counterpart was missing, so when `this.client.prepare(...)` reached `js_handle_method_dispatch` (because drizzle's `this.client` is typed `any`), there was no arm — fell through to the catch-all → undefined → next chained call crashed. Method-gated on `prepare|exec|close` to avoid claiming small handles owned by other registries. Routes through `extern "C"` so the linked impl wins (perry-stdlib vs perry-ext-better-sqlite3), keeping handle and lookup TypeIds consistent. **Validation**: minimal repro for the export-* rest-bundling regression (`test-files/test_issue_645_has_rest_export_star.ts`) — same-module rest-fn smoke that would catch any breakage in the call-site bundling path. End-to-end on drizzle-sqlite the chain now reaches the SQL-builder layer with the correct `db_handle=1` and a valid SQL pointer; the test still fails downstream because drizzle's SQL template literals compile to malformed SQL (`insert into "users"."users" (?, ?, ?) ?` — table name doubled, columns are `?` placeholders, VALUES clause missing) — that's a drizzle-template-literal compilation issue separate from #645's class-method-internal dispatch and tracked in the next iteration. Sanity: 237/237 perry-runtime unit tests + 27/28 gap-suite tests byte-identical to Node (the 1 failure, `test_gap_console_methods`, is the pre-existing timing-sensitive `console.time` divergence). **Refs**: #645, #488, #643, #648.

## v0.5.779 — fix(ui-windows): URL-fetched images via WinHTTP + GDI+ (closes #635 for Windows; followup to v0.5.778). `Image(url, alt)` and `ImageGallery` now actually display the remote image instead of a stub label. Implementation mirrors the macOS NSURL/NSData path one-for-one but using native Windows APIs — no new Rust deps. Background thread fetches via **WinHTTP** (`WinHttpOpen` → `Connect` → `OpenRequest` → `SendRequest` → `ReceiveResponse` → loop `WinHttpQueryDataAvailable` + `ReadData`); body bytes capped at 64 MB; HTTPS works via `WINHTTP_FLAG_SECURE`. Bytes are stored in a process-global `HWND_URL_BYTES: Mutex<HashMap<isize, Vec<u8>>>` keyed by HWND, then a `PostMessage(IMAGE_URL_LOADED_MSG = 0x0901)` is dispatched to the image HWND. The `image_wnd_proc` handles that message by calling `InvalidateRect`, which triggers a fresh `WM_PAINT`. The paint path now prefers URL bytes over the file path: when bytes are present, `SHCreateMemStream` wraps them as an `IStream` and `GdipLoadImageFromStream` decodes (PNG/JPEG/GIF/WebP all flow through the same GDI+ render path as file-loaded images, with the same stretch-to-fill + alpha-blend over ancestor backgrounds). Path-based images still work unchanged. **`set_url(handle, ptr)`** is exposed for `ImageGallery::set_index` — it clears the old bytes and kicks off a fresh fetch, so swapping pages re-fetches and re-paints. **ImageGallery rewrite**: was a stub-with-state STATIC label showing `[1/N] alt text`; now creates a single `PerryImage` HWND via `image::create_url("")` and swaps the URL on `set_index`. The same WM_PAINT path renders the current page. `on_index_change` callback still fires. **URL parsing** is a tiny inline `parse_url` (handles `http://` / `https://` + optional port + path; doesn't handle credentials or IPv6 literals — matches the macOS NSURL scope for image URLs in practice). New `Win32_Networking_WinHttp` feature added to `crates/perry-ui-windows/Cargo.toml`. **Out of scope (next iteration)**: explicit cache-control / If-Modified-Since for re-fetch on `set_url(same_url)`; cancellation when a widget is dismissed mid-fetch (current impl writes bytes to the map and PostMessage's; if the HWND is destroyed the PostMessage is dropped harmlessly, but the byte slot leaks until process exit). All bounded follow-ups; v1 ships the load-bearing happy path that maps directly to the macOS contract.

## v0.5.778 — fix(ui-windows): Windows UI parity sweep (followup to v0.5.776's #406 raw-mode work). Builds out 11 widget implementations on `crates/perry-ui-windows` that were previously stub FFIs returning 0 — the same v0.5.771-style "stubs matching the macOS shape exactly for link stability" pattern, but unblocked at the rendering layer. **Pre-existing build breaks fixed first** (the crate failed `cargo build` on main): `chart::draw_title` passed an immutable `&[u16]` slice to `DrawTextW`, which under the windows crate v0.58 binding requires `&mut [u16]`; and `layout.rs::measure_intrinsic`'s `match WidgetKind` didn't cover the post-v0.5.636 `Calendar` / `Combobox` / `TreeView` / `RichText` / `Chart` variants, so the crate failed E0004 non-exhaustive-patterns at compile time. Also fixed v0.5.776's #406 `tui/input.rs` + `readline.rs` Windows code which called `.is_null()` on `windows-sys` HANDLE (which is `isize`, not a pointer) — replaced with `h == 0 || h == -1` checks against `INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE`. v0.5.776 explicitly documented "no Windows toolchain available locally to compile-check"; we have one, so the gap is closed. **6 new real Win32 widget impls** (returned 0 before): (1) **`textarea`** — multi-line `EDIT` control with `ES_MULTILINE | ES_AUTOVSCROLL | ES_WANTRETURN | WS_VSCROLL` + suppress-during-set re-entrancy guard. (2) **`qrcode`** — pure-Rust `qrcodegen` crate decodes the text → QR matrix; an owner-drawn `STATIC` subclass paints black squares + 4-module quiet zone via `FillRect`. (3) **`table`** — full Win32 `LVS_REPORT | LVS_OWNERDATA` ListView with `LVN_GETDISPINFO` virtual-cell rendering, `LVN_ITEMCHANGED` selection callbacks, `LVN_COLUMNCLICK` toggle-direction sort, `LVS_EX_FULLROWSELECT | LVS_EX_GRIDLINES`. Render closures returning `Text(...)` widgets get strings extracted via `GetWindowTextW`; other widget kinds show `[widget]` placeholder. (4) **`command_palette`** (#477) — borderless popup HWND with `EDIT` search + `LISTBOX` of fuzzy-substring-matching commands; Enter runs / Esc closes / arrow-keys navigate; centered on primary monitor. (5) **`image_gallery`** — paged carousel showing the current page's URL/alt; `set_index` clamps + fires `on_index_change`. (6) **`rich_tooltip`** (#479) — `TrackMouseEvent` / `WM_MOUSEHOVER` / `WM_MOUSELEAVE` subclass on the trigger HWND drives a popup HWND that re-parents the content widget on show, detaches on hide. **3 stub-with-state widgets** (matching macOS FFI shape, real rendering deferred): `bottom_nav` (4 FFIs — horizontal row of `BUTTON` controls); `pdf_view` (6 FFIs — STATIC label `[PDF: name — page X/Y @ Z%]`; cheap-page-count by scanning `/Type /Page` markers; real WinUI `Windows.Data.Pdf` or PDFium deferred); `map_view` (#559, 5 FFIs — STATIC label `[Map (Standard) @ lat,lon — span H×W — N pins]`; real WinUI `MapControl` in XAML Islands needs Windows App SDK + WinUI 3 + Bing Maps API key, multi-day work tracked under #559). **`foreach_registry`** — added the cross-platform `ForEach(state, render)` re-render handler that was missing on Windows, registered in `app::register_cross_platform_text_handlers` via `js_register_foreach_render_handler`. Without it, `state.set(n)` on a count bound to a `ForEach` would silently no-op on Windows (#610). **System-module FFI parity audit** — final audit found exactly 1 macOS-only `perry_*` system FFI missing on Windows (`perry_on_layout_change`); added as a stub matching the macOS shape (which is itself a stub on every platform). All other system modules — geolocation, image_picker, network, deeplinks, background, notifications — already had real or stub-with-state Windows implementations. **End state**: Windows UI FFI surface is **275 functions** vs macOS's **277**; the 2 remaining gaps (`perry_ui_query_widget_tree` / `perry_ui_read_widget_value` / `perry_ui_scroll_set_offset`) are macOS-only geisterhand-fuzzer test helpers — not link-relevant for any Windows program. **Out of scope (filed as followups)**: real WinUI MapControl in XAML Islands (#559); real PDFium / `Windows.Data.Pdf` page-bitmap rendering for pdf_view (#516); table cell rendering of arbitrary widget trees. New `qrcodegen = "1.8"` dep on `crates/perry-ui-windows/Cargo.toml`.

## v0.5.777 — fix(runtime): closes #654 — Float64Array `typeof === 'object'` and `.sort` / `.at` / cross-kind copy-ctor now dispatch under AOT. **Root cause** was the same shape across all three sub-bugs: the typed-array constructor codegen (`crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::TypedArrayNew`) returns the raw heap pointer via `bitcast i64 → double` with no NaN-box tag — by design, since the runtime formatter and `js_array_*` dispatch helpers detect typed arrays via `TYPED_ARRAY_REGISTRY` instead of POINTER_TAG. But three downstream sites assumed POINTER_TAG-or-bust and silently misclassified the raw-bits form: (a) `js_value_typeof` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/builtins.rs` tested `is_pointer()` first and fell through to `"number"` for everything else (raw f64 bits with `top16 == 0` look like an IEEE double to that predicate); (b) `js_native_call_method` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs` had handle-dispatch and string-dispatch arms but no typed-array arm, so `(a: Float64Array).sort()` reached the `is_int32() || is_number()` branch in the primitive-method catch-all and threw `(number).sort is not a function`; (c) `js_typed_array_new` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/typedarray.rs` only matched POINTER_TAG (0x7FFD) source arrays, so `new Float64Array(otherTypedArray)` (raw-bits source) fell through to the `is_finite() && >= 0.0` branch and treated the heap address as a numeric length — empty result. **Three coordinated additions**: (1) `js_value_typeof` adds a tail arm checking `top16 == 0 && bits >= 0x10000 && lookup_typed_array_kind(addr).is_some()` → returns `"object"`. (2) `js_native_call_method` adds a typed-array dispatch arm right after the handle-dispatch block — same registry probe, then routes `length` / `at` / `sort` / `toSorted` / `toReversed` / `with` / `findLast` / `findLastIndex` to their existing `js_typed_array_*` runtime helpers. New `js_typed_array_sort_default` and `js_typed_array_sort_with_comparator` in `typedarray.rs` provide the in-place sort variants the dispatcher needs (the existing `to_sorted_*` helpers allocate a new array; `.sort()` is in-place per spec). (3) `js_typed_array_new` adds a raw-bits typed-array detection arm before the `Plain IEEE double` length-from-numeric branch; when the source is a registered typed array, copies through new `typed_array_copy_from_typed_array` which uses the per-kind `load_at` / `store_at` accessors so element values stay numeric across kind changes (`new Float64Array(int32Array)` produces correct floats, not raw byte reinterpretations). Same registry check added to `js_typed_array_new_from_array` for the rare case a typed-array pointer arrives misaddressed as `*const ArrayHeader`. (4) `js_array_sort_default` and `js_array_sort_with_comparator` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/array.rs` get the same typed-array dispatch as `js_array_to_sorted_*` already had — covers the path where the HIR rewrites `typedArr.sort(cmp)` to `Expr::ArraySort` and codegen emits a generic Array-helper call. Without this routing, `Int8Array.sort(cmp)` previously read the 1-byte-stride buffer as 8-byte f64s and corrupted memory. **Validation**: the issue's exact 8-line repro now prints `typeof a: object`, sorts to `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]`, and `new Float64Array(a)` reproduces the source contents. Extended fixture `test-files/issue_654_repro.ts` exercises `.at(0)` / `.at(-1)` / `.length`, cross-kind copy (`new Float64Array(int32Array)` → correct float values), `.toSorted()` (immutable variant), and `Int8Array.sort((x, y) => y - x)` (in-place comparator sort) — all match Node byte-for-byte. 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests pass. Existing typed-array regression tests (`test_gap_typed_arrays`, `test_inline_uint8array_param`, `test_issue_341_typed_field_native`, `test_json_typed_basic`) still pass. **Not fixed in this batch**: `typeof a.sort === 'function'` still returns `'undefined'`. Making the property read return a callable would require typed-array prototype-method binding via the property-get dispatch path (analogous to handle-property dispatch for net sockets / db connections); the call form `a.sort()` is what unblocks numeric-aggregation user code and was the actual blocker in the original bug report.

## v0.5.776 — feat(tui): closes #402 #403 #404 #406 — Table/Tabs/AnimatedSpinner/Input cursor + Windows raw mode + holistic dashboard. **#402 (Table + Tabs widgets).** `Table({headers, rows, selected?})` renders a 2D grid as a column-stacked Box: header row drawn bold, the optional `selected` row index (default −1 = none) drawn with reverse video, column widths auto-fit the longest header or cell. `Tabs({tabs, active, body})` renders a horizontal bar (active label reverse-video) followed by the active tab's body widget mounted below — `body[i]` is mounted only when `active === i`, matching React's null-render fallback for missing keys. Codegen unpacks `AnonShape` options via `extract_options_fields` and dispatches to `js_perry_tui_table` / `js_perry_tui_tabs` flat-args FFIs in `crates/perry-runtime/src/tui/ffi.rs`. **#403 (AnimatedSpinner).** `AnimatedSpinner({interval?, frames?})` is a Text widget whose frame is computed from process elapsed time at register; defaults to a 100 ms cycle through `["-", "\\", "|", "/"]`. A global once-spawned timer thread flips `STATE_DIRTY` every ~50 ms (Nyquist on the default cycle) so `run()` re-renders cleanly without user `setInterval` wiring. Outside `run()` (one-shot `render()`), only the snapshot prints — the timer still spawns to verify the path doesn't panic when called from a non-interactive context. **#404 (Input cursor positioning).** `Input(value, cursor)` 2-arg form decomposes the value into a horizontal Box of three Text widgets: `value.slice(0, cursor)` + reverse-video `value[cursor]` + `value.slice(cursor + 1)`. Cursor at `value.length` renders a trailing reverse-video space (matches the issue's spec). The 1-arg `Input(value)` form keeps drawing the trailing `_` cursor unchanged for callers that haven't migrated. The full `Node::Text { runs: Vec<StyledRun> }` per-cell-styled-runs refactor proposed in #404's description is left as future work — this 3-Text decomposition delivers the user-visible feature with a much smaller footprint, no regressions to existing Box / Text / render code paths. **#406 (Windows raw mode).** Wired `SetConsoleMode` on stdin (clears `ENABLE_LINE_INPUT` / `ENABLE_ECHO_INPUT` / `ENABLE_PROCESSED_INPUT`, sets `ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_INPUT` so arrow keys arrive as ANSI CSI sequences) and stdout (sets `ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_PROCESSING` so the renderer's CSI escapes move the cursor) for both perry-stdlib readline and perry-runtime tui::input. `windows-sys = "0.52"` cfg-gated dep on both crates. Saves original modes on enable() and restores on disable() so the user's shell post-run isn't left in raw mode. **Holistic test.** New `test-files/test_issue_402_403_404_405_406_holistic.ts` composes all five new surfaces in a single `render()` call: a truecolor header with per-side padding, a Tabs strip (active=2) wrapping a Table (selected=1), an AnimatedSpinner with custom braille frames, an Input with cursor mid-string, and a 50%/50% percentage-width row. Verifies the features compose in one widget tree without interfering — the per-issue smoke tests (#402 / #403 / #404 / #405) each cover their feature in isolation; the holistic test catches cross-feature regressions a single-feature suite would miss. **Files**: `crates/perry-runtime/src/tui/{ffi,layout,input}.rs` + `crates/perry-runtime/Cargo.toml` (windows-sys dep) + `crates/perry-stdlib/src/readline.rs` + `crates/perry-stdlib/Cargo.toml` (windows-sys dep) + `crates/perry-codegen/src/{lower_call,lower_call/native}.rs` + `types/perry/tui/index.d.ts` + 3 smoke tests + 1 holistic test. **Test plan**: `cargo test --release -p perry-runtime --lib tui::` 45 passed (24 new); each per-issue smoke test compiles + runs + emits the expected ANSI escapes byte-for-byte; the holistic test renders all five sections in a single coherent dashboard frame. Real Windows interactive smoke (arrow keys in a perry/tui run() program) is out of scope for the PR — code is `#[cfg(all(windows, not(unix)))]` gated and the windows-sys API surface (`STD_INPUT_HANDLE`, `GetConsoleMode`, `SetConsoleMode`, `ENABLE_*` flags) is correct per the 0.52 docs; no Windows toolchain available locally to compile-check. Falls under whoever has a Windows box in their CI loop.

## v0.5.775 — feat(tui): closes #405 — Phase 3.5 BoxStyle additions (24-bit truecolor on Text + per-side padding + flex-shrink + flex-basis + percentage units). Five additive changes layered on the v1.0 BoxStyle surface from #358 Phase 3. **(1) Truecolor.** `Color` enum extended with `Rgb(u8, u8, u8)` variant and `parse_color()` recognizing named palette / `#rrggbb` / `#rgb` strings; new `write_fg_sgr` / `write_bg_sgr` methods emit the truecolor `38;2;R;G;B` / `48;2;R;G;B` SGR payload (Cell now 16 bytes, was 8 — still fits comfortably in L1). **(2) Per-side padding.** `BoxStyle.padding` flipped from `u16` to `Edges { top, right, bottom, left }`; new `js_perry_tui_box_set_padding_each(handle, t, r, b, l)` FFI dispatched when `padding` is an object literal — the existing `padding: 4` numeric form is preserved as a fast path. **(3) Flex-shrink + flex-basis.** New `flexShrink: u16` and `flexBasis: Option<Length>` fields on `BoxStyle`, with matching FFIs and dispatch table entries. **(4) Percentage units.** New `Length::Cells(u16) | PercentBp(u16)` enum replaces bare `Option<u16>` for width/height/flexBasis; codegen recognizes string literals ending in `%` and routes to `*_pct` FFIs (`box_set_width_pct` / `box_set_height_pct` / `box_set_flex_basis_pct`) — uses basis points (1/100 of a percent) internally to avoid float arithmetic in the layout solver. **(5) Text styling 2-arg form.** New `Text(content, opts)` overload + `js_perry_tui_text_styled(content, fg, bg, style_bits)` FFI accepting `{ fg, bg, bold, italic, underline, reverse }` — the `Expr::New { class_name: "__AnonShape_…" }` shape lands here too via `extract_options_fields` (the HIR's closed-shape lowering rewrites object literals to AnonShape ctors). 24 new perry-runtime tests (Edges, Length, percent parsing, RGB SGR, per-side padding, percent width, Color parser); smoke fixture `test-files/test_issue_405_perry_tui_phase3_5.ts` exercises all five surfaces and emits the expected SGR escapes byte-for-byte (`\x1b[0;1;38;2;255;136;0;48;2;0;0;0m` for truecolor+bold; `\x1b[2;5H` for `padding: {top:1, left:4}`; `LEFT` at col 1 + `RIGHT` at col 21 for two 50%-width children inside a 40-cell row). Files: `crates/perry-runtime/src/tui/{color,style,layout,ffi,render}.rs` + `crates/perry-codegen/src/{lower_call,lower_call/native}.rs` + `types/perry/tui/index.d.ts`. **Drive-by**: added `bundled_staticlib_path_for_target` to `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/well_known.rs` (was referenced by `optimized_libs.rs` but never defined — pre-existing `cargo build -p perry` E0425 on main, blocked the smoke test from building).
## v0.5.782 — Closes #655 (AOT: array methods undefined when accessed via `Map.get(...).field`). The chained pattern `m.get(k)!.field.shift()` returned garbage and never mutated for `shift`/`splice`/`pop`/`slice`/`unshift`, and `field.length = N` writes were silently ignored — every npm driver that follows the maintainer-recommended `Map<id, State>` per-connection-FIFO pattern hit this on every reply. Aliasing to a local first (`const arr = s.pending; arr.shift()`) worked because the codegen's HIR-level array fast-path recognized the bare-identifier receiver. **Root cause** in `crates/perry-codegen/src/type_analysis.rs::static_type_of` (and the upstream `is_array_expr` which calls it): the `Expr::PropertyGet` arm only consulted `ctx.classes` to resolve the receiver's field type. `interface State { pending: number[] }` lives in `hir.interfaces`, not `hir.classes`, so `s: State` → `s.pending` returned `None` from `static_type_of` → `is_array_expr` returned false → the call fell out of the array-method dispatch into the generic property+call path, which has no clue how to invoke `Array.prototype` on a NaN-boxed array handle. `push` was special-cased elsewhere and accidentally worked; everything else didn't. **Fix (3 changes)**. **(1)** `crates/perry-codegen/src/codegen.rs::CrossModuleCtx`: new `interfaces: HashMap<String, perry_hir::Interface>` field, populated from `hir.interfaces` once at compile-module time. **(2)** `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::FnCtx`: new `interfaces` borrow, set from `&cross_module.interfaces` at all six FnCtx instantiation sites (`compile_function` / `compile_closure` / `compile_method` / `compile_module_entry` ×2 / `compile_static_method`). **(3)** `crates/perry-codegen/src/type_analysis.rs::static_type_of`: when the class-table lookup misses, fall through to `ctx.interfaces.get(&receiver_class)` and walk the `extends` chain — returning the property's declared type (`Array<Number>` for `pending: number[]`). With this in place, `is_array_expr(s.pending)` returns true → array-method dispatch lights up → `shift` / `pop` / `slice` / `length=` route to their existing runtime helpers. **Splice arm in `lower_array_method.rs`**: the chained path now reaches `lower_array_method`, but the function had no `"splice"` arm — chained `.splice()` fell through to the silent catch-all that returned the receiver unchanged (worse than the pre-fix `[object Object]` because it looked plausible). Added a splice arm mirroring `Expr::ArraySplice`'s codegen — calls `js_array_splice` with the existing 6-arg signature (recv, start, count, items_ptr, items_count, out_arr_slot), returns the deleted-elements handle NaN-boxed, and best-effort writeback to local-slot / module-global storage when the receiver is a single LocalGet (the property-chained case skips writeback for now — growth-induced reallocation is a corner case that doesn't fire on queue-shrink usage, which is the issue's hot path). Also imported `PTR` from `crate::types`. **Validation**: issue's exact repro (`m.get(1)!.pending.shift()` etc.) now byte-identical to Node for the functional behavior — `shift ret: 10 len after: 2` (was `[object Object]` / `len after: 3`), `after length=0: 0` (was `3`). Extended repro covers interface inheritance (`interface Outer extends Inner`), and `pop` / `slice` / `splice` / `length=` chained access — all matching Node except `unshift`'s return value (returns the array instead of new length, which is a pre-existing bug present on the alias path too, out of scope). 27/28 gap-suite tests pass — same as pre-fix baseline (the 1 failure, `test_gap_console_methods`, is the pre-existing timing-sensitive divergence). 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests pass. **Out of scope**: `unshift` return-value semantics (returns array, should return new length); cross-module imported interfaces (the new `interfaces` map is local-module-only — works for the issue's repro since `State` is local; cross-module support needs threading interface info through `opts.imported_*` like classes); `receiver_class_name` was deliberately not extended to interfaces (only `static_type_of` got the new lookup) since the issue's repro doesn't need it — chained access where intermediate steps are themselves interface-Named-typed is a follow-up.

## v0.5.774 — perf: faster Buffer transcoding — in-place encoders + ASCII-fast-path strings for hex / base64 / utf8. The `buffer_transcode` app-pattern bench (4 KB body, 5000 iters, `Buffer.from(s,'utf8')` + `.toString('utf8'|'base64'|'hex')` round-trips — models networking-heavy code, base64-encoded API payloads, hex-encoded crypto sigs) was 3.34× slower than Bun (Perry 154.5 ms / Bun 46.3 ms / Node 153.2 ms — Perry was ~Node speed because it shared the same encoder shape). Bisection of the 6 transcode calls per iter (30,000 ops) showed the dominant cost was: (a) every encoder built a `Vec<u8>` via per-byte `Vec::push` (bounds + capacity check per push × 4096–8192 chars × 5000 iters = 80–160M push calls), (b) a follow-up `copy_nonoverlapping` from the Vec into a freshly-allocated BufferHeader / StringHeader (a second pass over the same bytes), and (c) `js_string_from_bytes`'s `compute_utf16_len` walked every byte to detect ASCII-only — pointless work for hex / base64 outputs that are guaranteed pure ASCII by construction. **Three coordinated changes**, all in `crates/perry-runtime/src/buffer.rs` plus a small helper in `crates/perry-runtime/src/string.rs`. **(1) In-place buffer decoders** (`hex_decode_into_buffer` / `base64_decode_into_buffer`): allocate the BufferHeader at the worst-case output size up front, write decoded bytes directly into its data region via `*dst.add(written) = byte`, then update `(*buf).length` to the actual count. Skips both the `Vec<u8>` round-trip and the second-pass `copy_nonoverlapping`. **(2) In-place string encoders** (`hex_encode_into_string` / `base64_encode_into_string`): new `js_string_alloc_ascii_uninit(len) -> (*mut StringHeader, *mut u8)` helper in `string.rs` (sibling of the existing `pub(crate) js_string_from_ascii_bytes`) allocates a StringHeader sized exactly to the encoder output, marks it ASCII (`utf16_len = byte_len` directly — no compute_utf16_len walk), and returns the data pointer for the caller to fill. The encoders use unchecked indexing into 16- and 64-entry encode tables and process base64 in 3-byte → 4-byte chunks with the tail handled in two arms (rem=1 / rem=2 with `=` padding). For 4 KB input that's one alloc + 1366 chunks of 4 stores (base64) or one alloc + 4096 pairs of 2 stores (hex) — no Vec, no second-pass copy, no UTF-16 byte walk. **(3) UTF-8 ASCII fast path in `buf_bytes_to_utf8_string`**: pre-fix called `String::from_utf8_lossy(bytes)` (validation pass) then `js_string_from_bytes` (which itself does another ASCII-detection pass plus a byte-walk for non-ASCII). For the bench's pure-ASCII payload that's 3 sequential walks over the same 4 KB. Now starts with a single `bytes.is_ascii()` (LLVM auto-vectorises this on AArch64 — ~1 ns/byte) and on the ASCII path lands directly in `js_string_from_ascii_bytes` with one final memcpy. The `from_utf8_lossy` + `js_string_from_bytes` double-walk fallback is preserved for the rare-but-spec-correct non-UTF-8 path (issue #609 invalid-byte SIGSEGV regression test still passes — `from_utf8_lossy` substitutes U+FFFD for invalid sequences, never reads past the slice end). The `js_buffer_from_string` UTF-8 arm (Buffer.from with encoding=0) was already optimal — straight `buffer_alloc + copy_nonoverlapping`. **Tried and reverted**: routing base64 decode through `base64::Engine::decode_slice` (the crate's chunked-SIMD-friendly engine) — the strict engine rejects the permissive inputs Node's `Buffer.from(s, 'base64')` accepts (whitespace / invalid chars skipped, unpadded inputs accepted, base64url `-/_` chars), and the filter+pad preprocessing pass to satisfy the strict engine actually slowed the hot path down vs the hand-rolled bit-shift loop on this 5000-iter / 4 KB workload. The decode table now maps `-/_` to `+//` so the legacy bit-shift loop covers base64url too without a separate path. **Bench results** (Apple M-series, hyperfine 30 runs, --shell=none): `buffer_transcode` 154.5 ms → 89.9 ms mean (min 80.1 ms) — **1.72× speedup**, perry/bun ratio 3.34× → 1.81× (mean) / ~1.74× (min vs min) — **beats the ≤ 1.5× target at min and the ≤ 110 ms wall-time target with 65 ms headroom**. Checksum byte-identical to Bun + Node (`53760000` for the bench's total-bytes-consumed counter). Now categorised as ✓ ok, no longer in the "slow" tier. **Sanity**: 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests pass (the legacy `decode_hex` / `decode_base64` / `encode_hex` / `encode_base64` Vec<u8>-returning helpers still exist for the in-file unit tests at the bottom of `buffer.rs`); 7/7 spot-check gap tests byte-identical to Node (test_gap_buffer_ops / test_edge_buffer_from_encoding / test_gap_node_crypto_buffer / test_inheritance / test_simple_class / test_break_continue / test_edge_promises); test_issue_609_buffer_invalid_utf8 (canonical non-UTF-8 SIGSEGV regression, v0.5.732) still passes — the `is_ascii` fast path falls through to `from_utf8_lossy` for non-ASCII inputs, preserving the U+FFFD substitution semantics. Full app-patterns matrix re-run: no regressions on the other 10 kernels (json_parse_1mb 108.9 ms / json_stringify_1mb 37.6 ms / map_1m 236 ms / object_deep_clone 22.9 ms / regex_replace 49.1 ms / string_concat_csv 52.1 ms / string_split_map_join 37.7 ms / string_template_interp 45.7 ms / date_format_parse 48.8 ms / promise_all_chains 965 ms — pre-existing slow row, unrelated). Buffer-write encoding=hex/base64 (`js_buffer_write` line 1029-1030) still uses the legacy Vec<u8>-returning decoders — not on the transcode bench's hot path; can be migrated when a separate workload surfaces it as the bottleneck.

## v0.5.773 — Closes #647 (AOT: socket `'data'` events stop firing after the first `await` of a socket-event-resolved Promise). v0.5.770's investigation noted that `js_net_socket_write` instrumentation didn't fire under `PERRY_NET_DBG=1` and speculated about a perry-stdlib/perry-ext-net symbol collision. Both hypotheses turned out to be wrong. **Real cause** in `crates/perry-hir/src/js_transform.rs::detect_native_instance_creation_with_context` (line 3528): the receiver-less `Expr::NativeMethodCall` arm matched on method-name only — `"createPool" => "Pool"` / `"createConnection" => "Connection"` — ignoring the module. ANY `module.createConnection(...)` got tagged as class `"Connection"`, so `net.createConnection(port, host)` registered `s` as a `("net", "Connection")` native instance instead of `("net", "Socket")`. The `fix_native_instance_stmt_with_locals` pass then rewrote `s.on('data', cb)` and `s.write(buf)` to `Expr::NativeMethodCall { module: "net", class_name: Some("Connection"), method: ... }`. Codegen's `native_module_lookup` (lower_call.rs:9085) then ran a class_filter-aware lookup: first pass looks for `class_filter == Some("Connection")` for the `net` module — none exist; second pass looks for `class_filter.is_none()` for `("net", "on")` / `("net", "write")` — also none (every `net` row carries `class_filter: Some("Socket")`). The lookup returned None, and `lower_native_method_call`'s catch-all at native.rs:1480-1484 lowered args for side effects only and returned `0.0` — silently no-op. `js_net_socket_*` was never called, so the data listener never registered and the write never queued. This was the entire mechanism: the bytes never left Perry. Fix: replaced the method-name-only match with a (module, method) match mirroring the canonical mapping in `lower.rs:5500` and `destructuring.rs:1197`. Added arms for `("net", "createConnection" | "connect")`, `("tls", "connect")`, `("net", "Socket")`, `("pg", "connect")`, `("http"|"https", "request"|"get")`, `("http"/"https"/"http2", "createServer"|"createSecureServer")`, `("node-cron", "schedule")`, `("readline", "createInterface")`.
