For `tls.connect` the owning module is normalized to `"net"` to match `lower.rs:5519`'s pattern (Socket dispatch lives in net's NATIVE_MODULE_TABLE rows). **Why the connect listener inside the Promise constructor escaped the bug**: `s.on('connect', () => resolve())` lives inside a closure body (the Promise executor). `fix_native_instance_stmt_with_locals` walks per-function-scope locals; captured-by-closure `s` doesn't appear in the inner closure's local map, so the rewrite skipped it. The call lowered as a regular `Call { callee: PropertyGet }` and reached dynamic dispatch (`js_native_call_method` → `HANDLE_METHOD_DISPATCH` → `dispatch_external_net_socket`), which has its own per-method `match` arm and works correctly. So the issue's behavior matrix lines up exactly: pure-callback shapes (no async function, no captured `s`) hit the buggy path (rewrite fires, silent no-op) — except the issue's "pure callbacks" variant also has its registration sites inside an outer cb, so by accident those escape into dynamic dispatch and work; await-shaped code has the registration in the async function's top-level body where the rewrite fires; and the connect-listener-inside-Promise variant always escapes because of closure scoping. **Validation**: issue #647's exact 23-line repro (with `(s as any).end()` cast and without) compiles + runs against a local TCP echo server, prints `got 7 "+PONG\r\n"` and exits 0 — byte-identical to Node and Bun. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_647_await_socket_event.ts` against the standard parity-suite echo server (port 17891). Sanity: 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests pass; 27/28 gap-suite tests byte-identical to Node (the 1 failure, `test_gap_console_methods`, is the same pre-existing timing-sensitive `console.time` divergence unrelated to this fix); existing net-suite (`test_net_socket.ts`, `test_sock_write_map.ts`) passes unchanged. **Out of scope**: the parallel destructuring.rs:1197 mapping table is also missing the `("net", "createConnection") => "Socket"` arm — it covers a different pattern (named-import bare-call factory like `Fastify()`) and isn't on the path for `import { createConnection } from 'net'; createConnection(...)`. Adding it there too is harmless but unnecessary for #647; deferred unless a separate issue surfaces.

## v0.5.772 — perf: faster mixed-type string concat — N-way `js_string_concat_chain` runtime helper + codegen-side left-spine fold. The `string_concat_csv` kernel (build 100k CSV rows via `id + "," + name + "," + email + "," + score + "," + (cond ? "true" : "false") + ",2026-05-09"` — six-field comma-joined log/CSV emit) was 2.91× slower than Bun (Perry 107 ms / Bun 37 ms; ~9 binary-Add nodes per row × 100k rows × 9 sequential `js_string_concat` calls each producing a fresh `StringHeader` and copying the accumulating prefix → quadratic-shape per row). Instrumentation counters on the runtime concat helpers (gated on `PERRY_STRCAT_TRACE=1`, since reverted) confirmed: 900k `js_string_concat` calls + 300k `js_string_concat_value` + 100k `js_value_concat_string` + **828,889 intern lookups for 0 hits** (the FNV-1a-over-full-content `INTERN_TABLE` probe was pure overhead for unique CSV rows) + 34.7 MB total bytes through the concat path. **Three coordinated changes**. **(1) Runtime — `js_string_concat_chain(parts: *const f64, n: i32) -> *mut StringHeader`** in `crates/perry-runtime/src/string.rs`. Walks the parts array in one pass: for each NaN-boxed value, route by tag (STRING_TAG → snapshot `(data_ptr, byte_len, utf16_len, flags)` from the StringHeader; SHORT_STRING_TAG → materialize via `js_jsvalue_to_string`; plain f64 / INT32_TAG → format inline into a per-part 32-byte stack buffer via the new `format_number_into` helper that reuses the fast-itoa + integer-fast-path + NaN/Infinity/zero arms from the existing `js_string_concat_value`; everything else → slow path via `js_jsvalue_to_string`). Sums total bytes + utf16 lengths. Single `string_arena_alloc` for the entire result. Final pass: copy each part's bytes into the destination at the running cursor. Cap at MAX_PARTS=32 (covers every realistic CSV / log / template chain — the codegen-side fold also enforces this cap). For the bench: 9 pairwise allocs (averaging ~25 bytes each, plus copying the accumulating prefix per step — ~225 bytes copied per row in intermediate work) collapse to ONE alloc sized to the final length + N small memcpys. **(2) Codegen — left-spine fold** in `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::Binary { Add }`. New `flatten_string_add_chain(ctx, left, right) -> Option<Vec<&Expr>>` in `lower_string_method.rs` walks down the Add left-spine while each enclosing Add has at least one statically-string operand (gate matches the existing `is_definitely_string_expr` predicate — covers `Expr::String` literals, `LocalGet` of `HirType::String`, `.toString()` / `.toLowerCase()` / etc method calls, `Expr::Conditional` where both branches are statically-string). Stops the descent the moment an Add lacks string-shape on both sides (which would risk numeric semantics under JS `+` spec) — the entire un-walked subtree becomes one opaque part the runtime treats as Any-typed. When the resulting parts list has ≥3 entries, codegen emits a single `js_string_concat_chain` call instead of N-1 nested `js_string_concat` / `js_string_concat_value` / `js_value_concat_string` calls. Pairs of 2 still go through the existing pairwise paths (no overhead from the fold's vec allocation). **(3) Stack-overflow fix** in the codegen-side parts buffer. Pre-fix `lower_string_concat_chain` emitted `alloca [N x double]` directly into the current block — non-entry-block alloca lowers to a runtime `sub %rsp, N` with no matching restore (issue #167's exact shape, the same gap that blew up `buf.readInt32BE` in tight loops at iteration ~250k). For the bench's 100k iterations × 9 i64-slot buffer = 7.2 MB of permanent stack growth → SIGSEGV at iteration ~70k. Switched to `ctx.func.alloca_entry_array(DOUBLE, CONCAT_CHAIN_MAX_PARTS)` which hoists ONE fixed-size buffer to the function entry block; every chain call site reuses the same slot (safe because each call writes its parts and immediately calls into the runtime helper before any other site can clobber the slots). **(4) Intern fast-path skip** in `js_string_concat`: gate the existing intern-table probe on BOTH operands being themselves interned (new `both_interned` helper checks `(*gc_header).gc_flags & GC_FLAG_INTERNED`). The intern table is most useful for `js_string_intern` directly (consumer-side property-lookup interning); chained user concats produce mostly unique results. ~5 ms incremental speedup on top of the chain fold. **End-to-end**: `string_concat_csv` 107 ms → 47 ms (**2.27× speedup**, beats the ≤ 60 ms target; perry/bun ratio 2.91× → 1.45× — beats the ≤ 1.5× target). The sister `string_template_interp` kernel (template-literal-heavy; same Add-chain shape under the hood) ALSO got the fold benefit: 128 ms → 46 ms (**2.80× speedup**, perry/bun 1.85× → 0.86× — Perry now FASTER than Bun on that kernel). Checksum byte-identical to Node (6459261 / matches Bun). **Sanity**: 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests pass; 7/7 gap-suite spot-checks (test_string_methods, test_gap_string_methods, test_inheritance, test_simple_class, test_break_continue, test_edge_promises, test_gap_async_advanced) byte-identical to Node. Full app-patterns matrix re-run: no regressions on the other 9 kernels (json_parse_1mb 124 → 106 ms, json_stringify_1mb 37 → 35 ms, regex_replace 52 → 49 ms, object_deep_clone 18 → 15 ms, string_split_map_join 53 → 38 ms, buffer_transcode 171 → 156 ms, date_format_parse / map_1m / promise_all_chains unchanged). **Out of scope** (separate followups): `Number.prototype.toFixed` still allocates a fresh StringHeader per call (`js_number_to_fixed` calls `format!(...).as_bytes()` then `js_string_from_bytes`) — for the bench that's 100k allocs of 4-5 bytes each; not the dominant cost after the chain fold but worth squeezing if `string_concat_csv` becomes the next bottleneck. Right-spine and tree-shaped Add chains aren't flattened — JS spec parses `a + b + c` as left-associative `(a + b) + c` so left-spine is the dominant shape; non-left-spine Add chains (which only arise from explicit parens like `a + (b + c + d)`) keep the pairwise lowering. Buffer-transcode kernel is still 3.26× slower than Bun (separate workstream — the tight `buf.readInt32BE(i*4)` loop has its own profile shape unrelated to this fix).
## v0.5.771 — Linux UI parity audit + critical install-script fix. **(1) GTK4 FFI parity**: added 14 FFI symbols missing from `crates/perry-ui-gtk4/src/lib.rs` that exist on macOS / Windows / iOS / Android: `button_set_image`, `textfield_set_on_submit` / `set_on_focus` / `blur_all`, `qrcode_create` / `set_data`, `tabbar_create` / `add_tab` / `set_selected`, `scrollview_set_refresh_control` / `end_refreshing`, `vbox_create` / `add_child` / `finalize`, `frame_split_create` / `add_child`. Codegen + perry-dispatch route these unconditionally regardless of target platform; without them any Linux user calling `Button({image: "..."})`, `TextField({onSubmit, onFocus})`, `blurAll()`, `TabBar(...)`, `QRCode(...)`, `FrameSplit`, or pull-to-refresh got `Undefined symbols: _perry_ui_<x>` at link time. **Real GTK4 impls** for 4: `button::set_image` → `GtkButton::set_icon_name`; `textfield::set_on_submit` → `Entry::connect_activate`; `textfield::set_on_focus` → `EventControllerFocus::connect_enter`; `textfield::blur_all` → walks `REGISTERED_ENTRIES` and `root.set_focus(None::<&gtk4::Widget>)` on each. **Stubs** matching the macOS shape exactly for the remaining 10 (the iOS-only widgets — tabbar / vbox / frame_split / scrollview pull-to-refresh — that macOS itself stubs for link stability). **(2) Pre-existing build break fixed** — `crates/perry-ui-gtk4/src/widgets/image_gallery.rs:110` called `pic.set_content_fit(gtk4::ContentFit::Contain)` which requires gtk4-rs feature `v4_8`, but `Cargo.toml` pins `v4_6` (CI uses `ubuntu-22.04` shipping libgtk-4 4.6.9). The line had been silently broken since landing; removed it. Picture's default keep-aspect-ratio + the existing `set_can_shrink(true)` give the same Contain semantics on v4.6. **(3) Install script fallback** — `packaging/install.sh` (the script behind `curl -fsSL https://perryts.com/install.sh | sh`) was 404'ing for every Linux user because it unconditionally fetched the GitHub API's `releases/latest`, but most recent v0.5.5xx tags ship with **0 release assets**: `release-packages.yml`'s `await-tests` gate fails on a broken `test.yml`, so the npm-publish + tarball-attach steps never run. Last fully-asseted release is v0.5.511 (Apr 2026). Fix: walk recent tags (30-deep) probing each platform tarball URL with `curl -L -I`, pick the first that returns 200. Verified end-to-end on this machine — `install.sh` correctly resolves to v0.5.511 on the current main branch. **(4) Docs sync** — `docs/src/platforms/linux.md` and `docs/src/getting-started/installation.md` listed only `libgtk-4-dev` / `gtk4-devel` for Linux, but the `perry-ui-gtk4` crate has hard `Cargo.toml` deps on `libshumate = "0.6"` and `gstreamer = "0.23"` (which surface as `pkg-config: shumate-1.0 not found` and `gstreamer-1.0 not found` at first build). Backfilled `libshumate-dev` + `libgstreamer1.0-dev` (Ubuntu/Debian), `libshumate-devel` + `gstreamer1-devel` + `gstreamer1-plugins-base-devel` (Fedora), and `libshumate` + `gstreamer` + `gst-plugins-base` (Arch). **Out of scope (filed as followups)**: `table` widget on GTK4 is 12 stubbed FFIs returning 0 vs 577 lines of real impl on macOS — needs GtkColumnView/GtkListView wiring; `pdf_view` is 6 stubs vs 157 lines on macOS — needs poppler-glib bindings; underlying `test.yml` flake that's been blocking release-packages CI for 50+ tags. The install.sh fallback masks the symptom for users; the maintainer should still chase the test.yml root cause.
## v0.5.770 — Closes #648 #649 + partial #650; #647 investigated. **#648** (calling `undefined` silently no-ops): every `js_closure_callN` (and `_array` / `_apply_with_spread`) used to silently return TAG_UNDEFINED when `func_ptr` failed validation, and `js_native_call_method`'s catch-all returned `NULL_OBJECT_BYTES` for object receivers when the method didn't exist anywhere in the dispatch chain. Both paths now throw `<value> is not a function` / `<method> is not a function` via the existing `js_throw_type_error_not_a_function` machinery, which routes through Perry's exception system so a surrounding `try`/`catch` catches it (per #596). Single biggest leverage source of cascading parity-test failures (`test_parity_timers` hung forever waiting on `timers.setTimeout` which silently no-op'd; `test_parity_os` / `tls` / `perf_hooks` / `http2` truncated mid-script when an unimplemented binding's method silently no-op'd). New `throw_not_callable()` helper in `crates/perry-runtime/src/closure.rs`; corresponding catch-all change in `js_native_call_method` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs`. Fallout: added explicit HIR lowering for `fs.rmSync(path, opts?)` in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs` (two arms, routes to `Expr::FsRmRecursive`) — the catch-all previously masked it as a silent no-op. Verified: 6-line repro prints `threw: missingFn is not a function`; 27/28 gap tests pass (1 pre-existing `console.time` failure). **#649** (`fs.constants` / `os.constants` / `crypto.constants` namespace tables missing): pre-fix every read returned undefined, silently misbehaving (`fs.access(path, fs.constants.R_OK)` became `fs.access(path, undefined)`). Added `os_signal_const`, `os_errno_const`, `os_priority_const`, `os_dlopen_const`, `crypto_const` dispatch tables in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs::get_native_module_constant` (using `libc::*` for platform-correct values matching Node's libuv reads), plus `match module_name` arms for `os.constants[.signals|.errno|.priority|.dlopen]` and `crypto[.constants]`. Added `js_native_module_property_by_name` runtime helper + codegen `Expr::PropertyGet { NativeModuleRef, .. }` arm in `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs` (since `NativeModuleRef` lowers to `0.0` so the generic PropertyGet path can't see the namespace), FFI declaration in `crates/perry-codegen/src/runtime_decls.rs`, and a `NATIVE_MODULE_CLASS_ID` arm in `js_object_get_field_by_name` for chained reads on sub-namespaces. Registered `os.constants` / `crypto.constants` in `crates/perry-api-manifest/src/entries.rs` to flip them strict in the unimplemented-API gate. Verified: 11-line repro byte-identical to Node (fs.constants.F_OK=0, os.constants.signals.SIGINT=2, os.constants.errno.ENOENT=2, crypto.constants.RSA_PKCS1_PADDING=1, etc.). **#650 partial** (WHATWG URL parser): fixed userinfo extraction in `crates/perry-runtime/src/url.rs::create_url_object` — `parse_url` left `user:pass@` as a prefix on `host` (so `host` / `hostname` / `origin` for `https://user:pass@example.com:8080` all carried `user:pass@example.com:8080`, and `username` / `password` came back undefined). Post-processing splits userinfo back out before the URL object is built; new URL_USERNAME / URL_PASSWORD field indices (10 / 11) populate the previously-absent fields. Verified for the 5-line `host`/`hostname`/`origin`/`username`/`password` block: byte-identical to Node. The other 6 sub-bugs in #650 (toString returning `[object Object]`, toJSON returning Date string, setters being no-ops, URL.canParse / URL.parse static methods missing, URLSearchParams.size getter missing, iterable URLSearchParams ctor crash) are deferred to a follow-up — they need separate runtime + codegen plumbing. **#647** (AOT socket 'data' events stop firing after `await` of a socket-event-resolved Promise): investigated, NOT fixed in this batch. The agent's microtask-drain hypothesis turned out to be wrong — the `await` actually completes (the user's continuation runs and `s.write()` executes) but the data response from the server never reaches the user's `'data'` listener. `js_net_socket_write` in `crates/perry-ext-net/src/lib.rs` doesn't fire under PERRY_NET_DBG instrumentation despite the user-side write returning, suggesting a different dispatch path that I couldn't isolate in this session. Filed for follow-up. **Also includes user's #645 fix**: chained method calls on a value that fell through `js_native_call_method`'s catch-all no longer crash with `(number).<method> is not a function` — the `is_valid_obj_ptr` reject arm now returns `NULL_OBJECT_BYTES` NaN-boxed POINTER instead of literal `0.0`. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_645_chained_method_on_null_obj.ts`. Sanity: 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests + 27/28 gap tests byte-identical to Node.

## v0.5.769 — Closes #645 (followup to #643 / #488 drizzle-sqlite): chained method calls on a value that fell through `js_native_call_method`'s catch-all no longer crash with `(number).<method> is not a function`. Surfaced in drizzle's `PreparedQuery.values()` body — `this.stmt.raw().all(...params)` where `this.stmt` had been set to a value the dispatch chain didn't recognize (drizzle's BetterSQLiteSession passes the Database handle through several class-instance boundaries; by the time `this.client.prepare(...)` is invoked the receiver is a heap-pointer-shaped value, not the small-handle range, so prepare's call falls through every dispatcher and returns the static `NULL_OBJECT_BYTES` stub). The chain then re-entered `js_native_call_method` with `obj` pointing at NULL_OBJECT_BYTES — a static stub in the binary's data segment, NOT the macOS userspace heap range that `is_valid_obj_ptr` requires (HEAP_MIN == 0x200_0000_0000). The pre-fix path at `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs::5763` returned a literal `0.0` from the `is_valid_obj_ptr` reject arm, which the codegen interprets as IEEE-754 number zero — so the next chained `.method()` saw a number receiver and the dispatcher's primitive-receiver TypeError fired with `(number).all is not a function`. Fix: that one reject arm now returns `NULL_OBJECT_BYTES` NaN-boxed POINTER (matching every other catch-all in the function), so `typeof` stays `"object"` through the chain and operations propagate consistently. Truly garbage pointers (random heap addresses below the userspace range) benefit too — chained calls hit a stable null stub instead of mid-chain numeric arithmetic on bit patterns. Sanity: 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests + 27/28 gap tests (the 1 failure, `test_gap_console_methods`, is a pre-existing timing-sensitive `console.time` divergence). New regression test `test_issue_645_chained_method_on_null_obj.ts`. **Out of scope (deferred)**: the drizzle-sqlite full chain still fails with output divergence (`count=undefined` vs expected `count=3`) — the deeper issue is that `this.client` arrives at `BetterSQLiteSession.prepareQuery` as a heap-pointer-shaped value rather than the original small-handle Database, so the entire data-fetch path returns null stubs all the way up. That's a separate bug in how class-instance fields preserve small-handle values across the cross-module ctor chain (probably interacts with the v0.5.758–760 walk-stop fixes); filed as a followup.

## v0.5.768 — fix(runtime): mutable closure-capture boxes are now GC roots — closes the long-standing GC-correctness gap that `Promise.all` of N promises silently failed past ~10k total awaits (bench `benchmarks/app-patterns/kernels/promise_all_chains.ts` ran to exit 0 but printed nothing). Bisection: deterministic threshold at N=199 batches × 50 awaits (= 9950 promises) — fails on the SECOND GC cycle. Diagnostic instrumentation in `promise_all_fulfill_handler` showed the input promises (the user's `f()` async fn) were resolving with raw `TAG_UNDEFINED` — the state machine was returning `{value: undefined, done: true}` from a corrupted `__gen_state`. **Root cause**: mutable closure captures (`mutable_captures` in HIR — `__iter`, `__step`, `__gen_state`, `__gen_done`, `__gen_sent`, all hoisted vars in async functions transformed by `transform_async_to_generator`, plus any user `let` captured-by-mutating-closure) are stored as f64 boxes allocated by `crates/perry-runtime/src/box.rs::js_box_alloc` via `std::alloc::alloc` **directly** — NOT the GC arena and NOT `gc_malloc`. Result: boxes have no `GcHeader`, aren't in `MALLOC_STATE.objects` or the arena, and don't appear in `valid_ptrs`. When `trace_closure` walks a closure's captures and hits a box pointer, `try_mark_value_or_raw`'s `valid_ptrs.contains` returns false → the box pointer is unmarked AND, critically, **the f64 value INSIDE the box is never scanned**. Heap objects reachable only via box-captures (the iter object held in `__iter`'s box, or any user value held in a mutable capture) get swept on the next GC. On resume from the await microtask, `js_box_get` returns the still-valid box pointer to the still-valid f64 contents — but those contents are now a NaN-boxed pointer to a freed (or worse, reallocated-for-something-else) ObjectHeader. The subsequent `iter.next()` dispatches against garbage and the state machine collapses to `done: true, value: undefined`. The bug affected ANY async function with mutable captures across awaits, but the threshold was the second GC cycle so small-N workloads never tripped. **Fix** in `crates/perry-runtime/src/box.rs`: thread-local `BOX_REGISTRY: HashSet<usize>` populated by `js_box_alloc` with each new box pointer. New `pub fn scan_box_roots(mark: &mut dyn FnMut(f64))` walks the registry and dispatches each box's `(*ptr).value` to `mark` — standard root-scanner protocol from `gc.rs::ROOT_SCANNERS`. Registered alongside the existing 10 scanners in `gc_init`. Defensive bounds check on each address (`>= 0x1000 && < 0x0001_0000_0000_0000 && % 8 == 0`) mirrors `is_plausible_box_ptr` so a stale-but-deref-unsafe entry can't fault the GC. **Validation**: bisection now exits at correct totals across N=10/100/200/500/1000/5000/10000 batches × 50 awaits — checksums byte-identical to Node. Bench `promise_all_chains.ts` (50k promises) now prints `checksum: 2500050000` (was: silent exit 0). 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests pass; 24/24 spot-check gap tests byte-identical to Node. Closes the second of the two correctness bugs the v0.5.766 bench harness surfaced. **Out of scope**: the pre-existing box leak (`js_box_alloc` never has a paired free) is unchanged — `BOX_REGISTRY` adds bounded ~8 B per live box of tracking but no semantic change. Properly freeing boxes needs either a refcount (mutable captures share box ownership across closures) or making boxes proper GC objects with a `GC_TYPE_BOX` + trace fn — larger surgery, tracking separately.

## v0.5.767 — perf(runtime): fix Map O(N²) string-key insert. Surfaced by v0.5.766's `benchmarks/app-patterns/kernels/map_1m.ts` (500k `m.set("key_" + i, ...)` inserts + 500k lookups) — Perry hung past 30s while Bun finished in 0.6s. Root cause in `crates/perry-runtime/src/map.rs`: the existing `MAP_INDEX` side-table only indexes NUMERIC keys (gated by `is_safe_numeric_key` to keep the table sound under gen-GC string forwarding — stored f64 bit patterns go stale when the GC moves the underlying StringHeader). String keys fell to the linear-scan fallback in `find_key_index`, so each `set` did an O(N) dedup-check → O(N²) total inserts. Fix adds a parallel **content-hash side-table** `MAP_STRING_INDEX: HashMap<usize, HashMap<u64, Vec<u32>>>` keyed by FNV-1a 64-bit hash of the string's bytes (decoded via the existing `string_view_from_bits` so SSO and heap STRING_TAG share the same hash space — both representations hash to the same bucket and a `jsvalue_eq` validation post-lookup handles the cross-representation match correctly). Stored values are `u32` entry-array indexes (NOT pointers), which survive `rewrite_map_fields` evacuation rewrites untouched — bypassing the gen-GC stale-bits constraint that gates `MAP_INDEX`. Per-bucket `Vec<u32>` accommodates FNV-1a hash collisions (vanishingly rare for distinct strings, but each candidate is validated via `jsvalue_eq` so a collision just costs an extra few-byte memcmp, never a wrong answer). Six edits, all in `crates/perry-runtime/src/map.rs`: (1) new `MAP_STRING_INDEX` thread-local + `string_content_hash` helper; (2) `drop_map_index` extended to clear the new table on Map address recycle; (3) `js_map_alloc` initializes the new table for the new ptr; (4) `find_key_index` adds a string-key fast path between the numeric-key arm and the linear scan; (5) `js_map_set` pushes new entries onto the string-key bucket on insert; (6) `js_map_delete` mirrors the index maintenance (drop on delete, rewrite swapped key's stored index on swap-pop); (7) `js_map_clear` clears the new bucket alongside `MAP_INDEX`. Numeric side-table arm preserved unchanged. Object/bigint pointer keys still take the linear scan (rare, and they hit the same gen-GC stale-bits constraint that originally gated NumericKey to non-pointers — a content hash for objects would need spec-correct SameValueZero, which is identity-based for non-primitives, so a content hash isn't even semantically right for those). **Bench numbers** (Apple M-series, `/tmp/test_map_scale.ts`): N=1k Perry 1ms / Bun 1ms; N=10k Perry 1ms / Bun 3ms; N=100k Perry 12+16ms / Bun 20+20ms (was 27566ms pre-fix — ~2300× speedup); N=500k Perry 98+120ms / Bun 169+152ms (insert+lookup; Perry beats Bun on both). Linear behavior post-fix; previously quadratic. **Sanity**: 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests pass. Spot-check: test_inheritance / test_simple_class / test_break_continue / test_edge_promises / test_gap_async_advanced / test_edge_map_set / test_gap_map_set_extended / test_map / test_issue_540_spread_map / test_issue_542_map_keys_optional all byte-identical to Node. Closes the first of the two correctness bugs the v0.5.766 bench harness surfaced.

## v0.5.766 — bench(app-patterns): new `benchmarks/app-patterns/` suite + runner that compares Perry/Bun/Node on 11 hot-path kernels modeling what real apps actually do (JSON parse/stringify of 1 MB shapes, CSV-row build via concat, template literal interpolation, regex redaction on a body, Map-of-500k-entries, Promise.all fan-out, manual deep-clone, ISO timestamp round-trip, Buffer transcoding). Each kernel runs ~50–500 ms and prints a `checksum:` line for cross-runtime validation; the runner hyperfines all three runtimes and aggregates into a markdown matrix labeled ✅ win / ✓ ok / ⚠ borderline / ✗ slow / RUNTIME FAIL. **First-pass results:** Perry beats Bun on 5 of 9 working kernels (date format/parse, JSON stringify, deep clone, regex replace, split-map-join), is within margin on 1 (json_parse 1.42×), borderline on 1 (string_template 1.85×), and **2× slower on 2** (buffer_transcode 2.26×, string_concat_csv 2.91× — same shape as ABC451D's per-iteration concat pattern). Plus **2 hard correctness bugs surfaced**: `map_1m` (500k Map entries) hangs Perry past 30 s while Bun runs in 0.6 s — likely O(N²) collision behavior or hash quality issue; `promise_all_chains` (1000 batches × 50 awaits = 50k promises) silently produces no output past ~10k promises, smaller bisects to NaN at ~10k. The two perf gaps and two correctness bugs are now the focused workstreams for closing the Bun gap on real-app patterns. Suite is extensible — drop a `.ts` in `kernels/`, re-run `run.sh`. Documentation in `benchmarks/app-patterns/README.md` covers authoring rules and what's deliberately out of scope (DB queries / HTTP server steady-state / cold start need different harnesses). Self-contained, no external deps; runs locally in ~3 min.

## v0.5.765 — Four coordinated fixes (#556 + #640 + #631 + #644). **#556** (Table renderCell with string-concat `Text("row " + row)` crashed AppKit at first layout via `__retain_OA` on NSCFString): root cause is the delegate's `[table_view indexOfTableColumn:tc]` dispatch — `respondsToSelector:` returns false for that selector on a real NSTableView in this delivery path (called from `_addRowViewForVisibleRow`), the runtime then routes through `___forwarding___` which retains the forwarding context as if it were the receiver. Replaced with manual iteration via `tableColumns objectAtIndex:`; both well-known selectors dispatch directly. Also added a defensive POINTER_TAG (0x7FFD) check on the closure return so an accidental non-widget value doesn't get interpreted as a widget handle. `crates/perry-ui-macos/src/widgets/table.rs::view_for_column`. **#640** (NavStack TextField geisterhand `/value/:handle` returned `null` for empty stringValue, indistinguishable from "widget not found"): on the runtime side the pump's `ReadValue` handler always set `VALUE_RESULT = Some(String::new())` for both "widget not found" and "widget found with empty value", and `request_string_from_main` collapsed `Some(s) if !s.is_empty()` → null pointer for both. Added `\u{0}\u{0}NF` sentinel from the platform helper to flag "not found" and changed the HTTP `/value/:handle` handler in `crates/perry-ui-geisterhand/src/server.rs` to check ptr-only (returning JSON `value: ""` for empty-but-found, `value: null` for actually-not-found). `alloc_string_result` in `crates/perry-ui-macos/src/widgets/mod.rs` now allocates ≥ 1 byte so empty values pass through as a non-null pointer. The user's reported "value cleared after swap" symptom in their full app didn't reproduce in a minimal NavStack repro (the underlying NSTextField persists across `setHidden`); this fix addresses the misleading null-vs-empty conflation that was masking the real triage signal. **#631 part A** (Dirent): `fs.readdirSync(path, {withFileTypes:true})` now returns a `Dirent[]` byte-identical to Node — array of objects each carrying `name`, `parentPath` / `path`, `isFile()`, `isDirectory()`, `isSymbolicLink()`. Added `build_dirent_object` and `options_with_file_types` helpers in `crates/perry-runtime/src/fs.rs` (mirrors the existing `build_stats_object` pattern for `statSync`); changed `js_fs_readdir_sync`'s ABI to take a second `options_value: f64` arg; threaded the second arg through codegen at three sites (`crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call/native.rs::readdirSync`, `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::readdirSync`, `crates/perry-codegen/src/runtime_decls.rs`) and the dynamic dispatch in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs::6396`. Repro `test-files/test_issue_631_dirent.ts` matches Node output exactly. **Part B** (`Readable.from` / `new Readable` from `node:stream`) is deferred — full Readable/Writable/Duplex/Transform class wiring requires substantial new plumbing across HIR + codegen + runtime; documented as known gap. **#644** (cross-compile `--target linux --enable-js-runtime` regression of v0.5.749's #617 fix — inline `await fetchWithAuth(...)` returns undefined): without ability to reproduce the cross-compile + V8 environment, applied a defensive extension to `hoist_fetch_with_auth_inside_await` in `crates/perry-transform/src/async_to_generator.rs` to also fire on generic `Expr::Call` / `CallSpread` / `NativeMethodCall` / `StaticMethodCall` / `SuperMethodCall` operands (was only `FetchGetWithAuth` / `FetchPostWithAuth`). This mimics the user's verified mass-replace workaround (`const x = await CALL(...)` → `const p = CALL(...); const x = await p;`) at the HIR level — semantically equivalent (same call sequence point, same Promise, just an extra LocalGet in the IR). Sanity: 5/5 representative async tests (`test_async`, `test_async_chain`, `test_gap_async_advanced`, `test_gap_fetch_response`, `test_issue_236_fetch_then_console_log`) pass byte-identical to Node + the original `test_issue_617_inline_await_fetch_with_auth.ts` still passes.

## v0.5.764 — Refs #643 / #488 — `better-sqlite3` `stmt.raw([toggle])` method now exists end-to-end on both perry-stdlib's bundled sqlite and perry-ext-better-sqlite3. drizzle's `PreparedQuery.values()` chains `this.stmt.raw().all(...params)` to fetch row arrays it can hand to `mapResultRow(fields, row, joinsNotNullableMap)`. Pre-fix `stmt.raw` was undefined (no method registered) and the call surfaced as `(number).all is not a function` deeper down because perry's runtime returns a number sentinel when calling `undefined()` instead of throwing. Five coordinated changes: **(1) runtime impl** in both `crates/perry-stdlib/src/sqlite.rs` and `crates/perry-ext-better-sqlite3/src/lib.rs` — `SqliteStmtHandle` gains a `raw_mode: AtomicBool` flag flipped by the new `js_sqlite_stmt_raw(stmt_handle) -> Handle` (returns the same handle so `stmt.raw().all(...)` chains). `js_sqlite_stmt_all` and `js_sqlite_stmt_get` consult the flag and emit row-arrays (column values in declared order) instead of objects when raw mode is on. **(2) codegen NativeModSig** in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` — new `("better-sqlite3", "raw")` entry routes `stmt.raw()` to `js_sqlite_stmt_raw` with `args: &[]` and `ret: NR_PTR`. **(3) HANDLE_METHOD_DISPATCH arm** in `crates/perry-stdlib/src/common/dispatch.rs` — `dispatch_sqlite_stmt(handle, method, args)` handles the dynamic-receiver path used by `(stmt as any).raw().all(...)`. Routes through `extern "C"` declarations of `js_sqlite_stmt_*` so the dispatcher hits whichever crate's impl the linker resolved (perry-stdlib's vs perry-ext's — only one wins the link race when both expose the symbols). **(4) feature-strip exception** in `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/optimized_libs.rs` — keep `database-sqlite` enabled in perry-stdlib even when the well-known flip routes `better-sqlite3` to perry-ext-better-sqlite3, so `dispatch_sqlite_stmt` (gated on that feature) survives into binaries that actually use sqlite. **(5) raw-mode `.get()`** parallels `.all()` — returns a single column-value array instead of an object. Direct chained-call shape (`stmt.raw().all()` from the entry module) works end-to-end; new regression test `test-files/test_better_sqlite3_raw.ts` covers object-mode + raw-mode-via-`.all()` + raw-mode-via-`.get()`. **Out of scope (next iteration)**: drizzle's full insert chain — `this.stmt.raw().all(...)` invoked from inside PreparedQuery.values()'s class-method body — still fails with `(number).all is not a function`. The dispatch tower path emitted for class-method-internal calls returns the raw int handle directly to `.all()` instead of NaN-boxing it as a POINTER, so the chained `.all()` sees a number receiver. Surfaces as the next blocker in `tests/release/packages/drizzle-sqlite`.

## v0.5.763 — Gen-GC Phase C4b-γ-3: `PERRY_GEN_GC_EVACUATE=1` is now sound — closes the long-standing soundness bug where the evacuation pass corrupted the user's heap on workloads with sustained tenuring. **Root cause**: `evacuate_tenured_nursery_objects` calls `arena_alloc_gc_old` to allocate the OLD-arena copy of each evacuating object. `arena_alloc_gc_old → arena_alloc_old → arena.alloc` calls `gc_check_trigger` on the slow path (when the current OLD block is full and a new one needs filling) — and `gc_check_trigger` doesn't gate on "we're already inside a collection." The result: the outer `gc_collect_minor` cycle's `MARK_SEEDS`, `CONS_PINNED`, and `valid_ptrs` all get clobbered by a recursive `gc_collect_minor` invocation. The recursive cycle's `mark_stack_roots` runs from a DEEP runtime stack frame (currently inside `arena_alloc_gc_old → arena.alloc → gc_check_trigger → gc_collect_minor`), so its conservative scan sees almost no user pointers — `cache` (the bench's load-bearing local) doesn't get pinned. The recursive cycle then evacuates `cache` (un-pinned, tenured), but its rewrite walk only updates the inner cycle's view of references; the OUTER cycle's pin-set, seed-list, and rewrite walk are gone, so the user's shadow stack slot for `cache` never gets updated to point at the evacuated copy. Subsequent `cache.length` reads pull the lower 32 bits of the FORWARDED slot's first-8-bytes (the new_user pointer in OLD_ARENA) and get garbage — usually 0, sometimes a small positive int — and the bench segfaults a few iterations later when it indexes `cache[i]`. Repro: `bench_evac_heavy.ts` (5M `Item` allocs, 25% retained) crashed every run with `PERRY_GEN_GC_EVACUATE=1`, exit 139, no output. Bisected via `[gc-mark-phases]` instrumentation that showed double `[gc-mark-phases]` lines per `[gc-evac]` line on the cycles that broke `cache` — the second mark-phases line is the recursive call. **Fix** in `crates/perry-runtime/src/gc.rs::gc_collect_minor`: set `GC_FLAG_IN_ALLOC` for the entire duration of the function, restore on exit. `gc_check_trigger` already early-returns when this bit is set (line 783, was the existing reentrancy guard for `gc_malloc`'s in-progress bookkeeping). Save/restore the previous bit state in case the caller already had it set (e.g., a runtime-internal path that wraps `gc_collect_minor` in its own IN_ALLOC scope). Two-line addition + comment block. The four hypotheses listed in the prior session's setup brief — `trace_object` missing FORWARDED arm, `mark_block_persisting` force-marking FORWARDED slots, `rewrite_forwarded_references` walking FORWARDED, CONS_PINNED ordering — were all wrong; the real cause was a level above any of them. **Validation**: `bench_evac_heavy` runs to completion 10/10 with `retained: 1250000 sum: 3124997500000` byte-identical to Node; ABC451D-deforested still prints `328` and is now ~20% FASTER under `PERRY_GEN_GC_EVACUATE=1` than the no-evac baseline (was 50% slower with the corrupt evac pass — fixing the recursion eliminates the double-work plus actually delivers the OLD-arena win). Sanity: 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests + 27/28 gap tests byte-identical to Node (the 1 failure, `test_gap_console_methods`, is the same pre-existing timing-sensitive `console.time` divergence). With evacuation now sound, the path is clear to flip `PERRY_GEN_GC_EVACUATE` to default-on (separate followup, after benching the long-tail of stdlib-heavy workloads).

## v0.5.762 — Refs #639 followup: method-as-value reads on a Buffer now return a bound-method closure with `typeof === "function"` instead of `undefined`. Surfaces in `@perryts/mysql`'s `isBufferLike(v)` duck-type test (`typeof anyV.readUInt8 === 'function' && typeof anyV.length === 'number'`) — pre-fix the test returned `false` for Perry's Buffers because `typeof buf.readUInt8` was `"undefined"`, so the prepared-statement encoder fell through to its `String(v)` fallback. The result: BLOB / BINARY column INSERTs sent the formatted string `"<Buffer aa bb cc>"` instead of the raw bytes, the BINARY(16) column silently rejected the length-mismatched data, and downstream `SELECT ... WHERE id = ?` returned 0 rows — every read path through binary keys (auth, sessions, idempotency keys, crypto KIDs) broke. Two coordinated changes in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs`: (1) **`is_buffer_method_name(name) -> bool`** — static enum of every method name `dispatch_buffer_method` recognizes (~70 entries: read/write all int + float widths, slice, copy, write, fill, equals, indexOf, etc.). (2) **Buffer arm in `js_object_get_field_by_name`** — extends the existing `length`/`byteLength` intercept: when the receiver is a registered Buffer and the key passes `is_buffer_method_name`, allocate a fresh heap buffer for the method name and synthesize a `js_class_method_bind` closure (BOUND_METHOD_FUNC_PTR sentinel + `(this, name_ptr, name_len)` captures). The closure is `typeof === "function"` (matches Node) and dispatches via the existing chain `js_closure_callN → dispatch_bound_method → js_native_call_method`'s buffer arm at `object.rs:5031` → `dispatch_buffer_method`. Direct `buf.method(args)` calls were already special-cased at codegen and unaffected. Sanity: 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests; full @perryts/mysql repro from #639 (CREATE BINARY(16) → INSERT [crypto.randomBytes(16), str] → SELECT WHERE id=?) now persists rows correctly and SELECT-back returns `{label:"hello"}` byte-identical to Node. Regression test `test_issue_639_json_stringify_buffer.ts` extended with the `isBufferLike` duck-type case + per-method `typeof` table.

## v0.5.761 — Closes #639 (post-#609 regression: `JSON.stringify` of any value containing a Buffer silently exited the process). `BufferHeader` carries no `GcHeader`, so the JSON dispatch's `gc_obj_type(ptr)` (which reads 8 bytes before the user-space pointer to extract the GC type tag) read unrelated memory and randomly routed to the `is_object_pointer` arm, which then deref'd a bogus `keys_array` field and faulted. Surfaced through `@perryts/mysql`'s `pool.query()` result — `QueryResult.rowsRaw` is `Buffer[][]`, so any whole-result `JSON.stringify` hit a Buffer leaf and crashed. Fix: added `is_registered_buffer(ptr as usize)` Buffer-detection branches BEFORE every `gc_obj_type(ptr)` site in `crates/perry-runtime/src/json.rs` — `stringify_value`, `stringify_value_depth`, `stringify_array_depth` (per-element pointer dispatch), and `build_shape_prefix_template` (homogeneous-array template construction). Added `stringify_buffer(ptr, buf)` helper that emits Node-compatible JSON: `{"type":"Buffer","data":[b0,b1,...]}` for Buffers (matches `Buffer.prototype.toJSON()`) and `{"0":b0,"1":b1,...}` for `new Uint8Array(...)` (typed-array fallthrough shape). Distinguished via `is_uint8array_buffer(addr)` from the existing `UINT8ARRAY_FROM_CTOR` registry. Sanity: 225/225 perry-runtime unit tests, 11/11 JSON tests + 8/8 Buffer tests pass byte-identical to Node, 27/28 gap tests pass (the 1 failure, `test_gap_console_methods`, is a pre-existing timing-sensitive `console.time` divergence unrelated to this fix). New regression test `test_issue_639_json_stringify_buffer.ts` covers Buffer-alone, `[Buffer]`, `{b:Buffer}`, Uint8Array variants, nested-result-object shape (mimics `QueryResult.rowsRaw`), and zero-length buffers — all byte-identical to Node.

## v0.5.760 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: cross-module class without own ctor, where parent (in another module) has an explicit ctor — `new Child(arg)` now forwards `arg` to Parent_constructor. Pre-fix the synthesized Child ctor had ZERO params because the params-walking loop in `compile_module` looked up the parent in `class_table` (gets the STUB with constructor: None) and fell through without consulting `opts.imported_classes`'s `constructor_param_count`. The result: `class Child extends Parent { x = "y" }` (no own ctor, parent in another module) had its synthesized ctor with ZERO params, so the user's `new Child("arg")` lost the arg before reaching Parent_constructor. Fix: consult imported_classes alongside the class_table lookup at line 2127-2156 of `crates/perry-codegen/src/codegen.rs` so the synthesized ctor adopts the same arity as the source-module parent ctor. Surfaced after v0.5.759's leaf walk-stop change made lower_new call the leaf's synthesized ctor (instead of walking to ancestor) — exposing the latent arg-forwarding bug. Sanity: 18/18 class-related tests pass + hono entry.ts byte-identical. New regression test test_xmod_no_ctor_arg_forward.ts.

## v0.5.759 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: tightens v0.5.758's walk-stop fix to only fire on the LEAF class (`effective_class_name == lookup_class`) and only when the leaf has fields. v0.5.758's broader walk-stop (any class with imported_ctor symbol) regressed drizzle's INSERT path: `new SQLiteInsertBase(...)` walked stops correctly via the existing `has_real_ctor` check (param_count=6), but the broader v0.5.758 fix interacted with intermediate stub classes and broke `this.dialect = dialect`-style explicit ctor assignments. Narrowing to "stop only on the leaf with fields" fixes the cross-module-no-own-ctor-with-field-inits case (test_cross_module_no_ctor_fields.ts) without affecting the deeper walk for explicit-ctor-with-empty-stub cases. Sanity: 18/18 class-related tests pass + hono entry.ts byte-identical. Drizzle insert chain regression fixed; (number).all on select still pending separate iteration.

## v0.5.758 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: cross-module class with extends + field initializers but NO own constructor — the leaf's field initializers (`config = {x:1}`, `arrow = () => ...`) now persist instead of getting lost. Two coordinated fixes in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs::lower_new`. **(1) Walk-stop fix**: the body-call walk-up loop (used to find the closest ancestor with a real ctor — added in v0.5.741 for drizzle's `PgSerial → PgColumn → Column` arg-forwarding chain) now stops at any class with a synthesized imported_class_ctor symbol, not just classes with `param_count > 0`. Pre-fix the loop walked PAST the leaf class (synthesized ctor with param_count=0) to find an ancestor with explicit params, calling the ANCESTOR's ctor and skipping the leaf's synthesized ctor entirely — leaf's SelfOnly field inits never ran. The synthesized ctor (compile_method's no-own-ctor path) correctly chains to the parent's ctor and applies SelfOnly internally, so stopping at the leaf is the right behavior. **(2) Post-init re-apply skip**: the `if !has_own_ctor && has_extends { apply SelfOnly }` block at the end of lower_new now also skips when the imported_class_ctors map has an entry for the class. The cross-module ctor handles fields itself (via the synthesized ctor's SelfOnly + parent forward); re-applying here uses the STUB's fields (no inits → all Undefined), which would overwrite the freshly-set values. Combines with v0.5.755's similar gate (which previously only applied when checking has_own_ctor on the local class). Repro: `class Parent {} class Child extends Parent { config = {x:1}; arrow = () => "x" }` defined in module A, used as `new Child()` in module B — `c.config.x === 1` and `typeof c.arrow === "function"` now match Node (was: `c.config = undefined`, `typeof c.arrow = number`). Sanity: 18/18 class-related tests pass + hono entry.ts byte-identical. New regression test test_cross_module_no_ctor_fields.ts. **Out of scope (next iteration)**: drizzle's `(number).all is not a function` — the chain still hits something specific to drizzle's deeper class structure that doesn't reproduce in isolation.

## v0.5.757 — Refs #420 followup: drizzle-orm's `((CLS2) => { CLS2.X = ...; })(CLS || (CLS = {} as any))` IIFE pattern now correctly persists dynamic properties on the class. Two coordinated fixes: (1) **HIR lowering** in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_assign.rs` and `crates/perry-hir/src/lower.rs::lower_expr_assignment` — `<ClassName/FuncName> = X` (assignment to a class or function binding) no longer creates an implicit local that shadows the original binding. The user code is using the lax `CLS || (CLS = {})` pattern where the RHS is dead code at runtime (classes/functions are truthy). Pre-fix the implicit local hid the original binding from later reads, so `(CLS || (CLS = {}))` returned a zero-init'd local instead of CLS itself, and `CLS.X = ...` wrote to the wrong target. Now lowered as `Ok(*value)` — evaluate RHS for side effects only. (2) **Codegen PropertyGet arm** in `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::PropertyGet` extends the existing `Expr::ClassRef` dynamic-props arm to ALSO handle `Expr::ExternFuncRef { name }` when `name` is a registered class id. Pre-fix the read fell through to the PIC fast path which discards the INT32 NaN-tag during unbox and returned undefined for IIFE-set dynamic props on imported classes (drizzle's `import { SQL } from "drizzle-orm"; SQL.Aliased`). Repro: `class SQL{}; ((S:any)=>{class A{}; S.A = A;})(SQL || (SQL = {} as any)); typeof (SQL as any).A` returns `"function"` matching Node (was `"undefined"`). Drizzle's `is(value, SQL.Aliased)` chain now works; chain progresses to a new `(number).all is not a function` downstream. Sanity: 16/16 class-related tests pass + hono entry.ts byte-identical. New regression test test_iife_class_or_pattern.ts.

## v0.5.756 — Refs #446 / #420 followup: `obj.method` read-as-value on Any-typed receivers now returns a bound-method closure instead of undefined. Pre-fix the codegen's #446 bound-method-closure arm at `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::PropertyGet` (line ~3596) only fired when the receiver's static class was known via `receiver_class_name(ctx, object)`. For Any-typed receivers (function args, `(x as any)` casts) the static class wasn't recoverable so the path fell through to the runtime `js_object_get_field_by_name` which had no method-binding fallback — every read of a method-as-value returned undefined. Drizzle's `(ins as any)._prepare` chain (and many other library patterns: `arr.map(obj.method)`, `if (obj.method)`, `typeof obj.method === "function"`) depend on this. Fix: added a method-binding fallback at the END of `js_object_get_field_by_name`'s slow path in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs` — when the receiver has a registered class_id and the key matches a method via `lookup_class_method_in_chain`, allocate a bound-method closure (`BOUND_METHOD_FUNC_PTR` sentinel + (this, name_ptr, name_len) captures) via `js_class_method_bind`. The closure routes calls through `js_native_call_method` which walks the same vtable chain that direct calls do. The method-name buffer is freshly alloc'd so it survives independently of the keys_array. **Note**: this fallback runs AFTER the fields lookup AND the getter walk, so field reads + accessor getters are unaffected. Repro: `class P { m() { return "p"; } } class C extends P {} function check(c: any) { console.log(typeof c.m, c.m()); } check(new C())` returns `function p` matching Node (was: `undefined undefined`). Drizzle's chain now progresses past `_prepare()` to a new "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'name')" downstream — separate iteration. Sanity: 15/15 class-related tests pass + hono entry.ts byte-identical. New regression test test_method_bind_any_receiver.ts.

## v0.5.755 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: cross-module ctor body's `this.X = Y` assignments after `super(...)` no longer get overwritten by post-init field-initializer re-application. Pre-fix `lower_call.rs::lower_new` re-applied the leaf class's field initializers AFTER calling the cross-module constructor, because `class.constructor.is_some()` evaluated false on the imported-class STUB (the stub doesn't carry the constructor body — only `constructor_param_count`). The re-apply path wrote `undefined` (or the field's declared default) over whatever the source-module ctor body had freshly assigned. Drizzle's `class BetterSQLiteSession extends SQLiteSession { client; schema; logger; constructor(client, dialect, schema, options) { super(dialect); this.client = client; this.schema = schema; this.logger = options.logger ?? new NoopLogger(); } }` — the load-bearing site — was losing all three fields after `super()`, leaving `sess.client = undefined` so `this.client.prepare(query.sql)` in the inherited `prepareQuery` chain threw deeper down. Fix: gate the re-apply on `!has_imported_ctor_body` (consults `ctx.imported_class_ctors` for the leaf class). When the imported ctor handles its own field inits internally (via SuperCall + apply_field_initializers at the source-module codegen), don't re-apply at the call site. Repro: `import { Parent } from "./other-module"; class Derived extends Parent { client; constructor(c, d) { super(d); this.client = c; } } new Derived("X","Y").client` returns `"X"` matching Node (was `undefined`). Sanity: 14/14 class-related tests pass + hono entry.ts byte-identical. New regression test test_cross_module_ctor_assign.ts. **Out of scope (next iteration)**: drizzle's full insert chain still hits `_prepare().run()` returning undefined.run — the inherited `prepareOneTimeQuery` → `this.prepareQuery` → `new PreparedQuery(...)` chain produces undefined somewhere; needs separate bisection.

## v0.5.754 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: `obj[strKey](args)` computed-key method call now dispatches through the class vtable chain (parent inheritance included). Pre-fix this fell through to the generic call path that read `obj[index]` as a value (returning undefined for class methods on Any-typed receivers) and then tried to call undefined — drizzle's `this.session[isOneTimeQuery ? "prepareOneTimeQuery" : "prepareQuery"](...)` chain threw `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'run')` because `_prepare()` returned the result of calling-undefined. Two coordinated changes: (1) **runtime helper** `js_native_call_method_str_key(recv, name_str_handle, args_ptr, args_len)` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs` — extracts bytes/length from an already-unboxed StringHeader pointer for the method name and forwards to `js_native_call_method` (which walks the class vtable chain via `lookup_class_method_in_chain`); (2) **codegen arm** at the top of `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs::lower_call` — when the callee is `Expr::IndexGet { object, index }` AND the index is a string-typed expression (literal, string-typed local, conditional with both branches string), routes through `js_native_call_method_str_key`. Gated on string-index because numeric `obj[42]()` would have a different (non-method) semantic. The conditional-string case (`obj[cond ? "a" : "b"]()`) is the load-bearing drizzle shape; both `is_string_expr` and `is_definitely_string_expr` are consulted to pick up that shape. Repro: `class Parent { m() { return "p"; } } class Child extends Parent {} const c: any = new Child(); c["m"]()` returns "p" matching Node (was: TypeError calling undefined). Sanity: 12/12 class-related tests pass + hono entry.ts byte-identical. New regression test test_computed_method_call.ts. **Out of scope (next iteration)**: `typeof obj[strKey]` (read-as-value, no call) still returns undefined for class methods on Any-typed receivers — separate followup that needs a different code path. Drizzle's full insert chain still hits other blockers further down (e.g. `getSQL` on undefined dialect somewhere); separate iteration.

## v0.5.753 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: `Object.getPrototypeOf(<instance>)` returns the value itself instead of null, and `class_ref.constructor` synthesizes back to the class ref. Together this makes drizzle's `is(value, type)` chain — `Object.getPrototypeOf(value).constructor === value.constructor` — collapse to the leaf class ref correctly. Pre-fix the runtime helper returned null for any non-class-ref receiver, so drizzle's chain threw `null.constructor` immediately on the first chunk in `buildQueryFromSourceParams`. Two coordinated changes in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs`: (1) `js_object_get_prototype_of` for top16=0x7FFD heap pointers and top16=0x0000 raw I64 pointers (module-level object literals — Strings stored as F64 NaN-boxed, Arrays/Objects as raw I64 per CLAUDE.md "Module-level variables") returns `obj_value` itself — class-tagged instances then route `.constructor` through the v0.5.746 intercept (returns class ref), object literals/arrays return undefined which collapses drizzle's `if (cls)` chain to false safely; (2) `js_object_get_field_by_name`'s INT32-class-ref arm intercepts the `"constructor"` key BEFORE the CLASS_DYNAMIC_PROPS lookup and returns the class ref itself, so `class_ref.constructor === class_ref` (Node-incompatible — Node returns Function — but Perry has no Function meta-class, and this is what drizzle's chain needs). Sanity: 12/12 class-related tests pass + hono entry.ts. New regression test test_get_prototype_of_instance.ts. **Out of scope (next iteration)**: drizzle's full insert chain still fails further down — `Table[entityKind]` returns undefined (StringChunk/Param/Name work), suggesting an issue with how Perry compiles classes that have BOTH static [Symbol] AND instance [Symbol] field declarations (Table has many of both). Filing as next iteration.

## v0.5.752 — Three perry/ui fixes coming out of the sharity-perry-app port. **Closes #612** (`NavStack(state, routes)` rendered a blank screen on iOS-sim — macOS turned out to be broken too once tested with the issue's actual repro). Four-layer fix: (1) **state-desugar didn't recognize the capital `State<T>(initial)` factory** — `crates/perry-transform/src/state_desugar.rs::collect_state_bindings` only matched lowercase `method == "state"`, so the issue's repro (`const route = State<string>("a")`) bypassed every state rewrite, leaving `NavStack(state, routes)` as a 2-arg `NativeMethodCall` that the codegen catch-all silently dropped on the floor. Now matches both spellings — the d.ts at `types/perry/ui/index.d.ts:288` and `:458` declares them as aliases of the same factory. (2) **iOS NavStack was a `UIStackView`** (axis=vertical) hosting all route bodies as `addArrangedSubview`s, with `js_navstack_register_route` hiding all but the active route. UIStackView's `distribution=.fill, alignment=.fill, axis=vertical` defaults gave the visible arranged subview only its intrinsic content size, and `VStack(16, [Spacer, Text, Spacer])` bodies have intrinsic height ≈ Text's 36pt — rest of the screen stayed blank. Switched `crates/perry-ui-ios/src/widgets/navstack.rs::create()` to a plain `UIView`; standard `add_child` falls through to `zstack::add_child`'s "pin to fill parent" pinning. (3) **macOS NavStack** was the parallel `NSStackView` shape, same intrinsic-content-size collapse with all four `screen()` bodies pinned. Switched `crates/perry-ui-macos/src/widgets/navstack.rs::create()` to plain `NSView`, registered via the new `zstack::register_as_zstack(handle)` helper so `add_child` routes through the zstack pin-to-fill arm. (4) **Initial-visibility setHidden fired before the platform UI handler was registered** — `js_navstack_register_route` runs at App-build time inside the codegen-emitted IIFE, but the platform's `js_register_widget_hidden_handler` only fires inside `app_run` (after the IIFE has already returned). Every "hide non-active route" call against the null handler silently no-op'd, leaving every route body visible and overlapping (the most-recently-added one on top). Extended `js_register_widget_hidden_handler` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/ui_text_registry.rs` to drain the existing `NAVSTACK_REGISTRY` snapshot at handler-registration time, dispatching initial visibility for every pre-registered route based on the current `STATE_VALUES`. Imperative `navstackPush` / `navstackPop` still work — they already route through `super::add_child` and have always called `set_hidden` directly, never through the registry. **Closes #634** (helper-built widgets pushed to an array inside a for-loop and passed to `HStack`/`VStack` rendered nothing on iOS): the codegen at `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call/native.rs` only iterated children when the children expression was an `Expr::Array(...)` literal — `for (const x of xs) ys.push(chip(x)); HStack(8, ys)` fell into the else-arm that lowered the children expression for side effects only, so `perry_ui_widget_add_child` never fired and the loop-built widgets were silently orphaned. Replaced the else-arm with an inline LLVM loop (`ui_addch.header` / `body` / `exit`) that calls `js_array_get_length` once and then `js_array_get_element` + `unbox_to_i64` + `perry_ui_widget_add_child` per element. Cross-platform: the bug was codegen-side, so the fix unblocks the for-loop pattern on every UI target, not just iOS where it was first noticed. **Closes #635** (need a single `Image({url, alt})` widget — `ImageGallery` was the only URL-fetching primitive, and inline avatars / list-row thumbnails can't reuse a paged carousel): added `Image(url, alt?)` and `Image({url, alt})` surface (object-form destructured at HIR-codegen boundary in the same `extract_options_fields` pass `App({...})` uses). Real implementations on macOS (`crates/perry-ui-macos/src/widgets/image.rs::create_url`, `NSImageView` + `std::thread::spawn` background fetch + `dispatch_async_f` main-thread apply) and iOS (`crates/perry-ui-ios/src/widgets/image.rs::create_url`, `UIImageView` with `.scaleAspectFill` content mode + the same dispatch_async_f pattern from `image_gallery::load_remote`). Stubs on tvOS / visionOS / watchOS / GTK4 / Android / Windows / web / wasm so `compilePackages`d code that uses `Image()` still compiles + runs against an empty placeholder there. New `MethodRow` in `crates/perry-dispatch/src/lib.rs` (`Image` → `perry_ui_image_create_url`, `args: [Str, Str]`, `ret: Widget`); new `method("perry/ui", "Image", false, None)` in `crates/perry-api-manifest/src/entries.rs`; new `Image(url: string, alt?: string): Widget` + `Image(options: { url: string; alt?: string }): Widget` overloads in `types/perry/ui/index.d.ts`. `placeholder` and `contentMode` from the issue's spec are deferred to v2 (issue acceptance criterion only requires `url` + `alt`).

## v0.5.751 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: `Object.getPrototypeOf(<class ref>)` walks the CLASS_REGISTRY parent chain. Pre-fix the codegen wrapper at `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::ObjectGetPrototypeOf` returned the operand unchanged (passed `o` through `lower_expr` without any transformation), so `cur = Object.getPrototypeOf(cur)` in a while-loop walk produced `cur === cur` forever — drizzle's `is(value, type)` chain (which walks `value.constructor[entityKind]` up the prototype chain) hung in an infinite loop on subclass dispatch. Two coordinated fixes: (1) **runtime helper** `js_object_get_prototype_of` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs` — for INT32-tagged class refs (top16 == 0x7FFE), reads parent_class_id from `CLASS_REGISTRY` (already populated at module init via `js_register_class_parent` from codegen's parent-pairs loop) and returns the parent class ref as another INT32-tagged value, or null at the chain root; non-class-ref receivers still return null (Perry doesn't synthesize a real prototype chain for object instances); (2) **codegen wrapper** updated to actually call the runtime helper with the operand value instead of passing through. Repro: `class Base{}; class Mid extends Base{}; class Leaf extends Mid{}; let cur:any = Leaf; while (cur && cur.kind) { cur = Object.getPrototypeOf(cur); }` now terminates after Base (was: hung). End-to-end: drizzle's full `is(value, type)` chain — `value instanceof type` short-circuit + `value.constructor[entityKind]` walk + parent-chain traversal — works byte-identical to Node across SQL/Aliased/Other/Subclass cases. Sanity: 11/11 class-related tests pass + hono entry.ts. New regression test test_object_get_prototype_of_class.ts.

## v0.5.750 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: dynamic `instanceof` — `value instanceof type` where `type` is a runtime expression (function arg, local holding a class ref) — now dispatches correctly. Pre-fix, the HIR captured `ty` as a static IDENTIFIER name. When the right side was a local var like `function is(value, type) { return value instanceof type; }`, the lowering stored `ty = "type"` (the param name); codegen couldn't resolve "type" as a class id and fell through to `cid = 0`, so `js_instanceof(value, 0)` returned false for every dynamic instanceof — drizzle's `is(value, type)` chain returned false for every legitimate type match. Four coordinated layers: (1) **HIR variant extension** — added optional `ty_expr: Option<Box<Expr>>` field to `Expr::InstanceOf` in `crates/perry-hir/src/ir.rs`, populated when the right-side identifier resolves to a local in scope (via `ctx.lookup_local`); (2) **codegen dispatch** in `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::InstanceOf` — when `ty_expr.is_some()`, lowers the type expression as a runtime value and calls `js_instanceof_dynamic(value, type_ref)` instead of the static `js_instanceof(value, cid)`; (3) **runtime helper** `js_instanceof_dynamic` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs` — extracts class_id from the INT32 NaN-tag (top16=0x7FFE) and dispatches to `js_instanceof`; returns FALSE for non-class-ref type values defensively; (4) **walker fix** in `crates/perry-hir/src/walker.rs` — extracted `Expr::InstanceOf` from the single-child group into its own arm that visits BOTH `expr` and `ty_expr` so the inliner / closure-conversion / monomorph substitution see both. Without (4), inlined call sites had `LocalGet(N)` references inside `ty_expr` that never got substituted, defaulting to undefined. Repro: `function isType(value: any, type: any) { return value instanceof type; }; isType(new SQL(), SQL)` now returns `true` matching Node (was `false`). Also covers `const T: any = SQL; sql instanceof T`. Sanity: 15 tests pass + hono entry.ts byte-identical. New regression test test_instanceof_dynamic_type.ts.

## v0.5.748 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: static field access via Any-typed local holding a class ref now works. Pre-fix `const X: any = A; X.kind` returned `undefined` because the codegen's PIC fast path treated INT32-tagged class refs (top16=0x7FFE) as non-pointer values and routed to the invalid arm. Drizzle's `is(value, type)` chain reads `cls.kind` through Any-typed locals; without this fix the entire entity-kind dispatch returns the wrong answer. Three coordinated layers: (1) **codegen PIC arm** in `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::PropertyGet` adds an `is_int32_class` check (top16 == 0x7FFE) that BEFORE the existing pointer-validity arm routes through `js_object_get_field_by_name_f64` with the unmasked `obj_bits` so the runtime can detect the INT32 tag; (2) **runtime helper** `js_class_register_static_field(class_id, name_ptr, name_len, value)` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs` populates `CLASS_DYNAMIC_PROPS` so static-field lookups via the runtime path find the value — codegen calls this from `Expr::StaticFieldSet` lowering for every initializer in addition to writing the per-field module global; (3) **synthetic `constructor` intercept** in `js_object_get_field_by_name` returns the class ref for an instance receiver (reads `(*obj).class_id` and returns the INT32-tagged ref if registered), so `instance.constructor` matches Node behavior. Repro: `class A { static kind = "A" } const X: any = A; X.kind` returns `"A"` matching Node (was `undefined`). The IC-miss-side parallel intercept was tried but reverted — small handle ids from native modules (axios / fastify / fetch handles) numerically collide with class_ids, and the runtime can't distinguish them after the codegen unbox; the codegen-side approach preserves the tag and is unambiguous. Sanity: 15 tests pass + hono entry.ts byte-identical (r1=200/r2=200/r3=404). New regression test test_static_field_via_local.ts.

## v0.5.747 — perf(transform): interprocedural deforestation pass (`crates/perry-transform/src/deforest.rs`). Detects the **producer/consumer fusion shape** — a function that allocates a fresh array, fills it via `out.push(...)` (and possibly recursive self-calls whose results are spliced into `out` via the canonical `for(j) out.push(child[j])` consume loop), and returns the array — and rewrites the producer to take an accumulator out-parameter, the consume loops to fuse with the recursive call, and the top-level callers to pre-allocate the accumulator. **End-to-end on aya_koto's ABC451D bench** (Zenn article reader's "Perry is 6.5× slower than Bun" complaint, originally diagnosed in v0.5.736 as GC-throughput-bound on string[] allocation pressure with no single-commit fix): Perry drops from **3165 ms → 760 ms** (4.16× faster than baseline), within **1.75× of Bun** (411 ms) — was 7.3× slower pre-fix. The transform fires INSIDE the recursive `search()` call: each call's intermediate `child` array is eliminated (the inner recursion writes directly into the top-level accumulator, no per-call allocation, no copy loop), so a previously O(N) array-allocation chain collapses to O(1). **Three coordinated guards** keep the rewrite sound (initial implementation broke 4 of 8 affected tests until they landed): (a) skip exported functions — their callers may live in other modules this intra-module pass can't see; (b) skip producers whose body contains any closure expression — the safe-pattern analyzer doesn't walk closure bodies, so a `forEach((x) => out.push(x))` would slip past detection but break at substitution time; (c) skip producers whose calls appear in expression positions (`f(args).join(",")`, `someFn(f(args))`, `return f(args)`) — the rewritten producer returns `undefined`, so any caller using the return as a value would silently break. Detection pass: 3 phases — (1) per-function shape analysis, (2) producer-as-value detection (bail if `Expr::FuncRef(f)` ever appears in non-callee position, e.g. `const g = f; g(args)`), (3) call-site shape verification (bail if any call lives outside the supported `let X = f(args);` or bare `f(args);` shapes). Transformation: subst `LocalGet(out_local)` → `LocalGet(out_param)` everywhere including HIR variants with direct `LocalId` fields (`ArrayPush.array_id`, `ArrayPushSpread.array_id`, `ArrayPop`, `ArrayShift`, `ArrayUnshift.array_id` — not Expr children, missed by the generic walker). Runs BEFORE `inline_functions` so the inliner sees the rewritten signatures. Scope: intra-module only (cross-module deforestation needs whole-program analysis or a wrapper-shim layer that preserves the original signature for external callers — separate followup). 11 unit tests cover bucket math + gating state machine; 5 detection-edge tests cover async / multi-return / reassignment / out-passed-to-call / capture-into-closure rejection. Detection fires on 8 functions across the test-files corpus; all 8 affected tests + 12 sanity tests + 27/28 gap tests pass byte-identical to Node (the 1 failure, `test_gap_console_methods`, is a pre-existing timing-sensitive `console.time` divergence unrelated to deforestation). Stretch goal of 1.5 s on ABC451D not hit (Bun's V8 also gets escape analysis for the `const Y = a + b` intermediate string allocations, which Perry's intra-function escape analysis already handles for object literals but not strings yet — the Bun gap is now string-allocation-side, not array-allocation-side).

## v0.5.746 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: `instance.constructor` now returns the class ref (was `undefined`). The keys_array lookup never finds `"constructor"` because the class isn't stored as a field on the instance — the chain fell through to `undefined`. Drizzle's `is(value, type)` chain uses `value.constructor[entityKind]` to identify class types; without `.constructor` working the entire entity-kind dispatch returns false. Fix: added a `"constructor"` key intercept in `js_object_get_field_by_name` (in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs`) that fires AFTER the type-tag bailouts but BEFORE the keys_array lookup. Reads `(*obj).class_id`; if non-zero AND registered (consults v0.5.745's `is_class_id_registered` helper), returns the INT32-tagged class ref (`0x7FFE_0000_0000_0000 | class_id`). Pure object literals (class_id=0) and unregistered class refs still return undefined — matches Node behavior where bare object literals don't have a custom `.constructor`. Repro: `class SQL {}; const s = new SQL(); typeof s.constructor` returns `"function"` and `s.constructor === SQL` is true (was: `"undefined"` and false). Drizzle's `is()` chain now correctly returns `true` for `is(new Aliased(), Aliased)` and `false` for `is(new Aliased(), SQL)`. Sanity: 8 tests pass. New regression test test_instance_constructor.ts.

## v0.5.745 — Refs #420 / #618 followup: `typeof` of a class ref obtained via `PropertyGet` (or `LocalGet` after dynamic assignment) now returns `"function"` instead of `"number"`. Class refs are NaN-boxed as `INT32_TAG | class_id` (top16=0x7FFE); `js_value_typeof`'s is_int32 branch fell through to `"number"` for them. The previous v0.5.743 fix only worked for direct ClassRef expressions (special-cased at codegen TypeOf lowering); chained access like `(SQL as any).Aliased` lowered via the runtime path and missed. Three layers: (1) new `REGISTERED_CLASS_IDS: RwLock<Option<HashSet<u32>>>` thread-local registry in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs` with public `is_class_id_registered(class_id) -> bool` helper; (2) new `js_register_class_id(class_id: i32)` runtime FFI declared in `crates/perry-codegen/src/runtime_decls.rs` and called once per class at module init from `emit_string_pool` in `crates/perry-codegen/src/codegen.rs` (collects ids from `hir.classes` + `class_ids` map, dedupes, registers all); (3) `js_value_typeof` is_int32 arm in `crates/perry-runtime/src/builtins.rs` consults the registry — registered class id → `"function"`, otherwise → `"number"`. The CLASS_VTABLE_REGISTRY fallback inside is_class_id_registered (kept for safety) covers any class that registers methods even if the explicit id-register hasn't fired. Repro from drizzle's load-bearing site: `class SQL {}; ((S:any)=>{class Aliased{}; S.Aliased=Aliased;})(SQL); typeof (SQL as any).Aliased` returns `"function"` matching Node (was `"number"`). Sanity: 7 tests pass (test_inheritance / test_simple_class / test_break_continue / test_edge_promises / test_gap_async_advanced / test_gap_typeof_instanceof / test_gap_class_advanced). New regression test test_typeof_class_via_get.ts. **Out of scope (next iteration)**: `instanceof` for INT32-tagged class refs still returns truthy for non-matching classes — drizzle's `is(value, type)` chain has its own remaining blocker.

## v0.5.744 — Refs #420 / #618: Symbol-keyed static class fields now readable via `cls[symbol]` and `symbol in cls`. Pre-fix `class A { static [KIND] = "A-kind" }` registered the value in `CLASS_STATIC_SYMBOLS` at module init via `js_class_register_static_symbol`, but: (1) `js_object_get_symbol_property` only consulted the per-instance `SYMBOL_PROPERTIES` map and returned undefined for class refs; (2) `js_object_has_property` (the `in` operator) bailed early for non-pointer receivers, so `KIND in A` returned false. Both runtime fns now detect INT32-tagged class refs (`top16 == 0x7FFE`) and route through `class_static_symbol_lookup`. Drizzle's `cls[entityKind]` and `entityKind in cls` chain inside `is(value, type)` now correctly identifies class types. Sanity: 9 tests + hono entry.ts byte-identical (r1=200, r2=200, r3=404). New regression test test_class_static_symbol.ts. **Out of scope (separate followup)**: drizzle's `db.insert(...).run()` still fails — the next blocker is in the `is(value, type)` chain, where `value instanceof type` returns truthy for non-matching classes (Perry's instanceof for INT32-tagged class refs has its own dispatch quirks). Filing as next iteration.

## v0.5.743 — Refs #618 / #420 (drizzle): static properties added to imported classes via the IIFE pattern `((SQL2) => { SQL2.Aliased = Aliased; })(SQL)` now persist.

## v0.5.742 — Refs #420 / #488 (drizzle): tightened the `.join()` array-fold gates in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs` so user objects with their own `.join` static method are not misrouted to `Array.prototype.join`.

## v0.5.741 — Refs #420 / #631 followup: 4-level inheritance with intermediate-class field initializers depending on parent body state.

## v0.5.740 — Refs #637 final followup: forEach-captured-array IndexSet with Any-typed string key now works.

## v0.5.739 — Refs #637 followup: extends `js_array_set_string_key` to also handle Object/Closure receivers (gc_type detection at entry — falls through to `js_object_set_field_by_name` for non-array). Routes the polymorphic IndexSet's str_set arm at `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::IndexSet` through this helper instead of the unconditional object-setter call.

## v0.5.738 — JSON.stringify cosmetic followup to v0.5.737: sparse-array gap-fill in `js_array_set_f64_extend` now writes `TAG_HOLE` instead of raw `0.0`.

## v0.5.737 — Closes #637 / #603 (hono r2 param routes): three more coordinated fixes that get param-route extraction working end-to-end.

## v0.5.736 — BigInt small-int fast path + slow-path early-exit (`crates/perry-runtime/src/bigint.rs`).

## v0.5.735 — Closes #638 (hono r2 RegExpRouter trie regex corruption): `regexp.replace(/regex/g, callback)` where receiver was Any-typed (e.g. result of `this.#privateField.method()`) and callback was a closure literal returning a string literal had the closure coerced to "[object Object]" via `js_native_call_method`'s string-replacement fallback path.

## v0.5.734 — Refs #637 (hono r2 RegExpRouter): two more coordinated fixes that get the param-route detection unblocked.

## v0.5.733 — Refs #637 (hono r2 RegExpRouter): two coordinated fixes that unblock `regex.source` / `.flags` survival across function boundaries and the property-IC false-hit on non-Object headers sharing GC_TYPE_OBJECT.

## v0.5.732 — Closes #609 (`Buffer.toString('utf8')` on bytes that aren't valid UTF-8 SIGSEGV'd the runtime).

## v0.5.731 — Refs #633 (hono r2 box_get warnings): closes the closure-capture-from-hoisted-async-fn-decl bug surfaced in the synthetic compose pattern.

## v0.5.730 — Closes #636 (namespace member call dispatches receiver as TAG_TRUE → `(boolean).fn is not a function`).

## v0.5.729 — Closes #610 (`ForEach(state<number>, render)` didn't re-render when the bound state changed via `.set()`). #599 (state.text) shipped in v0.5.706 but the `ForEach` half from the same reproducer wasn't covered. Mirrors the v0.5.706 architecture (text_registry pattern).

## v0.5.728 — Closes #633 partial (`.then()` on async-arrow's call result silently dropped its callback). `is_promise_expr` in `crates/perry-codegen/src/type_analysis.rs` only recognized `Expr::FuncRef` calls (top-level async function decls registered in `cross_module.local_async_funcs`) as Promise-returning — it did NOT recognize `Expr::LocalGet(let_id)` calls where the…

## v0.5.727 — Refs #611 (Effect SIGSEGV bisected): `globalThis[<computedKey>] = value` and `(globalThis as any)[id] ??= new Map()` patterns wrote to a 0-pointer sentinel and read back undefined.

## v0.5.726 — Closes #632 (hono r1 regressed from 200 to 404 after v0.5.722's dynamic-dispatch override probe).

## v0.5.725 — Closes #627 (`axios.get(url).then(r => r.data.ok)` — `r.data` should auto-parse JSON when Content-Type is `application/json`, matching npm axios).

## v0.5.724 — Closes #630 (`Buffer.allocUnsafe(size)` and `Buffer.byteLength(s, encoding)` failed at codegen Phase 2 — "expression BufferAllocUnsafe not yet supported").

## v0.5.723 — Closes #629 (`import * as fsp from "node:fs/promises"` resolved to TAG_TRUE → `typeof fsp === "boolean"` → confusing `(boolean).readFile is not a function` errors). Two-part fix.

## v0.5.722 — Refs #603 / #620 followup: `this.method = X` override now also fires on the dynamic-dispatch path.

## v0.5.721 — Closes #628 (`const m = obj.method; m(...)` returned undefined for class-method bound closures).

## v0.5.720 — Closes #618 (function-static properties via IIFE pattern — `((sql2) => { sql2.identifier = ...; })(sql)` — drizzle-orm's `sql` tag and TypeScript's namespace-on-function emit).

## v0.5.719 — Closes #616 (TypeError uncaught-throw output diverges from Node — `Uncaught exception:` prefix line + duplicate stack header).

## v0.5.718 — Closes #624 (compilePackages: synthetic node_modules pkg with `module.exports = { fn }` failed at link, then on link-fix surfaced an unrelated runtime call-returns-undefined regression in IIFE-wrapped CJS modules).

## v0.5.717 — Closes #623 (`typeof process` / `typeof console` / `typeof globalThis` returned `"number"` instead of `"object"`). `import process from "node:process"` lowers to HIR `NativeModuleRef("process")`, which the codegen represents as a `0.0` stub double (`crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs:8927`); `js_value_typeof` correctly identifies that as `"number"`.

## v0.5.716 — Refs #603 (hono routes-with-params): closes the three bisected blockers (#619 / #620 / #621).

## v0.5.715 — **Closes #604 #625 #626 #627** (axios.get + node:http hangs after `server.close()` — full architectural fix).

## v0.5.714 — v0.5.713 broke `cargo build --release -p perry` because `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/optimized_libs.rs:170` calls `super::well_known::bundled_staticlib_path_for_target(workspace_root, binding, rust_target_triple(target))` (3-arg, target-aware) but `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/well_known.rs:74` only had `bundled_staticlib_path` (2-arg, host-only).
