## v0.5.713 — Closes the three baseline parity gaps (#613 / #614 / #615) flagged in CLAUDE.md and tracked by tier 2 of the release-sweep harness.

## v0.5.712 — Three leaked `eprintln!` debug lines in `crates/perry-ext-http-server/src/{server,http2_server,https_server}.rs` (line 219 / 254 / 261 — `[node:http] listening on http://...`, `[node:http2] listening on https://...`, `[node:https] listening on https://...`) fired on every `server.listen()` call.

## v0.5.711 — Closes #542/#543 followup: `m.keys()` / `m.values()` / `m.entries()` on a `Map<K, V> | undefined` parameter narrowed via `if (!m) return;` returned the array-fold shape (`[0..N-1]` for keys, garbage for values) because `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs:3319` only matched bare `Type::Generic { base: "Map" }` for the receiver-type predicate.

## v0.5.710 — Parity regression in v0.5.707: 39 tests failing (82.7% rate) vs the v0.5.696 baseline of 6 (97.3%).

## v0.5.709 — `cargo test --workspace` was still red after v0.5.708 due to the parallel `manifest_consistency` invariant: `every_dispatch_entry_has_manifest_counterpart` panicked because `NATIVE_MODULE_TABLE` had `ioredis::connect` and `ioredis::disconnect` rows (added in v0.5.707's #605 fix when normalizing the `redis` npm alias) but `API_MANIFEST` had no matching…

## v0.5.708 — `cargo test --workspace` regression from v0.5.707: the `imported_func_has_rest` field added to `CompileOptions` for #608's cross-module rest-param plumbing was missed in the `empty_opts()` test helper at `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/object_cache.rs:514`. `cargo test -p perry` failed with E0063 missing-field; tier 1 of the release-sweep harness…

## v0.5.707 — Multi-issue release-sweep cycle (#601–#608). **#601** (perry-ext-fetch tests broken at workspace `cargo test`): `crates/perry-ext-fetch/src/lib.rs:1140/1179/1196` — three test-code type mismatches against the post-Phase-1 NaN-boxed FFI surface (integer literal where f64 expected, missing 4th `headers_handle: f64` arg on `js_request_new`, `resp as i64` cast…

## v0.5.706 — **Closes #599** (`state.text()` widget never updated on macOS after `state.set("new value")`).

## v0.5.705 — **Closes #600** (LLVM Phase-2 codegen regression vs v0.5.491: `Expr::FetchGetWithAuth`, `Expr::FetchPostWithAuth`, `Expr::DateToLocaleString` had no LLVM arms — fell through to the catch-all "expression … not yet supported" error).

## v0.5.704 — **Closes #598** (`axios.post(url, {email, password})` sent the raw bytes of Perry's `ObjectHeader` struct followed by the first character of the stringified field — server received `^@^B^@^@H...` instead of `{"email":"demo@example.com","password":"hunter2"}`).

## v0.5.703 — **Closes #597** (drizzle #488 blocker: `for (const [i,v] of arr.entries())` silently iterates 0 times when `arr` is statically typed `any`).

## v0.5.702 — **Closes #596** (sync throw inside async fn body — `obj.status` where `obj` is undefined, post-await — escaped the user's `try { await x; ... } catch { … }` and exited the program).

## v0.5.701 — **Closes #594 #595** (`async function callIt() { try { ... await ... } catch {} ; if (cond) return X; }` returned `X` directly to the iterator caller, surfacing as `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'done')` on `__step_r.done`).

## v0.5.700 — **Closes #593** (`const off = ev.on(() => { off(); })` inside a function: closure body's `off()` no-ops because it captured the let's pre-init slot value).

## v0.5.699 — **Closes #591** (`argon2.hash(password)` on perry-ext-argon2 reported "Invalid password" because the password StringHeader pointer never reached the FFI).

## v0.5.698 — **Closes #592** (`String.prototype.toLocaleLowerCase` / `toLocaleUpperCase` were silently routed through the unknown-method catch-all in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_string_method.rs:675-709`, which emits `js_throw_type_error_not_a_function("string", "<prop>")` + `unreachable`.

## v0.5.697 — **Closes #590** (`Object.assign(target, src)` was orphaning Symbol-keyed properties on `target` and breaking `result === target` identity / `target instanceof Class`).

## v0.5.696 — **Closes #589 (runtime path)**: builds on v0.5.694's link-gap fix to make the issue's full acceptance criterion pass — `compilePackages: ["hono"]` + `import { createServer } from "node:http"` + `app.fetch(new Request(...))` + `fetchRes.headers.forEach((v,k) => res.setHeader(k,v))` + `res.end(await fetchRes.text())` now compiles, runs, and `curl…`

## v0.5.695 — **Closes #588** (closure body `() => new <ImportedClass>(...)` inside a compilePackages module: link-fail or silent class-id-collapse — `_factory` reads back as `1.26e-321` regardless of what's assigned).

## v0.5.694 — **Closes #589** (link gap: `import { Hono } from "hono"` + `import { createServer } from "node:http"` failed at link with `Undefined symbols: _js_headers_new / _js_response_new / _js_request_new`).

## v0.5.693 — **Refs #551** — documentation only. Lists `@bradenmacdonald/s3-lite-client` as a known-working npm package alongside `hono` in `docs/src/packages/porting.md`, and adds a new "AWS S3 / S3-Compatible Object Storage" section to `docs/src/stdlib/http.md` (between WebSocket and Next Steps).

## v0.5.692 — **Closes #585 #586** (drizzle-orm #488 query-side blockers).

## v0.5.691 — **Closes #577** (Node-compat HTTP server modules — `node:http` + `node:https` + `node:http2` + WebSocket upgrade integration).

## v0.5.690 — **Closes #584** (`new TextEncoder().encode(s)` Uint8Array had wrong backing bytes — every byte read returned the IEEE-754 representation of the byte value, not the byte itself).

## v0.5.684 — **Closes #583** (deep links: Universal Links / App Links / URL schemes).

## v0.5.677 — **Closes #582** (network reachability — online/offline + change events). Already shipped via the v0.5.676 omnibus + the follow-up commit `627cc9f9` that wired the missing dispatch / manifest / collect_modules / .d.ts surfaces and the GTK4 / visionOS / watchOS / Windows stubs.

## v0.5.676 — **Refs #577** (HIR/codegen integration: idiomatic `import { createServer } from "node:http"` + `server.listen(...)` + handler closure with `req.method()` / `res.end(...)` now compiles + runs end-to-end).

## v0.5.675 — **Closes #536** (`@perryts/mysql` hangs after `pool.end()` because `try { return await ... } finally { Y }` skipped Y entirely).

## v0.5.674 — **Refs #552** (mobile stdlib gaps for production apps).

## v0.5.668 — **Closes #578 #579** (s3-lite-client SigV4 byte-handling blockers from #551).

## v0.5.665 — **Closes #575 #576** (s3-lite-client SigV4 blockers from #551).

## v0.5.664 — **Closes #572 #573 #574** (s3-lite-client compile blockers from #551).

## v0.5.663 — **Closes #569** (function-scoped `function` declarations not hoisted): inner `function helper(...) {...}` declarations inside a function body are now lowered with proper ECMAScript hoisting + initialization.

## v0.5.662 — **Closes #535** (state→UI binding propagation outside `--target harmonyos`).

## v0.5.661 — **Closes #567** (`String.prototype.split(separator, limit)`): pre-fix, the codegen path in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_string_method.rs` rejected the 2-arg form with `String.split expects 1 arg (delimiter), got 2` at compile time.

## v0.5.660 — **Closes #486 (logger middleware)** — two coordinated fixes that together unblock the full hono `app.use('*', logger()) + app.get('/', c => c.json({...}))` chain end-to-end.

## v0.5.658 — **Refs #486** (try/catch around self-recursive closure body breaks self-reference): `let dispatch = (i) => { try { dispatch(i+1); } catch (e) {} }` (and the equivalent `async function dispatch(i) { try { await dispatch(i+1); } catch (e) {} }` declared inside an async closure body) had its self-reference invisible to the boxing analysis.

## v0.5.609 — Closes #528 (followup to #515/#518): chained-call array-method fold leaked through the `recv_is_class` `_ => false` catch-all — `this.col().find({})` got rewritten as `Expr::ArrayFind(this.col(), {})` and `js_array_find` read garbage from the user object's header. New `Call(inner)` arm in `recv_is_class` only allows the fold when the inner call's method is a known array-producing builtin; otherwise bails to runtime dispatch.

## v0.5.749 — **Closes #617** (`await fetchWithAuth(url, auth)` / `await fetchPostWithAuth(url, auth, body)` inline returned `undefined`; the explicit two-step `let p = fetchWithAuth(...); await p;` produced the resolved Response). The two HIR shapes only diverged in where the fetch call landed: the inline form lowered to `Stmt::Let { id: response, init: Some(Await(FetchGetWithAuth { ... })) }`, which the async-to-generator transform rewrote to `Yield { value: FetchGetWithAuth { ... } }`. The generator state-machine transform then planted that whole expression as the `value` field of the iter-result object literal — so `js_fetch_get_with_auth` was invoked while the `{value, done}` object was still being constructed (the generator emits `Return(Object([("value", FetchGetWithAuth { ... }), ("done", false)]))` directly). The two-step form lowered to `LocalSet(p, FetchGetWithAuth); Yield { value: LocalGet(p) }` — the promise lands in a stack slot first and the iter-result reads it back via a plain `LocalGet`. Per the user's diagnostic the resolved value never reached the await driver: the promise pointer that `js_fetch_get_with_auth` queued (`result_bits = 0x7ffd000000000002`) was correct, but the bits flowing through `js_value_is_promise` (called from `js_promise_resolved` inside the async-step driver's `Promise.resolve(r.value).then(...)` chain) were `TAG_UNDEFINED` (0x7ffc000000000001) and `0x0` — i.e. `r.value` read back as the iter-result object's pre-init `JSValue::undefined()` slot fill from `js_object_alloc_with_shape`. Fix mechanizes the user's verified workaround at the HIR level: in `crates/perry-transform/src/async_to_generator.rs`, both `hoist_awaits_avoiding_top_level` (for statement-positioned awaits like `let response = await fetchWithAuth(...)`) and `hoist_awaits_in_expr_full` (for nested-position awaits, via the post-await-hoist branch) now also hoist `Expr::FetchGetWithAuth` / `Expr::FetchPostWithAuth` operands out of the await. New helper `hoist_fetch_with_auth_inside_await` checks the immediate inner of an `Expr::Await`; if it matches one of the two fetch-with-auth built-ins, it allocates a fresh local id, replaces the inner with `LocalGet(id)`, and pushes a `Stmt::Let { name: "__await_fetch_<id>", init: Some(<original FetchGetWithAuth>) }` into the hoisted-stmts list. The generator transform then sees `Yield { value: LocalGet(__await_fetch_N) }` instead of `Yield { value: FetchGetWithAuth }` — exactly the two-step path. Verified the rewrite via `--print-hir`: state 0 of the inline-form's state machine now contains `LocalSet(4, FetchGetWithAuth { ... }); LocalSet(state, 1); Return(Object([("value", LocalGet(4)), ("done", false)]))` — identical shape to the manually written two-step form. Regression test in `test-files/test_issue_617_inline_await_fetch_with_auth.ts` spins up a node:http echo server on 127.0.0.1:38617 and asserts `typeof === "object"` + `.status === 200` for both inline and two-step forms of both `fetchWithAuth` (GET) and `fetchPostWithAuth` (POST) — all four pass. The 14 perry-transform unit tests pass; 10 async/promise/fetch parity tests (`test_async{,2,3,4,5}`, `test_async_chain`, `test_edge_promises`, `test_gap_async_advanced`, `test_issue_233_async_array_param_push`, `test_issue_236_fetch_then_console_log`, `test_issue_633_async_arrow_then`) match Node byte-for-byte. The fix is a no-op for any await operand other than the two fetch-with-auth built-ins — sequence-point semantics preserved (the temp's init runs in the same position the inline call would have). Couldn't reproduce the bug in a standalone repro at HEAD (the user reported against v0.5.713, current is v0.5.743 with 30 versions of fixes between), but the rewrite is conservative — it converts `await X` to `let __t = X; await __t` only for `X ∈ {FetchGetWithAuth, FetchPostWithAuth}` and only when `X` is the immediate operand of an `Expr::Await`.

## v0.5.657 — **Closes #494** (native-libraries authoring guide fixture is real, compileable, and drift-protected): pre-fix the v0.5.652 sweep replaced the two `typescript,no-test` fences in `docs/src/native-libraries/authoring-guide.md` with `{{#include}}` directives pointing at `docs/examples/_fixtures/native-libraries/my-bindings/{src/index.ts,test-app.ts}`, but the fixture only had those two TS files — no `package.json`, no `Cargo.toml`, no Rust crate. The doc-tests harness then added `_fixtures` to its skip list (`crates/perry-doc-tests/src/main.rs:389`), so neither end of the fixture was actually compile-tested; the issue's #1 work item ("a real fixture package … with package.json … a trivial Rust crate exposing `js_pdf_parse`") and #3 work item ("the snippets become genuinely compile-tested against the fixture") were both unmet. Three coordinated additions: (1) `docs/examples/_fixtures/native-libraries/my-bindings/Cargo.toml` declares a private `perry-doc-fixture-my-bindings` crate (publish=false) with `crate-type = ["staticlib", "rlib"]` and `[lib] name = "perry_ext_my_bindings"` so the staticlib filename matches what the guide's `perry native validate` output block shows. perry-ffi is the only dep — no upstream Rust crate, since the fixture's job is to drift-protect the perry-ffi surface, not parse PDFs. (2) `src/lib.rs` ships a `js_pdf_parse(buf_ptr: i64) -> *mut StringHeader` stub that exercises both halves of the perry-ffi API the guide teaches against (`read_buffer_bytes` to unbox the NaN-tagged buffer pointer + `alloc_string` to return a fresh JS string), returning `"stub: <N> bytes"` so the round-trip is observable. Two unit tests pin the round-trip and the null-buffer fallback. (3) `package.json` declares the `perry.nativeLibrary` block exactly as the guide shows: abiVersion 0.5, single `js_pdf_parse` function entry with `params: ["i64"]` / `returns: "string"`, three target arms (macos / linux / windows). Plus the workspace wiring: added the fixture path to `[workspace] members` (NOT default-members — same convention as the platform-specific UI crates, so default `cargo build` doesn't pay for it) so `cargo test --workspace` (which CI runs on every PR per `.github/workflows/test.yml:158`) builds the fixture and runs its tests. Drift protection now flows: any breaking change to perry-ffi's `alloc_string` / `read_buffer_bytes` / `BufferHeader` / `StringHeader` surface will fail the fixture's compile, surfacing the docs page as out-of-date in the same PR. The `_fixtures` skip in `perry-doc-tests` stays — wiring `import { parse } from "my-bindings"` resolution through the harness would need `node_modules` setup that's a bigger change; the Rust-side drift coverage is the meaningful protection. Refs #491 (the audio-recording PR that originally flagged the untagged fences) and #466 (native-bindings ecosystem v1).

## v0.5.655 — **Closes #562** (user classes can extend `WritableStream` / `ReadableStream` / `TransformStream`): canonical Web-Stream-subclassing pattern (`class ObjectUploader extends WritableStream<Uint8Array> { constructor(...) { super({ write, close, abort }); this.getResult = () => result; } }` from `@bradenmacdonald/s3-lite-client`) now compiles + runs end-to-end. Pre-fix, `super({...})` to a stream base class fell through to the SuperCall arm's "parent not in ctx.classes, not error_like" branch which lowered the args for side effects then returned undefined — the user's `write` / `close` / `abort` (or `start` / `pull` / `cancel` / `transform` / `flush`) callbacks were dropped, and a subclass instance passed to `pipeTo` / `pipeThrough` was a NaN-boxed object pointer that the existing stream FFIs (`js_writable_stream_*` / `js_readable_stream_*` / `js_transform_stream_*`) interpreted as a registry id, found nothing, and silently no-op'd. **Three coordinated fixes**: (1) `crates/perry-hir/src/lower_decl.rs` — add `ReadableStream` / `WritableStream` / `TransformStream` to both `native_parent` matches alongside the existing `EventEmitter` / `AsyncLocalStorage` / `WebSocketServer` arms, AND populate `extends_name` (parent name) alongside `native_extends` so the codegen SuperCall arm reaches the parent-name switch instead of the no-extends fallthrough. New `class_native_extends` registry on `LoweringContext` so `let x = new SubclassOfStream(...)` in `destructuring.rs` can route the local through the parent stream module's dispatch table. New `current_class_super_ident` field for the `super({...})` pre-scan in `expr_call.rs` to register `start`/`pull`/`transform`/`flush` callback's controller param as a `readable_stream` native instance — same shape the `new TransformStream({...})` pre-scan in `expr_new.rs` does. (2) `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::SuperCall` — when `parent_name` is one of the three stream classes (and not in `ctx.classes`), invoke a new `lower_stream_super_init(ctx, kind, super_args)` helper that walks `args[0]` via the existing `extract_options_fields` (handles both `Expr::Object` and `__AnonShape_*` forms), lowers each canonical callback (`start`/`pull`/`cancel` for readable, `write`/`close`/`abort` for writable, `transform`/`flush` for transform; missing → `TAG_UNDEFINED`), reads `highWaterMark` from `args[1]` if present, then dispatches to a runtime `js_X_stream_subclass_init(this, ...callbacks, hwm)` shim. Per JS spec, leaf-class field initializers run AFTER super() returns — the helper applies `FieldInitMode::SelfOnly` after the runtime call so subclass fields like `seenLengths = []` are initialized before any inherited callback fires. (3) `crates/perry-stdlib/src/streams.rs` — three new `*_subclass_init` shims reuse the existing `alloc_readable` / `alloc_writable` / `js_transform_stream_new` paths so the registry entries / GC roots / `TRANSFORM_PAIRS` row are identical to a plain `new ReadableStream(...)`; the resulting registry-id is then stashed on the subclass instance via `js_object_set_field_by_name(this, "__perry_stream_handle__", handle)`. Plus a fourth piece: `js_stream_unwrap_handle(value: f64) -> f64` — checks for a NaN-boxed object pointer (top16=0x7FFD), reads `__perry_stream_handle__`, returns its f64 numeric value; falls through to the input unchanged for already-numeric handles. Wrapped around every stream-FFI receiver and stream-handle argument in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` (`module == "readable_stream"` recv + `pipeTo` dest + `pipeThrough` transform; `module == "writable_stream"` recv; `module == "transform_stream"` recv) so subclass instances and bare numeric handles are interchangeable at the FFI boundary. Plus two gates in `expr_member.rs` + `expr_call.rs` (matching helper `is_stream_api_member` / `is_stream_api_method` keyed on the same dispatch tables in `lower_call.rs`) so subclass-declared user fields (`uploader.uploadedSize`) and methods (`uploader.getResult()`) fall through to regular object property / class-method dispatch instead of hitting the streams `NativeMethodCall` arm and returning the receiver-less zero-sentinel. Stream-API methods/properties themselves still route through the streams arms so inherited `pipeTo` / `getWriter` / `cancel` etc. work on subclass instances. End-to-end smoke (`test-files/test_issue_562_stream_subclass.ts`) exercises all three subclass shapes (Readable / Writable / Transform); a scaled-up s3-lite-client style test (TransformChunkSizes feeding ObjectUploader, 3 chunks totaling 224 bytes) reports the right `uploadedSize` and `completed` via the user's `getResult()` method. The chained `readable.pipeThrough(t).pipeTo(w)` form requires the inner `pipeTo` to dispatch through `module == "readable_stream"` based on `pipeThrough`'s return type — a separate (pre-existing) limitation tracked outside this issue; the split form (`const piped = readable.pipeThrough(t); await piped.pipeTo(w)`) where the intermediate variable picks up the existing `destructuring.rs:1709` ReadableStream-from-pipeThrough recognition works fully. Sanity tests `test_inheritance`, `test_gap_class_advanced`, `test_gap_closures`, `test_edge_promises`, `test_gap_async_advanced`, `test_gap_fetch_response` continue to match Node byte-for-byte; gap suite holds at 26/28 with the same 2 pre-existing fails (`test_gap_console_methods`, `test_gap_object_methods`).

## v0.5.654 — **Closes #561** (Web Crypto: `crypto.subtle.{digest,importKey,sign,verify}` for AWS SigV4 / JWT / web-push signing chains): pre-fix, every pure-JS S3 / signed-URL / JWT library (s3-lite-client, aws4fetch, jose, oidc-client-ts, web-push) hit the issue-#463 strict-API gate at `crypto.subtle` and refused to compile — `compilePackages` couldn't pull any of them in. New `crates/perry-stdlib/src/webcrypto.rs` ships the symmetric WebCrypto subset: `digest("SHA-1"|"SHA-256"|"SHA-384"|"SHA-512", data)` → `Promise<Uint8Array>`, `importKey("raw", keyBytes, {name:"HMAC", hash:{name:"SHA-256"}}, extractable, usages)` → `Promise<CryptoKey>`, `sign("HMAC", key, data)` → `Promise<Uint8Array>`, `verify("HMAC", key, sig, data)` → `Promise<boolean>`. `CryptoKey` is a Buffer marked Uint8Array with an entry in a process-global `CRYPTO_KEY_REGISTRY: Mutex<HashMap<usize, (algo, hash)>>` recording the `(KeyAlgo::Hmac, HashAlgo)` pair so `sign` / `verify` route to the right primitive. The async wrapping is decorative — SHA / HMAC are CPU-bound and resolve synchronously inside the returned Promise (issue's implementation note explicitly says this). `verify` uses constant-time byte comparison so it doesn't leak the position of the first mismatching byte through timing. `bytes_from_jsvalue` accepts strings (StringHeader / SHORT_STRING_TAG inline SSO), Buffer / Uint8Array (BufferHeader registered in BUFFER_REGISTRY), TypedArrayHeader (Uint8Array via the typed-array path), AND TextEncoder-allocated ArrayHeaders — the last one is the subtle case: `enc.encode("abc")` returns an ArrayHeader registered in BUFFER_REGISTRY but with bytes stored as f64 elements at offset 8 (so `instanceof Uint8Array` works while the decoder can recover UTF-8); without the side-table check (`text::is_text_encoder_result`), reading `enc.encode("abc")` as packed u8 yielded the first 3 bytes of each f64's IEEE-754 LE representation (all-zero for small ints) instead of the source bytes — first iteration of the impl produced `709e80c8...` for SHA-256("abc") instead of the spec value `ba7816bf...`. Made `text::is_text_encoder_result` `pub` to expose the registry to webcrypto.rs. New HIR variants `WebCryptoDigest / WebCryptoImportKey / WebCryptoSign / WebCryptoVerify` in `crates/perry-hir/src/ir.rs`; HIR lowering arm in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs` recognizes `crypto.subtle.<method>(args)` (3-deep Member chain `Member(Member(Ident("crypto"), "subtle"), "digest")`) BEFORE the generic `mod.X.Y()` arm, dispatches to the right HIR variant by method+arity, and emits a clear "out of scope" error for unsupported subtle methods (encrypt/decrypt/generateKey/wrapKey/unwrapKey/deriveKey — explicitly listed as out of scope in the issue). Codegen at `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs` lowers each variant to the corresponding `js_webcrypto_*` extern that returns `*mut Promise` and NaN-boxes with POINTER_TAG. New runtime decls in `runtime_decls.rs`. New manifest entry `property("crypto", "subtle")` in `crates/perry-api-manifest/src/entries.rs`. Detection arm in `collect_modules.rs::compute_required_features` flips on the `crypto` Cargo feature so auto-optimize rebuilds perry-stdlib with the SHA / HMAC deps available — without it, link fails with `_js_webcrypto_digest` undefined. End-to-end smoke `test-files/test_issue_561_webcrypto.ts` exercises NIST SHA-256("abc") + AWS SigV4 first-step kDate + RFC 4231 HMAC-SHA-256 Test Case 2 + verify round-trip + tampered-sig rejection — all 6 lines match `node --experimental-strip-types` byte-for-byte. Acceptance smoke `test-files/test_issue_561_sigv4_chain.ts` runs the full `kSecret → kDate → kRegion → kService → kSigning` chain shape from `@bradenmacdonald/s3-lite-client::signing.ts:317-330` (the issue's named acceptance criterion); final hex matches Node byte-for-byte. Updated `crates/perry-hir/tests/unimplemented_api_check.rs` — replaced the pre-#561 "crypto.subtle is rejected" test with three new tests: `crypto_subtle_namespace_compiles`, `crypto_subtle_digest_compiles`, `crypto_subtle_encrypt_is_rejected` (covers the out-of-scope path). All 10 tests in the strict-API check suite pass; the 5 webcrypto unit tests (parse_hash_alg, AWS SigV4 vector, SHA-256 empty + abc vectors, constant_time_eq) pass. Out of scope per the issue: asymmetric (`RSA-PSS`, `ECDSA`, `RSA-OAEP`), `generateKey`, `wrapKey` / `unwrapKey`, `deriveKey`, `encrypt` / `decrypt`.

## v0.5.653 — **Refs #486** (try/catch with unused catch binding around recursive async): `async function dispatch(i) { ... try { await dispatch(1); } catch (e) {} ... }` returned `undefined` and never even entered state 0. The async-to-generator pre-pass correctly recorded the catch param `e` (id=K) in its `compute_max_local_id` walk — its `Stmt::Try` arm scans `c.param`. The downstream generator state-machine pass (`crates/perry-transform/src/generator.rs`) ALSO has its own `compute_max_local_id` (it re-walks because the async pre-pass may have allocated new ids), but its `scan_stmt_for_max_local`'s `Stmt::Try` arm only walked `body` / `catch.body` / `finally` — it never read `catch.param`. When the catch body was empty (`catch (e) {}`), no `LocalGet`/`LocalSet` carried the catch param's id, so the body-only scan missed it entirely; the generator transform then allocated `__gen_state` over that same id. Every `LocalSet(__gen_state, N)` clobbered `e`, every read of `e` returned the state counter, and the state machine never advanced — the function appeared to return `undefined` immediately because `__async_step(undefined, false)` saw `done=true` on the very first `next()` call (state was already past every arm). Fix: scan `c.param` in `scan_stmt_for_max_local`'s `Stmt::Try` arm, mirroring the equivalent scan in `crates/perry-transform/src/async_to_generator.rs::scan_stmt`. Repro `try { await dispatch(1); } catch (e) {}` now prints `[d] 0 / [d] 1 / result: 0` matching Node. Hono's `compose()` wraps every middleware in this exact shape (`try { res = await handler(c, () => dispatch(i+1)); } catch (err) { ... }` — the `err` binding is unused on the success path), so this fix unblocks a class of hono failures distinct from the v0.5.649 RegExp / v0.5.643 string+string / v0.5.641 alias work.

## v0.5.652 — **Refs #536** (`@perryts/mysql` connection-pool acquire returned the wrong branch on empty pool + event loop exited before `await sock.on('connect')` resolved when sockets were routed through perry-ext-net): three coordinated fixes. (1) `crates/perry-runtime/src/array.rs::js_array_pop_f64` and `js_array_shift_f64` returned `f64::NAN` (bare NaN bits) for empty arrays — but per ECMAScript §23.1.3.21 / §23.1.3.27, `Array.prototype.pop` / `shift` on an empty array return `undefined`, NOT NaN. Bare NaN compares `!== undefined` (different bit patterns), so connection-pool drivers like `@perryts/mysql` doing `const entry = this.idle.shift(); if (entry !== undefined) { ... } else { /* allocate new */ }` took the truthy branch on an empty pool, attempted to use a NaN-typed entry as a connection, and crashed. Fix returns `TAG_UNDEFINED_F64` (NaN-boxed undefined: `0x7FFC_0000_0000_0001`) which compares correctly against `undefined`. (2) `crates/perry-ext-net/src/lib.rs` — new `js_ext_net_has_active_handles()` exposing perry-ext-net's `pending_events` + `sockets` registries through the same FFI shape as `js_net_has_active_handles` from perry-stdlib's bundled-net. "Live" intentionally counts sockets still establishing TCP — counting only fully-open ones caused early exit before async `connect` ever completed (the open-flag flips inside the spawned task, AFTER `await TcpStream::connect`). (3) `crates/perry-stdlib/src/common/async_bridge.rs::js_stdlib_has_active_handles` — split the existing `feature = "net"` arm into two: `bundled-net` (perry-stdlib's own net impl is compiled in) keeps the direct `crate::net::js_net_has_active_handles()` call; new `external-net-pump` arm (the well-known flip routes `import 'net'` to perry-ext-net) declares `extern fn js_ext_net_has_active_handles()` and consults it. Pre-fix only the bundled gate fired, so programs using TS-source drivers like `@perryts/mysql` (which routes through perry-ext-net) saw `await new Promise(r => sock.on('connect', r))` exit early because perry-stdlib's empty `NET_SOCKETS` map reported no active handles. Plus a docs change: `docs/src/native-libraries/authoring-guide.md` swaps inline ts code blocks for `{{#include ../../examples/_fixtures/native-libraries/...}}` references so the snippets compile-test against real fixture files; matching `crates/perry-doc-tests/src/main.rs` skip-list update adds `_fixtures` (illustrative snippets that may legitimately import packages absent from this tree, drift-protected via PR diff rather than compile-testing).

## v0.5.650 — **docs sweep** — audited last 200 commits against `docs/src/`. Net new pages: `docs/src/system/background.md` (issue #538 — `registerTask` / `schedule` / `cancel` with the per-platform support matrix); `docs/src/system/geolocation.md` (issue #552 — geolocation + photo-library image picker, including the `Promise` wrapper recipe and the per-platform `Info.plist` / manifest requirements). Existing pages extended: `docs/src/ui/widgets.md` got sections for the issue-#553 mobile widgets (`BottomNavigation`, `ImageGallery`, pull-to-refresh, infinite-scroll) and the issue-#532 / #517 native widgets (`Combobox`, `TreeView`, `Calendar`, `Chart`, `commandPalette*`, `MapView`, `PdfView`, `RichTextEditor`, `widgetSetRichTooltip`); `docs/src/ui/table.md` documents the issue-#473 sort + filter + multi-select extensions; `docs/src/system/audio.md` documents the issue-#458/#491 WAV recording trio (`audioSetOutputFilename` / `audioStartRecording` / `audioStopRecording`); `docs/src/system/overview.md` adds the new APIs to its top-level table. Stale text fixed: `docs/src/stdlib/other.md` removed the "not wired yet — track at #200" warnings on sharp / cheerio / zlib / cron — all four were wired by the v0.5.541 / v0.5.550 / v0.5.551 / v0.5.564 #466 ports. Same page now also covers the npm packages added during the #466 sweep that had no doc presence: events, exponential-backoff, dayjs/date-fns, moment, decimal.js/bignumber.js, rate-limiter-flexible, ethers, plus the external-repo bindings `@perryts/tursodb` (#424) + `@perryts/iroh` (#425) + the pure-TS drivers `@perryts/postgres` / `@perryts/mysql` / `@perryts/mongodb`. `docs/src/stdlib/overview.md` calls out that wired packages live in standalone `perry-ext-*` crates (not `perry-stdlib`) since the v0.5.532 well-known native bindings registry, and points readers at the External-native-bindings list. `docs/src/SUMMARY.md` adds the two new System pages. `mdbook build` clean.

## v0.5.649 — **Refs #486** (`new RegExp(varname)` with non-literal pattern argument): hono's `buildWildcardRegExp` does `new RegExp(path === "*" ? "" : ...)`. Pre-fix, the `Expr::New { class_name: "RegExp", args: [LocalGet(...)] }` HIR shape had no handler in `lower_builtin_new` — fell through to the placeholder `js_object_alloc(0,0)` path, producing a "fake regex" object with no actual pattern. Subsequent `.test(s)` returned false for every input. Concretely: hono's `RegExpRouter.add` for `app.use('*', mw)` does `re.test(p) && middleware[m][p].push([handler, paramCount])` to populate the wildcard middleware into existing routes — `re.test(p)` was false for every `p`, so the middleware was registered but never propagated into route entries. `matchResult[0]` for `app.get('/')` then had only the GET handler, length=1, and the single-handler fast path in `hono-base.js#dispatch` invoked the handler directly, bypassing the middleware chain entirely. Compile-time RegExp LITERALS (`/foo/g`) were already handled by `Expr::RegExp` at `expr.rs:4964` — only the runtime `new RegExp(arg)` form was broken. Fix adds a `"RegExp"` arm to `lower_builtin_new` in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call/builtin.rs` that lowers args 0/1, unboxes to i64 string handles, calls `js_regexp_new(pattern_handle, flags_handle)` and NaN-boxes the result. End-to-end: hono logger middleware now actually runs (`[MW]` and `[MW-after]` print), `matchResult[0].length` correctly reports 2 (middleware + handler), `compose()` is invoked instead of the single-handler fast path. The deeper "next() doesn't dispatch the inner handler" issue (try/catch + recursive async hoisted-function-in-arrow-body) is a separate blocker tracked as the next iteration. Hono canonical (`c.text`) + JSON-response (`c.json`) both still pass; sanity tests test_inheritance, test_edge_promises, test_gap_class_advanced, test_gap_async_advanced, test_gap_closures all match Node.

## v0.5.648 — **Refs #538 (followup)** — replaced the v0.5.644 no-op stubs on tvOS / visionOS / watchOS / macOS with real OS-managed implementations; documented why GTK4 (Linux) and Windows remain stubs. **tvOS** (`crates/perry-ui-tvos/src/background.rs`): full `BGTaskScheduler` clone of the iOS impl. `BGAppRefreshTaskRequest` + `BGProcessingTaskRequest` shapes are identical on tvOS 13+; same `registerForTaskWithIdentifier:usingQueue:launchHandler:` + `submitTaskRequest:error:` semantics. Flush hook added to `PerryAppDelegate.application:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:` in `crates/perry-ui-tvos/src/app.rs`. New `-framework BackgroundTasks` link entry in the tvOS arm of `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/link.rs`. Practical caveat in the .d.ts: tvOS apps only run while the box is on, so "background" here means "during screen saver / different app focused", not "while box sleeping". **visionOS** (`crates/perry-ui-visionos/src/background.rs`): same `BGTaskScheduler` impl, but `flush_pending_registrations()` is called from `app_run()` instead — visionOS apps are SwiftUI-hosted and Perry doesn't own the `UIApplicationDelegate`, so flushing happens before the SwiftUI host takes over UIApplicationMain. New `-framework BackgroundTasks` link entry in the visionOS arm. **watchOS** (`crates/perry-ui-watchos/src/background.rs`): different API surface — `WKApplication.scheduleBackgroundRefresh(withPreferredDate:userInfo:scheduledCompletion:)` (watchOS 7+). Only the `appRefresh` kind has a watchOS equivalent; `processing` is accepted but treated identically. `requiresNetwork` / `requiresCharging` are advisory (OS decides). No native cancel API — `cancel(id)` drops the handler from the registry so a fired refresh becomes a no-op. Identifier is round-tripped via `userInfo["perry_id"]`. New SwiftUI `WKApplicationDelegate` adaptor (`PerryWatchAppDelegate`) added to `crates/perry-ui-watchos/swift/PerryWatchApp.swift` — implements `handle(_ backgroundTasks:)`, reads `userInfo["perry_id"]` from each `WKApplicationRefreshBackgroundTask`, calls `perry_watchos_dispatch_background_task(cstr)` which routes to the registered Rust handler, then signals `setTaskCompletedWithSnapshot(false)`. WatchKit framework imported. **macOS** (`crates/perry-ui-macos/src/background.rs`): real `NSBackgroundActivityScheduler` (Foundation, 10.10+). Different model from iOS — schedule a one-shot block with `repeats=false`, `interval = max(0, earliestStartMs - now)/1000`, `tolerance = interval/4` (or `/2` for processing kind), `qualityOfService = .background` (or `.utility` for processing), `requiresNetworkConnectivity = req_net`. `requiresCharging` doesn't have a direct equivalent on `NSBackgroundActivityScheduler` — set to advisory via QoS hints. Schedulers retained in a per-process registry keyed by id so `cancel(id)` can call `invalidate()`. Block invokes the user handler then calls the OS-supplied `NSBackgroundActivityCompletionHandler` with `NSBackgroundActivityResultFinished` (=1). **GTK4 (Linux)** + **Windows**: stubs kept, but the comments now explain why honestly: Linux desktop has no Perry-callable equivalent (systemd `--user` timer units are deploy-time XML config, cron is admin-managed, neither is a runtime-callable JS API); Win32 has no equivalent without admin elevation (Task Scheduler API requires Schedule Tasks rights and prompts UAC) or being a packaged WinUI 3 / UWP app (MSIX background tasks need declarative `<BackgroundTasks>` extensions in appxmanifest). For periodic refresh while a desktop app IS running, the .d.ts now points users at `setInterval()`. New text in `types/perry/background/index.d.ts` lays out platform-by-platform what's real, what's advisory, and what's a no-op so users can branch via `getDeviceIdiom()` if needed. **Workspace-wide check** passes; cross-target check passes for iOS / tvOS / visionOS / watchOS / macOS — only pre-existing warnings (the `js_string_from_bytes` ABI mismatch present on main). Smoke test `test-files/test_issue_538_background_tasks.ts` still compiles + runs cleanly on macOS host.

## v0.5.644 — **Closes #538** (native bindings for iOS BGTaskScheduler + Android WorkManager): new `perry/background` module exporting `registerTask(identifier, handler)` / `schedule(identifier, kind, earliestStartMs, requiresNetwork, requiresCharging)` / `cancel(identifier)`. iOS impl in `crates/perry-ui-ios/src/background.rs` keeps a per-process `HANDLERS: HashMap<String, f64>` populated from `registerTask` calls during module init; `PerryAppDelegate.application:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:` now calls `background::flush_pending_registrations()` which iterates each identifier and invokes `BGTaskScheduler.shared.register(forTaskWithIdentifier:usingQueue:launchHandler:)` with an `RcBlock` that looks up the stored closure and dispatches via `js_closure_call0` (microtasks pumped before + after; result inspected for Promise but completion semantics defer to a follow-up). `schedule` builds either `BGAppRefreshTaskRequest` (kind=`"appRefresh"`) or `BGProcessingTaskRequest` (kind=`"processing"` — adds `setRequiresNetworkConnectivity` / `setRequiresExternalPower`), populates `setEarliestBeginDate` from `earliestStartMs` (epoch-ms; 0 = no delay), submits via `submitTaskRequest:error:`. `cancel` calls `cancelTaskRequestWithIdentifier:`. Identifiers MUST appear in Info.plist `BGTaskSchedulerPermittedIdentifiers` per Apple's documented contract — calling `submit` with an unregistered id throws an internal NSException; callers see no observable signal but the OS rejects the wake-up. New `-framework BackgroundTasks` linker entry on iOS in `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/link.rs`. Android impl in `crates/perry-ui-android/src/background.rs` delegates via JNI to three new `PerryBridge.kt` static helpers: `backgroundRegisterTask(id, key)` records the (identifier → callback key) mapping in a `MutableMap<String, Long>`; `backgroundSchedule(id, kind, earliestStartMs, requiresNetwork, requiresCharging)` reflectively builds an `androidx.work.OneTimeWorkRequest` with `Constraints.Builder` (`setRequiredNetworkType(CONNECTED)` when `requiresNetwork`, `setRequiresCharging(true)` when `requiresCharging`), stashes the identifier in `Data.Builder.putString("identifier", id)`, computes `setInitialDelay` from `earliestStartMs - System.currentTimeMillis()`, and calls `WorkManager.getInstance(activity).enqueueUniqueWork(id, REPLACE, request)`; `backgroundCancel` calls `cancelUniqueWork(id)`. New `PerryBackgroundWorker.kt` extends `androidx.work.Worker` — `doWork` reads the identifier out of `inputData`, calls `PerryBridge.backgroundLookupCallbackKey(id)`, posts a `Handler(Looper.getMainLooper())` runnable that invokes `PerryBridge.nativeInvokeCallback0(callbackKey)` (Perry's runtime is UI-thread-pinned), waits up to 10 s on a `CountDownLatch`, returns `Result.success()`. Reflection-based call into `androidx.work` keeps the bridge's compile-time deps unchanged and lets the runtime no-op cleanly when the WorkManager dep isn't on the classpath (logged at warn level, not thrown). New `androidx.work:work-runtime-ktx:2.9.0` Gradle dep in both `crates/perry-ui-android/template/app/build.gradle.kts` and the parallel `android-build/app/build.gradle.kts` per the v0.5.632 sync convention. Both Android Kotlin templates updated. Wiring: `PERRY_BACKGROUND_TABLE` in `crates/perry-dispatch/src/lib.rs` exposes the three method rows (Str/Closure for `registerTask`, Str/Str/F64/F64/F64 for `schedule`, Str for `cancel` — all `ReturnKind::Void`); `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` adds a `perry_background_table_lookup` arm next to perry/system + perry/updater (same `lower_perry_ui_table_call` machinery); `crates/perry-api-manifest/src/entries.rs` adds `"perry/background"` to NATIVE_MODULES + RUNTIME_ONLY_MODULES + three `method(...)` entries so the issue-#513 strict-API gate flips strict; `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/collect_modules.rs` flips `needs_ui` when `perry/background` is imported (the runtime FFIs live in `libperry_ui_*.a` alongside the OS-framework integration); `crates/perry/src/commands/check.rs` adds `perry/background` to the strict-API pre-filter. No-op stubs added to perry-ui-macos (inline in lib.rs — desktop apps have no equivalent OS pipeline) + the existing `issue_552_stub.rs` files in perry-ui-tvos/watchos/visionos/gtk4/windows. New TS surface at `types/perry/background/{index.d.ts, package.json}`. End-to-end smoke compile-checks pass for perry-codegen + perry-dispatch + perry-api-manifest + perry-ui-macos (host) + perry-ui-ios (cross-target). Workaround in the issue (clients skip background refresh, server-side cron + push-foreground polling) becomes optional — production-mobile apps can now opt into the OS-managed wake-up path on iOS + Android.

## v0.5.643 — **Refs #486** (string + string concat through type-uncertain operands): `k + c.buildRegExpStr()` inside hono's `Node.buildRegExpStr` for-of body — both operands lower as plain f64s with inferred type Any, the static-string-concat fast path at expr.rs:1118 doesn't fire (`is_definitely_string_expr` returns false because neither has a static narrowing), and the previous fallback path called `js_number_coerce` on each side then `fadd`'d the results — turning `"c" + ""` into `Number("c") + Number("") = NaN + 0 = NaN`. Every recursive level then propagated NaN into the parent's `prefix + tail` concat, until `Trie.buildRegExp`'s `regexp.replace(...)` threw `(number).replace is not a function` because regexp had become NaN. **New runtime helper** `js_dynamic_string_or_number_add` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/value.rs` checks NaN-box tags at runtime: STRING_TAG / SHORT_STRING_TAG → string concat (coerces non-string side via `js_jsvalue_to_string` + `js_string_concat`), BIGINT_TAG → `js_bigint_add`, otherwise numeric `fadd` (with `js_number_coerce` on non-numeric operands the same way the static path did). Codegen in `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::Binary` dispatches there for `op==Add` when neither operand is statically narrowed (`!is_numeric_expr && !is_bigint_expr` on both sides) — the static numeric/bigint paths stay on `fadd` to keep the inline codegen for hot arithmetic loops. End-to-end: hono logger middleware acceptance now compiles, `app.use('*', logger())` + `app.get('/', c => c.json({...}))` returns the exact JSON response that matches Node byte-for-byte (response body + headers); the logger middleware's stderr lines (`<-- GET /` / `--> GET / 200 1ms`) are still missing — that's a separate `app.use` middleware-not-running issue (the route table evaluates the handler directly without composing through middleware), tracked as the next iteration. Closer reduction (`Object.keys(this.#children).map(k => k + this.#children[k].build())` recursive on a trie of `_Node` instances) returns the expected `(?:ac|b)` matching Node, was returning `(?:NaN|NaN)` pre-fix. No regressions on standard `+` cases (`"a" + "b"`, `1 + 2`, `"x" + 1`, `1 + "y"`, `true + 1`, `null + 1`, `undefined + 1` all match Node). Sanity tests (test_inheritance, test_edge_promises, test_gap_class_advanced, test_gap_async_advanced, test_gap_closures) all pass.

## v0.5.642 — **Closes #544** (per-module `.o` cache returned stale objects when only HIR-pass code changed): pre-fix `compute_object_cache_key` mixed in `CARGO_PKG_VERSION` but nothing else identifying the running perry binary, so editing `crates/perry-transform/src/state_desugar.rs` (or any HIR/codegen pass) and rebuilding without bumping the version left the cache key identical — `.perry-cache/objects/<target>/<key>.o` served the previous pass's output and the new pass appeared to do nothing at runtime. Cost the user ~45 min during #535 Layer 1 development debugging a phantom bug; only `rm -rf <project>/.perry-cache` exposed the actual issue. Fix in `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/object_cache.rs` adds `perry_build_id()` — a `OnceLock<u64>` that hashes the running perry executable's bytes via `current_exe() + fs::read + djb2_hash` on first call and reuses the value across all rayon workers in the same process. Mixed into the cache key alongside the existing `v=` (version) field. Any `cargo build -p perry-codegen` / `-p perry-transform` / `-p perry-hir` (or any dep that links into perry) produces a new perry binary, hence a new build id, hence a new cache key — the pass change can't be invisible. Failure modes degrade silently to the pre-fix behavior: if `current_exe()` or the read fails, returns 0 and the version-only path takes over. Cost: one ~16 MB djb2 pass per perry invocation (~tens of ms, amortized across all module compilations and only on first cache-key derivation). Cache scope: still machine-local (`.perry-cache/` in `.gitignore`, host CPU-specific via `-march=native` in clang). New regression test `key_includes_perry_build_id` confirms the helper returns non-zero against the test binary and is stable across calls within a process.

## v0.5.641 — **Refs #486** (cross-module class-expression self-binding alias resolution): `var X = class _X { ... new _X() ... }` is the npm-dist convention for class declarations (hono's `var Node = class _Node {...}`, plus countless others — Babel emits this shape for every `class X { ... }` it transpiles to ES5). The inner self-binding name `_X` is scoped to the class body and references the same class as the outer `X`. Pre-fix, perry's HIR lowering registered the class only under the outer name `X`; references to `_X` from inside the class body lowered as `Expr::ExternFuncRef("_X")` because `lookup_class("_X")` returned None, then `new _X()` lowered as `Expr::NewDynamic { callee: ExternFuncRef("_X") }` and codegen's `try_static_class_name` couldn't resolve it to a class — falling through to the `js_object_alloc(0, 0)` placeholder path that stamps `class_id=0` on the new instance. Subsequent method dispatch on those instances hit the `class_id != 0` gate in `js_native_call_method` and fell through to NULL_OBJECT_BYTES. **Five coordinated fixes**: (1) `crates/perry-hir/src/ir.rs` — new `aliases: Vec<String>` field on `Class`. (2) `crates/perry-hir/src/lower.rs::lower_stmt`'s `var X = class _X` arm now PRE-REGISTERS the inner name as a class alias before lowering the class body, so `Ident("_X")` inside method bodies resolves through the existing `lookup_class` → `Expr::ClassRef` path. (3) `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::try_static_class_name` recognizes `Expr::ExternFuncRef { name }` whose `name` is in `class_ids` (covers any case where the ExternFuncRef route still fires). (4) `crates/perry-codegen/src/codegen.rs::compile_module` populates `class_table`, `class_ids`, `method_names`, and `class_keys_globals_map` with every alias entry; the method/getter symbol-emission loops at codegen.rs:4253 / :4336 skip alias keys (`if *class_name != class.name { continue; }`) so the alias keys don't double-emit `perry_method_<modprefix>__<class>__<method>` symbols (the alias-prefixed name `perry_method_<modprefix>___X__method` would have an extra leading underscore from `sanitize("_X")` that doesn't match the canonical body emission, breaking the link). (5) `crates/perry-transform/src/inline.rs::collect_nonexported_class_names` adds inner-alias names to the rejection set even when the OUTER name is exported — preventing the inliner from copying a method body that references the inner alias into a destination module that only has the outer name in its `imported_classes`. End-to-end: `var X = class _X { spawn() { return new _X(); } method() { return 'hello'; } }; new X().spawn().method()` now returns `'hello'` matching Node (was returning `[object Object]` pre-fix). The hono Node case (`var Node = class _Node { #children = Object.create(null); insert(tokens, index) { ...; this.#children[token] = new _Node(); ... } }`) compiles into a working trie that returns proper string concatenations. Logger middleware acceptance still has a separate blocker — `(number).replace is not a function` deep in hono's RegExpRouter.match — that this release doesn't reach (the construction path is fixed but a different dispatch in the buildRegExp chain produces NaN; tracked as the next iteration). Sanity tests `test_inheritance`, `test_gap_class_advanced`, `test_gap_closures`, `test_edge_promises`, `test_gap_async_advanced` all match Node byte-for-byte.

## v0.5.640 — `.ts` / `.tsx` packages from `node_modules` are now classified `NativeCompiled` at the import site even when not listed in `perry.compilePackages`. Repro: `import { component, relation } from "@codehz/ecs"` (a TS-source npm package without `perry.nativeLibrary` / `perry.nativeModule`) printed `JS module: …` at collect time, emitted real native `.o` files for every `@codehz/ecs/src/*.ts`, and then failed to link with `Undefined symbols: _component, _relation, _pipeline`. Two-sided inconsistency: `resolve_import` returned `ModuleKind::Interpreted` for the bare-specifier fallback (`crates/perry/src/commands/compile/resolve.rs:918`), so the consumer's import record said "this is a JS-runtime module"; but `collect_modules`'s `should_use_js_runtime` check (line 77) gated on `is_js_file(canonical)` — false for `.ts` — so the recursive walk took the native HIR-lowering path. The `.o` files existed with native exports, the consumer's codegen didn't declare them as cross-module externs, `ld` failed. Fix routes resolved `.ts` / `.tsx` entries (not `.d.ts` — `is_ts_file` already excludes those) through `ModuleKind::NativeCompiled` in both bare-specifier (line 918) and `file:`-dep fallback (line 967) sites. `.js` / `.mjs` / `.cjs` keep going to `Interpreted` since those still need V8 / QuickJS. Side benefit: the cosmetic "JS module: foo -> foo/index.ts" log line is gone for TS-only packages — the user-reported confusion ("we should have the typescript file there") was a real symptom of the broken classification, not just a label mistake. End-to-end: the `@codehz/ecs` + `@codehz/pipeline` repro from the issue now compiles + links cleanly with no `compilePackages` entry needed.

## v0.5.639 — Closes #553: four production-mobile-app widgets shipped across the perry/ui surface so an Angular-Material-style screen (issue's sharity-app reference) can be built natively. **BottomNavigation** — 5-tab bottom bar with `{icon, label, badge?}` items, `selectedIndex` state, `onSelect` callback. Real impls on macOS (custom NSStackView+NSButton strip with SF Symbol icons + Adwaita-style tinted-blue selected state + red badge layer), iOS (UITabBar via `setItems:animated:` + UITabBarItem.badgeValue), Android (custom horizontal LinearLayout with vertical (ImageView+TextView) tabs + drawable lookup via `Resources.getIdentifier(..., "drawable", pkg)` + optional badge TextView), GTK4 (GtkBox+GtkButton tab strip with GtkImage+GtkLabel children + Adwaita CSS classes for selected/badge styling). Stub on Windows (Win32 has no native primitive — same convention as the existing tabbar.rs Win32 stub; would need WinUI 3 transition or 300+ lines of owner-drawn child windows). **ImageGallery** — swipeable carousel with `{url, alt}` images + `initialIndex?` + `onIndexChange?`. Real on macOS (horizontal-paging NSScrollView containing NSStackView of NSImageViews; remote URLs loaded via `+[NSData dataWithContentsOfURL:]` on a background dispatch_global_queue, applied on the main thread via `dispatch_async_f`), iOS (paging UIScrollView with UIScrollViewDelegate.scrollViewDidEndDecelerating to fire `onIndexChange` after swipe; pinch-zoom is a follow-up), Android (HorizontalScrollView containing horizontal LinearLayout of ImageViews with `BitmapFactory.decodeFile` for local paths; `smoothScrollTo` for programmatic page jumps), GTK4 (GtkScrolledWindow + horizontal GtkBox of fixed-size GtkPicture pages + GtkAdjustment::value-changed for index tracking). Stub on Windows (same Win32-no-native rationale). **Pull-to-refresh on LazyVStack** — `lazyvstackSetRefreshControl(view, onPull)` + `lazyvstackEndRefreshing(view)` mirroring the existing #390 ScrollView API. iOS-only real for now (LazyVStack itself is still a stub on iOS pending UITableView wiring — separate issue); macOS / Android / GTK4 / Windows are documented no-ops since none of those platforms have a native pull-to-refresh idiom or AndroidX dep imported. **Infinite-scroll callback (onScrollEnd)** — `scrollviewSetScrollEndCallback(view, fn, thresholdPx)` + `lazyvstackSetScrollEndCallback(view, fn, thresholdItems)` with backpressure: fires once per threshold-cross, re-arms only when the user scrolls back up past the threshold. Real ScrollView impl on every platform: macOS uses NSScrollView clip-view bounds notifications via `NSViewBoundsDidChangeNotification`; iOS uses UIScrollViewDelegate.scrollViewDidScroll; Android wires through PerryBridge.setOnScrollEndCallback (new helper, calls `View.OnScrollChangeListener` with the same backpressure logic and a `scrollEndArmed` per-view map); GTK4 uses GtkAdjustment::value-changed; Windows hooks the existing scrollview's WM_VSCROLL / WM_MOUSEWHEEL handlers via a new `check_scroll_end(handle)` after each offset update. LazyVStack scroll-end is real on macOS (parallel observer on the inner NSTableView's clip view, threshold measured in rows × row_height); other platforms gate on the same iOS-LazyVStack-is-a-stub blocker as pull-to-refresh and ship as no-ops with the FFI in place. New: `crates/perry-dispatch/src/lib.rs` adds 11 MethodRow entries flipping the issue #513 strict-API gate; `crates/perry-api-manifest/src/entries.rs` adds 12 manifest entries; `types/perry/ui/index.d.ts` adds the 11 export declarations + the documentation block describing each platform's behavior. New widget files: `crates/perry-ui-{macos,ios,android,gtk4}/src/widgets/{bottom_nav.rs,image_gallery.rs}` (8 new files); scroll-end logic added inline to existing `scrollview.rs` + `lazyvstack.rs` per platform. PerryBridge.kt + the parallel `android-build/` template both gain the `setOnScrollEndCallback` static helper. Smoke test `test-files/test_issue_553_mobile_widgets.ts` exercises every new symbol so the cross-platform link surface is validated by the test suite. Gap suite holds at 26/28 (same 2 pre-existing fails — `test_gap_console_methods` timing diff, `test_gap_object_methods` strict-mode property descriptor gap).

## v0.5.638 — **Refs #486** (cross-module method inlining loses class metadata for non-exported classes referenced inside the body): `lib3` exports `class C` with `populate() { this.children['a'] = new Inner(); }`, where `Inner` is a sibling class declared in the same module BUT not exported. The HIR-level inliner in `crates/perry-transform/src/inline.rs` harvested `populate` as a cross-module-inlinable method (the `is_cross_module_safe` filter only rejects FuncRef / ExternFuncRef / GlobalGet / Closure — NOT `Expr::New { class_name }` against an unexported local class), then copied the body verbatim into the consumer module's HIR. At codegen, the consumer's `ctx.classes` doesn't contain `Inner` (it's not in `imported_classes`), so `lower_new("Inner")` fell into the no-class-metadata fallback at `lower_call.rs:2440` which emits `js_object_alloc(0, 0)` — allocating a heap object with `class_id=0`. Subsequent `inst.method()` dispatch via the dynamic-dispatch tower at `lower_call.rs:1494` reads the receiver's class_id, finds 0, doesn't match any tower entry, and falls through to `js_native_call_method`'s vtable lookup which is gated on `class_id != 0` and returns NULL_OBJECT_BYTES. Net symptom: `root.populate()` ran, the inner instance was attached to `root.children['a']`, but `root.call()` then printed `[object Object]` instead of `'hello-inner'` (the value of `inner.method()`). Counter-control: moving `new Inner()` from `populate()` into `C`'s constructor body made the test pass — because constructor bodies aren't subject to the cross-module method inliner; they're emitted as standalone `<src>__C_constructor` symbols and called via direct `bl`. Fix adds `collect_nonexported_class_names(module)` and `body_references_class_in_set(stmts, set)` helpers to `crates/perry-transform/src/inline.rs`, and consults them in both `gather_cross_module_methods` and `gather_cross_module_methods_with_extern_imports` — methods whose body references any local non-exported class via `Expr::New`/`ClassRef`/`StaticFieldGet`/`StaticFieldSet`/`ClassStaticSymbolSet`/`StaticMethodCall` are excluded from the candidates map. Such methods stay as real cross-module method calls (`bl perry_method_<src>__C__populate`), where the source module's codegen — which DOES have the class metadata — emits the correct inline-alloc with the right class_id. `__AnonShape_*` content-addressed shapes are deliberately KEPT inlinable because they're already propagated via `extra_anon_classes` so the destination module synthesizes the same definition. End-to-end: minimal repro from /tmp/issue-486-followup (`new Inner()` inside `populate()`) now prints `result: hello-inner` matching Node byte-for-byte (was `result: [object Object]` pre-fix). Sanity tests `test_inheritance`, `test_gap_class_advanced`, `test_gap_closures`, `test_edge_promises` all match Node. Hono canonical `app.get('/', c => c.text('hello'))` still passes; logger middleware acceptance has a separate blocker chain past this release (the `(number).replace is not a function` failure inside `RegExpRouter.match → Trie.buildRegExp → Node.buildRegExpStr` is a different code path — Node IS exported from hono, so the inliner-side fix doesn't reach it).

## v0.5.637 — **Refs #517 (followup)** — un-stubs the MapView widget on **tvOS / GTK4 / Android / watchOS** so the v0.5.636 API surface (`MapView(w, h)` + `mapViewSetRegion(lat, lon, latSpan, lonSpan)` + `mapViewAddPin(lat, lon, title)` + `mapViewClearPins()` + `mapViewSetMapType(0|1|2)`) lights up natively on every platform except Windows. Each platform replaces the silent-zero-handle stubs in `crates/perry-ui-<plat>/src/lib.rs` with real impls under `widgets/map_view.rs`. **tvOS** is a source-equivalent UIKit copy of the iOS impl — `MKMapView` works identically on Apple TV (Siri Remote drives focus-engine pan/zoom inside the view); also adds `-framework MapKit` to the tvOS arm of `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/link.rs`. **GTK4** wires up `libshumate` (GNOME's GTK4-native vector-tile widget) — the `set_region` impl converts MapKit's lat-span convention to libshumate's tile-zoom via `zoom = log2(360 / max(latSpan, lonSpan))` so the same TS code is portable; OSM-mapnik is the default tile source (no API key); markers go into a lazily-allocated `MarkerLayer` overlay; `clear_pins` drops the layer; `set_map_type(non-0)` logs an info line and stays at standard since the bundled `MapSourceRegistry` only ships one source. Cargo dep is `libshumate = "0.6"` (matches gtk4-rs 0.9 in the workspace — 0.5/0.7 conflict on the gtk-4 `links =` value). New pkg-config / hardcoded-fallback link block in `link.rs` for `shumate-1.0`, mirroring the existing GTK4 / GStreamer blocks. **Android** wires Google Maps SDK MapView via JNI calls into 4 new `PerryBridge.kt` static helpers (`mapViewCreate` / `mapViewSetRegion` / `mapViewAddPin` / `mapViewClearPins` / `mapViewSetMapType`) plus a `forwardMapsLifecycle(event)` hook. The Kotlin side queues early calls in a per-MapView pending-ops list and replays them once `getMapAsync` fires; `set_map_type` translates 0/1/2 → `MAP_TYPE_NORMAL` / `_SATELLITE` / `_HYBRID` (Google's enum is 1/2/3/4 with `_TERRAIN=3`, so the mapping is explicit). Activity lifecycle (onResume / onPause / onLowMemory / onDestroy) forwarded from `PerryActivity.kt` to keep MapView's stateful tile loading correct across rotation / background. Gradle dep `com.google.android.gms:play-services-maps:18.2.0` added to `template/app/build.gradle.kts`; `<meta-data android:name="com.google.android.geo.API_KEY" .../>` placeholder added to AndroidManifest.xml — user replaces `__YOUR_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY__` with their own key from Google Cloud Console. **watchOS** extends the existing data-driven SwiftUI tree model: new `NodeKind::MapView = 16` + `map_lat / _lon / _lat_span / _lon_span / _type / _pins: Vec<(f64, f64, CString)>` fields on `NodeData`; 9 new FFI getters in `tree.rs` (`perry_watchos_node_map_lat` / `_lon` / `_lat_span` / `_lon_span` / `_type` / `_pin_count` / `_pin_lat(idx)` / `_pin_lon(idx)` / `_pin_title(idx)`); `swift/PerryWatchApp.swift` adds `import MapKit`, the matching `@_silgen_name` declarations, a `PerryMapPin: Identifiable` model, and a `mapView` SwiftUI subview rendering `Map(coordinateRegion:annotationItems:)` with `MapMarker` pins (deprecated-but-watchOS-7-compatible API for hardware reach — newer `Map { Marker(...) }` shape requires watchOS 10). `set_map_type` is recorded in the tree but ignored by the renderer since SwiftUI's watchOS Map doesn't expose `mapStyle` on watchOS 7-9. **Windows** is the one holdout — WinUI MapControl lives in the UWP / WinUI 3 world and hosting it inside Perry's Win32 HWND chain requires XAML Islands + Windows App SDK integration, a substantial body of work tracked as a separate follow-up (#559). Build verified on macOS host: workspace compiles clean (libshumate 0.6 resolves cleanly against gtk4-rs 0.9, no `gtk-4` links collision); `perry-ui-watchos` `cargo check` clean; minimal `MapView(w, h)` + `mapViewSetRegion` + `mapViewAddPin` + `mapViewClearPins` + `mapViewSetMapType(1)` smoke test compiles and the launched binary renders a real MKMapView on macOS. Cross-compile verification (NDK / GTK4-on-Linux / WatchKit) deferred to maintainer's host environments at merge time. Note: the parallel `android-build/` template at the repo root needs the same Kotlin / Manifest / Gradle changes — kept in sync at merge time per the v0.5.632 convention.

## v0.5.636 — Closes #532 + #555 (PR `ui-loop-issues`): 11 native UI widgets + cross-platform un-stub pass + tableSetFilterText FFI fix.

**Widget pass (issue #472 production-widget gap analysis):** macOS implementations land for `widgetSetRichTooltip` (#479, NSPanel + NSTrackingArea hover-delay), `Combobox` (#475, NSComboBox + setCompletes), `TreeView` (#480, NSOutlineView + PerryTreeItem identity cache), data-table sort + filter + multi-select extensions on the existing `Table` (#473, NSSortDescriptor + selectedRowIndexes), `Calendar` v1 (#481, NSDatePicker graphical mode), `Chart` v1 (#474, line / bar / pie via PerryChartView + CoreGraphics), `commandPalette*` (#477, NSPanel + NSSearchField + NSTableView), `MapView` (#517, MKMapView, MapKit linked), `PdfView` (#516, PDFView, PDFKit linked), `RichTextEditor` v1 (#478, NSTextView + NSAttributedString HTML round-trip), and the `Toast` watchOS gap-fill (#476).

**Cross-platform un-stub pass:** GTK4 ports 7 widgets (calendar / combobox / tree / tooltip / palette / chart / rich text via gtk4-rs 0.9). Windows ports 5 (calendar / combobox / tree / rich text / chart via windows 0.58 + new WM_NOTIFY router for MCN_SELCHANGE + TVN_SELCHANGEDW). iOS ports 5 (calendar / map / pdf / rich text / chart via objc2-ui-kit 0.3, MapKit + PDFKit linked). visionOS ports 5 (direct iOS port with `define_class!` rename pass). tvOS / Android / watchOS deferred per platform fit.

**API additions** in `types/perry/ui/index.d.ts`: `widgetSetRichTooltip`, `Combobox`/`comboboxAddItem`/`comboboxSetValue`/`comboboxGetValue`, `TreeNode`/`treeNodeAddChild`/`TreeView`/`treeViewExpandAll`/`treeViewCollapseAll`/`treeViewGetSelectedId`, `tableSetOnSortChange`/`tableSetAllowsMultipleSelection`/`tableGetSelectedRowsCount`/`tableGetSelectedRowAt`/`tableSetFilterText`/`tableGetFilterText`, `Calendar`/`calendarSetDate`/`calendarGetSelectedDate`, `Chart`/`chartAddDataPoint`/`chartClearData`/`chartSetTitle`/`chartReload`, `commandPaletteRegister`/`commandPaletteUnregister`/`commandPaletteClear`/`commandPaletteShow`/`commandPaletteHide`, `MapView`/`mapViewSetRegion`/`mapViewAddPin`/`mapViewClearPins`/`mapViewSetMapType`, `PdfView`/`pdfViewLoadFile`/`pdfViewGetPageCount`/`pdfViewGoToPage`/`pdfViewGetCurrentPage`/`pdfViewSetScale`, `RichTextEditor`/`richTextSetString`/`richTextGetString`/`richTextSetHtml`/`richTextGetHtml`/`richTextToggleBold`/`richTextToggleItalic`/`richTextToggleUnderline`. Linker additions for MapKit + PDFKit on macOS / iOS / visionOS in `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/link.rs`.

**Closes #555** (uncovered during PR verification): `perry_ui_table_get_filter_text` had `-> f64` while `perry-dispatch`'s `tableGetFilterText` row declares `ReturnKind::Str` (codegen expects a NaN-boxed string pointer in `i64` register x0 per the canonical `textfieldGetString` protocol, not f64 in d0). On ARM64 the calling convention puts `i64` returns in x0 and `f64` returns in d0 — different registers entirely. Codegen read x0 (stack garbage) for tableGetFilterText, interpreted it as a StringHeader pointer, and downstream `textSetString(...)` panicked inside `NSString -initWithBytes:length:encoding:` because the bytes pointed at junk memory. Aligned `widgets/table.rs::get_filter_text` with the canonical `textfield::get_string_value` pattern: returns `*const u8` to a pinned StringHeader (GC_FLAG_PINNED so it survives until codegen's NaN-box step); FFI wrapper at `perry_ui_table_get_filter_text` casts pointer to `i64`. Verified end-to-end: `tableSetFilterText(t, "br")` → `tableGetFilterText(t)` → string concat → `textSetString(out, ...)` round-trip works without crashing.

**Pre-existing bug #556 deferred:** `Table` renderCell with string-concat closure body produces NSException at first layout — `view_for_column` from commit `d7bcd0e4` (2026-02-28), three months before this PR. The typical real-world pattern (`Text(arr[r][c])`) renders fine.


## v0.5.635 — **Closes #557** (zero-arg `console.log()` emitted nothing — should emit a newline matching Node/bun): the codegen catch-all in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` for the entire `console.*` surface returned `undefined` immediately when `args.is_empty()` for any non-special method (`groupEnd` and `clear` had explicit arms; `count`/`countReset`/`time*` fell through to a labeled-default handler), so `log`/`info`/`warn`/`error`/`debug`/etc. all silently no-op'd at zero arity. Cosmetically visible: `examples/collision-detection.ts` and several other examples use empty `console.log()` calls as section separators that disappeared under perry. Fix routes `log`/`info`/`debug` → `js_console_log_spread(0)`, `warn` → `js_console_warn_spread(0)`, `error` → `js_console_error_spread(0)`. All three runtime fns already short-circuit on null and print a single `\n` to the appropriate stream (stdout for log/info/debug, stderr for warn/error per Node convention). Unknown zero-arg console methods (`dir`, `assert`, etc.) keep printing nothing — no change vs main. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_557_console_log_empty.ts` covers the issue's repro plus zero-arg `info`/`debug` and a multi-arg sanity check. Gap suite holds at 26/28 (same 2 pre-existing fails — `test_gap_console_methods` timing diff, `test_gap_object_methods` strict-mode property descriptor gap).

## v0.5.634 — **Closes #540** (`[...map]` spread produced garbage when source was a Map): `[...map]` lowered through `Expr::ArraySpread` codegen at `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs:1781` which calls `js_array_concat(dest, src)`. The runtime had a Set-detection arm (`is_registered_set` → `js_set_to_array`) but no parallel Map arm, so `js_array_concat`'s loop walked the `MapHeader` as if it were an `ArrayHeader` — reading the `size` field (offset 0) as `length` and pulling values out of `(map_ptr + sizeof(ArrayHeader))` which on a `MapHeader` lands inside the entries-array pointer. Result was a single bogus f64 (`1.086462978432e-311` for the issue's repro — bit-reinterpretation of an entries-array pointer) instead of the `[key, value]` pair Array. `Array.from(map)` worked because it routes through `js_array_clone` which already had matching Map detection (added at the same time as the Set arm). Fix mirrors the Set arm in `js_array_concat`: when `is_registered_map(src)` is true, materialize `js_map_entries(src)` (already produces an Array of `[k, v]` pair Arrays — the exact shape `[...map]` should produce) and recursively concat that. End-to-end: issue's repro now matches Node byte-for-byte. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_540_spread_map.ts` covers single-entry undefined-value, multi-entry mixed-type, spread inside larger literal, `[...set]` regression, and empty Map. Gap suite holds at 26/28 (same 2 pre-existing fails).

## v0.5.633 — **Closes #554 (followup)** — the v0.5.629 fix landed only on the top-level for-of lowering path in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower.rs`. The exact same `{ key: [a, b] }` for-of binding inside a function body (e.g. codehz/ecs `examples/serialization.ts::printWorldState`) still produced `pos = 0` because function bodies route through a parallel `ast::Stmt::ForOf` arm in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower_decl.rs` that had the same pair of structural gaps the top-level path had pre-fix: the pre-pass at line ~4012 walked `ObjectPatProp::KeyValue` props and only pre-defined leaf locals when the value was `Pat::Ident` (nested patterns silently fell through and `a`/`b` never entered `ctx.locals`); the binding-emit phase at line ~4184 had the matching gap (non-Ident KeyValue values dropped off the bottom of the match block, producing no `Stmt::Let` for the leaves). Fix promotes the v0.5.629 helpers (`collect_for_of_pattern_leaves` / `emit_for_of_pattern_binding`) from private to `pub(crate)` in `lower.rs` and wires them into the matching sites in `lower_decl.rs`'s function-body for-of arm — the same recursion shape that fixed the top-level path. Pre-pass now recurses into nested `Pat::Array`/`Pat::Object`/`Pat::Assign`/`Pat::Rest` values inside KeyValue; binding-emit routes non-Ident KeyValue values through `emit_for_of_pattern_binding` mirroring `destructuring::lower_pattern_binding_into` but reusing the pre-allocated leaf ids. Also picked up the `PropName::Str` arm that the top-level path got along the way (string-literal keys on object patterns now work in function bodies too). End-to-end: minimal repro `function printResults(items: typeof results) { for (const { entity, components: [a, b] } of items) … }` now matches Node byte-for-byte (was `a=0 b=0` pre-fix). Existing `test-files/test_issue_554_nested_destructure_for_of.ts` extended with three function-body cases (canonical, triple-nested, single-element `[pos]` mirroring the issue's followup repro). Gap suite holds at 26/28 (same 2 pre-existing fails — `test_gap_console_methods` timing diff, `test_gap_object_methods` strict-mode property descriptor gap). Note: nested patterns inside an array pattern (`for (const [a, [b, c]] of arr)` at function scope) still have the same structural gap at the `Pat::Array` arms here in `lower_decl.rs` (lines ~3992 and ~4073) — symmetric with the surviving gap noted in v0.5.629 and left for a separate change to keep blast radius small.

## v0.5.632 — **Refs #552** (Android side of the mobile stdlib): replaces the v0.5.631 Android stub with a real LocationManager + Photo Picker pipeline. Five new public symbols on Android (`perry_system_geolocation_get_current` / `_watch` / `_stop_watch` / `_request_permission` / `image_picker_pick`) backed by `crates/perry-ui-android/src/{geolocation.rs,image_picker.rs}` which delegate via JNI to new `PerryBridge.kt` static methods (`requestGeolocationGetCurrent(successKey, errorKey)`, `requestGeolocationWatch(callbackKey): Long`, `stopGeolocationWatch(id)`, `requestGeolocationPermission(callbackKey)`, `requestImagePickerPick(maxCount, allowMultiple, callbackKey)`). Two new JNI invoke shapes added to `crates/perry-ui-android/src/callback.rs`: `invoke4(key, a, b, c, d)` for the 4-double geolocation success callback (lat/lng/accuracy/timestamp_ms) plus matching `Java_com_perry_app_PerryBridge_nativeInvokeCallback4` entry, and `invoke_with_string_array(key, paths)` plus `Java_..._nativeInvokeCallbackWithStringArray` for the image-picker callback (each path is allocated as a NaN-boxed Perry string and pushed into a `js_array_alloc` array; the array pointer is then NaN-boxed and passed as the sole arg). Geolocation flow: Kotlin checks `ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION` / `ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION`, falls back to ACCESS_COARSE if FINE not granted, prefers `getLastKnownLocation` for low latency, falls back to `requestSingleUpdate` on the network provider (or GPS if network is disabled). Watch listeners stored in `watchListeners: MutableMap<Long, LocationListener>` keyed by an auto-incrementing watch id; Rust side blocks briefly on the UI thread via a `CountDownLatch` to obtain the synchronous return value. Image picker flow: API 33+ uses `MediaStore.ACTION_PICK_IMAGES` (Photo Picker — privacy-preserving, no permission required); older devices fall through to `ACTION_GET_CONTENT` with `EXTRA_ALLOW_MULTIPLE`. Selected URIs are copied via `contentResolver.openInputStream` into the app's cacheDir as `perry_pick_<ms>_<idx>.<ext>` (extension inferred from MIME type — jpg/png/gif/webp/heic/heif/bmp), absolute paths returned to JS so the user can `fs.readFileSync` or upload directly. Activity-result wiring: pre-existing FILE dialog had a documented but unwired `onFileDialogResult` — added `onActivityResult` override to `PerryActivity.kt` that routes both request 42 (file dialog) AND request 46 (#552 image picker) back to PerryBridge. Permission flow: new `GEOLOCATION_PERMISSION_REQUEST = 45` case added to `onRequestPermissionsResult`, so a `requestGeolocationPermission` that needs to prompt and a `requestGeolocationGetCurrent` that's stalled waiting on a permission both fulfill correctly through `onGeolocationPermissionResult(granted)`. AndroidManifest.xml gains `READ_MEDIA_IMAGES` (consumed only by the API 33+ ACTION_GET_CONTENT fallback path; the Photo Picker itself needs no permission). Pre-existing `request_location` 2-arg legacy API stays untouched. Both `crates/perry-ui-android/template/` and the parallel `android-build/` checked-in template are updated to keep the existing build flow's source of truth in sync. `cargo check --release -p perry-ui-android` passes; full NDK cross-build needs the toolchain on the developer's host (unchanged from main).

## v0.5.631 — **Refs #552** (mobile stdlib gaps for production apps — geolocation + photo-library image picker + image compression): adds `geolocationGetCurrent` / `geolocationWatch` / `geolocationStopWatch` / `geolocationRequestPermission` / `imagePickerPick` to `perry/system`; geolocation success callback signature is `(lat, lng, accuracy, timestamp_ms)` with a separate `onError(message)` callback. Five `MethodRow` entries in `crates/perry-dispatch/src/lib.rs::PERRY_SYSTEM_TABLE` so the LLVM backend's table-driven dispatch resolves the names without per-platform codegen wiring; matching entries in `crates/perry-api-manifest/src/entries.rs` so the issue-#513 strict-API gate flips for them. Full implementation on **macOS** (CoreLocation via dynamic ObjC `PerryGeolocationDelegate` registration in `crates/perry-ui-macos/src/geolocation.rs`; `NSOpenPanel` filtered to image UTIs in `image_picker.rs`) and **iOS** (CLLocationManager via parallel `crates/perry-ui-ios/src/geolocation.rs`). Stubs that invoke `onError("unsupported-platform")` / empty-array on Android (replaced in v0.5.632 with the real impl), tvOS, watchOS, visionOS, GTK4, Windows via shared `issue_552_stub.rs` per crate. Image compression (third bullet of issue) verified end-to-end: `crates/perry-ext-sharp/src/lib.rs` already exposes `js_sharp_resize` + `js_sharp_jpeg(quality)` + `js_sharp_to_buffer` — covers `compressJpeg(buf, {quality, maxWidth, maxHeight})` via the npm `sharp` package through `perry.compilePackages` (issue #193 wiring confirmed; no new code needed). The non-native targets (Web/wasm) reach the catch-all browser warning since the geolocation FFIs aren't aliased in `crates/perry-codegen-js/src/emit.rs`'s `emit_system_method_call` — an explicit `navigator.geolocation` shim there would be a small follow-up. End-to-end smoke: `import { geolocationGetCurrent } from "perry/system"; geolocationGetCurrent((lat, lng, acc, ts) => console.log(lat, lng), e => console.error(e))` compiles cleanly on every platform; on macOS it delivers a real CoreLocation reading after the user accepts the OS permission prompt. Plus a `collect_modules.rs` change (`needs_ui = true` flips when `perry/system` imports any of the audio-recording / geolocation / image-picker symbols, namespace imports, or the catch-all) so the libperry_ui_*.a archive actually links — pre-fix the symbol set lived in the platform UI crates but `needs_ui` only flipped on `perry/ui` / `perry/media`, leaving programs that import only `perry/system` with `Undefined symbols` at link time.

## v0.5.630 — **PR #534 (dazhe)** — `codegen-wasm`: a user function named `main` whose body has no `return` produced an invalid Wasm module — `func_section` declared `main` as `() -> i64` whenever the function name is `main` (lines 1338 and 1557 of `crates/perry-codegen-wasm/src/emit.rs`), but `compile_function` (line 2076) only emitted the trailing `TAG_UNDEFINED` constant when `has_return(body)` was true. Fallthrough left the operand stack empty at the final `End`, so the declared `i64` result type didn't match — `WebAssembly.compile` / `instantiate` rejected the module with a stack-depth mismatch. Fix: align the body emission predicate with the type-declaration predicate (`hir_func.body.iter().any(has_return) || hir_func.name == "main"`). Plus extend `has_return` to recurse into `Stmt::DoWhile`, `Stmt::For.init` (an `Option<Box<Stmt>>`), and `Stmt::Labeled` — previously the bare `For { body, .. }` pattern only walked `body`, and `DoWhile` / `Labeled` had no arm at all, so any `return` reachable only through those paths was invisible to the signature decision and led to the symmetric pre-fix bug (signature said no result, body left a value on the stack). The fix is symmetrical with the `func_section` predicate already present at the type-declaration sites.

## v0.5.629 — **Closes #554** (nested destructuring in for-of `const` binding produced 0 instead of array elements): `for (const { entity, components: [a, b] } of results)` bound `a` and `b` to literal 0 because `crates/perry-hir/src/lower.rs`'s for-of binding handler had two parallel gaps in its object-pattern path. (1) Pre-pass at line ~5952 walked `ObjectPatProp::KeyValue` props and only pre-defined leaf locals when the value was `Pat::Ident`; nested patterns (`components: [a, b]`) silently fell through and `a`/`b` never entered `ctx.locals`, so the body lowering reported `unknown identifier 'a' — assuming global` and emitted a literal 0 read.
