(2) Binding-emit phase at line ~6147 had the matching gap — non-Ident KeyValue values fell off the bottom of the match block, producing no `Stmt::Let` for the leaves. The pure-array form `for (const [a, b] of arr)` and the two-step workaround `const { entity, components } = ...; const [a, b] = components;` both worked because the latter routes through `crates/perry-hir/src/destructuring.rs::lower_pattern_binding_into` which already handles arbitrary nested patterns recursively. Fix adds two helpers in `lower.rs` next to `lower_stmt`: `collect_for_of_pattern_leaves` walks an arbitrary destructure pattern and pre-defines every leaf identifier (handling `Array` / `Object` / `Assign` / `Rest` recursively, plus shorthand `Assign` props inside `Object`); `emit_for_of_pattern_binding` mirrors `destructuring::lower_pattern_binding_into` but reuses the pre-allocated leaf ids passed in `var_ids` (in source order) instead of calling `define_local` itself, so the loop body — already lowered against those ids — sees the correct bindings. The KeyValue arms in both pre-pass and binding-phase now route non-Ident values through these helpers. Array-pattern temp's `ty` is `Type::Array(Box::new(Type::Any))` (not `Type::Any`) to match `destructuring.rs`'s convention; the wrong type pulled OOB reads through the typed-element fast path which returned 0 and broke `[first, second = 99]` default-value handling. End-to-end: issue's repro `for (const { entity, components: [a, b] } of results)` now prints `a={"x":50,"y":30} b={"x":1,"y":2}` matching bun. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_554_nested_destructure_for_of.ts` covers the canonical case plus triple-nesting (`components: [{ x: ax }, { x: bx }]`), nested-with-default, and nested-with-rest. Gap suite holds at 26/28 (same 2 pre-existing fails — `test_gap_console_methods` timing diff, `test_gap_object_methods` strict-mode property descriptor gap). Note: nested patterns *inside* an array pattern (`for (const [a, [b, c]] of ...)`) still have the same structural gap at the `Pat::Array` arms (lines ~5923 and ~6014); not in the issue-#554 repro and left for a separate change to keep blast radius small.

## v0.5.628 — **Closes #549** (codehz/ecs followup — Set/Map object key collisions): `set.add({}); set.add({})` returned size 1, `map.set(a, "a"); map.set(b, "b")` returned `"b"` for both keys, and same-shape objects (`{x:1}` / `{x:1}`) collided. `is_string_like` in both `crates/perry-runtime/src/set.rs` and `.../src/map.rs` returned `true` for any POINTER_TAG value because `extract_string_ptr_from_value` accepted the tag without validating the GC type at the pointee. The follow-on `jsvalue_eq` content-comparison branch then reinterpreted each `ObjectHeader` as a `StringHeader`: `class_id` (offset 4) became `byte_len`, the data pointer landed at offset 20 (size_of::<StringHeader>) which falls inside `keys_array`'s upper 4 bytes plus whatever was past the 24-byte `ObjectHeader`, and two empty `{}` literals (same class_id = 0, no fields) compared as equal because zero-length string slices match. Same root cause for the same-shape `{x:1}` collision (both have identical class_id from the AnonShape cache plus a single field). Fix: validate the GC header in `is_string_like` so only `GC_TYPE_STRING` pointees enter the content-comparison path. STRING_TAG (0x7FFF) values are accepted unconditionally (the tag is itself the type proof); POINTER_TAG (0x7FFD) and raw pointers are now subjected to a `(ptr - GC_HEADER_SIZE)->obj_type == GC_TYPE_STRING` check before content comparison. Non-string POINTER_TAG values (objects, closures, maps, etc.) fall through to the bit-identity fast path that JS Set/Map `SameValueZero` semantics call for on object keys. End-to-end: codehz's repro (`set.add(a); set.add(b)`) now reports size 2 matching bun; `map.get(a)` / `map.get(b)` return `"a"` / `"b"`; same-shape-after-mutation paths produce 4 entries; `Map.get(JSON.parse('"hello"'))` (issue #434 cross-representation string equality) still works; SameValueZero `0` / `-0` collapse still works; mixed object+string keys still dispatch to the right buckets. Map/Set test suite (`test_edge_map_set`, `test_gap_map_set_extended`, `test_edge_closure_module_map`) all pass; gap suite holds at 26/28 (same 2 pre-existing fails — `test_gap_console_methods` timing diff, `test_gap_object_methods` strict-mode property descriptor gap).

## v0.5.627 — **Closes #546, #547, #548** (codehz/ecs followup): three issues from the codehz/ecs library all rooted in array-method dispatch on Any-typed receivers with one defensive JSON.stringify cleanup. **#546** (`world.hook` on_set never fires while on_remove works): `triggerMultiComponentHooks` in `src/world/hooks.ts` does `requiredComponents.some(c => anyComponentMatches(addedComponents, c))` where `requiredComponents` is destructured out of an `entry: LifecycleHookEntry` parameter — the destructured local lost its `EntityId<any>[]` type and ended up as `Type::Any`. The HIR fast-path lowering at `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs:2766` (ident-receiver) bails on `is_unknown_recv && is_class_overlapping_method` for `some`/`every`/`find`/`findIndex`/etc. (so user-class methods with the same name still dispatch correctly), and the runtime fallback `js_native_call_method` had arms for `map`/`filter`/`forEach`/`slice`/`with` but **not** `some`/`every`/`find`/`findIndex`/`findLast`/`findLastIndex`. The `arr.some(cb)` call then fell through to the object field-scan and returned the array itself, producing `typeof = object` instead of a boolean — every `shouldTriggerSet` evaluation got a truthy non-boolean for `anyRequiredAdded`, but the chain `enteredMatchingSet || (...)` short-circuited on the typeof-object value. on_remove worked because that branch's logic happens to fire on a different code path that didn't depend on the `some` result. Two coordinated fixes: (a) `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs::js_native_call_method` adds the missing array-method arms for any-typed receivers (mirrors the existing #267/#493/#291/#515 arms — gates on `obj_type == GC_TYPE_ARRAY || GC_TYPE_LAZY_ARRAY`, dispatches directly to `js_array_some` / `js_array_every` / `js_array_find` / `js_array_findIndex` / `js_array_find_last` / `js_array_find_last_index`; `findIndex` / `findLastIndex` return `i32` so cast through `as f64` for the f64 ABI). (b) `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs` adds `"some"` and `"every"` arms to the member-receiver match block at line ~3988 — siblings of the existing `"map"` / `"filter"` / `"find"` / `"findIndex"` arms that `e.arr.some(...)` previously fell past, lowering directly to `Expr::ArraySome` / `Expr::ArrayEvery`. End-to-end: codehz/ecs `examples/state-machine.ts` now emits all `[SET]` lines (28 lines under bun, 0 under perry pre-fix); `world.hook([Health], { on_set, on_remove })` fires both callbacks; minimal repro `const { arr } = e; arr.some(p)` returns boolean true matching Node. **#547** (wildcard relation queries on `dontFragment` relations): repro from issue passes on v0.5.624 already — apparently fixed by the v0.5.624 Map-iteration-through-optional-Map fix (#541/#542/#543). `examples/inventory-system-relations.ts` and `examples/parent-child-hierarchy.ts` now match `bun run` (modulo Map iteration ordering). Closing as already-fixed against the v0.5.622 repro. **#548** (JSON.stringify panic at `crates/perry-runtime/src/json.rs:1489`: "byte index N is not a char boundary; it is inside '\0'"): the exact repro from the issue passes on v0.5.624 in my environment, but the panic site IS reachable in principle — `str_from_header` uses `from_utf8_unchecked` on bytes from a StringHeader pointer, and the GC-type fallback heuristic in `stringify_value`'s `_ =>` arm can misclassify an unrecognised pointer as a string when the array-shape sniff fails. If the underlying bytes aren't valid UTF-8, the resulting `&str` triggers UB at the first `&s[start..i]` slice operation that lands mid-multibyte. Defensive fix in `write_escaped_string`: switch the slow-path slice operations from `&s[start..i]` (UTF-8-boundary-checked) to `buf.as_mut_vec().extend_from_slice(&bytes[start..i])` (raw bytes). The fast path stays unchanged (`buf.push_str(s)` on valid UTF-8 inputs). Output is byte-identical for valid UTF-8 input; non-UTF-8 input writes through verbatim instead of aborting the process. Replaces a SIGABRT with at-worst an ill-formed UTF-8 String — every consumer in the codebase treats stringify output as a byte stream. Parity verified: existing gap tests (`test_gap_array_methods`, `test_gap_class_advanced`, `test_gap_closures`) all match Node byte-for-byte; codehz/ecs `examples/serialization.ts` JSON-roundtrip step no longer panics (separate Position-data-not-propagating bug remains, out of scope).

## v0.5.626 — **Closes #420** (drizzle-orm compiles + runs end-to-end via `compilePackages`): two layered fixes get `pgTable("users", { id: serial("id").primaryKey(), name: text("name") })` + `eq(users.name, "alice")` + `and(eq(...), gt(...))` byte-for-byte matching Node. (1) `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::ExternFuncRef`: when an imported binding name resolves in `ctx.class_ids` (populated from `opts.imported_classes` for cross-module classes too), emit the same INT32-tagged class-id NaN-box that local `Expr::ClassRef` produces. Pre-fix, `import { Table } from "drizzle-orm"` lowered to a closure-pointer NaN-box (POINTER_TAG), so `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(Table, entityKind)` consulted the regular-object Symbol-property path which returned false on every drizzle ancestor — `is(value, type)` then fell through to the "doesn't look like an entity" throw at every brand-check. With the INT32-tagged route, the receiver is recognised as a class ref and `js_object_has_own`'s class-static-symbol branch (v0.5.622) consults `CLASS_STATIC_SYMBOLS` correctly. Side effect: `typeof ImportedClass` reports "number" instead of "function" — same documented tradeoff as v0.5.622's local `ClassRef`. (2) `crates/perry-hir/src/lower.rs` `is_exportable` matcher (the gate that decides whether a `const X = ...; export { X }` shape lands in the module's `exported_objects` and from there the importer's `imported_vars` set): pre-fix the matcher had arms for `Closure`, `Object`, `Array`, `Call`, `New`, `LocalGet`, `FuncRef`, `ExternFuncRef`, `PropertyGet`, primitive literals — but **not** `Expr::SymbolFor`. drizzle's `entity.js` declares `const entityKind = Symbol.for("drizzle:entityKind")` followed by `export { entityKind }` — the init is `Expr::SymbolFor(...)` which fell off the matcher and the const never reached `exported_objects`. Importing modules then saw `entityKind` outside `imported_vars` and routed every read through the closure-pointer fallback (returning the closure global address as if it were the symbol's bits). Static-symbol registration in `class_register_static_symbol` saw a non-Symbol pointer for the key, the magic-check failed, `sym_key` came back 0, and the `(class_id, sym_key)` insert was rejected — so even though both halves of the v0.5.622 wiring existed, nothing ever made it into the side table. Adding `Expr::SymbolFor(_)` to the `is_exportable` arm threads the const through correctly. End-to-end smoke: drizzle's `test_query.ts` (the issue-#420 minimal) now prints `condition built: object\nok` matching Node byte-for-byte. The full pgTable + eq/gt/and chain works without any drizzle-side throws. Parity: 211 pass / 3 known fails / 13 skipped (98.5%, +1 vs main; the 3 fails are pre-existing).

## v0.5.625 — Refs #420 (drizzle followup — class field initializers must run AFTER parent ctor body, not before): pre-fix, perry applied ALL field initializers (root → leaf) at the TOP of constructor lowering, BEFORE any constructor body ran. drizzle's `class PgText extends PgColumn extends Column { enumValues = this.config.enumValues }` then read `this.config` (set in Column's ctor body via `this.config = config`) before Column's body ever ran — `this.config` was undefined, the field init threw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'enumValues')`, and `pgTable("users", { name: text("name") })` blew up at `colBuilder.build(rawTable)` time. Per ECMAScript spec, derived-class field initializers run AT THE TOP of [[Construct]] but logically AFTER super() returns — i.e. after the parent body has run. Fix interleaves field-init application with body inlining across three lowering paths: (1) `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` — new `FieldInitMode` enum (`All` / `AncestorsOnly` / `SelfOnly`) on the existing `apply_field_initializers_recursive`. `lower_new` now applies `AncestorsOnly` up front, then inlines the chosen body (own-ctor or walked-parent), then applies `SelfOnly` for the leaf class after the parent-body chain has run. Base classes (no extends) keep the simpler "All up front" path. (2) `crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::Expr::SuperCall` — after inlining the parent body inside an own-ctor body, applies the current class's `SelfOnly` field inits so the JS-spec "fields run immediately after super()" semantics is preserved. (3) `crates/perry-codegen/src/codegen.rs` standalone-ctor emit (used for cross-module class metadata) — uses `AncestorsOnly` for derived classes plus a `SelfOnly` apply after the explicit parent-ctor symbol call (no-own-ctor case). (4) `crates/perry-codegen/src/stmt.rs` scalar-replacement path — independent bug discovered along the way: `Stmt::Let { init: Expr::New { .. } }` for non-escaping construction took its own scalar-replace fast path that **never inlined the parent body when the leaf had no own ctor** — `class.constructor.clone()` returns `None` for default-ctor classes, so the parent body was simply skipped. Updated to mirror `lower_new`'s logic: walk parent chain, inline first ancestor body with forwarded args, then apply leaf `SelfOnly`. End-to-end smoke: minimal 2-deep + 3-deep repros (`class Leaf extends Mid extends Base { enumValues = this.config.enumValues }`) match Node byte-for-byte; drizzle's `test_query.ts` no longer throws the enumValues TypeError (progress past the brand-check + column-builder chain exposes a downstream SIGSEGV in the query-builder phase tracked separately). Sanity tests `test_inheritance` and `test_gap_class_advanced` still pass byte-for-byte. Parity unchanged: 210 pass / 3 known fails / 13 skipped (98.5%).

## v0.5.624 — **Closes #541, #542, #543** (codehz/ecs library — three-issue stack reduces to two coordinated HIR fixes once the surface symptoms are unwound). #541 (link error: `@codehz/ecs` package name passed to `ld` as a response file) was already addressed in v0.5.522 — `read_app_metadata` in `crates/perry/src/commands/run.rs:1497` strips `@` and `/` via `sanitize_app_name`. Closing as already-fixed against the v0.5.503 repro. #542 (entity ID corruption from `world.spawn().build()`) and #543 (`query.forEach` callback never fires) collapse to the same root cause once the false leads are followed: codehz/ecs encodes relations as `-(componentId * 2^42 + targetId)` — negative f64s outside i32 range — and stores them as keys in `Map<EntityId, any>` parameters typed `Map<K, V> | undefined`. Two HIR-lowering bugs corrupted iteration of those Maps. **(a)** `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs:2991` (the array-method fast path's `entries`/`keys`/`values` arms) unconditionally folded the call to `Expr::ArrayKeys`/`ArrayValues`/`ArrayEntries` when the receiver type was unknown. `keys`/`values`/`entries` are shared between Array, Map, and Set — for an `Any`-typed receiver (e.g. an interface method return) the optimistic fold ran `js_array_keys` against a real `MapHeader`, which read `size` as `length` and produced `[0..N-1]` (a 1-entry Map produced `[0]`, masquerading as a valid array of one zero key). Replace the unconditional fall-through with a Union-aware Map/Set/Array detector — only emit `ArrayKeys`/`ArrayValues`/`ArrayEntries` when the receiver is statically known to be `Type::Array(_)` or `Type::Tuple(_)`; otherwise fall through to the runtime `js_native_call_method` dispatcher whose `is_registered_map` check picks the right path. Apply the same Union check to the `forEach` arm (line 2740). **(b)** `crates/perry-hir/src/lower_decl.rs:3815` and `crates/perry-hir/src/lower.rs:5709` (the for-of fast path's `is_iterable_map` / `is_iterable_set` checks) only matched the bare `Type::Generic { base: "Map", .. }` shape. A `Map<K, V> | undefined` parameter shows up as `Type::Union([Generic{Map}, Void])`, even after the `if (!m) return;` narrowing guard — perry doesn't propagate the narrow through the union type. The for-of fell through to array iteration, treating the Map as Array-of-`__arr.length`-many elements indexed via `IndexGet(__arr, i)` — produced zero iterations or yielded swapped/garbage `[k, v]` pairs depending on which f64 bytes were aliased. Extend `is_iterable_map`/`is_iterable_set` to also accept `Type::Union(variants)` where any variant is `Generic{Map}`/`Generic{Set}`. The companion `arr_expr` wrapping match in both files is rewritten to dispatch on the new flags directly (so the Union shape gets `MapEntries`/`SetValues` wrapping when the fast path doesn't apply). The K/V type-args extraction in `lower.rs` walks the Union to find the Map variant. End-to-end: codehz/ecs at commit 159e292 — `examples/inventory-system-relations.ts`, `examples/parent-child-hierarchy.ts`, `examples/spatial-grid.ts`, `examples/tag-filtering.ts`, `examples/state-machine.ts`, `examples/collision-detection.ts`, `examples/advanced-scheduling.ts`, `examples/simple.ts` — now all match `bun run` byte-for-byte (modulo trailing-blank-line whitespace and one Map-iteration ordering difference that's both spec-allowed). `examples/serialization.ts` still has a separate JSON/TypedArray encoding mismatch beyond this release's scope. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_542_map_keys_optional.ts` exercises both paths against Node. Parity: 207 pass / 3 known fails / 13 skipped (98.5%, no regressions vs main; the 3 environmental compile failures for `test_issue_248_phase2_*` / `test_issue_255_jsruntime_reentrancy` are unrelated `libperry_jsruntime.a not found` build-dep issues that also fail on main).

## v0.5.623 — Refs #535 (Layer 2 — `NavStack(state, routes)` lowering for native multi-screen): builds on v0.5.621's Layer 1 desugar to make the documented multi-screen pattern (`types/perry/ui/index.d.ts:370-374`) actually work on macOS/iOS/Android/GTK4/Windows. Pre-fix the 2-arg form silently fell through to the 0-arg `perry_ui_navstack_create()` and the state/routes args were dropped — the app showed an empty NavStack and `route.set("detail")` had no effect. Three coordinated changes: (1) `crates/perry-transform/src/state_desugar.rs` adds a `try_rewrite_navstack` arm that detects `NavStack(LocalGet(state), Array([{name, body}, ...]))` for any state-bound local and rewrites it to an IIFE (closure-as-`Call` with empty args, same shape as `try_desugar_reactive_text` at `lower.rs:7367`) whose body allocates the host via 0-arg `NavStack()`, then for each route binds the body widget to a fresh local, calls `widgetAddChild(host, body)`, and emits a synthetic `__navstack_register_route("__state_X", "name", body)` call. Routes with non-string `name` literals or non-canonical shapes silently bail to the 0-arg fallback. Fresh `LocalId`/`FuncId` allocation via new `compute_max_local_id` / `compute_max_func_id` walkers (mirrors `async_to_generator.rs:204`'s shape but exhaustively scans class methods + try/catch/switch arms so allocations don't collide with `ctx.fresh_local()` ids). (2) `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call/native.rs` adds the `__navstack_register_route` lowering arm: lowers the body to a widget handle, unboxes to i64, calls the runtime FFI, returns the body unchanged so the caller can keep chaining. (3) `crates/perry-runtime/src/ui_text_registry.rs` adds the `NAVSTACK_REGISTRY: Mutex<HashMap<String, Vec<NavRoute>>>` per-state-id route table, the `js_navstack_register_route(synth_id, name, handle)` FFI (records + sets initial visibility by hiding the widget if its name doesn't match the current state value), the `SetWidgetHiddenHandler` registration FFI (`js_register_widget_hidden_handler`) called by perry-ui-macos at app startup with a thin wrapper around `widgets::set_hidden`, and a `navstack_dispatch_state_change` hook called from `js_state_set` after every state write that walks the registered routes for the changed synth id and toggles each route's visibility through the platform handler. End-to-end: `NavStack(route, [{name: "home", body: ...}, {name: "detail", body: ...}])` lowers to the construction IIFE, builds both screens at App-build time, hides the inactive one at first paint, and `route.set("detail")` flips visibility through the registered handler chain. Visual verification of the running app requires a GUI session (compile-time HIR rewrite + runtime registration both verified). Skipped routes / dynamic state / nested NavStacks: each route's body is evaluated eagerly at App-build time so closures referenced from buttons stay alive; routes with computed (non-literal-string) names bail to the 0-arg fallback for now. The arkts harvest's existing visibility-binding pass stays the source of truth on harmonyos (gated off in `collect_modules.rs`).

## v0.5.622 — Refs #420 (drizzle entityKind hasOwnProperty): pre-fix, `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(SomeClass, Symbol.for("entityKind"))` always returned `false` on classes carrying `static [Symbol.for(...)] = "..."` fields, because perry's `lower_decl.rs` `lower_class_prop` skipped computed-key static fields entirely (only the instance variant was lowered). drizzle's `is(value, type)` brand-check (used by `eq()`/`gt()`/`pgTable()` internally) hit this path on every Column / Table / Selection ancestor, returning false at the first ancestor and falling through to the "doesn't look like an entity" path which threw further downstream. Six coordinated edits: (1) `lower_decl.rs` no longer skips computed-key static fields — they flow through `lower_class_prop` like instance fields, populating `class.static_fields` with `key_expr: Some(...)`; (2) new HIR variant `Expr::ClassStaticSymbolSet { class_name, key, value }` in `ir.rs` + walker arms in `walker.rs`; (3) `lower.rs` emits `ClassStaticSymbolSet` from both class-decl arms (export and non-export, lines 4123 / 5174) when a static field has a `key_expr`; (4) `codegen.rs` `static_field_globals` skips computed-key static fields (no global slot — they live in a side table) and `init_static_fields` dispatches them to a new `js_class_register_static_symbol(class_id, sym, val)` extern; `Expr::ClassRef` returns the class id NaN-boxed with `INT32_TAG` so it's distinguishable at runtime; (5) `runtime_decls.rs` declares the new extern; (6) `symbol.rs` adds `static CLASS_STATIC_SYMBOLS: Mutex<HashMap<(class_id, sym_key), value_bits>>` populated by `js_class_register_static_symbol` at module init, plus `class_static_symbol_lookup(class_id, sym_f64)` consumer. `object.rs::js_object_has_own`'s Symbol-key branch checks if the receiver is an INT32-tagged class ref — if so, look up `(class_id, sym_key)` in `CLASS_STATIC_SYMBOLS` and return TAG_TRUE / TAG_FALSE accordingly; otherwise fall through to the existing `js_object_has_own_symbol` instance-Symbol-property path. End-to-end smoke: `class C { static [Symbol.for("k")] = "hello" }; Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(C, Symbol.for("k"))` returned `false` pre-fix, returns `true` post-fix matching Node. drizzle's `test_query.ts` no longer throws "doesn't look like an entity" at brand-check time — progress past `is()` exposes a downstream `enumValues` undefined read (separate followup, not in this fix). The static-field VALUE on the perry side is the actual runtime value (works for the brand-check return), but `(C as any)[sym]` to read it back through normal property access still returns undefined since the Symbol-keyed get path on class refs isn't wired — left for a follow-up since drizzle's pattern only needs the `hasOwnProperty.call` side.

## v0.5.621 — Refs #535 (Layer 1 — `perry/ui` `state<T>` desugar for non-HarmonyOS targets): pre-fix, `state<T>(initial)` had no codegen lowering on macOS / iOS / Android / GTK4 / Windows and the strict-API gate (#513) hard-errored with `'state' is not a known function`. Only `--target harmonyos` worked, because `perry-codegen-arkts::emit_index_ets` runs its own harvest that rewrites these shapes into `setText` calls before LLVM ever sees them; the d.ts at `types/perry/ui/index.d.ts:263-265` documented this as "deferred to v6.5", which blocked any multi-screen native app per #535. New `crates/perry-transform/src/state_desugar.rs` ports the arkts pass's `collect_state_bindings` + `rewrite_state_calls_in_stmts` (lib.rs:1233 / :3725) into a target-agnostic HIR rewrite that runs in `collect_modules` for every target except harmonyos: walks `module.init` for `let x = state(initial)` declarations, assigns each a synth id (`__state_<N>`), replaces the let initializer with `Expr::Undefined` so any not-yet-rewritten escape stays compilable, prepends a synthetic `__state_init("synth_id", initial)` call to seed the runtime registry, then rewrites every `x.set(v)` / `x.get()` / `x.value` / `x.text()` access (recursively into closure bodies and nested expressions — children-first traversal so `state.set(state.get() + 1)` evaluates the inner read once and pre-rewrite). Three new perry/ui method arms in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call/native.rs` lower the synthetic methods (`__state_init` / `__state_set` → `js_state_init` / `js_state_set`; `__state_get` → `js_state_get`). Three new FFIs in `crates/perry-runtime/src/ui_text_registry.rs` back them with a `STATE_VALUES: Mutex<HashMap<String, f64>>` registry; `__state_set` writes through to the existing `perry_arkts_set_text` so any `Text("initial", "<synth_id>")` widget bound under the same id (via `perry_ui_text_create_with_id` → `text_registry.rs::register_text_id`) re-renders. End-to-end: issue #535's repro `state.text()` form lowers to `Text("loading", "__state_0")` and `state.set("home")` triggers the bound widget update through the existing setText pipeline. Limitations: only matches `state(...)` declared at the top level of `module.init` (function-body declarations TBD); only matches the canonical method-call shapes (state escaping through a function arg or array misses the rewrite, but the `let x = undefined` shim keeps the program compilable); `.text()` snapshots the literal initial-value string at compile time (computed initials get an empty initial, corrected at runtime by the first `.set()`). Layer 2 (visibility binding + `NavStack(state, routes)` for native multi-screen) is the next iteration.

## v0.5.620 — Refs #486 (forward-port of the unmerged v0.5.614 hono branch onto main): three layered fixes for `app.get('/', c => c.json({...}))` end-to-end via `app.fetch(req)`, now combined with the v0.5.617 cross-module-class-metadata fix so the dependency tree from the original branch lines up against current main. (1) Class fields declared without an initializer (`#x;` / `x: any;`) used to leave the field slot uninitialized; arrow-class-field bodies that load `this.x` through the captured-`this` slot read raw zero bytes and `0 ?? fallback` took the wrong branch (0 is falsy but not nullish). Hono's `Context.#newResponse` does `this.#preparedHeaders ?? new Headers()`, evaluated to `0`, and `responseHeaders.set(k, v)` threw `(number).set is not a function`. `apply_field_initializers_recursive` in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` now writes `Expr::Undefined` for declared-but-uninitialized fields (both string- and computed-key forms — merged with v0.5.614's `key_expr` handling). (2) `response.headers` returned `undefined` because `dispatch_response_property` in `crates/perry-stdlib/src/fetch.rs` had no `"headers"` arm. Added a lazy-allocated cached Headers handle on `FetchResponse` (snapshot copy of the response's HashMap; spec-perfect "live header view" that mutates back through FetchResponse storage is a wider refactor). (3) Cross-module class getters — e.g. hono's `get req()` declared in `hono/dist/context.js` and read from `c.req.url` in main.ts — were never registered in `CLASS_VTABLE_REGISTRY`. Codegen now emits `js_register_class_getter(class_id, name, fn_ptr)` for every defined getter alongside the existing method-registration loop, and `js_object_get_field_by_name` consults `vtable.getters` (walking the class→parent chain via `get_parent_class_id`) before falling through to undefined. With v0.5.617's relative-import-inside-compile-package fix already on main, these three changes now have the cross-module class metadata they need to actually fire for hono. Verified: `app.get('/', c => c.json({message: 'hello', ok: true}))` returns status 200 + `application/json` content-type + correct body. Logger middleware acceptance still has a separate blocker chain past this release.

## v0.5.619 — Closes #533: `for (const [k, v] of c)` silently produced zero iterations when `c = m.get(k)!` and `m` was a Map-of-Maps. Two coordinated type-inference gaps in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower_types.rs`: (1) `infer_call_return_type` had no `Map.get` / `Set.has` / etc. arms — receiver-typed-as-`Map<K,V>` `.get(...)` returned `Type::Any`. (2) `new Map([entries])` without explicit `<K, V>` returned `Type::Generic { base: "Map", type_args: vec![] }` even when the literal entries arg made K and V trivially recoverable, so even with the Map.get arm there'd be nothing to forward. Together: `b.get(0)!` lowered with `Type::Any`, the for-of arm at `lower_decl.rs:3815` (`is_iterable_map`) didn't match, the loop fell through to the array `__arr.length` path which read 0 from a Map and skipped the body. Fix: add `infer_map_entries_type` / `infer_set_elements_type` walkers that scan the first non-elided element of the literal entries arg (Map's first 2-tuple → K,V; Set's first elem → T) and feed Generic.type_args; add Map/WeakMap/Set/WeakSet method-return arms to `infer_call_return_type` matching the existing `Array<T>.pop() → T` (not `T|undefined`) precedent so downstream type-driven dispatch sees the right element type. End-to-end: original repro now prints `0 1` matching Node. The annotation-only escape hatches users were previously forced to write (`const c: Map<...> = b.get(0)!`) are no longer required. The console-log formatter still shows `Map {}` instead of expanding entries — that's a separate `format_jsvalue` Map-pretty-print bug, not the for-of bug. Parity: 210 pass / 3 known fails / 13 skipped (98.5%, no regressions vs main).

## v0.5.618 — Refs #420 (drizzle-orm followup — `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call` idiom): perry doesn't expose `Object.prototype` as a real object, so the chain `Object.prototype.X` reads as undefined and `.call` throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'call')`. v0.5.612-era code already special-cased `Object.prototype.toString.call(x)` → `js_object_to_string(x)` at HIR lowering; this release adds the parallel arm for `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(o, k)` → `js_object_has_own(o, k)`. Refs #420 because drizzle's `is(value, type)` in `entity.js` uses this exact idiom on every entityKind check — without the rewrite, `new SQL([…])` (called inside `eq()`, `gt()`, every comparison helper) crashes with the `.call` throw before drizzle's actual logic runs. Plus a runtime fix: `js_object_has_own` previously coerced the key to a string and returned false for Symbol keys; now dispatches Symbol-typed keys through a new `js_object_has_own_symbol` helper in `crates/perry-runtime/src/symbol.rs` that consults the `SYMBOL_PROPERTIES` side table directly. **Partial unblock**: the `.call` crash is gone, but drizzle's `is(value, type)` still returns false for `is(stringChunk, Table)` because `Table.[entityKind]` is a static field with a Symbol-typed COMPUTED key (`static [entityKind] = "Table"`), and perry's HIR lowering at `lower_decl.rs::lower_class_from_ast` explicitly skips static computed-key fields. drizzle's `is` then throws `Class "<unknown>" doesn't look like a Drizzle entity`. test_query.ts moves from a SIGSEGV to a clean drizzle-thrown exception — meaningful structural improvement but doesn't unblock the full path yet. Implementing static computed-key Symbol fields on classes (write `(class_id, sym) → value` to a side table at module init, consult from hasOwn for class-ref receivers) is the natural next step. The string-key form of `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call` (the more common idiom in npm packages — lodash, jquery, every safe-property-checker library) IS fully unblocked by this release.

## v0.5.617 — Refs #486 (hono middleware followup — cross-module classes from re-exports inside compile packages): `import { C } from 'lib3'` where `lib3/index.js` does `export { C } from './c.js'` produced an empty object on `new C()` because `lib3/c.js` never entered `ctx.native_modules`. The relative-import branch of `resolve_import` (`crates/perry/src/commands/compile/resolve.rs`) classified `./c.js` as `ModuleKind::Interpreted` whenever `is_in_compile_package` returned false — and that helper checks only the literal `node_modules/<pkg>/` substring. `file:./` deps and symlinked package roots both canonicalize to paths *outside* any `node_modules/` segment, so even though `compile_package_dirs["lib3"]` was already populated by the bare-specifier resolution of `'lib3'`, the relative import inside the package looked external. Fix mirrors the membership check that `collect_modules.rs` already uses for the entry-file classification — accept either the substring match OR `canonical.starts_with(dir)` for any value in `compile_package_dirs` (excluding nested `node_modules/`). End-to-end: minimal repro `lib3/index.js` re-exporting `class C` from `lib3/c.js` now constructs C instances with the constructor body actually running and `c.test()` dispatching to `C.prototype.test`. Hono logger middleware (issue #486) now progresses past the previous `(number).replace is not a function` failure that was rooted in this same missing-class-metadata path. Five sanity tests (test_inheritance, test_gap_class_advanced, test_gap_closures, test_gap_async_advanced, test_edge_promises) all pass.

## v0.5.616 — Refs #420 (drizzle-orm continued — squashed batch covering 7 followup fixes that together get drizzle's `pgTable` schema construction byte-for-byte matching Node and column-builder dispatch through cross-module 3-level inheritance chains): **(a)** `crates/perry-stdlib/src/common/dispatch.rs`'s v0.5.608 Web Fetch handle dispatch made unconditional calls to `crate::fetch::*`, but `pub mod fetch;` is gated on `feature = "http-client"` — when auto-optimize compiled perry-stdlib for drizzle (which doesn't use http-client), 4× E0433 errors appeared and the build silently fell back to prebuilt libs. Wrap the four calls in `#[cfg(feature = "http-client")] { ... }`. **(b)** `compile.rs::imported_vars` lookup at the cross-module Call site keyed `(resolved_path_str, exported_name)` against `exported_var_names`, but the latter is keyed by ORIGIN path. drizzle exports `pgTable` from `pg-core/table.js` and re-exports through `pg-core/index.js`; user code imports from `drizzle-orm/pg-core` which resolves to `index.js` — the lookup `(index.js, "pgTable")` missed and the cross-module call took the direct-getter-call path which treated the zero-arg getter's return value AS the call result. Fix walks `all_module_exports` to find the ORIGIN path before the lookup. Filtered the synthetic `__computed_field_<lo>_<hi>` names out of `ImportedClass.field_names` and `field_types` at all 6 population sites (12 filters) so they don't surface as enumerable own props on cross-module instances. **(c)** `class_field_global_index` in `type_analysis.rs` walked `class.fields.len()` and `class.fields.iter().position(...)` UNFILTERED, but the inline-slot layout (`packed_keys`) skips computed-key fields per v0.5.614/v0.5.615 — for any class mixing computed-key + regular fields, the slot index used for `this.field = X` writes shifted past where readers look, surfacing as `instance.field === number 0`. Filter `f.key_expr.is_some()` in both the parent-count walk and own-fields position loop. **(d)** Cross-module super() chain through ctor-less ancestors. Per JS spec, `class Mid extends Base {}` (no ctor) gets a default `constructor(...args) { super(...args); }`. perry's `Expr::SuperCall` only inlined the IMMEDIATE parent's constructor body — drizzle's `PgSerialBuilder extends PgColumnBuilder extends ColumnBuilder` (only ColumnBuilder defines `this.config = {…}`) silently produced `instance.config === number 0`. Fix walks past empty-bodied ancestors and through cross-module ctors with `param_count==0` until an ancestor with a real ctor is found, then forwards args. `compile_method` standalone-ctor emission adopts the closest ancestor-with-ctor's params as the synthesized signature so the LLVM function has slots for the args; emits a call to the parent's standalone ctor with all args forwarded. **(e)** `lower_new` for a class without own ctor walked the parent chain looking for an inlineable body; when the chain ran through ctor-less imported stubs (drizzle's `class PgSerial extends PgColumn extends Column` where PgColumn is imported with `constructor: None` on the consumer side and Column has the real body), the fallback at `imported_class_ctors.get(class_name)` missed because `class_name` is the LOCAL class. Fix walks past empty-bodied ancestors AND through cross-module ctors with `param_count==0` until an ancestor with a real cross-module ctor is found, then dispatches there. **(f)** Inherited methods in dynamic-dispatch table. The `obj.method()` dispatch tower for `obj: any` only registered classes that DIRECTLY declared the method — subclasses inheriting weren't represented. Walk every `class_id`'s parent chain to find the first ancestor that declares the method, registering `(start_cid → ancestor's fn_name)` so inherited dispatch works. **(g)** Runtime `js_native_call_method`'s vtable lookup walks parent chain. For receivers whose class isn't in the importing module's `class_ids` (return values of not-by-name-imported functions), the codegen-side dispatch tower has nothing to match. Fix walks `get_parent_class_id` chain up to 32 levels in both vtable-lookup sites of `object.rs`. End-to-end: drizzle's `serial("id").primaryKey()` now returns the same instance with `config.primaryKey: true`; probe7's `users.id` shows full real column data ({name:'id', primary:true, dataType:'number', columnType:'PgSerial', config:{...}}) matching Node byte-for-byte. The minimal 3-level cross-module repro (`Sub extends Mid extends Base` with Mid in a separate file) also matches Node. test_query.ts still SIGSEGVs because `eq(users.name, "alice")` uses drizzle's `sql\`...\`` tagged template — which itself works in single-file but the function-property assignment pattern `((sql2) => { sql2.empty = empty })(sql)` isn't supported (perry function values have no associated property storage); the `sql.empty()` call returns `number 0` cross-module. Function-property assignment is a separate feature gap, not the cross-module class chain — tracked as next iteration.

## v0.5.615 — Refs #420 (drizzle-orm followup — cross-module static class fields): when `class Sub extends Base` is in a different file from `Base`, child-class declarations like `[Base.Symbol.X] = init` produced `undefined` because (1) HIR lowering at `expr_member.rs:323` only routes `obj.prop` through `Expr::StaticFieldGet` when `ctx.has_static_field(obj_name, prop)` returns true, and `class_statics` is per-module — imported classes' static fields aren't tracked, so cross-module `Base.Symbol` lowered as `PropertyGet { ExternFuncRef("Base"), "Symbol" }` instead. (2) Even when the lowering DID emit `StaticFieldGet`, the codegen lookup against `ctx.static_field_globals` missed because that map is populated only from `hir.classes` (this module's classes) — imported classes' static fields had no entry, falling back to `0.0`. (3) Source-side static-field globals were `internal` linkage, invisible to other modules' IR even after a declaration. Three coordinated fixes: **(a)** flip `add_internal_global` to `add_global` (external linkage) for the source module's static-field globals. **(b)** Add `static_field_names: Vec<String>` to `ImportedClass` (populate at all 6 sites in `compile.rs`); declare external globals on the consumer side and register in `static_field_globals`. **(c)** `Expr::PropertyGet` codegen — when receiver is `ExternFuncRef("Base")` and `(Base, prop)` is in `static_field_globals`, route to the global load.

## v0.5.614 — Refs #420 (drizzle-orm compile-end-to-end shake-out): two coordinated fixes for class declarations whose static initializers depend on top-level consts AND for computed-key class fields. (a) Computed-key class fields like `[Parent.Symbol.X] = init` and `[SymA] = []` were previously **silently dropped** at AST→HIR lowering — `lower_decl.rs` matched only `PropName::Ident` / `PropName::Str` and `continue`d on `PropName::Computed`. drizzle's `Table` and `PgTable` use this shape ~6 times (`[Table.Symbol.ExtraConfigBuilder]`, `[Table.Symbol.ExtraConfigColumns]`, `[TableName]`, `[Schema]`, `[Columns]`, …); without the field declarations reaching codegen, the constructor preamble had nothing to emit and `instance[Symbol] === undefined` for every Symbol-keyed field. Fix: extend `ClassField` with `key_expr: Option<Expr>` (the lowered key expression for computed-key fields; `name` becomes a synthetic placeholder `__computed_field_<lo>_<hi>` for HIR identity), update `lower_class_prop` to handle `PropName::Computed` (lowers the key expression), remove the skip in `lower_class_from_ast`, and update `apply_field_initializers_recursive` in `lower_call.rs` to emit `Expr::IndexSet { This, key_expr, init }` instead of the string-keyed `PropertySet` for computed-key fields. The IndexSet path already dispatches Symbol-keyed writes through `js_object_set_symbol_property`. (b) Static field initializers that reference top-level consts (drizzle's `static Symbol = { Schema, Columns, ... }` where `const Schema = Symbol.for("...")` is declared earlier in the same module) were initialized to `undefined` because `init_static_fields` in `codegen.rs` runs ALL static fields at the very top of `main`, before any user `Let` bindings have run — so the static-field-init lowered `lower_expr(Schema)` to a load of `@perry_global_*` whose value was still 0.0. Fix: at `lower.rs::ast::Decl::Class` lowering (both export and non-export arms), inject `Stmt::Expr(Expr::StaticFieldSet { class_name, field_name, value })` into `module.init` for each static field with an init, AT THE POSITION the class declaration appears in source order. The upfront `init_static_fields` pass stays — it's now harmless duplicate work that the inline statements correctly overwrite once their referenced consts are bound. The two fixes together unblock drizzle's `pgTable("users", { id: serial(...).primaryKey(), name: text(...) })` schema construction so columns actually attach to the returned table object. End-to-end smoke verified with the issue-#420 minimal repro: `c[SymCol]` returned `"col-default"` matching Node (was `undefined` pre-fix). Drizzle's full query-builder still has additional blockers (next iteration: `Object.assign(target, src)` doesn't mutate target — separate spec violation).

## v0.5.613 — **Closes #421** (hono compiles + runs end-to-end via `compilePackages`): `await app.fetch(req)` returns a Response with `status: 200` and `body: 'hello'` for the canonical `app.get('/', c => c.text('hello'))` repro. Three remaining bugs fixed this release: (1) `console.log(<Web Fetch handle>)` and `<handle> instanceof Promise` segfaulted because `format_jsvalue` and `js_instanceof` dereferenced the small registry-id payload as an ObjectHeader pointer (reading `obj - 8` for the GC type tag landed on unmapped memory). Both now skip handle-shaped pointers (`< 0x100000` after POINTER_TAG strip). (2) `dispatch_request_property` returned `Some(undefined)` for unknown properties on a known-Request handle, but Web Fetch handle id namespaces are disjoint between registries (Request id 1 ≠ Response id 1), so `response.status` on a Response handle whose id collided with a Request id resolved through Request first and shadowed the legitimate Response read. All four `dispatch_*_property` arms now return `None` for unknown properties so the dispatcher falls through. (3) Method dispatch (`response.text()` / `response.json()` / `headers.get(k)` / etc.) on any-typed Web Fetch handles wasn't wired into `js_handle_method_dispatch` at all — `await res.text()` returned NaN. Added `dispatch_response_method` / `dispatch_blob_method` / `dispatch_headers_method` in fetch.rs (each with the same membership + name-gate discipline as the property dispatchers) and wired them in. End-to-end: hono's full request → handler → Response → text() chain now works for the canonical 4-line program; further hono surface (middleware, routes with params, mergePath chain, JSON responses, custom headers) is beyond this release's scope but the structural blockers are gone. Parity: 210 pass / 3 known fails / 13 skipped (98.6%, +1 vs main; the 3 fails are pre-existing `known_failures.json` entries — `test_edge_destructuring`, `test_edge_objects_records`, `test_gap_object_methods`).

## v0.5.612 — Refs #421 (two more hono internals fixes): (1) v0.5.607's any-typed string-method dispatch tower returned `JSValue::int32(i).bits()` (NaN-boxed INT32_TAG) for `indexOf`/`lastIndexOf`, but INT32_TAG values are NaN — comparisons like `idx < url.length` always returned false because NaN comparisons are false. Hono's `getPath()` does `for (; i < url.length; i++)` after `i = url.indexOf("/", colonIdx + 4)` — the loop never iterated, getPath returned empty string, the route never matched, dispatch fell through to compose, "Context is not finalized" fired. Fix: return `i as f64` directly (matches typed-path's `sitofp` from `lower_string_method.rs`). Same pattern for `lastIndexOf`. Also added `replace`/`replaceAll` arms to the same dispatch tower (string + RegExp pattern; function-replacement deferred). (2) Closures called with fewer args than declared got garbage in missing slots — calling `c.text('hello')` (1 arg) on a `(text, arg, headers) => …` arrow stored as a class field went through `js_native_call_method` field-scan → `js_native_call_value(..., args_len=1)` which transmuted func_ptr to a 1-arg signature; the closure body's args 2 and 3 read from random stack registers. Hono's `c.text`'s `headers` slot evaluated to a tiny denormal float, fast-path condition `!headers` was false, slow-path `setDefaultContentType(TEXT_PLAIN, headers)` ran and the resulting header object was processed through `responseHeaders.set(k, v)` which then hit the issue #510 catch-all `(number).set is not a function`. Fix: new `CLOSURE_ARITY_REGISTRY` thread-local in `crates/perry-runtime/src/closure.rs` mapping non-rest closures' func_ptr → declared arity, populated at module init via new `js_register_closure_arity` extern (parallel to the existing closure-rest registry). `js_native_call_value` consults the registry; when the closure's declared arity exceeds `args_len`, pads trailing slots with TAG_UNDEFINED and dispatches to the matching higher-arity `js_closure_callN` (1-8). Codegen `crates/perry-codegen/src/codegen.rs` builds a `closure_arities: HashMap<u32, u32>` alongside `closure_rest_params` and `emit_string_pool` registers each entry. End-to-end: hono's `app.fetch(req)` runs through `c.text` → `new Response('hello')` correctly returning a Response (was hitting "Context is not finalized" → `(number).set` chain pre-fix). Remaining hono blocker is a SIGSEGV deeper in the fetch dispatch chain — separate, distinct from the arity / int-tag / handle-NaN-boxing fixes. Parity: 209 pass / 4 known fails / 13 skipped (98.1%, no regressions vs main).

## v0.5.611 — Closes #531: `ops.push(await runOp('name', N, async () => ...))` silently elided the inner closure's body — `compute_max_func_id` / `compute_max_local_id` in `crates/perry-transform/src/generator.rs` had no `Expr::ArrayPush` / `Expr::ArrayPushSpread` arms, so the user closure buried inside `ArrayPush.value: Await(Call { args: [..., Closure { func_id: K, ... }] })` was invisible to both scanners. The generator transform's `next_func_id` started below K, and the synthesized `__async_iter` `next`/`return`/`throw` closures got func_ids that collided with K. Codegen emits one LLVM function per func_id, so one definition wins and the other closure invokes it through a mismatched-shape capture array — calling `js_box_set` with a NULL or stale pointer (the bench's `LocalSet(__gen_done, true)` writes to whatever slot ended up in the capture position). Symptom in the @perryts/mongodb bench: `findOne_by_id_movie` and `find_movies_*` op closures' bodies never executed, with 3× `[PERRY WARN] js_box_set/get: invalid box pointer 0x0` warnings during their warmup loops. Fix: add `Expr::ArrayPush { value, .. }` and `Expr::ArrayPushSpread { source, .. }` arms to both scanners (mirrors the existing #154 / #393 / #212 arms for ArrayForEach/NativeMethodCall/etc.). Verified: bench's 9 ops now all execute correctly with 0 warnings, fingerprints match (find_movies = count 50, was count 1). Parity: 209 pass / 4 known fails / 13 skipped (98.1%, no regressions vs main).

## v0.5.610 — Refs #421 (followup to v0.5.608): `recv.method(...args)` on any-typed receivers silently no-op'd — `Expr::CallSpread`'s closure-callee fallback evaluated `recv.method` as a property read which returned `undefined` for class-prototype methods, and `js_closure_call_apply_with_spread` then no-op'd. Hono's SmartRouter.match's inner `router.add(...routes[i])` hit this — inner routers never received the route entries, so match returned empty `[[],[]]` even when routes were registered. Fix: new `recv.method(...args)` arm in `Expr::CallSpread` (`crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs`) detects PropertyGet callees and routes through the new `js_native_call_method_apply` runtime helper which materialises a JS args array into a temp f64 buffer and forwards to `js_native_call_method`. Plus `replace`/`replaceAll` added to the any-typed string method dispatch tower (`crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs`) — pattern can be string or RegExp; replacement must be string. Detect RegExp pattern via NaN-box POINTER_TAG check. Function replacements deferred (need closure dispatch). Hono's `await app.fetch(req)` now runs through the dispatch chain past route matching; remaining errors are deeper closure-box / context-finalization issues in hono internals (separate bug classes — pre-existing). Parity: 209 pass / 4 known fails / 13 skipped (98.1%, no regressions vs main).

## v0.5.608 — **Refs #421** (Phase 1 of the handle-NaN-boxing unification — Web Fetch slice; unblocks hono `request.url` in untyped position): perry's runtime carried two parallel object-identity systems: NaN-boxed POINTER_TAG values for ObjectHeader/ArrayHeader/StringHeader, and raw small-integer f64 handles for Web Fetch (Request/Response/Headers/Blob), streams, ws, net, DB, UI widgets, etc. Each new bug along hono's compile-end-to-end path (#421) — v0.5.594/596/601/603 — added another codegen branch to "detect which system we're in and dispatch correctly," accumulating without converging. v0.5.603 specifically registered Request/Response/Headers TYPED parameter annotations as native instances at HIR lowering, but hono's compiled JS (via `compilePackages`) has TS types stripped, so `(request) => request.url` had no type info to register; codegen fell through to `js_object_get_field_by_name` which bit-cast the raw float-form handle (`1.0` = bits `0x3FF0_..._0000`) as an ObjectHeader pointer, dereferenced garbage, returned undefined, and downstream `url.indexOf("/")` threw `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'indexOf')`. The structural fix instead of more codegen branches: NaN-box Web Fetch handles at the FFI boundary so they look like every other heap pointer to the runtime's existing dispatchers. Five coordinated changes:

**(1)** `crates/perry-stdlib/src/fetch.rs` — new `handle_to_f64(id: usize) -> f64` (wraps `js_nanbox_pointer(id as i64)` → bit pattern `0x7FFD_..._N`) + tolerant `handle_id(value: f64) -> usize` that accepts BOTH the new NaN-boxed form AND the legacy raw-float form (`1.0` → 1 via float-truncation; denormal `0x0000_..._N` → N as raw bits). Tolerance kept during the cross-subsystem migration so streams/ws/net/DB (still Phase 2) can keep round-tripping their handles unchanged.

**(2)** All 25 Web Fetch FFI functions migrated: constructors (`js_headers_new` / `js_request_new` / `js_response_new` / `js_response_clone` / `js_response_get_headers` / `js_response_static_json` / `js_response_static_redirect` / `js_blob_slice` / `js_response_blob`'s promise-resolution path + the 5 async-fetch resolution paths in `js_fetch_get` / `js_fetch_post` / etc.) wrap returns with `handle_to_f64`; accessors (`js_headers_set/get/has/delete/for_each` / `js_request_get_url/method/body` / `js_response_get_headers` / `js_response_array_buffer/blob` / `js_blob_size/type/array_buffer/bytes/text/slice/stream`) unbox via `handle_id` on entry. The 5 i64-typed `js_fetch_response_status/_status_text/_ok/_text/_json` flipped to `f64` signatures and use `handle_id` — the `i64` variant was the legacy float-form's only consumer; replacing it with a tolerant unbox is cleaner than keeping two ABIs. `js_blob_stream` and `js_response_body` keep their inner stream-handle as legacy raw-float form (Phase 2 boundary, comment marks it).

**(3)** Untyped Web Fetch property dispatch wired into perry-stdlib's `js_handle_property_dispatch` (`crates/perry-stdlib/src/common/dispatch.rs`). Codegen's untyped property-get path strips POINTER_TAG, sees a small id (< 0x100000), routes here. Four new internal dispatch helpers in `fetch.rs` (`dispatch_request_property` / `dispatch_response_property` / `dispatch_headers_property` / `dispatch_blob_property`) each do their own registry-membership check and return `None` to fall through. Property name + registry membership disambiguates between Request/Response/Headers/Blob handles whose registry IDs collide (Request id 1 and Response id 1 are different objects from independent counters). Order matches observed disjointness: Request (`url` / `method` / `body`) → Response (`status` / `statusText` / `ok`) → Headers (no scalar properties; method-only) → Blob (`size` / `type`).

**(4)** Codegen `crates/perry-codegen/src/runtime_decls.rs` — flipped the 5 `js_fetch_response_*` declarations from `&[I64]` to `&[DOUBLE]` and updated the leading comment block (was: "Handles flow as plain numeric f64 values (not NaN-boxed) so codegen passes them as DOUBLE. Where the runtime takes i64, codegen converts via fptosi"; now: "Handles are NaN-boxed POINTER_TAG f64 values — codegen passes them through as DOUBLE without conversion. Untyped property access routes through `HANDLE_PROPERTY_DISPATCH`"). `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` — removed the 5 `fptosi` conversions in the `module == "fetch"` arm; now passes `recv_handle` directly as `(DOUBLE, &recv_handle)`.

**(5)** Filled v0.5.510 / v0.5.593's any-typed string-method dispatch tower in `js_native_call_method` (`crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs::4070+`) — was `at` / `charAt` / `charCodeAt` / `slice` / `toString` / `valueOf` / `match` / `matchAll` / `search`; added `indexOf` / `includes` / `lastIndexOf` / `startsWith` / `endsWith` / `concat` / `toUpperCase` / `toLowerCase` / `trim` / `trimStart` / `trimLeft` / `trimEnd` / `trimRight` / `substring` / `repeat` / `split`. Each arm calls the existing `js_string_*` runtime helper. Hono's `request.url.indexOf("/")` (where `url` is in any-typed position because hono's compiled JS strips the `Request` annotation) needed this — without it, the previous-iteration progress past `request.url`-returns-undefined immediately hit `(string).indexOf is not a function`. The catch-all stays as `(string).<method> is not a function` for any future unhandled methods.

End-to-end smoke verified against the issue's exact 8-line repro (`new Request("http://localhost/")` + `app.get('/', cb)` + `app.fetch(req)`): `routes: 1` / `typeof fetch: function` / route-registration intact; `(request) => request.url` returns the URL correctly through both the typed and untyped paths; `request.url.indexOf("/")` returns 7 instead of throwing. The next downstream blocker is hono's internal `RegExpRouter.match` returning `[[], []]` (empty match arrays even though `app.routes[0]` shows the route registered) — separate bug class documented in v0.5.601's notes ("route registration is a separate downstream concern"), not in scope for this commit. **Phase 2** (streams/ws/net/DB handle migration) and **Phase 3** (codegen cleanup — remove the `obj_handle > 0x100000` IC miss guard and the dispatcher's range-check fallback once all subsystems NaN-box) follow the same canonical pattern: each subsystem's constructors wrap with `js_nanbox_pointer`, accessors unbox via the `handle_id` analog, untyped property dispatch arms register in `js_handle_property_dispatch` — net result is each phase DELETES detection branches rather than adds them. Parity sweep: 209 pass / 4 known fails / 13 skipped (98.1%, no regressions vs main; all 4 failures are pre-existing entries in `test-parity/known_failures.json` — `test_edge_destructuring`, `test_edge_objects_records`, `test_gap_object_methods`, `test_net_upgrade_tls`).


## v0.5.607 — **Closes #529** (followup to #515): `obj["method"](args)` and `obj["prop"]` on a class instance returned `undefined` because the Computed `MemberProp` arm in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_member.rs::537+` always emitted `Expr::IndexGet`, which codegen routes through the generic property-by-name read (`js_dyn_index_get`) — that path only sees object fields and misses class methods entirely (methods live in the vtable populated by `js_register_class_method`, not as inline object slots). The dot form `obj.method` lowers to `Expr::PropertyGet` which codegen routes through `js_class_method_bind` + vtable lookup, so the two syntactically-equivalent forms diverged. End-to-end demonstration with the v0.5.605 #515 round-2 commit's deferred variant C: `class B { build(): number { return 99; } }; class W { spawn(): B { return new B(); } }; new W().spawn()["build"]()` returned `undefined` and chained `.build()` then threw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'build')`; non-`with` method names (`["tag"]`, `["greet"]`) failed identically — confirming the bug was generic computed-property-on-class dispatch, not anything `with`-specific. Fix is one fold in `expr_member.rs::537+`: when the `MemberProp::Computed` index lowers to `Expr::String(key)` AND `key` is NOT a non-negative integer literal (`"0"` / `"1"` / `"42"`, where JS spec demands array-element semantics), emit `Expr::PropertyGet { object, property: key }` instead of `Expr::IndexGet`. Numeric-string indices keep IndexGet so `arr["0"] = v` retains spec-compliant element-write semantics. Mirrored fold in `expr_assign.rs::317+` for the assignment form so `obj["count"] = 7` parallels `obj.count = 7`. End-to-end smoke verified: `c["greet"]("world")` on a class instance returns `"hello world"` (was `undefined`); `const fn = c["greet"]; typeof fn` returns `"function"` (was `"undefined"`); `new World().spawn()["build"]()` returns `99` (was `TypeError`); the original variant C `new World().spawn()["with"](1, {x:0,y:0}).build()` returns `99`; plain object computed access (`obj["foo"]` / `obj["bar"]`) works unchanged; numeric-string keep IndexGet path so `arr["0"]` / `arr["length"]` continue to work as before; class field read via computed (`p["count"]`) returns the field value. Parity sweep: 210 pass / 3 known fails / 0 compile fails / 13 skipped (98.5%) — IMPROVED from the pre-fix 209/4 baseline (one test recovered, zero regressions). The recovered test is `test_gap_array_methods` whose `Int32Array.with(1,99)` step likely now reaches the typed-array runtime fallback through a different (post-#515 layered defense) path.

## v0.5.606 — **Closes #526** (DX: `tsc -p .` against a fresh `perry init` project failed out of the box because the auto-generated `.d.ts` emitted `export function delete(...)` for `axios.delete` — `delete` is a JS reserved word, `tsc` rejects it as a function-declaration name with TS1359). Fix in `crates/perry-api-manifest/src/emit.rs::emit_dts`: new `is_ts_reserved_word(s)` helper covers the strict-mode reserved set + the strict-only contextual keywords (`break`/`case`/`catch`/`class`/`const`/…/`yield` plus `implements`/`interface`/`let`/`package`/`private`/`protected`/`public`/`static`/`await`); when a module-level method's name matches, the emitter routes through the alias pattern `function _<name>(...): any; export { _<name> as <name> };` instead of the broken `export function <name>(...)` shape. The `as <reserved>` rename slot accepts arbitrary identifiers including reserved words — same trick TypeScript itself uses for `lib.dom.d.ts` reserved-word symbols. The existing `default` special case stays as `export default function (...)` since `export default` has its own ESM syntax that handles the keyword cleanly. Two new regression tests in `emit::tests`: `dts_axios_delete_does_not_use_reserved_word_as_fn_name` asserts the axios block contains the alias pattern and does NOT contain `export function delete(`; `dts_no_reserved_word_function_declarations` is a parity sweep that walks the live `API_MANIFEST` and asserts no module-level method whose name is in `is_ts_reserved_word` ever leaks as `export function <reserved>(` — pre-emptive coverage for future manifest additions like `axios.try` or `axios.new`. Today's manifest has only `axios.delete` matching (fastify + lru-cache `delete` are instance methods that hang off `[key: string]: any;` on their class, unaffected by this path). End-to-end smoke verified: `perry init my-app && bun install typescript && tsc --noEmit -p .` exits 0 (was exit 1 with TS1359/TS1109/TS1011/TS1005/TS1128 cascade); user code that imports `axios.delete(url)` and `axios.get(url)` and the default callable export all type-check cleanly through the alias-export shape. `docs/api/perry.d.ts` regenerated through `scripts/regen_api_docs.sh` so the api-docs-drift CI gate stays green; `docs/src/api/reference.md` got the version bump only since markdown is forgiving on the same offending name.


## v0.5.605 — **Closes #515 (round 2)**: the v0.5.595 fix gated the `with`-fold catch-all (`crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs:4029`) on `!recv_is_class`, but `recv_is_class` only matched four specific shapes (Ident with class type, `new C()`, `this`, `this.field`). Three new variants from codehz fell through: (1) **object-literal receivers** — `const obj = { with(a, b) { return 99 } }; obj.with(1, 2)` returned `[]` because `Type::Object(_)` flowed through to the OTHER `with` arm at line 2897 (the bare-ident array fast path) where unknown-typed Idents get folded as "array-only methods" via the `is_not_string` final-else; (2) **chained method-call receivers** — `world.spawn().with(1, {…}).build()` returned `[ 6.79e-313 ]` because `ast::Expr::Call(_)` falls into `_ => false` in `recv_is_class`; (3) **computed-property** — `world.spawn()["with"](1, {…}).build()` throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'build')` — confirmed pre-existing, unrelated to `with` (a 1-line repro with `["tag"]` on a class instance fails identically), tracked separately. Three coordinated fixes: **(a)** `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs:2897` — gate the bare-ident `with` arm on a strong "is array-like" predicate (`Type::Array(_) | Type::Tuple(_)` or one of the 11 typed-array `Type::Named` shapes — `Int8Array` / `Int16Array` / `Int32Array` / `Uint8Array` / `Uint8ClampedArray` / `Uint16Array` / `Uint32Array` / `Float32Array` / `Float64Array` / `BigInt64Array` / `BigUint64Array`); the typed-array list mirrors the existing `is_typed_array` predicate at line 2730+ verbatim. Without the typed-array branch, `new Int32Array([10,20,30]).with(1,99)` regresses since `Int32Array` is `Type::Named` and the bare-Array-only check would bail to runtime where the typed-array `js_typed_array_with` path lives behind `js_array_with`'s typed-array dispatch — but that runtime arm isn't reachable when codegen took the `is_array_expr` shortcut, see (b). **(b)** `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs:4029` — replace `!recv_is_class` with an inverted "is array-like" predicate that ONLY folds for `Ident` with `Type::Array(_) | Type::Tuple(_)` and inline `ast::Expr::Array(_)` literals. The 4029 arm previously folded for any non-class receiver, including `ast::Expr::Call(_)` (variant B) and ident receivers with `Type::Object(_)` (variant A's first half before line 2897 caught it). Other ident-receiver array-method arms (`map`/`filter`/`reduce`/`forEach`/`find`/`findIndex`) keep their current optimistic `!recv_is_class` gate because they overlap with String/Array/user-class methods and the runtime `js_native_call_method` has dispatch fallbacks for them at lines 4120/4127/4145/4159 in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs` — `with` had no such fallback before this commit. **(c)** Two new `with` arms close the layered defense: `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_array_method.rs::538+` — `lower_array_method`'s catch-all at line 531 silently returned the receiver unchanged for unknown methods, which post-tightening would mean `is_array_expr`-recognized receivers (e.g. `let a4 = doWith([1,2,3])` after the inliner inlines `doWith(x: any) { return x.with(0,42); }` and the binding's declared type stays `Type::Any` while `is_array_expr` recognizes the Array initializer) silently fail with no .with() effect. New arm calls `js_array_with` directly through the `(I64, recv_handle), (DOUBLE, idx), (DOUBLE, val)` extern signature already declared in `runtime_decls.rs:585`. `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs:4179+` — defensive `with` arm in `js_native_call_method`'s array dispatch tower (sibling to the existing `map`/`filter`/`forEach`/`slice` arms) for receivers that bypass codegen's `is_array_expr` (e.g. function-param `x: any` where the inliner does NOT inline). End-to-end smoke verified: original v0.5.595 5-line repro (class with own `with` method) keeps producing `result === obj: true` / `result.val: 3` / `obj.val: 3` / `chain: 7` / `1-param chain: 10`; new variant A (object literal) returns `99`; new variant B (chained `world.spawn().with(...).build()`) returns `99`; `[1,2,3,4,5].with(2, 99)` continues to fold correctly to `[1,2,99,4,5]`; `arr.with(-1, 0)` (negative index) still works; `(x: any).with(0,42)` on a real array works through the runtime fallback (and via codegen's `lower_array_method` when inlined); `new Int32Array([10,20,30]).with(1,99)` returns `Int32Array(3) [ 10, 99, 30 ]` (would have regressed in iteration 1 of the fix without the typed-array branch in (a)). Variant C is documented as a separate pre-existing computed-property-on-class dispatch issue — `world.spawn()["tag"](...).build()` (replacing `with` with a non-fold name) fails identically with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'build')`, so the bug is in the computed-property branch of HIR member access, not in `with` handling. Parity sweep: 209 pass / 4 known fails / 0 compile fails / 13 skipped (98.1%, identical to pre-fix main baseline; the 4 fails are pre-existing — `test_edge_destructuring`, `test_edge_objects_records`, `test_gap_array_methods` (Int32Array typed-array path), `test_gap_object_methods`).


## v0.5.604 — **Closes #525** (DX: unify R005/#463 message format across all unimplemented-API rejection paths): the v0.5.528 unimplemented-API gate fired with the canonical `… is not implemented in Perry — see \`perry --print-api-manifest\` …` message for property-read forms (`ui.bogusProperty`) and 3-deep static-method-call forms (`crypto.subtle.encrypt(…)`), but the 2-deep call form `m.method()` for native-module namespace imports short-circuited past the `lower_member` gate at `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs:907` and bailed in codegen's `lower_call/native.rs` with a per-module message (`perry/ui: 'bogusWidget' is not a known function (args: 0). Check types/perry/ui/index.d.ts …`) — different wording, different escape hatch, buried under a 3-level wrapping chain (`Error compiling module … lowering entry of module … lowering init statements of module …`). Same class of mistake, four DX dimensions out of sync. Fix: one block in `expr_call.rs` mirroring the 3-deep gate at line 244+ — before constructing `Expr::NativeMethodCall { module, class_name: None, …, method, args }`, consult `perry_api_manifest::module_has_any_entries(module_name)` + `module_has_symbol(module_name, &method_name)` and `lower_bail!` with the canonical message+span when the symbol isn't registered. Same `PERRY_ALLOW_UNIMPLEMENTED=1` escape hatch flag as the existing gates. The four `bail!` sites in `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call/native.rs` (perry/media:594, perry/plugin:617, perry/updater:631, perry/ui:693) become unreachable for any module enumerated in the manifest — left in place as defensive asserts since the reverse-direction drift test from #513 (`every_native_module_has_at_least_one_manifest_entry`) is what makes them unreachable. New parity sweep test `every_supported_module_rejects_bogus_call` in `crates/perry-hir/tests/unimplemented_api_check.rs` mirrors the existing `every_supported_module_rejects_bogus_member` read-form sweep but uses `m.bogus()` call form for every entry in `NATIVE_MODULES` (skip list identical: `dotenv/config` + external `tursodb`/`iroh`); asserts the canonical `not implemented` + `#463` message text fires uniformly. End-to-end smoke verified against the issue's exact 4-line repros: `perry/ui.bogusWidget()` / `perry/media.bogusMethod()` / `perry/plugin.bogusMethod()` / `perry/updater.bogusMethod()` / `fs.bogusMethod()` all emit the same canonical R005 message at compile time. Property-read forms (`ui.bogusProperty` standalone) and chained calls (`crypto.subtle.encrypt(…)`) continue to emit identical wording. Legitimate `fs.writeFileSync(…)` / `crypto.randomUUID()` etc. compile unchanged. Parity sweep: 210 pass / 3 known fails / 13 skipped (98.5%, no regressions vs main).


## v0.5.603 — Refs #519 / #421 (hono fetch chain — Web Fetch API typed-parameter native-instance registration): the v0.5.601 IMPLICIT_THIS fix unblocked hono's `RegExpRouter.match` chain, but the next blocker downstream — `app.fetch(req)` reading `req.url` inside hono's compiled `fetch` arrow — surfaced because `Request` / `Response` / `Headers` parameter type annotations weren't registered as native instances at HIR lowering. Without the registration, `r.url` on a typed `(r: Request)` parameter went through the generic-object-property-get fallback which tried to interpret the runtime handle id (a small integer like `1.0`) as an ObjectHeader pointer — `unbox_to_i64` of `1.0` returns 0, so `js_object_get_field_by_name(NULL, "url")` returned undefined, and downstream `url.indexOf("/")` threw `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'indexOf')`. Fix is one block in each of `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_function.rs` (arrow params, line 67+) and `crates/perry-hir/src/lower_decl.rs` (function-decl params, line 292+): when a parameter's `Type::Named` matches `"Request"` / `"Response"` / `"Headers"`, call `ctx.register_native_instance` with the same module/class strings the local-init paths in `destructuring.rs` use for `const r = new Request(…)`. After registration, codegen's `Request.url` / `Response.status` / `Headers.get` static dispatches at `lower_call.rs:3503+` fire and route directly to `js_request_get_url(handle)` etc. — the runtime handle reaches the getter as a raw f64, where `handle as usize` correctly recovers the registry id. End-to-end smoke verified with the minimal repro `function takeReq(r: Request): string { return r.url }` — pre-fix returned `undefined`, post-fix returns the URL. Hono itself is still blocked because its compiled JS (via compilePackages) has TypeScript types stripped, so `(request) =>` (no annotation) doesn't trigger the registration; proper hono support needs a runtime-side handle-detection path (separate, larger followup — likely NaN-boxing handle returns with POINTER_TAG or detecting raw small-integer values as handles in the property-get dispatcher). Parity sweep: 210 pass / 3 known fails / 13 skipped (98.5%, no regressions vs main).


## v0.5.602 — **Closes #509** (parallel-codegen race producing colliding object files): two rayon codegen workers compiling sibling modules within the same microsecond ended up writing to the same `/tmp/perry_llvm_<pid>_<nanos>.ll` path. Both overwrote the same `.ll`, both `clang -c` invocations compiled the same IR, both read back the same `.o` bytes — leaving sibling `.o` files (e.g. `consumer_type_only.o` and `consumer_value.o`) with one module's symbols stamped onto the other's filename. Linker then rejected the unresolved cross-module symbols (`_perry_fn_..._consumeTypeOnly`, `_fixtures_issue_446_pkg_consumer_type_only_ts__init`). Surfaced after v0.5.589 (closure rest registry shifted codegen timing into the failure window); the underlying race had been latent since `compile_ll_to_object` was written. Root cause in `crates/perry-codegen/src/linker.rs::compile_ll_to_object`: the temp-path nonce was `SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).as_nanos()`, but macOS `SystemTime` resolves to microseconds — so two threads racing the path construction within ~1µs produced byte-identical `nanos` values. Fix: new module-level `static TEMP_NONCE_COUNTER: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0)` is `fetch_add(1, Relaxed)`-incremented on every call and mixed into the path alongside the existing pid + wall-clock nanos. The atomic counter guarantees in-process uniqueness regardless of clock resolution; pid + wall nanos preserve cross-process distinctness for two `perry` invocations sharing `/tmp`. New regression test `temp_nonce_counter_is_unique_across_concurrent_calls` spawns 16 threads × 16 fetches and asserts all 256 values are distinct. With the fix, `test_issue_446_import_type_method_typeof` and `test_issue_310_namespace_reexport` (the two `known_failures.json` entries tracking this race) link cleanly and produce correct output; both removed from the skip list.


## v0.5.601 — **Closes #519** (hono `app.fetch()` chain unblocked: cross-instance method-style calls now bind `this` correctly): `instance.fn(args)` where `fn` is a closure-typed class field — hono's `RegExpRouter { match = match; }` (where `match` is the imported function declaration from matcher.js) — was reaching its function body with `this = 0` because perry's closure ABI takes `(closure_ptr, arg0, …)` with no `this` slot. The function body's `this.buildAllMatchers()` then fell through to issue #510's catch-all `(number).buildAllMatchers is not a function`. Three coordinated fixes:

**(1)** New thread-local `IMPLICIT_THIS` (`crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs::31+`, `Cell<u64>` initialized to `TAG_UNDEFINED`) plus `js_implicit_this_get/set(value: f64) -> f64` extern "C" helpers. `set` returns the previous value so callers can save+restore around the binding. Defaults to undefined, matching JS strict-mode top-level semantics.

**(2)** Codegen for `Expr::This` (`crates/perry-codegen/src/expr.rs::4067`) — when the lexical `this_stack` is empty (top-level module code, top-level function declarations, or non-arrow function expressions without `captures_this`), call `js_implicit_this_get` instead of returning the 0.0 sentinel. Class methods and arrow closures with `captures_this` keep their existing `this_slot`-based path unchanged. Runtime decls in `runtime_decls.rs::2024+` so the LLVM module declares the symbol.

**(3)** Two complementary call-site bindings save+set+call+restore IMPLICIT_THIS around the dispatch:
  - `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs::js_native_call_method` field-scan path (around line 4197-4250): when the receiver is an object whose `keys_array` contains a closure-valued field matching the method name, save IMPLICIT_THIS, set it to the receiver, call the closure via `js_native_call_value`, restore. Covers the dispatch path for any-typed receivers + Map/Set field access.
  - `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` closure-call fallback (around line 2270): when the callee shape is `recv.method(args)` (a `PropertyGet`) and we're emitting `js_closure_call<N>`, also emit `js_implicit_this_set(recv)` before the call and `js_implicit_this_set(prev)` after. Skips `Expr::GlobalGet` / `Expr::NativeModuleRef` / `Expr::ExternFuncRef` receivers since those aren't user objects whose identity should propagate as `this`. Direct function calls (`fn(args)`) leave IMPLICIT_THIS untouched.

**Follow-on fix** in the same release: `js_native_call_method`'s string-receiver dispatch arm (`crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs::4082+`) was missing `match` / `matchAll` / `search`. Hono's matcher.js uses `path2.match(matcher[0])` where `matcher[0]` is a regex-typed `Expr::Member` access — perry's HIR-level `Expr::StringMatch` optimistic lowering only fires when the regex arg is a literal or a static `RegExp`-typed `Ident`, so the `Member` form fell through to the dynamic dispatch and hit the issue #510 catch-all (`(string).match is not a function`). Added the three method arms routing through `crate::regex::js_string_match{,_all}` and `js_string_search_regex` — same dispatch shape the HIR-level path uses.

End-to-end smoke verified: hono's `app.router.match('GET', '/')` chain now runs through `SmartRouter.match` → `RegExpRouter.match` (the imported `match` function with `this` correctly bound to the RegExpRouter instance) → `this.buildAllMatchers()` → returns the empty-match `[ [], [] ]` (correct shape; route registration is a separate downstream concern). `await app.fetch(req)` progresses past the previous TypeError; the next blocker downstream is `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'indexOf')` inside the dispatch chain — separate followup, not part of #519. The minimal repro `class Foo { match = plainFn; } const f = new Foo(); f.match()` correctly observes `this === f` (was `this = 0` pre-fix). Parity sweep: 210 pass / 3 known fails / 13 skipped (98.5%, no regressions vs main; the 3 fails are pre-existing `known_failures.json` entries). The runtime-only IMPLICIT_THIS approach intentionally doesn't enforce JS spec for nested non-method calls (`f()` inside an outer method-style call still sees the outer's `this` since direct calls don't reset IMPLICIT_THIS) — that's an incremental gap the codegen could close by emitting `js_implicit_this_set(undefined)` on direct-call sites if a future test demands it; today's pre-fix behavior was "return 0.0" for all such cases, so any test that depended on the looser observed behavior keeps working.
