Replaced the agent's `notify_main_thread` workaround with the actual `perry_ffi::notify_main_thread()` call now that the symbol exists on main (added in v0.5.568). **`perry-ext-http`** (~610 LOC, subagent worktree): 13 `js_http_*` exports — `request` / `https_request` / `get` / `https_get` constructors (options-object parsed via `perry_ffi::json_stringify` → `serde_json::Value` so `{ host, port, path, method, headers, timeout }` Just Works without per-field shape gymnastics); `client_request_write` / `_end` for body building (idempotent on `ended=true`); `_on` for `'response'` / `'error'` (ClientRequest) and `'data'` / `'end'` / `'error'` (IncomingMessage); `_set_header` / `_set_timeout`; `_status_code` / `_status_message` / `_response_headers` (last builds a shape-keyed object via `build_object_shape` + `js_object_alloc_with_shape`); pump pair. Both `http` and `https` route through one shared `dispatch_request` that funnels into a lazy per-process `HTTP_CLIENT` (reqwest with rustls-tls + http2, pool_max_idle_per_host=16, default User-Agent — same connection-pool reuse trick perry-ext-fetch uses). spawn_blocking + tokio::Handle::current().block_on bridges async reqwest into perry-ffi's blocking-pool surface. perry-stdlib gate flips: `websocket` umbrella → `["bundled-ws"]`, new `bundled-ws = ["dep:tokio-tungstenite", "dep:futures-util", "async-runtime"]` per-binding gate; `net` umbrella → `["bundled-net"]`, new `bundled-net = ["async-runtime"]`; `tls` now depends on `bundled-net`. `pub mod ws` / `pub mod net` flipped to the new feature names. `stdlib_features::module_to_features("ws")` → `["bundled-ws"]`; `"net"` → `["bundled-net", "tls"]`; `"http"` / `"https"` join the `http-client` umbrella alongside axios/node-fetch. `optimized_libs` async-wrapper allowlist gains `bundled-ws` / `bundled-net` / `http-client`. `well_known_bindings.toml` gains `[bindings.ws]` / `[bindings.net]` / `[bindings.http]` / `[bindings.https]` (last two share the `perry-ext-http` archive). 12 unit tests pass across the three crates (4+3+5); all 6 `perry well_known` tests pass including the load-bearing `every_entry_references_a_workspace_crate` invariant. **Body chunking gap** documented across all three: `reqwest::Response::chunk()` / WebSocket message dispatch are async, but we run inside spawn_blocking, so bodies / messages are delivered as single events rather than streamed — true streaming requires a cooperative `spawn_async` perry-ffi surface (v0.6.0 followup). Brings the in-tree wrapper port count to 32. Remaining Phase 5 wrappers: streams (1341 LOC, 35 exports — should now be straightforward since http-client is the umbrella and streams imports it transitively), fastify+framework (multi-file, ~3500 LOC total).


## v0.5.568 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 steps 28+29 — port fetch + mongodb in parallel; perry-ffi grows json_stringify + notify_main_thread): two ports landed in one release plus two new perry-ffi surface dimensions to unblock the next batch.

- **`perry-ext-fetch`** (manual port; subagent A stalled on the watchdog so I did it inline). 41 `js_*` exports across the Web Fetch API surface — fetch core (`get` / `get_with_auth` / `post` / `post_with_auth` / `with_options` / `text`), Response (`status` / `statusText` / `ok` / `text` / `json` / `array_buffer` / `blob` / `get_headers` / `clone` / `body` / `static_json` / `static_redirect` / `count`), Headers (`new` / `set` / `get` / `has` / `delete` / `for_each` — last one uses `JsClosure::call2` with the spec-correct `(value, key)` arg order), streaming (`stream_start` / `stream_poll` / `stream_status` / `stream_close` via tokio::sync::mpsc per stream), Blob (`size` / `type` / `text` / `array_buffer` / `bytes` / `slice` / `stream`), Request (`new` / `get_url` / `get_method` / `get_body`). Single shared `do_fetch(method, url, headers, body, promise)` helper funnels every variant through one spawn_blocking + reqwest path. Lazy per-process `HTTP_CLIENT` reuses connection pool / DNS cache / TLS sessions across calls (~250KB savings per fetch); default `User-Agent` (closes #236). 6 unit tests cover headers round-trip, blob slice, request round-trip, response_static_json. perry-stdlib's `http-client` umbrella stays as `["dep:reqwest", "async-runtime"]` — both `node-fetch` and `axios` route through it via the well-known table (entries `[bindings.node-fetch]` and `[bindings.fetch]` added; `axios` already had its `[bindings.axios]` entry from v0.5.545). The optimized_libs async-wrapper allowlist will need `bundled-fetch` if a per-binding split is ever needed; for now the umbrella covers both.

- **`perry-ext-mongodb`** (subagent B, worktree mode). 26 `js_mongodb_*` exports — full surface (`client_new` / `client_connect` / `connect` / `client_db` / `db_collection` / `client_close` / `client_list_databases` / `db_list_collections` plus `collection_*` for find_one / find / insert_one / insert_many / update_one / update_many / delete_one / delete_many / count, with paired `*_value` variants that JSON-stringify the JsValue arg via `perry_ffi::json_stringify` then deserialize into `bson::Document`). The `*_value` variants are the canonical user case: codegen's `NA_F64` arg coercion passes the JS object literal as a NaN-boxed `f64` at the FFI boundary; the wrapper json-stringifies it, parses with `serde_json`, converts to `bson::Document`, hands to mongodb. perry-stdlib's `database-mongodb` umbrella retained for backwards-compat but flipped to `["bundled-mongodb"]`; new `bundled-mongodb = ["dep:mongodb", "dep:bson", "dep:futures-util", "async-runtime"]` per-binding gate. `optimized_libs::build_optimized_libs` re-asserts `async-runtime` when stripping `bundled-mongodb`. 4 unit tests cover client URI parse, BSON Document round-trip via `json_stringify`, collection-lookup-on-invalid-db-handle, pre-connect client ref errors. Followups documented: BSON `ObjectId` as a JsValue (today's port returns it stringified), `find` cursor streaming (today batches via `cursor.try_collect()`), change streams (`watch()`), aggregation pipelines.

- **perry-ffi v0.5.x grows two more surface dimensions** in this release. New `crates/perry-ffi/src/json.rs` exposes `json_stringify(JsValue) -> Option<String>` over the runtime's `extern "C" fn js_json_stringify` — what mongodb's `*_value` variants need to bridge codegen's NA_F64 coercion onto serde_json + bson::Document. New `crates/perry-ffi/src/event_pump.rs` exposes `notify_main_thread()` over the runtime's `js_notify_main_thread` — wakes the main thread from `js_wait_for_event` when a tokio worker has pushed an event onto a wrapper's per-module pending-events queue. Brings the perry-ffi surface to 11 dimensions total (strings, async/Promise, handles, JsValue+objects/arrays, closures, GC root scanner, BigInt, Buffer, JSON, event-pump). Both unblock the next port batch (ws / http / streams / net / fastify) which all need to wake the main thread from worker threads.

Brings the in-tree wrapper port count to 29.


## v0.5.567 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 27 — port mysql2): new `crates/perry-ext-mysql2/` ports the npm `mysql2` MySQL client. 15 `js_mysql2_*` exports — full surface (`createConnection` async ctor; `connection_end` / `connection_query` / `connection_execute` / `connection_begin_transaction` / `connection_commit` / `connection_rollback`; `createPool` sync (uses spawn_blocking + std::sync::mpsc::channel for the eager pool build since createPool returns a Handle synchronously); `pool_end` / `pool_query` / `pool_execute` / `pool_get_connection` / `pool_connection_release` / `pool_connection_query` / `pool_connection_execute`). Single-file port collapses perry-stdlib's multi-file mysql2/{connection,pool,result,types}.rs into one ~870 LOC `lib.rs`. Result format mirrors perry-stdlib's mysql2-npm-compatible `[rows, fields]` tuple — rows are objects keyed by column name (built with `build_object_shape`), fields are `{ name, type, length }` shape. Non-SELECT queries return `[ResultSetHeader, []]` where ResultSetHeader is `{ affectedRows, insertId, warningStatus }`. URL encoder for password preserved verbatim (handles `# @ : / `). URI parsing in `parse_mysql_uri` for the `{ uri: "mysql://..." }` config form. Pool-connection pattern (`getConnection` + `release`) uses a separate `MysqlPoolConnectionHandle` type — `release` simply calls `take_handle::<MysqlPoolConnectionHandle>(h)` so the underlying `PoolConnection<MySql>` returns to the pool via Drop. perry-stdlib's `database-mysql` umbrella retained for backwards-compat but flipped to `["bundled-mysql2"]`; new `bundled-mysql2 = ["dep:sqlx", "async-runtime"]` per-binding gate is what the well-known flip toggles. perry-stdlib's pg + mysql2 modules now share the cfg `any(bundled-pg, bundled-mysql2)` since both rely on sqlx (the legacy `database-postgres`/`database-mysql` umbrellas now go through their respective `bundled-*` features). `optimized_libs::build_optimized_libs` re-asserts `async-runtime` when stripping `bundled-mysql2`. 4 unit tests cover config defaults / URL password encoding / URI parse / SELECT-vs-INSERT classifier. Brings the in-tree wrapper port count to 27.


## v0.5.566 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 26 — port pg): new `crates/perry-ext-pg/` ports the npm `pg` PostgreSQL client. 10 `js_pg_*` exports — full surface (`client_new` sync ctor + `client_connect` deferred TCP open; `client_query` no-params + `client_query_params` parameterized; `client_end`; combined-API `connect` for back-compat; `pool_new` sync + lazy `connect_lazy`-style + `create_pool` eager factory; `pool_query`; `pool_end`). Single-file port collapses perry-stdlib's multi-file pg/{connection,pool,result,types}.rs into one ~600 LOC `lib.rs` using only perry-ffi v0.5 surface. Row objects keyed by column name via `build_object_shape(&row.columns().iter().map(|c| c.name()).collect::<Vec<_>>())` + `js_object_alloc_with_shape` + `js_object_set_field` (replaces perry-stdlib's `js_object_alloc(0, n)` no-shape pattern). Result wrapper builds `{ rows, fields, rowCount, command }` shape; field-def objects are `{ name, dataTypeID, tableID }`. Param-array extraction handles `Null` / `String` / `Int` / `Number` / `Bool`; BigInt deferred (perry-ffi BigInt surface is in place from v0.5.556 but the JS array iteration shape needs an extra adapter — followup once a wrapper actually demands it). `dispatch`-style helper not used here because each method's `get_handle_mut` + connection-or-error logic is structurally different (pre-connect / connected / pool / pool-pending). perry-stdlib's `database-postgres` umbrella retained for backwards-compat but flipped to `["bundled-pg"]`; new `bundled-pg = ["dep:sqlx", "async-runtime"]` per-binding gate is what the well-known flip toggles. perry-stdlib's pg + mysql2 modules share the sqlx dep; for now both compile when EITHER `bundled-pg` OR `database-mysql` is on (mysql2 keeps its existing umbrella until its own port lands). `optimized_libs::build_optimized_libs` re-asserts `async-runtime` when stripping `bundled-pg` (joins the existing async-wrapper allowlist) so perry-stdlib's perry_ffi_async.rs shim still compiles. 5 unit tests cover PgConfig defaults / to_url with and without database / SELECT-vs-INSERT classifier / client_new returns a non-zero handle. Brings the in-tree wrapper port count to 26.


## v0.5.565 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 25 — port ioredis): new `crates/perry-ext-ioredis/` ports the npm `ioredis` Redis client. 18 `js_ioredis_*` exports — full surface (`new` constructor sync; `connect` / `set` / `setex` / `get` / `del` / `exists` / `incr` / `decr` / `expire` / `ping` / `hget` / `hset` / `hgetall` / `hdel` / `hlen` / `quit` async via `spawn_blocking + JsPromise + tokio::Handle::current().block_on`; `disconnect` sync). Lazy connection: `MultiplexedConnection` cached per handle in a global `HashMap` (matches perry-stdlib's existing copy). Env-var-driven URL construction (`REDIS_HOST` / `REDIS_PORT` / `REDIS_PASSWORD` / `REDIS_TLS`) preserved verbatim. Internal `dispatch<F, Fut, T>(handle, op_label, op)` helper takes ownership of the connection-acquisition + 10s timeout + result-mapping boilerplate, so each command function is two lines: read the args, call dispatch with a closure that runs the redis op. The new `ToJsValue` trait converts Redis return types (`()`, `i64`, `Option<String>`, `String`, `bool`) into `JsValue` for promise resolution — `()` → `"OK"` string per ioredis semantics, `Option<String>` → `JsValue::NULL` on None. `hgetall` returns a dynamically-keyed object built via `build_object_shape(&keys)` + `js_object_alloc_with_shape` + `js_object_set_field` (replaces perry-stdlib's `js_object_alloc(0, n)` no-shape pattern; observable behavior is identical since user code accesses fields through dynamic property lookup). perry-stdlib's `database-redis` umbrella retained for backwards-compat but flipped to `["bundled-ioredis"]`; new `bundled-ioredis = ["dep:redis", "async-runtime"]` per-binding gate is what the well-known flip toggles. `optimized_libs::build_optimized_libs` re-asserts `async-runtime` when stripping `bundled-ioredis` (joins the existing async-wrapper allowlist) so perry-stdlib's `perry_ffi_async.rs` shim still compiles. Brings the in-tree wrapper port count to 25.


## v0.5.564 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 24 — port cron + clear external-fetch blocker): two related landings.

First, the rogue `.claude/worktrees/agent-*` submodule gitlinks (mode 160000) that perry's index has carried since v0.4.23 without a matching `.gitmodules` were untracked. Cargo's `git = "https://github.com/PerryTS/perry"` fetch from external bindings repos failed with `failed to update submodule .claude/worktrees/agent-a1599b8e — no URL configured for submodule`. With those entries removed and pushed, `PerryTS/tursodb-bindings` and `PerryTS/iroh-bindings` (and any future external wrapper) `cargo build` cleanly against the live `main` branch. `docs/src/native-libraries/abi.md` updated to document the v0.5.x git-URL approach as the supported consumption mechanism + the v0.6.0 plan to invert type ownership and unblock crates.io publication.

Second, new `crates/perry-ext-cron/` ports the npm `node-cron` scheduler. 14 `js_cron_*` exports — full surface (`validate` / `schedule` / `job_start` / `job_stop` / `job_is_running` / `next_date` / `next_dates` / `describe` for user-facing API; `timer_tick` / `timer_has_pending` for the codegen event-loop hooks; plus the 4 `set_interval` / `clear_interval` / `set_timeout` / `clear_timeout` symbol-surface placeholders matching perry-stdlib's no-op behavior). Architecture parallels perry-stdlib: global `CRON_TIMERS` queue + main-thread `js_cron_timer_tick` walks expired entries and invokes `JsClosure::call0` + GC root scanner pins each callback closure pointer so a malloc-triggered sweep between schedule and tick can't free it. Codegen calls `js_cron_timer_tick` and `js_cron_timer_has_pending` by symbol name from `module_init.rs`, so as long as perry-ext-cron's `.a` exports them, the well-known flip can swap perry-stdlib's copy out without touching codegen. perry-stdlib's `scheduler` umbrella retained for backwards-compat but flipped to `["bundled-cron"]`; new `bundled-cron = ["dep:cron", "dep:tokio-cron-scheduler", "async-runtime"]` per-binding gate is what the well-known flip toggles. `module_to_features("cron"|"node-cron") => &["bundled-cron"]`. 5 unit tests cover validate (5-field + 6-field + garbage), schedule-then-stop round-trip, describe rendering. Brings the in-tree wrapper port count to 24.


## v0.5.563 — Refs #469 (status audit: unified plan harmonious end-to-end as of v0.5.562): comprehensive cross-phase audit confirming the v0.5.528 schema in `perry-api-manifest::ApiEntry` IS the foundation Phases B / C / E all reuse, with no schema drift between phases. Phase B (HIR `module_has_symbol` from two sites — standalone access in `lower/expr_member.rs`, chained calls in `lower/expr_call.rs` after the v0.5.562 fix), Phase C (`emit_markdown` / `emit_dts` walk `API_MANIFEST` directly), Phase E (`resolve.rs::parse_native_library_manifest` parses the same conceptual shape from `package.json`'s `perry.nativeLibrary` block; `abi_version` flows through the same `ApiEntry::abi_version` field). The `source` enum's `Stdlib → WellKnown → External` migration path works without changing user-visible behavior — the unimplemented-API check still fires, the docs serializer still emits, the linker still finds the symbol regardless of which arm sources the entry. Audit posted as a comment on #469. Closure status: #463 / #464 / #465 closed; #424 / #425 closed (external repos); #466 still open pending Phase 5 cleanup (5 wrapper batches remain: `ws` / DB drivers / `fetch` / `fastify` / misc — plus the `PERRY_USE_WELL_KNOWN=1` opt-in flip and the perry-stdlib defaults shrink).

## v0.5.562 — Closes #463 (compile-time error for unimplemented Node / Web APIs — chained-call leak fix): the v0.5.528 unimplemented-API gate fired for standalone property accesses (`const x = crypto.subtle;` errored cleanly) but missed chained method calls (`crypto.subtle.encrypt(...)` compiled cleanly and silently returned undefined). Root cause: the `module.Class.staticMethod()` arm in `crates/perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs` pattern-matches the AST shape directly and short-circuits the chain into a single `NativeMethodCall` HIR variant, never recursing through `lower_member` where the gate lived. Without recursion the gate never inspected the inner `crypto.subtle` access, so `Expr::NativeMethodCall { module: "crypto", class_name: Some("subtle"), method: "encrypt" }` flowed through to codegen, which silently failed to find a handler and emitted `undefined`. Fix is a parallel manifest check at the same site: before constructing the `NativeMethodCall`, consult `perry_api_manifest::module_has_symbol(module_name, &class_name)` and bail with the same `(#463)` diagnostic when the manifest knows about the module but doesn't list the class/sub-namespace. Same `PERRY_ALLOW_UNIMPLEMENTED=1` escape hatch as the lower_member gate. The 4 sub-namespaces in the existing exclude list (`fs.promises` / `fs.constants` / `path.posix` / `path.win32`) keep working — they're allowed-listed before the manifest check fires. End-to-end smoke verified: `crypto.subtle.{generateKey, encrypt, foo}` and `await crypto.subtle.encrypt(...)` all error correctly; `crypto.randomBytes(16)` (legitimate API) still compiles. All perry-hir + perry-api-manifest + perry-codegen unit tests pass. Closes the last unchecked #463 acceptance criterion ("`crypto.subtle` reference produces a compile error pointing at the source line" — for the chained-call form).

## v0.5.561 — Refs #465 (stretch goal — ship the auto-generated `.d.ts` to user editors): `perry types` now writes the auto-generated stdlib `.d.ts` to `.perry/types/stdlib/index.d.ts` alongside the hand-curated `perry/{ui,thread,i18n,system,media,tui}` stubs. The `docs/api/perry.d.ts` artifact is `include_str!`-embedded into perry, so the binary always carries a current snapshot — the v0.5.560 drift gate guarantees the embedded copy matches the manifest. `perry init`'s default tsconfig.json picks it up via `include: ["src", ".perry/types/stdlib/index.d.ts"]`, so user code that imports `dotenv` / `crypto` / `mysql2` / etc. gets editor squiggles in VSCode / WebStorm before the user runs `perry`. Together with v0.5.560's drift gate, this closes the entire #465 acceptance list — including the "Stretch — `.d.ts` emitted alongside, opt-in via package config" item, which v0.5.561 makes opt-out (default-on for `perry init` projects) since the cost of carrying it is just one `include_str!` at compile time.

## v0.5.560 — Closes #465 (release-workflow drift gate for the auto-generated API docs): closes the last unchecked acceptance criterion of #465 — "Release workflow regenerates docs automatically (no drift from code)". New `api-docs-drift` job in `.github/workflows/test.yml` runs `./scripts/regen_api_docs.sh` on every push + PR, then `git diff --exit-code`s `docs/src/api/reference.md` and `docs/api/perry.d.ts`. If a code change updates `crates/perry-api-manifest/src/entries.rs` without re-committing the artifacts, the job fails with an explicit fix recipe (`./scripts/regen_api_docs.sh && git add ... && git commit ...`) and a stat-summary + first 200 diff lines so reviewers see immediately what drifted. Caught 45 lines of staleness on first run (artifacts were last regenerated at v0.5.529; tursodb queryAll/queryOne, iroh peer-conn surface, and a handful of new perry-stdlib bindings had landed since). Regenerated `docs/src/api/reference.md` (now 397 entries across 45 modules, was 378/43) and `docs/api/perry.d.ts` (auto-stamped with `Perry version: 0.5.560`). The flow now: PR touches the manifest → CI re-runs the regen → drift gate fails until the PR also commits the regenerated artifacts → reviewer sees the doc diff alongside the code diff. With the drift gate in place, the docs stay in sync without depending on the maintainer remembering to run a script.

## v0.5.559 — Refs #466 (Phase 3 — `perry native` CLI scaffolding): new `perry native init` / `validate` / `list` subcommands drop the barrier to writing a native-bindings wrapper from "hours of archaeology in Bloom's source" to "minutes of init+validate". `perry native init <name>` scaffolds a new wrapper package — package.json (perry.nativeLibrary block with abiVersion + functions[] + per-target build config), src/index.ts (TypeScript surface), Cargo.toml (perry-ffi git dep + user's upstream crate), src/lib.rs (one example #[no_mangle] entry that reads/echoes a string), README, MIT LICENSE, .gitignore, and a multi-target `.github/workflows/release.yml` that prebuilds staticlibs for x86_64 + aarch64 macOS/Linux + Windows on tag and attaches them to the GH release. `perry native validate` parses package.json, runs `cargo build --release` (skippable with `--no-build`), locates the resulting `.a` / `.lib` / `.dylib` / `.so`, walks `nm -gP` output for defined `js_*` symbols, and diffs against the manifest's `functions[].name` array — reports missing-from-staticlib (❌, exits 1) and undeclared-in-manifest (⚠) lists. `perry native list` enumerates the well-known table (text + JSON formats); JSON includes package / crate / lib / tracking issue. Templates ship as `include_str!` blobs from `crates/perry/templates/native-package/` so `perry native` works without any external resource lookups. End-to-end smoke verified: `init` produces a directory that `cargo build --release` accepts (modulo the user adding their upstream Rust crate dep), and `validate` correctly diffs declared-vs-exported on a fixture. Together with the v0.5.557 external-repo move (tursodb + iroh) and the v0.5.532-onwards well-known table, this completes #466's Phase 3 — the developer-experience tooling story external wrapper authors needed. **Open questions still outstanding:** prebuilt artifact distribution (npm postinstall vs vendor in tarball — tracked separately), `perry-ffi` publication to crates.io.

## v0.5.558 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 23 — port nodemailer): new `crates/perry-ext-nodemailer/` ports the npm `nodemailer` SMTP transport package via `lettre`. 3 `js_nodemailer_*` exports — `createTransport` (sync, parses nested SMTP config object via `js_object_get_field` indexed lookups: host=0, port=1, secure=2, auth={user=0, pass=1}=3 — same shape-ordered layout perry-runtime emits at the call site), `sendMail` (async via `spawn_blocking` + `JsPromise` + `tokio::Handle::current().block_on`, builds `lettre::Message` from the mail-options object's `from`/`to`/`subject`/`text`/`html` fields, resolves with an info object built via `build_object_shape(&["messageId","response"])` + `js_object_alloc_with_shape` + `js_object_set_field`), `verify` (async, tests SMTP connection). The async exports take options as `f64` (NaN-boxed JsValue at the FFI boundary) to avoid SysV AMD64 ABI mismatches with `JsValue`-typed args — same trick perry-stdlib uses. perry-stdlib's `email = ["dep:lettre", "async-runtime"]` umbrella retained for backwards-compat but flipped to `["bundled-nodemailer"]`; new `bundled-nodemailer = ["dep:lettre", "async-runtime"]` per-binding gate is what the well-known flip toggles. `optimized_libs::build_optimized_libs` re-asserts `async-runtime` when stripping `bundled-nodemailer` (joins the existing `bundled-bcrypt` / `bundled-argon2` async-wrapper match) so perry-stdlib's `perry_ffi_async.rs` shim still compiles after the flip. 3 unit tests cover SMTP-config defaults, handle round-trip, null-config fallback. Brings the in-tree wrapper port count to 23.

## v0.5.557 — Refs #466 (move tursodb + iroh to external repos, validating the third-party-wrapper hypothesis end-to-end): the perry-ext-tursodb (#424) and perry-ext-iroh (#425) wrappers are no longer in the perry monorepo. They moved to standalone third-party repositories at https://github.com/PerryTS/tursodb-bindings (`bun add @perryts/tursodb`) and https://github.com/PerryTS/iroh-bindings (`bun add @perryts/iroh`). Both ship a Cargo.toml that depends on `perry-ffi = { git = "https://github.com/PerryTS/perry", branch = "main" }`, the existing src/lib.rs unchanged, a `package.json` with a full `perry.nativeLibrary` block (abiVersion `"0.5"` + `functions[]` declarations + per-target `cargo_features`/`frameworks`/etc.), a README with usage examples, and an MIT LICENSE. perry's compile-time resolver already walks `node_modules/<pkg>/package.json` for `perry.nativeLibrary` blocks (see `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/resolve.rs` + `collect_modules.rs`), so user code that does `import * as tursodb from "tursodb"` after a `bun add @perryts/tursodb` Just Works without any well-known-table magic. perry workspace surgery: removed `crates/perry-ext-tursodb/` and `crates/perry-ext-iroh/` directories; removed both from `[workspace.members]` / `[workspace.default-members]` / `[workspace.dependencies]` in the root Cargo.toml; removed `[bindings.tursodb]` and `[bindings.iroh]` from `well_known_bindings.toml` (with a doc comment explaining why and pointing readers at the external repos); kept `module_to_features("tursodb"|"iroh") => &[]` in `stdlib_features.rs` (no perry-stdlib feature gate to manage either way); kept the API_MANIFEST entries (the API surface description still applies — it's just sourced externally now). `cargo build --release -p perry-stdlib -p perry` clean post-removal; all 6 well-known tests pass (including `every_entry_references_a_workspace_crate` — no entries reference removed crates anymore). Wrapper port count drops to 22 in-tree, plus 2 external. The 3 wrappers that originated as net-new functionality outside perry-stdlib (#424 tursodb, #425 iroh) now live in their natural home; the 22 wrappers that port existing perry-stdlib code stay in the monorepo as the "well-known" set the compiler ships out of the box.

## v0.5.556 — Refs #466 (Phase 1.6 + Phase 5 step 22 — perry-ffi BigInt+Buffer surface, port ethers): perry-ffi gains its 8th + 9th surface dimensions. New `crates/perry-ffi/src/bigint.rs` re-exports `BigIntHeader` + `BIGINT_LIMBS` from perry-runtime, declares `js_bigint_from_string` extern, and exposes safe `alloc_bigint_from_str(&str) -> *mut BigIntHeader` + `read_bigint_limbs(ptr) -> Option<[u64; 16]>`. New `crates/perry-ffi/src/buffer.rs` re-exports `BufferHeader`, exposes `alloc_buffer(&[u8]) -> *mut BufferHeader` (calls perry-runtime's `buffer_alloc` + sets length + memcpy's the bytes) + `read_buffer_bytes(ptr) -> Option<&[u8]>`. Both modules' types are part of the perry-ffi semver promise — runtime renumbering bumps perry-ffi major. 7 new unit tests cover BigInt round-trip / zero / null + Buffer round-trip / empty / null / non-UTF-8 binary bytes. New `crates/perry-ext-ethers/` ports the npm `ethers` utility surface — 8 `js_ethers_*` + `js_keccak256_native` / `js_keccak256_native_bytes` exports — full surface (getAddress / Wallet.createRandom / parseEther / formatEther / parseUnits / formatUnits / keccak256_native / keccak256_native_bytes). The `Wallet.createRandom` constructor uses perry-ffi's `build_object_shape(&["address","privateKey"]) + js_object_alloc_with_shape + js_object_set_field` — same shape construction pattern commander/sharp/etc use. Hand-rolled keccak256 implementation (same as perry-stdlib's) — no extra crypto crate dep. perry-stdlib's `pub mod ethers` flipped to `bundled-ethers` feature gate (default-on through `crypto`); module_to_features("ethers") now returns `&["bundled-ethers"]` instead of the broader `crypto` umbrella since the keccak256 dep is hand-rolled. 4 unit tests cover keccak256 known vector, EIP-55 reference vector (Vitalik's address), format-with-decimals basic, parse-units round-trip. Brings the wrapper port count to 24 + future BigInt-using wrappers (postgres int8, mongodb decimal128, web3.js, ethereum-cryptography) unblocked.

## v0.5.555 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 21 — port commander): new `crates/perry-ext-commander/` ports the npm `commander` CLI parser. 15 `js_commander_*` exports — full surface: fluent setters (`name` / `description` / `version` / `option` / `requiredOption` / `command` / `action`), `parse` entry that dispatches to `.action(opts => ...)` after binding `--flag value` / `-f value` / `--flag=value` / `--flag` (boolean) / positional args / subcommands, post-parse query accessors (`opts` / `getOption` / `getOptionNumber` / `getOptionBool` / `argsCount` / `getArg`), automatic `--help` / `-h` and `--version` / `-V` (process::exit(0) on hit). Replaces perry-stdlib's `js_object_alloc(0, count)` + `js_object_set_field_by_name` with perry-ffi's shape-aware `build_object_shape(&keys)` + `js_object_alloc_with_shape` + `js_object_set_field` (by index in the shape) — same observable behavior since user code accesses `options.port` through dynamic property lookup either way. GC root scanner registered via `iter_handles_of::<CommanderHandle> + gc_register_root_scanner` so `.action(cb)` closures stay alive across malloc-triggered GC cycles between `.action()` registration and `.parse()` invocation. `closure.call1(opts_value.bits())` invokes the user's action via perry-ffi's closure surface. 5 unit tests cover flag-spec parsing (long+short / boolean / optional-value variants), fluent-setter round-trip, option registration. perry-stdlib's `pub mod commander` flipped to `#[cfg(feature = "bundled-commander")]`; default-on through `full`. Brings the wrapper port count to 23.

## v0.5.554 — Refs #425 (iroh peer-connection + stream surface): perry-ext-iroh gains the full request-response peer-to-peer surface — `connect` / `acceptOne` / `openBi` / `acceptBi` / `streamWrite` / `streamFinish` / `streamReadToEnd` / `connClose`. Server flow: `bind() -> acceptOne() -> acceptBi() -> streamReadToEnd() + streamWrite() + streamFinish()`. Client flow: `bind() -> connect(serverNodeId) -> openBi() -> streamWrite() + streamFinish() + streamReadToEnd()`. ALPN is hardcoded to `b"perry-iroh/0"` for v0 — `js_iroh_bind` switched from `Endpoint::bind(presets::N0)` to `Endpoint::builder(presets::N0).alpns(vec![PERRY_IROH_ALPN.to_vec()]).bind()` so the same endpoint can serve both client and server roles. New handle types: `IrohConnection` (wraps `iroh::endpoint::Connection<HandshakeCompleted>`), `IrohBiStream` (wraps `(SendStream, RecvStream)` in a pair of `tokio::sync::Mutex`es so the async `write_all` / `read_to_end` / `finish` calls can hold a lock across awaits without deadlocking). `js_iroh_conn_close` follows the take_handle pattern from `js_iroh_close` so closing is idempotent. `EndpointId::from_str` parses the base32-hex node id strings users share. `streamReadToEnd` returns the bytes UTF-8-decoded as a JS string (binary-bytes payload variant deferred — needs the same JsValue::from_object_ptr trick zlib uses). API_MANIFEST gains 8 new method entries so #463's check accepts user code that calls them. Together with v0.5.553's tursodb queryAll/queryOne extension, this completes the meaningful-implementation pass on both #424 and #425. Followups: per-call ALPN strings (we hardcode v0), connection-event callbacks (closure invocation already shipped in v0.5.542; just needs an `on()` surface), broadcast-style fan-out across connections.

## v0.5.553 — Refs #424 (tursodb queryAll / queryOne — close the row-object gap): perry-ext-tursodb gains `js_turso_query_all` and `js_turso_query_one` so user code can `await db.queryAll("SELECT * FROM users")` and get back `Array<{ id, name, email, … }>` row-objects keyed by column name. Implementation walks `Statement::columns()` for the column-name list, calls `build_object_shape(&[name1, name2, …])` to get the (packed_keys, shape_id) pair perry-ffi v0.5 wants, then for each row builds the object with `js_object_alloc_with_shape` and fills fields by index using `js_object_set_field`. Values map turso `Null` → `JsValue::NULL`, `Integer` (i32-fitting) → `from_int32`, `Real`/large-Integer → `from_number`, `Text` → `from_string_ptr` over a fresh `alloc_string`, `Blob` → lowercase-hex `from_string_ptr` (matches perry-stdlib sqlite.rs convention to avoid pulling the `hex` crate into the link). All shape construction runs inside `spawn_blocking` so the main thread stays responsive while turso fetches — `JsValue` is `repr(transparent) u64` and `Send`, so resolving the promise from the worker thread crosses the boundary cleanly. API_MANIFEST gains `tursodb.queryAll` / `queryOne` so #463's check accepts user code that calls them. Param-binding (`?` placeholders) is the next gap — needs a JS `Array<JsValue>` reader on the perry-ffi side that maps onto turso's `IntoParams`. Closes #424's row-object construction TODO from v0.5.543.

## v0.5.552 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 20 — port rate-limiter-flexible): new `crates/perry-ext-ratelimit/` ports the npm `rate-limiter-flexible` token-bucket rate-limiting package via `governor`. 11 `js_ratelimit_*` exports — 2 sync constructors (`new` / `new_keyed`), 6 async (`consume` / `get` / `delete` / `block` / `penalty` / `reward`) bridged through `spawn_blocking` + `JsPromise`, 3 sync (`check` / `remaining` / `reset`). All async exports return JSON-encoded result strings (same format as perry-stdlib's existing copy: `remainingPoints` / `msBeforeNext` / `consumedPoints` / `isFirstInDuration`). `rate-limit` umbrella feature retained for backwards-compat but flipped to `["bundled-ratelimit"]`; new `bundled-ratelimit = ["dep:governor", "async-runtime"]` per-binding gate is what the well-known flip toggles. New stdlib_features mapping `"rate-limiter-flexible" => &["bundled-ratelimit"]`. 5 unit tests cover handle creation, check passes when under quota, remaining for unknown key, reset clears keyed limiters, invalid-handle returns permissive true. Brings the wrapper port count to 22.

## v0.5.551 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 19 — port sharp): new `crates/perry-ext-sharp/` ports the npm `sharp` image-processing package — image transforms via the `image` crate. 18 `js_sharp_*` exports — full surface (`fromFile` / `fromBuffer` constructors; `resize` / `rotate` / `flip` / `flop` / `grayscale` / `blur` / `sharpen` / `crop` sync transforms; `jpeg` / `png` / `webp` format selectors; `toFile` / `toBuffer` / `metadata` async exports via spawn_blocking + JsPromise; `width` / `height` accessors). The 3 async exports use the same `JsPromise::new() + spawn_blocking { promise.resolve_string(...) | promise.reject_string(...) }` pattern as bcrypt/argon2 — image processing is CPU-bound, not async-Rust, so spawn_blocking is the right bridge. `read_buf` (perry-ffi's `read_bytes` wrapper) handles the binary-bytes path for `fromBuffer` since image payloads aren't UTF-8. perry-stdlib's `image` umbrella feature retained for backwards-compat but flipped to `["bundled-sharp"]`; new `bundled-sharp = ["dep:image"]` per-binding gate is what the well-known flip toggles. 6 unit tests cover width/height, resize-with-aspect-ratio, rotate-90 dimension swap, jpeg quality setting, invalid-handle return paths. Brings the wrapper port count to 21.

## v0.5.550 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 18 — port cheerio): new `crates/perry-ext-cheerio/` ports the npm `cheerio` HTML-parsing package — jQuery-like traversal via the `scraper` crate. 19 `js_cheerio_*` exports — full surface (`load` / `loadFragment` for document creation; `select` / `find` / `children` / `parent` / `first` / `last` / `eq` for traversal; `text` / `html` / `attr` / `length` for accessors; `hasClass` / `is` for predicates; `toArray` / `texts` / `attrs` for array-returns). Uses perry-ffi v0.5 surface only — `register_handle` for CheerioHandle / CheerioSelectionHandle (handle stores html + selector strings, not Html parse trees, since `Html` isn't `Send`); `js_array_alloc` / `js_array_push` / `JsValue::from_string_ptr` for the array-returning variants. perry-stdlib's `html-parser` umbrella feature retained for backwards-compat but flipped to `["bundled-cheerio"]`; new `bundled-cheerio = ["dep:scraper"]` per-binding gate is what the well-known flip toggles. 5 unit tests cover load + select + text, length, attr, hasClass, first traversal. Brings the wrapper port count to 20.

## v0.5.549 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 17 — port moment): new `crates/perry-ext-moment/` ports the npm `moment` package — momentjs-compatible date manipulation via chrono. 28 `js_moment_*` exports — full surface (now/from_timestamp/parse + format/toISOString + valueOf/unix + year/month/date/day/hour/minute/second/millisecond + add/subtract/startOf/endOf/diff + isBefore/isAfter/isSame/isBetween/isValid + clone/fromNow/toDate). MomentHandle carries `is_valid` flag (preserved from perry-stdlib's existing copy) so `moment("garbage").isValid()` returns false instead of throwing. Boolean predicates return NaN-boxed `TAG_TRUE` (0x7FFC_..._4) / `TAG_FALSE` (0x7FFC_..._3) f64s — same ABI as perry-stdlib so codegen-side dispatch keeps working unchanged. perry-stdlib's `pub mod moment` flipped to `#[cfg(feature = "bundled-moment")]`; default-on through `full`. 7 unit tests cover now / round-trip / add-subtract / comparison predicates / clone / parse-valid / parse-garbage. Brings the wrapper port count to 19.

## v0.5.548 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 16 — port dayjs / date-fns): new `crates/perry-ext-dayjs/` ports the npm `dayjs` and `date-fns` date-manipulation packages. Same shape as decimal (sync, handle-based, chrono under the hood) — 24 `js_dayjs_*` exports (now/from_timestamp/parse + format/toISOString + valueOf/unix/year/month/date/day/hour/minute/second/millisecond + add/subtract/startOf/endOf/diff + isBefore/isAfter/isSame/isValid) + 11 `js_datefns_*` exports (format/parseISO + addDays/Months/Years + differenceInDays/Hours/Minutes + isAfter/isBefore + startOfDay/endOfDay). Uses perry-ffi v0.5 surface only: `register_handle` / `get_handle` for DayjsHandle wrapping `chrono::DateTime<Utc>`, `alloc_string` / `read_string` for the format-pattern + ISO-string round-trip, `Handle` ↔ `f64` bit-cast via the same trick perry-stdlib uses (NaN-boxed handle as f64 return). chrono is already an always-on perry-stdlib dep so the `bundled-dayjs` feature gate just toggles whether `pub mod dayjs;` compiles in (default-on through `full`; the well-known flip strips it). Both `dayjs` and `date-fns` route to the same wrapper. 6 unit tests cover now / from_timestamp round-trip / parse-ISO / add-subtract-days / comparison predicates / datefns difference. Brings the wrapper port count to 18.

## v0.5.547 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 15 — port decimal): new `crates/perry-ext-decimal/` ports the npm `decimal.js` / `bignumber.js` arbitrary-precision arithmetic packages. 30 FFI exports — full surface (from_number/from_string + plus/minus/times/div/mod/pow/sqrt + abs/neg/round/floor/ceil + toFixed/toString/toNumber + eq/lt/lte/gt/gte/cmp/isZero/isPositive/isNegative + per-binop number variants). Same dispatch as perry-stdlib's existing copy: `rust_decimal` under the hood, divide-by-zero returns ZERO (perry-stdlib convention), pow/sqrt fall back to f64 round-trip since rust_decimal lacks them. Macros (`binop_handle!` / `binop_number!`) dedupe the basic arithmetic operators. Sync, no closures, no GC scanner — fits perry-ffi v0.5 string + handle surface exactly. perry-stdlib's `rust_decimal` flipped to `optional = true`; new `bundled-decimal = ["dep:rust_decimal"]` feature gates `pub mod decimal;`. Both `decimal.js` and `bignumber.js` npm names route to the same wrapper. 4 unit tests cover arithmetic round-trip, comparison predicates (lt/gt/eq/cmp), divide-by-zero handling, toFixed rounding. Brings the wrapper port count to 17.

## v0.5.546 — Refs #466 (Phase 1.5 + Phase 5 step 14 — GC root scanner surface, port events): perry-ffi gains its 7th surface dimension. New `iter_handles_of<T, F>` walks every registered handle whose stored type matches `T`; new `gc_register_root_scanner(scanner)` is a thin re-export of perry-runtime's existing `extern "C"` GC scanner registration. Wrappers that store user closures (or any other GC-managed pointer) inside their handles need the scanner pair to keep those values alive across malloc-triggered GC cycles — without it, a GC between `.on(cb)` and `.emit()` sweeps `cb` (issue #35 pattern). New `crates/perry-ext-events/` ports Node's `events` module — full EventEmitter pattern (`new` / `on` / `emit` (no-args + 1-arg) / `removeListener` / `removeAllListeners` / `listenerCount`). Closures are stored as raw i64 pointers (Send + Sync — same trick perry-stdlib uses); the GC scanner reconstructs each pointer as a POINTER_TAG-tagged f64 and forwards to the runtime's `mark` callback. perry-stdlib's `pub mod events` becomes feature-gated (`bundled-events = []`, default-on through `full`). 4 unit tests cover empty / add / remove-listener / remove-all flows. Brings the wrapper port count to 16. cron + net + future wrappers that need GC-scanner integration unlocked by this commit.

## v0.5.545 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 13 — port axios): first HTTP-client wrapper port. New `crates/perry-ext-axios/` ports the npm `axios` HTTP client using reqwest under the hood (same as perry-stdlib's existing copy). 5 method exports (get/post/put/delete/patch — all return Promise<Response>) + 3 response accessors (status / statusText / data). Uses perry-ffi v0.5.x's existing surface — no new helpers needed: `JsPromise` + `spawn_blocking` + `tokio::Handle::current().block_on` for the async bridge, `register_handle` for the AxiosResponseHandle, `JsValue::from_object_ptr` for POINTER_TAG-tagged response handles (issue #340 NaN-box trick preserved from perry-stdlib's original — without it, the awaiter sees a subnormal float that decays to undefined on `r.status` accesses). Refactored the 5 methods around a single `run_request` helper that takes a `FnOnce(reqwest::Client, String) -> reqwest::RequestBuilder` to dedupe the per-method boilerplate. `well_known_bindings.toml` gains `[bindings.axios]`. `module_to_features("axios") => &["http-client"]` (already mapped — no perry-stdlib feature split needed since `http-client` is already a per-binding gate). Brings the wrapper port count to 15.

## v0.5.544 — **Closes #425** (Iroh P2P native bindings): new `crates/perry-ext-iroh/` ports the [Iroh](https://www.iroh.computer/) Rust framework — direct peer-to-peer QUIC connections with hole punching + relay fallback. Same architecture as #424's tursodb port — uses perry-ffi v0.5.x's full surface (handle registry + spawn_blocking + JsPromise + tokio::Handle::current().block_on bridge), pulls `iroh = "0.98"` directly with no perry-stdlib involvement. MVP API surface — `iroh.bind()` → Promise<handle> using N0 relay preset (sane defaults: discovery via n0 number-DNS + n0 relay servers for hole-punch fallback), `iroh.nodeId(handle)` → Promise<string> (hex-encoded Ed25519 public key, what users share for peer connections), `iroh.close(handle)` → Promise<undefined> (graceful endpoint shutdown). Full peer-connection / stream API (open_bi / read_to_end / event callbacks) deferred to followups — needs longer-lived per-stream handles + ergonomic Promise / AsyncIterable mapping over QUIC stream semantics, not blocked on perry-ffi (closure invocation already shipped in v0.5.542). NATIVE_MODULES gains `iroh`; `well_known_bindings.toml` gains `[bindings.iroh] tracking = "#425"`. API_MANIFEST gains 3 method entries. Brings the wrapper port count to 14 — covers all original Phase 5 plan + both #424/#425 from issue #466's "external bindings ecosystem v1" mandate. Both #424 and #425 confirm the third-party-wrapper hypothesis end-to-end: Rust-crate-backed bindings outside perry-stdlib live alongside the existing wrappers without conflict, on the same well-known infrastructure.

## v0.5.543 — **Closes #424** (Tursodb native bindings): new `crates/perry-ext-tursodb/` ports the pure-Rust SQLite-compatible Tursodb engine to Perry as the first wrapper that *originated* outside perry-stdlib (i.e., not a port of an existing in-tree implementation — net-new functionality enabled by #466's bindings ecosystem). Pulls `turso = "0.2"` directly, uses perry-ffi v0.5.x's full surface (handle registry + spawn_blocking + JsPromise + JsValue). MVP API surface — `tursodb.open(filename)` → Promise<handle>, `tursodb.exec(sql)` → Promise<rowsAffected>, `tursodb.execBatch(sql)` → Promise<undefined>, `tursodb.lastInsertRowid()` (sync), `tursodb.isAutocommit()` (sync), `tursodb.close()`. Prepare / run / get / all are followups (need JsValue object construction across the spawn_blocking boundary, straightforward incremental work but not blocking). turso's API is async-first, bridged via `tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async { ... })` inside spawn_blocking closures. tursodb is the first binding accessible ONLY through the well-known path (no perry-stdlib copy exists), so `module_to_features("tursodb") => &[]` (no feature to strip; well-known table picks up by module-name match alone). NATIVE_MODULES gains `tursodb`; `well_known_bindings.toml` gains `[bindings.tursodb] tracking = "#424"`. API_MANIFEST gains 6 method entries so #463's check accepts `tursodb.*` calls. Brings the wrapper port count to 13 and validates the third-party-wrapper hypothesis end-to-end: a Rust-crate-backed binding that's NOT in perry-stdlib lives alongside the existing wrappers without conflict.

## v0.5.542 — Refs #466 (Phase 1.4 + Phase 5 step 12 — closure invocation surface, port exponential-backoff): perry-ffi gains its 6th surface dimension. New `crates/perry-ffi/src/closure.rs` exposes `JsClosure(*const ClosureHeader)` with `from_raw` / `as_raw` / `is_null` / `call0` / `call1` / `call2` / `call3` / `call4` plus a `RawClosureHeader` re-export for FFI signatures. perry-runtime's `js_closure_call0..4` are already extern "C", so perry-ffi just declares them and wraps with type safety. `JsClosure` is `Send` so wrappers can hand closures to `spawn_blocking` for retry loops or background workers. Higher-arity calls (5..8) deferred until a real wrapper demands them. New `crates/perry-ext-exponential-backoff/` ports the npm `exponential-backoff` package's `backOff(fn)` retry-with-exp-delay loop — first wrapper that exercises the closure surface. Pure-Rust, no extra deps. Carried over the trick from perry-stdlib's existing copy where `is_valid_result(f64)` distinguishes a "real" callback result (any non-NaN OR a NaN-boxed pointer/string/int32/bool) from a "treat as failure, retry" raw IEEE NaN. perry-stdlib's `pub mod exponential_backoff` becomes feature-gated (`bundled-exponential-backoff = []`, default-on through `full`). 2 unit tests cover `is_valid_result`'s tag-aware predicates and the null-closure null-return path. Brings the wrapper port count to 12. Closure surface unblocks ws + fastify + db.transaction(fn) + #425 Iroh's event callbacks.

## v0.5.541 — Refs #466 (perry-ffi binary-bytes helpers + Phase 5 step 11 — port zlib): perry-ffi gains `read_bytes(JsString) -> Option<&[u8]>` (binary view, no UTF-8 validation) and `alloc_bytes(&[u8]) -> JsString` (allocate without validation), plus `JsPromise::resolve(JsValue)` / `reject(JsValue)` for resolution paths that don't fit the string/bool/number shortcuts. New `crates/perry-ext-zlib/` ports gzipSync/gunzipSync/deflateSync/inflateSync (sync) + gzip/gunzip/deflate/inflate (async via spawn_blocking). Async results NaN-box as `JsValue::from_object_ptr` (POINTER_TAG) rather than STRING_TAG since gzip output isn't valid UTF-8 — matches the trick perry-stdlib's existing zlib uses. End-to-end smoke verified: `zlib.gzipSync` → `zlib.gunzipSync` round-trips a 60-char ASCII input through both default and well-known paths with matching output. The well-known flip strips perry-stdlib's `compression` feature (already a per-binding gate, no Cargo.toml split needed). Pre-existing `perry-runtime::compute_utf16_len` debug-mode UB check on `from_utf8_unchecked` blocks unit tests that exercise the FFI round trip with non-UTF-8 binary data — release-mode TS smoke tests cover the path correctly. Brings the wrapper port count to 11.

## v0.5.540 — Refs #466 (Phase 1.3 + Phase 5 step 10 — JSValue + object/array helpers, port better-sqlite3): perry-ffi grows its 4th surface dimension (after strings v0.5, async v0.5.1, handles v0.5.x). New `crates/perry-ffi/src/jsvalue.rs` exposes a `#[repr(transparent)]` `JsValue(u64)` with constructors (`UNDEFINED`/`NULL`/`TRUE`/`FALSE` consts plus `from_bool`/`from_number`/`from_int32`/`from_string_ptr`/`from_object_ptr`), type predicates (`is_undefined`/`is_null`/`is_bool`/`is_string`/`is_pointer`/`is_int32`/`is_number`), accessors (`to_bool`/`to_number`/`to_int32`/`as_string_ptr`/`as_pointer<T>`), and `bits()`/`from_bits()` for round-tripping through promise resolutions. NaN-box tag values (TAG_UNDEFINED/NULL/FALSE/TRUE, STRING_TAG, POINTER_TAG, INT32_TAG, masks) are now part of perry-ffi's stable contract — runtime renumbering bumps perry-ffi major. Also exports `extern "C"` declarations for `js_array_alloc`/`js_array_push`/`js_array_get`/`js_array_set` and `js_object_alloc_with_shape`/`js_object_get_field`/`js_object_set_field` (the runtime symbols are already stable extern "C" exports — perry-ffi just declares them with `JsValue` instead of `JSValue`). Re-exports `ArrayHeader` + `ObjectHeader` for FFI signatures. New `build_object_shape(keys: &[&str]) -> (Vec<u8>, u32)` helper produces the (packed_keys, shape_id) pair the runtime's `js_object_alloc_with_shape` expects — `0x4646_0000` ("FF" prefix) namespaces perry-ffi-built shapes from perry-stdlib's `0x5143_0000` ("SQ"). 6 unit tests cover constants, number / int32 round trip, type predicate disjointness, shape-hash determinism, packed-keys format. New `crates/perry-ext-better-sqlite3/` (12 FFI exports — open/exec/prepare/run/get/all/pragma/begin/commit/rollback/close/inTransaction; transaction-with-closure deferred to the closure-invocation expansion) is the acceptance test for the new surface; uses `rusqlite = "0.32"` with `bundled` feature for static SQLite C source link. Caught a pre-existing perry-stdlib bug while porting (`db.pragma('user_version')` returned `""` instead of `"0"` because integer columns can't be `row.get::<String>()` — fix in this port tries String → i64 → f64 in order). 5 unit tests in perry-ext-better-sqlite3 + end-to-end TS smoke that creates a 3-row users table, queries with ORDER BY, prints rows + count. Both default and well-known paths produce identical output. **First database wrapper port — also the canonical reference for #424 (Tursodb): `perryts/tursodb-bindings` will look almost identical with `rusqlite::Connection` swapped for `tursodb::Connection`.**

## v0.5.539 — Refs #466 (Phase 1.2 + Phase 5 step 9 — handle registry surface, port lru-cache): perry-ffi grows a 3rd surface dimension after strings (v0.5) and async (v0.5.1) — handles. New `crates/perry-ffi/src/handle.rs` exposes a generic `register_handle<T>` / `get_handle<T>` / `with_handle<T>` / `with_handle_mut<T>` / `take_handle<T>` / `drop_handle` / `handle_exists` API plus `pub type Handle = i64` and `INVALID_HANDLE = 0`. Storage is a process-wide `DashMap<i64, Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>>` with `OnceCell`-lazy init; counter starts at 1 so `0` is reserved as the JS-falsy null sentinel. perry-ffi gets `dashmap = "6"` and `once_cell = "1"` deps (already in the link surface via perry-stdlib's transitive deps — no binary growth). Generics are Rust-side (the registry can't go through extern "C" because each `T` monomorphizes per call site); the `pub static HANDLES` symbol from perry-ffi's rlib gets linked once, so all wrapper crates that link perry-ffi share the same registry. Wrapper crates use private newtypes (`pub struct LruCacheHandle { ... }`) to namespace their entries within the shared map — type-id-based downcast in `with_handle_mut::<MyHandle>` ensures handles never leak across wrappers. perry-stdlib's `crates/perry-stdlib/src/common/handle.rs` keeps its OWN copy of the same registry; the two are separate integer spaces (the well-known flip means only one is used per program, so no collision). New `crates/perry-ext-lru-cache/` (8 FFI exports — new/get/set/has/delete/clear/size/peek) is the acceptance test; uses `lru = "0.16"` directly. perry-stdlib's `lru` dep flipped to `optional = true`; new `bundled-lru-cache = ["dep:lru"]` feature gates `pub mod lru_cache;`. `default = ["full"]` rolls in `bundled-lru-cache`. `stdlib_features::module_to_features("lru-cache") => &["bundled-lru-cache"]`. `well_known_bindings.toml` adds the `[bindings.lru-cache]` entry. End-to-end smoke verified: identical TS output across default and well-known paths (`size: 3`, correct LRU eviction order). 6 unit tests in perry-ext-lru-cache cover basic round trip, eviction-at-max-size, delete+clear, peek-doesn't-bump-recency, missing-key-returns-NaN, invalid-handle-is-no-op. Brings the wrapper port count to 9 (dotenv, nanoid, uuid, slugify, bcrypt, argon2, jsonwebtoken, validator, lru-cache); remaining Phase 5 wrappers (ws, db drivers, fetch/axios, fastify) need additional perry-ffi surface (closure invocation + JSValue object/array helpers) — separate followups.

## v0.5.538 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 steps 7+8 — port jsonwebtoken + validator): two more sync, string-only wrappers join the well-known path. New `crates/perry-ext-jsonwebtoken/` (5 FFI exports — sign, sign_es256, sign_rs256, verify, decode) uses jsonwebtoken 10.3 with `default-features = false, features = ["rust_crypto", "use_pem"]` — explicit crypto-backend selection is required from jsonwebtoken 10.x onwards (default panics with "Could not automatically determine the process-level CryptoProvider"; docs not loud about this). New `crates/perry-ext-validator/` (15 FFI exports covering isEmail/isURL/isUUID/isAlpha/isAlphanumeric/isNumeric/isInt/isFloat/isHexadecimal/isEmpty/isJSON/isLength/contains/equals/isLowercase/isUppercase). `OnceLock`-cached UUID regex inside isUUID since the regex compiles to ~1KB+ and the original perry-stdlib copy paid that cost on every call. perry-stdlib's `crypto` umbrella forks `bundled-jsonwebtoken = ["dep:jsonwebtoken"]` (umbrella keeps it transitively for backwards compat); `validation` forks `bundled-validator = ["dep:validator", "dep:regex"]` (validation umbrella expands to bundled-validator). `stdlib_features::module_to_features` dispatches per-binding for both. `well_known_bindings.toml` gains both entries. End-to-end smoke verified: identical output across default and well-known paths. JWT-encoded token printing `eyJ` prefix mismatch (default + well-known both show `false`) is a pre-existing `NATIVE_MODULE_TABLE` dispatch issue unrelated to this port — `validator.isEmail`/`isURL` outputs match expected behavior on both paths. 8 unit tests across the two crates (3 jwt + 5 validator).

## v0.5.537 — Refs #466 (Phase 1.1 + Phase 5 steps 5+6 — async surface for perry-ffi, port bcrypt + argon2): closes the issue's "Open question 5" by extending perry-ffi with `JsPromise`, `spawn_blocking`, `nanbox_string_bits`, and a `Promise` re-export, then ports the two password-hashing wrappers as the contract test. Surface delivery: new `crates/perry-stdlib/src/perry_ffi_async.rs` exposes four `#[no_mangle] extern "C"` shims (`perry_ffi_promise_new`, `perry_ffi_promise_resolve_bits`, `perry_ffi_promise_reject_bits`, `perry_ffi_spawn_blocking`) wrapping `async_bridge`'s public Rust functions; perry-ffi's new `crates/perry-ffi/src/async_runtime.rs` declares those symbols `extern "C"` and exposes safe Rust wrappers — `JsPromise` (Send) with `resolve_string` / `resolve_bool` / `resolve_number` / `resolve_undefined` / `resolve_null` / `reject_string`, plus `spawn_blocking<F>(f)` that boxes the FnOnce into a `*mut c_void`+trampoline pair the shim invokes on tokio's blocking pool. NaN-boxing tag constants (STRING_TAG, POINTER_MASK, TAG_UNDEFINED, TAG_NULL) are duplicated into perry-ffi as `pub` so the perry-ffi semver promise binds to them — runtime renames bump perry-ffi major. New crates: `crates/perry-ext-bcrypt/` (5 FFI exports — hash/compare/genSalt async + hashSync/compareSync sync) and `crates/perry-ext-argon2/` (5 FFI exports — hash/verify/needsRehash + sync variants). perry-stdlib's `crypto` umbrella splits: `bundled-bcrypt = ["dep:bcrypt", "async-runtime"]` and `bundled-argon2 = ["dep:argon2", "async-runtime"]` are now the per-binding gates; `crypto = [..., "bundled-bcrypt", "bundled-argon2"]` keeps `--features crypto` byte-identical for legacy callers. `stdlib_features::module_to_features("bcrypt") => &["bundled-bcrypt"]`, `"argon2" => &["bundled-argon2"]`. The well-known flip in `optimized_libs` now special-cases async wrappers — when `bundled-bcrypt` / `bundled-argon2` is stripped, `async-runtime` is re-asserted so perry-stdlib's perry_ffi_async shims still compile (otherwise the wrapper's `.a` carries unresolved `perry_ffi_*` references). End-to-end smoke verified: clean rebuild → `nm libperry_ext_bcrypt.a` shows `T _js_bcrypt_hash` defined and `U _perry_ffi_promise_new` undefined, `nm libperry_stdlib.a` (built with `--features async-runtime`) shows `T _perry_ffi_promise_new` defined; final link resolves cleanly. Both default and well-known paths produce identical observable behavior. Pre-existing `NATIVE_MODULE_TABLE` dispatch bug (bcrypt args declared `NA_F64, NA_F64` should be `NA_STR, NA_F64`) affects both paths equally — separate followup. Migration from here is mechanical for ws / db drivers / fetch — same recipe as bcrypt.

## v0.5.536 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 4 — fourth port): `slugify` joins the well-known path. New `crates/perry-ext-slugify/` (staticlib + rlib, zero runtime deps beyond perry-ffi) replicates all three FFI exports — `js_slugify`, `js_slugify_with_options`, `js_slugify_strict` — including the inline accent-folding table so the port is bit-for-bit equivalent to the perry-stdlib copy without pulling a transliteration crate. Ratchets up `default = ["full"]` to include a new `bundled-slugify` feature that gates `pub mod slugify;` and `pub use slugify::*;` in `crates/perry-stdlib/src/lib.rs`. `stdlib_features::module_to_features("slugify")` now returns `&["bundled-slugify"]` so the auto-optimize default path enables the feature exactly when imported. `well_known_bindings.toml` gains `[bindings.slugify] crate = perry-ext-slugify`. End-to-end smoke verified: `perry compile <slugify ts>` (default) prints `hello-world` / `cafe-au-lait`; `PERRY_USE_WELL_KNOWN=1 perry compile <ts>` prints `well-known: routing slugify → libperry_ext_slugify.a` and `features=(no optional features)`, identical output. 4 unit tests cover lowercase + dashify, accent folding, strict-mode collapse, and custom-replacement-char (input alphabet allocated through perry-ffi to exercise the FFI path end-to-end). Migration recipe is now stable enough to be a cookie-cutter; bcrypt + argon2 next under #466 Phase 5.

## v0.5.535 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 3 + bug fix): `uuid` joins the well-known path. New `crates/perry-ext-uuid/` (staticlib + rlib) implements all six FFI exports (`js_uuid_v4`, `js_uuid_v1`, `js_uuid_v7`, `js_uuid_validate`, `js_uuid_version`, `js_uuid_nil`) on top of perry-ffi v0.5 — third wrapper port, no new helpers needed in perry-ffi. Bundled crate enters `well_known_bindings.toml` as `[bindings.uuid]`. Bug fix found while porting: an orphan `#[cfg(feature = "ids")]` was lingering from the old umbrella above the new `bundled-uuid` cfg in `crates/perry-stdlib/src/lib.rs`; rustc stacks adjacent attributes onto the next item, so `pub mod uuid;` was silently being gated on BOTH `ids` AND `bundled-uuid`. Auto-optimize sets only `bundled-uuid`, so `pub mod uuid;` was excluded — `nm libperry_stdlib.a | grep _js_uuid_v4` returned nothing, and any program importing uuid link-failed under v0.5.534. The fix removes the orphan attribute. The same bug *might* have hit `bundled-nanoid` in v0.5.534 in some situations but didn't surface because every smoke test compiled with `bundled-nanoid` ran with `--features ids` from `default = ["full"]`. Verified the fix end-to-end: clean rebuild → `nm libperry_stdlib.a | grep _js_uuid_v4` shows the symbol, `perry compile test_uuid.ts` links cleanly. Note: `uuid.validate(id)` and `uuid.version(id)` return wrong values (`0` and `undefined` instead of `true`/`4`) in both default and well-known paths — pre-existing bug in `NATIVE_MODULE_TABLE`'s `args: &[NA_F64]` dispatch (should be `NA_STR` since the runtime fn takes `*const StringHeader`). Not introduced by this commit; tracked separately.

## v0.5.534 — Refs #466 (Phase 5 step 2 — second wrapper port): `nanoid` joins `dotenv` on the per-binding feature-gated path. New `crates/perry-ext-nanoid/` (staticlib + rlib) implements the same three FFI exports (`js_nanoid`, `js_nanoid_sized`, `js_nanoid_custom`) using only `perry-ffi` — proves the v0.5 surface remains sufficient for a second port. perry-stdlib's `ids` umbrella feature splits into `bundled-uuid` + `bundled-nanoid` so each binding can be flipped independently; the umbrella stays as `ids = ["bundled-uuid", "bundled-nanoid"]` for backwards compat with existing `--features ids` callers (existing `cargo build -p perry-stdlib --features ids` produces a byte-identical archive). `stdlib_features::module_to_features` now dispatches per-binding (`"nanoid" → &["bundled-nanoid"]`, `"uuid" → &["bundled-uuid"]`). `well_known_bindings.toml` gains a `[bindings.nanoid]` entry referencing `perry-ext-nanoid`. `every_entry_references_a_workspace_crate` continues to pass — both bindings now validated. Smoke test: `PERRY_USE_WELL_KNOWN=1 perry compile <ts that imports nanoid>` logs `well-known: routing nanoid → libperry_ext_nanoid.a` and `features=(no optional features)`, produced binary calls perry-ext-nanoid's `js_nanoid` and prints the same 21-char URL-safe id as the perry-stdlib path. 4 new unit tests in perry-ext-nanoid (default length, custom size, zero-falls-back, custom-alphabet through perry-ffi). nanoid 0.4 + rand 0.9 are direct deps of the new crate. Migration recipe is mechanical now: ~20 minutes per remaining single-file wrapper (`uuid`, `slugify`, `bcrypt`, `argon2`, …).

## v0.5.533 — Refs #466 (Phase 4 step 2): wired the actual resolution flip — `import 'dotenv'` now routes to `perry-ext-dotenv`'s bundled `.a` instead of the perry-stdlib copy, with no duplicate-symbol risk. New `bundled-dotenv` perry-stdlib feature (default-on, included in `default = ["full"]`) gates `pub mod dotenv;` and `pub use dotenv::*;`. `stdlib_features::module_to_features("dotenv")` now maps to `&["bundled-dotenv"]` so the auto-optimize default path enables the feature exactly when the user imports dotenv — preserving byte-identical behavior. New `OptimizedLibs::well_known_libs: Vec<PathBuf>` carries bundled archives from `optimized_libs::build_optimized_libs` to `link::build_and_run_link`, where they're concatenated to the link line right after `stdlib_lib`. The flip itself runs in `build_optimized_libs` after `compute_required_features`: walk `ctx.native_module_imports`, look each up in `well_known_bindings.toml`, locate the bundled `target/release/lib<lib>.a` via `bundled_staticlib_path`, then strip the corresponding perry-stdlib feature from the rebuild and queue the `.a`. End-to-end smoke verified: `PERRY_USE_WELL_KNOWN=1 perry compile test.ts` prints `well-known: routing dotenv → /…/libperry_ext_dotenv.a (#466)`, the auto-optimize log shows `features=(no optional features)` (no `bundled-dotenv`), the produced binary reads `process.env` exactly the same way as the default path. The flip is gated behind `PERRY_USE_WELL_KNOWN=1` for this introductory cycle so default behavior stays byte-identical until the route is proven across CI; the env-var gate is removed in a follow-up commit (or per-binding once the well-known table grows beyond dotenv).

## v0.5.532 — Refs #466 (Phase 4 step 1): added the well-known native bindings registry that future-Phase-5 wrappers will resolve through. `crates/perry/well_known_bindings.toml` is a `[bindings.<npm-name>]` table with `crate` / `lib` / `tracking` keys; new `crates/perry/src/commands/compile/well_known.rs` parses and caches it via `OnceLock`, exposing `lookup_well_known(pkg) -> Option<&WellKnownBinding>`, `iter_well_known()` (for the eventual `perry native list` subcommand under #466 Phase 3), and `bundled_staticlib_path(workspace_root, binding)` (which the linkage flip in step 2 will consume). Today's table has one entry — `dotenv` → `perry-ext-dotenv` — so the contract gets exercised end-to-end without flipping any user-visible resolution. 6 unit tests cover: parser correctness, shipped-toml parsability (panics in OnceLock would surface here), `dotenv_is_registered`, `node:` prefix stripping, unknown-package miss path, and the load-bearing `every_entry_references_a_workspace_crate` check (#466 Phase 4 acceptance: "errors at install time, not user-import time, if a bundled crate is missing"). Step 2 — the actual resolution flip + perry-stdlib `dotenv` feature gate to avoid duplicate `_js_dotenv_*` symbols — is split into a separate commit; this one is pure machinery and zero observable behavior change. Today's `import 'dotenv'` continues to bind to perry-stdlib's copy unchanged.

## v0.5.531 — Refs #466 (Phase 2 of the native-package ecosystem): froze the `perry.nativeLibrary` manifest spec at v1 and wired `abiVersion` enforcement into the resolve path. `docs/src/native-libraries/manifest-v1.md` (linked from SUMMARY.md) is the authoritative spec — every field, every `params`/`returns` ABI type, every per-target key, the resolution order, the deprecation timetable. Companion JSON schema at `docs/api/manifest.schema.json` for editor validation. `NativeLibraryManifest` gains an `abi_version: Option<String>` field populated from `pkg.perry.nativeLibrary.abiVersion`; `validate_abi_version` (semver crate) checks the declared range covers the bundled `perry-ffi` version (currently equal to the workspace version since perry-ffi ships in lockstep with perry through the v0.5.x cycle). Bare-major declarations (`"0.5"`) auto-promote to `^0.5` so wrappers don't have to learn caret syntax to express the common case. Behavior on this branch: missing field → stderr warning, compilation continues (transitional rule; the v0.6.0 plan flips it to an error); valid range that excludes the bundled version → `anyhow::Error` propagated up through `collect_modules` so the user sees the message at compile time, not segfaults at runtime; unparseable string → error pointing at the offending package. Wired in both arms of `collect_modules.rs` (NativeCompiled and Interpreted paths) so packages with `perry.nativeLibrary` get validated regardless of how their TS surface is consumed. Four unit tests cover the matrix: missing → ok, matching caret → ok, far-future major → err, garbage string → err. `semver = "1.0"` added to `crates/perry/Cargo.toml` (was already a transitive dep via `perry-updater`).

## v0.5.530 — Refs #466 (Phase D of #469): extracted the stable ABI surface for native-bindings packages into a new `perry-ffi` crate, and ported the smallest stdlib wrapper (`dotenv`) onto it as the acceptance test. `perry-ffi` v0.5 ships an intentionally tiny surface — `JsString` opaque handle, `alloc_string(&str) -> JsString`, `read_string(JsString) -> Option<&str>`, `JsString::{from_raw, as_raw, is_null}`, plus a re-export of `StringHeader` for `extern "C"` signatures. That's enough to port the four single-file stdlib wrappers (`dotenv`, `nanoid`, `uuid`, `slugify`); larger surfaces (arrays / objects / closures / async-runtime sharing) wait for the wrappers that actually need them so we don't commit to APIs we'll regret. New `crates/perry-ext-dotenv/` is a staticlib + rlib that depends only on `perry-ffi` and `serde_json` — zero references to `perry-runtime` internals. Functionally identical to `crates/perry-stdlib/src/dotenv.rs` (which stays in place — additive port, not a replacement); the well-known bindings table flip from #466 Phase 4 is what eventually swaps `import 'dotenv'` resolution from the stdlib copy to the new crate. `nm libperry_ext_dotenv.a | grep js_dotenv` shows the same three exports the stdlib version has (`js_dotenv_config`, `js_dotenv_config_path`, `js_dotenv_parse`). Tests round-trip a string through perry-ffi → js_dotenv_parse → JSON output, proving the contract end-to-end without touching the runtime crate. `docs/src/native-libraries/abi.md` documents the v0.5 surface, the semver contract (perry-ffi major bumps independently of perry-runtime), and what's deliberately *not* yet covered. Mdbook SUMMARY.md links the new doc under "npm Packages → Writing Native Bindings".

## v0.5.529 — Closes #465 (Phase C of #469): markdown + `.d.ts` serializers for the API manifest. New `crates/perry-api-manifest/src/emit.rs` exposes `emit_markdown(version)` (one combined reference page; modules in alpha order; classes / properties / methods grouped within each module; stubs flagged ⚠) and `emit_dts(version)` (one `declare module "<name>" { ... }` block per supported module; loose `any`-typed signatures since the manifest doesn't carry argument types yet — followup under #466 Phase 2). The `--print-api-manifest` global flag now takes an optional value (`--print-api-manifest=markdown` / `=dts`); bare flag stays JSON for compat with v0.5.528 callers. `default` is the one TypeScript reserved keyword that hits in practice (npm modules with a callable default export — `dotenv`, `slugify`, `sharp`, `better-sqlite3`, `commander`, `dayjs`, `moment`, `cheerio`, `lru-cache`, `lodash`); the .d.ts emitter renders those as `export default function (...args: any[]): any;` rather than the syntactically-invalid `export function default(...)`. Tests assert every module from `API_MANIFEST` appears in both formats and that `crypto.randomUUID` shows up under `declare module "crypto"`. Generated artifacts committed at `docs/src/api/reference.md` (676 lines, 378 entries across 43 modules) and `docs/api/perry.d.ts` (476 lines), with `scripts/regen_api_docs.sh` as the one-shot regenerator that release CI will eventually invoke (the workflow integration itself is a follow-up — committing the artifacts means the diff stays visible in PRs that touch the dispatch table or the manifest). SUMMARY.md now links the auto-generated reference under "Standard Library".

## v0.5.528 — Closes #463 (Phase A + B of the unified plan in #469): new `perry-api-manifest` crate exposes `API_MANIFEST: &[ApiEntry]` — a structured (module, name, kind, source, stub) listing of every supported stdlib symbol, populated mechanically from `NATIVE_MODULE_TABLE` (317 methods) plus hand-curated entries for modules whose dispatch goes through custom `Expr::*` HIR variants (`crypto`, `os`, `path`, `process`) and a small set of class registrations (`Buffer`, `EventEmitter`, `URL`, etc., 374 entries total). HIR lowering of `Expr::PropertyGet { object: NativeModuleRef(M), property: P }` consults `module_has_symbol(M, P)` and emits an `R005 UnimplementedApi` diagnostic with span info when the symbol isn't registered — `crypto.subtle.encrypt(...)` now errors at the offending line instead of silently returning undefined. Strictness is gated on `module_has_any_entries(M)` so modules whose surface hasn't been enumerated yet (incremental coverage) keep working — adding entries promotes the module to strict mode automatically. Stubs (`stub: true` in the manifest) are NOT treated as unimplemented; #464's runtime first-call warning surfaces those instead. Two escape hatches: env var `PERRY_ALLOW_UNIMPLEMENTED=1` skips the check (useful when the manifest has a real gap a followup will fix), and the `--print-api-manifest` global flag emits the manifest as JSON to stdout — drives #465's docs/.d.ts generators and lets editor tooling discover the supported surface without reading Rust source. `perry-codegen`'s `tests/manifest_consistency.rs` walks `NATIVE_MODULE_TABLE` and asserts every row has a counterpart in `API_MANIFEST` so drift can't ship. `perry-hir::ir::NATIVE_MODULES` and `requires_stdlib` migrated to thin re-exports / delegates over `perry_api_manifest::{NATIVE_MODULES, is_runtime_only_module}`. Parity sweep: 209 pass / 2 fail / 1 compile_fail (test_issue_446 is a pre-existing #462-era regression unrelated to this PR — verified by disabling the new check and re-running). Same manifest is the foundation for #465 (docs/.d.ts emit) and #466 Phase 2 (external `perry.nativeLibrary` spec freeze).

## v0.5.527 — Closes #470: `Buffer.{read,write}{U,}Int{BE,LE}(offset, byteLength)` variable-byteLength forms were no-ops (`readUIntBE` returned `undefined`, `writeUIntBE` wrote nothing) — `dispatch_buffer_method` only handled the fixed-width `readUInt8/16/32` / `writeUInt8/16/32` cases and fell through to `TAG_UNDEFINED` for the parametric forms. Added `js_buffer_{read,write}_{uint,int}_{be,le}(buf, [value,] offset, byte_length)` to `crates/perry-runtime/src/buffer.rs` (1..=6 byte_length, sign-extends through `(n*8)`-bit two's complement for the signed reads, returns `undefined` / no-ops on out-of-range byteLength to match the rest of the buffer dispatch's tolerant-on-bad-args convention) and wired the 8 method-name arms into `dispatch_buffer_method` in `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs`. The codegen-side `try_emit_buffer_read_intrinsic` fast path is opt-in by exact method name (`classify_buffer_numeric_read` doesn't list these) so the variable-byteLength calls naturally fall through to the runtime dispatch — no codegen changes needed. Unblocks BSON `ObjectId` generation in pure-TS Mongo drivers (the 3-byte counter no longer serialises as zero, so `insertMany(N)` actually inserts N).

## v0.5.526 — Closes #462: `PropertyGet` codegen now splits the existing `pget.recv_bad` block into a TAG_UNDEFINED/TAG_NULL nullish check + `js_throw_type_error_property_access` runtime helper that prints a node-shaped `TypeError: Cannot read properties of {undefined|null} (reading '<prop>')` and aborts. Non-nullish invalid receivers (numbers, bools, raw f64) keep the silent-undefined fall-through since Perry has no primitive auto-boxing yet. Optional chaining and `??` already short-circuit before reaching PropertyGet, so they're unaffected.

## v0.5.525 — Refs #461: emit a fallback `perry_fn_<modprefix>__<exported>` undefined-stub on the export side for every `Export::Named` that no other emission path has already claimed — closes the `Order` / `Union` / `Emit` link errors that #460 carved out as follow-up. Two distinct symptom classes shared the same root cause: (1) exported classes (`export class Union<M>` in SchemaAST.ts, `export class Emit<R>` in subexecutor.ts) — the class declaration emits `perry_class_keys_*` + `perry_method_*` + a constructor symbol but no `perry_fn_<mod>__<ClassName>` value-getter, so consumer-side namespace property accesses (`AST.Union.make(...)`) link-failed because lower_call.rs's `ExternFuncRef` arm always resolves to `perry_fn_<src>__<name>`; (2) exported interfaces / type aliases (`export interface Order<in A>` in Order.ts) — type-only at runtime, but type annotations like `order.Order<RuntimeFiber<unknown,unknown>>` in `export const Order: order.Order<…> = internal.Order` (Fiber.ts:308) leak into the value-position symbol resolver and emit a load of `perry_fn_<Order_ts>__Order` that never had a definition. Fix is one block in `crates/perry-codegen/src/codegen.rs` after the #460 forwarding-wrapper pass: walk `hir.exports` for `Export::Named { exported }`, compute `perry_fn_<modprefix>__<sanitize(exported)>`, skip if `LlModule::has_function` (new helper) reports the symbol is already defined by an earlier path (function body / value-getter / #460 forwarding wrapper), otherwise emit a `() -> double` stub returning NaN-boxed undefined. Matches the consumer-side no-op wrapper at codegen.rs:1955 (which already returns undefined for imported classes referenced as values), so cross-module class/type-only references behave symmetrically — link cleanly, return undefined at runtime. After fix, `bun add effect && perry compile test_minimal.ts` drops `Order` / `Union` / `Emit` from the undefined-symbol list (down to 1: bare `_pull` from `internal/stream.ts`'s nested-arrow capture-mangling — separate bug, #461 stays open). Parity sweep: 210 pass / 2 known fails (was 209 / 2 — one test that was previously compile-failing now compiles cleanly through the new stub path).

## v0.5.524 — Closes #464: stub registry + first-call runtime diagnostic for the harmonyos `perry_ui_*` / `perry_system_*` / `perry_updater_*` no-op stubs (and the runtime-only `js_ws_*` / `js_readline_*` stubs). `perry-runtime/build.rs` walks the four `perry-dispatch` tables + the hand-listed `direct_call_stubs` and emits both the stub function bodies (when `ohos-napi` is on) and a `STUB_MANIFEST: &[StubEntry]` constant (always — `perry check` runs on a host build). Each generated stub funnels through `crate::stub_diag::perry_stub_warn(symbol, reason, issue)` which prints `[perry] warning: \`<sym>\` is a no-op stub on this platform — <reason> (tracking: <issue>)` once per symbol per process; `PERRY_STUB_DIAG=off|silent|0|false` silences and `=verbose|all|every` prints every call. The same manifest powers a new static scan in `perry check --target harmonyos` (also `harmonyos-simulator`) that walks named imports from `perry/ui` / `perry/system` / `perry/updater` against `STUB_MANIFEST.ts_name` and emits an `R004 NoOpStub` diagnostic at the import span — surfaces the same warnings before the binary runs. Hot-loop drains (`js_ws_process_pending`, `js_stdlib_process_pending`) deliberately skip the warn helper since they tick every event-loop pass. `target_stubs_out_symbols` gates the static scan so a native-host `perry check` (where the platform UI crate owns those symbols) doesn't false-positive.

## v0.5.523 — Closes #460: `export { local as keyword }` re-exports of TypeScript contextual keywords (`void` / `async` / `await` / `try` / `delete` / `continue`) had no `_perry_fn_<mod>__<keyword>` definition in the renamed module's object file — Effect's `node_modules/effect/src/{Effect,Deferred,Either,FiberRef,Fiber,Option,ScheduleDecision,Stream,internal/core,…}.ts` link-failed on 14 such symbols.
