Mango's `function makePill(label) { ... return pill; }` is declared INSIDE refreshConnectionList (so it's a `Stmt::Let { id, init: Closure }` not a module-level function); the four `makePill('Databases & Collections')` etc. calls in the welcome card now inline correctly, surfacing the feature-pill text. Simple-return shape constraint (last `Stmt::Return(Some(LocalGet(id)))`) covers makePill's pattern; more complex returning-fns punt. (4) **Early-return → if/else rewrite**: `rewrite_early_returns` runs on each cloned function body before the local-id remap. Pattern `if (cond) { ...; return; } <rest>` rewrites to `if (cond) { ... } else { <rest> }` so the dead-branch elim's then-branch pick correctly drops `<rest>` (Mango's refreshConnectionList: the welcome card branch had a trailing `return`, the connection-list build code lived as siblings AFTER the if; without this rewrite the after-if code emitted unconditionally alongside the welcome card content). The rewrite recurses into nested if/while/for/do-while bodies. **`emit_text` now uses bindings**: `Text(t('Welcome to Mango'))` previously fell through to `[non-literal Text arg]` because the resolved string-arg path required a String literal at the leaf. Now it falls through `resolve_string_arg` (with the new i18n unwrap arms). Two pre-existing tests updated to match the new dead-branch-elim semantics: `issue_408_conditional_widget_add_child_emits_if_else` (was pinning both-branches-emit, now asserts then-branch-only) and `issue_413_addchild_inside_unfoldable_runtime_condition_still_emits_if` (renamed to `issue_490_unfoldable_unresolvable_condition_walks_only_then_branch`). End-to-end on Mango via the OHOS Pura emulator: cumulative harvest output 28667 → 25329 bytes (slight shrink from removing duplicate else-branch content), 14 → 10 unrecognized-body placeholders, 8 → 7 closure registrations. Visible welcome screen: brand toolbar with logo + title, "Welcome to Mango" header, "Connect to your MongoDB instance..." hint, all 4 feature pills (Databases & Collections / Query & Filter / Edit & Insert / Index Viewer), "+ New Connection" CTA (single occurrence), analytics notice, About button — visually matches the macOS reference. cargo test 95+2=97 passing.

## v0.5.490 — Closes #414: `db.query(sql, [param])` against MySQL failed with `1835 (HY000): Malformed communication packet` and bricked the connection — `[token]` parameter binding through perry's `mysql2/promise` stdlib was unusable, forcing `perry-hub` to a string-built fallback. Root cause: codegen dispatch table for the three mysql2 query/execute methods declares `args: &[NA_STR, NA_PTR]` so call sites emit 3 args (`handle, sql, params`), but the runtime functions `js_mysql2_pool_query` / `js_mysql2_pool_connection_query` / `js_mysql2_connection_query` declared only 2 args (`handle, sql`) — silently dropping the params on the floor. sqlx then sent the binary-protocol execute frame with 1 placeholder but 0 bind values; MySQL rejected the malformed frame with error 1835 and the connection stuck. **Fix in two files**: thread `params: JSValue` through the three `*_query` runtime functions in `crates/perry-stdlib/src/mysql2/{pool,connection}.rs` and bind via the same `extract_params_from_jsvalue` → `ParamValue` → `sqlx::query.bind` path the `*_execute` siblings have used since v0.5.182. `extract_params_from_jsvalue` already short-circuits on `0` / undefined / null / non-array, so the codegen's no-args pad (literal i64 `0`) keeps the existing no-param call shape working without a behavior change. 4 new unit tests in `mysql2::pool::tests` pin the four shapes the codegen can pass to the params slot: (a) raw `0` pad, (b) TAG_UNDEFINED, (c) TAG_NULL, (d) raw lower-48 array pointer (post-NA_PTR-unbox), (e) defensive NaN-boxed array pointer. Documentation regression test `test-files/test_issue_414_mysql_query_params.ts` mirrors the issue's literal repro (`platforms: skip` banner — CI runners have no MySQL server, run locally against `mysql://perry:perry@localhost:3306/perry_hub` to verify). Pre-fix: `[1] OK rows=[{"one":1}]` then `[2] FAILED: Query failed: error returned from database: 1835 (HY000): Malformed communication packet.` then connection hangs and `[3]` / `[4]` never run. Post-fix: all four cases succeed.

## v0.5.489 — codegen-arkts: top-level user-function calls are now inlined into a per-harvest analysis copy of `module.init` so the procedural-mutation collectors see widget mutations inside function bodies. Mango's pattern: `const connListContainer = VStack(10, []); function refreshConnectionList() { if (connectionNames.length === 0) { const welcomeCard = VStack(16, []); widgetAddChild(welcomeCard, welcomeTitle); widgetAddChild(connListContainer, welcomeCard); } } refreshConnectionList();` — pre-fix the harvest emitted an empty Column for `connListContainer` because `collect_mutations` only walked module.init and the widgetAddChild calls lived inside `refreshConnectionList`'s body. **Fix in `crates/perry-codegen-arkts/src/lib.rs`**: new `inlined_analysis_init(module) -> Vec<Stmt>` builds a CLONE of `module.init` with each top-level call to a user-defined function (`Stmt::Expr(Expr::Call { callee: FuncRef(id) })`) replaced by the function's body. Param substitution lands as synthesized `Stmt::Let` BEFORE the cloned body so `collect_const_bindings` (which already recurses into Stmt::If post v0.5.486) picks them up the same way as real top-level lets. The collectors (`collect_const_bindings`, `collect_mutations`, `collect_compile_time_constants`) now consume `analysis_init` instead of `module.init` directly. Critically `module.init` itself is NOT mutated — the same module then goes through the LLVM backend, and inlining a void function's `return` into module init would otherwise become a top-level `return` from `main()` and fail the LLVM type checker. **Local-id collision** is handled by walking `Stmt::Let` (and `Stmt::For`-init / catch-param) in the cloned body, allocating fresh ids from `max_local_id_in_module(module) + 1`, and applying the remap via `perry_hir::analysis::remap_local_ids_in_stmts` for LocalGet/LocalSet/Update refs PLUS a new local helper `remap_let_ids_in_stmts` for the *declaration* sites (Stmt::Let.id / catch.param.id / for-init.id). The shipped `remap_local_ids_in_stmts` deliberately doesn't rewrite Let.id (it was designed for #212 field propagation where outer-scope captured ids must NOT change), but my use case needs both — the let creates a new binding and references to it must agree. Without the let-id-remap, the cloned body's `Stmt::Let { id: 4 }` stays at id 4 while every `LocalGet(4)` reference inside got remapped to `LocalGet(710)`, so `collect_const_bindings` registered binding[4] but `emit_widget` looked up bindings[710] and missed → `[unrecognized body]`. **Bounds**: skip async/generator functions, ≤16 inlines per harvest call, skip recursive direct calls (visited HashSet). **`is_cleanly_serializable_condition` companion fix**: `serialize_condition` previously degraded only unresolvable `Expr::LocalGet` to `true` at the leaf level — but a PropertyGet on top of an unresolvable LocalGet (Mango's `connectionNames.length === 0`) emitted `true.length === 0` which ArkTS strict-mode rejects with "Property 'length' does not exist on type 'true'". New `is_cleanly_serializable_condition` walks the condition expression and returns false when ANY leaf isn't cleanly representable (unresolvable LocalGet, PropertyGet, function call, etc.). When false, the entire condition degrades to the literal `"true"` — so the conditional always renders its then-branch and ArkTS compiles cleanly. The two existing #410 / #413 unit tests that asserted the buggy old leaf-only behavior were updated to match the now-correct shape. **`pub mod analysis`** in perry-hir's lib.rs: was `pub(crate)`, needed to be exposed for the inliner to call `remap_local_ids_in_stmts` from outside the crate. **End-to-end on Mango via the OHOS Pura emulator**: cumulative harvest output 19150 → 28667 bytes (+50%); registered closures 6 → 8; the welcome-card content from `refreshConnectionList()` now renders ("Welcome to Mango" header, "+ New Connection" button, About button). Some inner placeholders remain — the welcome card uses `Text(t('...'))` (i18n function call) and `makePill(label)` (nested function defined inside refreshConnectionList) which my inliner doesn't recursively expand — but the structural mutations are now captured. 95 + 2 = 97 codegen-arkts tests passing (2 pre-existing tests updated to match the new `serialize_condition` gate behavior).

## v0.5.488 — codegen-arkts: image asset paths + ternary numeric resolution + HAP rawfile bundling. Three fixes that take Mango on HarmonyOS from "branded toolbar visible but logo missing" to "branded toolbar with logo image rendered". (1) **`emit_image` resolves through bindings + ternaries**: pre-fix the `Image()` / `ImageFile()` emit required a direct `Expr::String` arg and fell through to `Text('[non-literal Image src]')` for anything else. Mango's `ImageFile(mobile ? 'assets/icon-40.png' : 'assets/icon-44.png')` (ternary returning a string) was the placeholder. New `resolve_string_arg(expr, bindings)` walks `Expr::LocalGet` chains + `Expr::Conditional` branches (16-hop cap) — same heuristic as the v0.5.487 widget Conditional handler: const-fold the condition via `evaluate_condition`, pick the resolved branch; default to then-branch when unresolvable. (2) **`numeric_arg_resolved` learns ternaries**: same shape applied to `widgetSetWidth(logo, mobile ? 40 : 44)` and `widgetSetHeight` and `setPadding` (4-channel) and `setCornerRadius` and `stackSetDistribution` and `stackSetAlignment` — every numeric mutator that previously used `numeric_arg(...)` (literals only) now uses `numeric_arg_resolved(args, idx, bindings)` which walks LocalGet + Conditional. `widgetSetWidth` / `widgetSetHeight` switched to skip-on-unresolved (`return` instead of `unwrap_or(0.0)`) — emitting `.width(0)` zeros the widget which is *worse* than no modifier. (3) **Image asset bundling**: `assets/X.png` paths in `emit_image` translate to `$rawfile('X.png')`; `crates/perry/src/commands/harmonyos_hap.rs::copy_assets_to_rawfile` (new) walks the project's `assets/` folder and recursively copies into `resources/rawfile/`. New `assets_dir: Option<&'a Path>` field on `HapBuildArgs` plumbs the project root through; `compile.rs` walks up from the entry file's parent looking for an `assets/` directory (4-level cap, handles `<root>/src/app.ts` + `<root>/assets/`). 2 new harmonyos_hap unit tests cover nested-dirs copy + missing-dir skip. **Verified end-to-end on Mango**: the brand orange gradient toolbar now renders with the actual `mango-app-icon-40.png` image displayed alongside the "Mango" title — was: blank space + 0×0 invisible image. 95 + 2 codegen-arkts tests + 2 new harmonyos_hap tests = 99 passing.

## v0.5.487 — codegen-arkts: ternary `cond ? widget : widget` (HIR `Expr::Conditional`) now resolves to one of its branches instead of falling through to `[unrecognized body]`. Mango's `const toolbarRow = mobile ? HStack(10, [mobileChildren]) : HStack(10, [desktopChildren]); const toolbarBox = VStack(0, [toolbarRow])` was const-binding-resolving correctly (post v0.5.486) but `emit_widget` had no `Expr::Conditional` arm — every ternary widget reference fell through to the placeholder. Added a Conditional arm that const-folds the condition via the existing `evaluate_condition` (handles `__platform__ === N`, `mobile`, etc. through bindings); when the condition is statically determinable picks the corresponding branch, when runtime-bound picks the then-branch heuristically (the "primary"/first-listed case, matching what users typically write first). Verified end-to-end on Mango: the browserScreen toolbar now emits `Row { Button('Explorer'), Spacer, Text('Connected'), Button('About'), ... }` with real ArkUI Buttons and onClick callback bridges (was: `[unrecognized body]` placeholder text). Cumulative harvest output for Mango grew from 11292 → 17759 bytes (+57%) and registered closures from 3 → 6. 95 unit + 2 integration = 97 tests still passing.

## v0.5.486 — codegen-arkts: const bindings declared inside `Stmt::If` branches are now visible to the harvest. Pre-fix `collect_const_bindings` only iterated top-level `module.init` statements, so a pattern like `if (mobile) { const connInfoBtn = Button('About', ...); widgetAddChild(connBody, HStack([Spacer(), connInfoBtn, Spacer()])); }` lost the binding to `connInfoBtn` — `widgetAddChild`'s body harvested fine (the Row + two Blanks rendered in the emit) but the middle child collapsed to `[unrecognized body]` because the LocalGet never resolved. Switched the collector to a recursive `walk_collect_const_bindings(stmts, map)` helper that descends into `Stmt::If`'s `then_branch` + `else_branch`. Limitation: if two branches both `const foo = ...` with different RHS, last one wins (no scoping); Mango doesn't hit this and full scoped resolution is meaningfully bigger. Verified end-to-end on Mango: the `if (mobile)` "About" button — created inside the if-block and added to `connBody` via the same conditional `widgetAddChild` — now renders as a real Button below the brand toolbar on the OHOS Pura emulator (was: `[unrecognized body]` placeholder text where the Button should have been). 95 unit + 2 integration = 97 tests still passing.

## v0.5.485 — #408/#410/#413 polish round driven by Mango's procedural UI on the OHOS Pura emulator. Five fixes that all converge on "Mango's brand toolbar actually renders". (1) **Text styling mutators** — `textSetFontSize` / `textSetFontWeight` / `textSetFontFamily` / `textSetColor` / `textSetWraps` were falling through to the unrecognized-mutator path, producing `// not yet handled by codegen-arkts` comments instead of real ArkUI modifiers. Mango uses these heavily (every branded title) so without them every Text rendered with default styling. New mutator arms in `crates/perry-codegen-arkts/src/lib.rs` map each to `.fontSize(N)` / `.fontWeight(N)` / `.fontFamily('X')` / `.fontColor('rgba(R,G,B,A)')` / `.maxLines(0|1).textOverflow({...})`. (2) **Binding-aware numeric resolver** — `numeric_arg` was direct-literal-only, so `textSetFontSize(w, size)` where `size` is a `const size = 28` bound local resolved to None and the existing `unwrap_or(0.0)` emitted `.fontSize(0)` (invisible text). New `numeric_arg_resolved(args, idx, bindings)` walks `Expr::LocalGet` chains through `bindings` (bounded to 16 hops to avoid pathological cycles) and bottoms out at `Expr::Number / Expr::Integer`. Returns `None` when the chain hits a non-numeric leaf — caller skips emit rather than emitting bogus values. Used by all five text mutators + the gradient channel resolver. (3) **Real `widgetSetBackgroundGradient`** — the v0.5.480 stub emitted a hard-coded `'#ffffff'→'#000000'` gradient regardless of the actual call args, producing white-on-white invisible text on Mango's brand toolbar. New impl resolves the 9 channel args (r1/g1/b1/a1/r2/g2/b2/a2/direction) through `bindings` (so `widgetSetBackgroundGradient(box, moR, moG, moB, ...)` with const-bound brand colors works), maps direction 0→angle 0 (vertical) and direction 1→angle 90 (horizontal), formats colors as 6-char hex (`#ff9f1c`). Fallback to a `Mutation::Comment` when channels can't be resolved — better to emit no gradient than the wrong one. (4) **Comment-eats-modifier fix** — `Mutation::Comment` was emitting as `\n// X` line comment. ArkUI modifier chains run inline (`}.padding(...).visibility(...)`); a `\n// X` between two modifiers caused the second modifier to be silently dropped because `// X.visibility(...)` parses as a single comment line to EOL. Switched to inline `/* X */` block comments + reused `sanitize_for_block_comment` from #410 for the `*/`-leak guard. (5) **`stackSetAlignment` value-name table is now axis-aware** (already shipped as v0.5.484, mentioned here for context — the same Mango run surfaced both): `VerticalAlign` doesn't have `Start`/`End` members, only `Top`/`Center`/`Bottom`. New regression tests: `text_styling_mutators_emit_arkui_modifiers` (full 4-mutator chain on a single Text), `unrecognized_mutator_comment_does_not_swallow_following_modifier` (sandwich shape: known-modifier → unknown-mutator → known-modifier — both knowns must survive). Pre-existing `issue_413_hstack_set_alignment_emits_vertical_align_enum` updated to assert `VerticalAlign.Top` (was `VerticalAlign.Start`, which was pinning the broken behavior). 95 unit + 2 integration = **97 tests, all passing**. End-to-end on Mango: titles + brand orange gradient (`#ff9f1c → #ffbf69`) now render on the OHOS Pura emulator.

## v0.5.484 — #413 follow-up: `stackSetAlignment` value-name table is now axis-aware. v0.5.483's #413 fix made the cross-axis enum *name* axis-aware (`Column.alignItems(HorizontalAlign.X)` vs `Row.alignItems(VerticalAlign.X)`), but the value-name table was shared — `0`→`Start`, `1`→`Center`, `2`→`End` regardless of enum. ArkUI's `VerticalAlign` doesn't have `Start` or `End` members (it uses `Top` / `Center` / `Bottom`); ArkTS strict-mode rejected `Row(...).alignItems(VerticalAlign.Start)` with "Property 'Start' does not exist on type 'typeof VerticalAlign'". Surfaced on Mango's browserContent HStack which calls `stackSetAlignment(0)` (= `start` semantics) — failed at `hvigorw assembleHap` immediately after the v0.5.483 fix made the enum-name part of the codegen-arkts emit correct. **Fix in `crates/perry-codegen-arkts/src/lib.rs::stackSetAlignment` arm**: keep the same semantic input (0=start, 1=center, 2=end) but map to the axis-correct value-name via `match (enum_name, n)` — `("VerticalAlign", 0) => "Top"`, `("VerticalAlign", 2) => "Bottom"`, `(_, 1) => "Center"`, `("HorizontalAlign", 2) => "End"`, fallback `"Start"`. Center stays Center across both enums. **New regression test** `stack_alignment_value_names_match_axis_enum` exercises the full 6-case matrix (HStack × {0,1,2} → Top/Center/Bottom; VStack × {0,1,2} → Start/Center/End). Verified: `cargo test -p perry-codegen-arkts --release` 93 unit + 2 integration = 95 tests passing (was 92+2=94). End-to-end Mango via DevEco's hvigor passes the previously-failing line.

## v0.5.483 — Closes #413: v0.5.481's #410 fix made `serialize_condition` resolve `Expr::LocalGet` chains through `bindings` + inline `__platform__` as a numeric literal, but two follow-up defects surfaced when running Mango (the user's flagship demo) through DevEco's `hvigorw assembleHap` — 16 ArkTS strict-mode errors, 15 of them in this family. **Bug 1 (literal-only comparisons)**: with `__platform__` resolving to `9` and `let mobile = __platform__ === 1` resolving its binding, the harvest emitted `if (9 === 1) { ... } else { ... }` blocks — which ArkTS rejects with `This comparison appears to be unintentional because the types '9' and '1' have no overlap`. Fix: new `evaluate_condition(e, bindings, compile_time_consts) -> Option<bool>` constant-folder in `crates/perry-codegen-arkts/src/lib.rs` that walks Compare / Logical / Unary / LocalGet expressions and returns `Some(true|false)` when every leaf bottoms out in a literal (Bool / Number / Integer / String / Null / Undefined). Inner `Lit` enum + `to_lit / lit_truthy / lit_strict_eq / lit_loose_eq` helpers handle the spec'd JS coercion subset (strict-eq for matching kinds, loose-eq adds `null == undefined`, numeric ops gated on `Lit::Num`, `&& / || / ??` short-circuit). The `Stmt::If` arm of `collect_mutations_in_stmt` now calls `evaluate_condition` first: `Some(true)` walks the then-branch with the *enclosing* condition (no new group/condition allocated — the resolved branch isn't a real if/else from the emitter's perspective), `Some(false)` walks the else-branch (or empty if no else), `None` falls through to the existing path. The same constant-folder also makes `if (1 === 2) widgetAddChild(p, child)` drop the dead branch entirely instead of emitting `if (1 === 2) { ... }`. **Bug 1b (operator precedence)**: `serialize_condition` was concatenating sub-expressions without parens, so `mobile = __platform__ === 1 || __platform__ === 2 || (!isIOS && something)` would inline as `9 === 1 || 9 === 2 && !9 === 1 && true === 1` — note `!9 === 1` (parses as `(!9) === 1` instead of `!(9 === 1)`) and `true === 1`. Fix: new `needs_parens(e, bindings)` predicate identifies Binary / Logical / Unary expressions (post-resolve through bindings) and `wrap(e, bindings, ts)` adds `(...)` around them when they appear as operands of an outer Binary / Logical / Unary. Leaf shapes (literals, PropertyGet, LocalGet that resolved to a literal) don't get wrapped. **Bug 2 (HorizontalAlign on HStack)**: `stackSetAlignment(widget, alignment)` always emitted `.alignItems(HorizontalAlign.X)`, but ArkUI's cross-axis enum is axis-dependent — Column (= VStack) takes `HorizontalAlign.X` (cross-axis is horizontal), Row (= HStack) takes `VerticalAlign.X` (cross-axis is vertical). ArkTS strict-mode rejected `Row(...).alignItems(HorizontalAlign.Start)` with "type 'HorizontalAlign' is not assignable to parameter of type 'VerticalAlign'". Fix: new `stack_axis_align_enum(target_id, bindings)` helper looks up the target widget's binding and returns `"VerticalAlign"` for HStack, `"HorizontalAlign"` for VStack (and as the default fallback when the binding can't be resolved or doesn't name a stack constructor). `collect_mutations_in_expr` extended with `bindings: &HashMap<LocalId, Expr>` so the helper can be called at collection time — the resolved enum string lands in the `Mutation::Modifier(String)` directly, no axis-resolution at emit time. **Tests**: 15 new `issue_413_*` unit tests pin every invariant — `evaluate_condition` folds literal `===` to true/false, returns None for runtime values (PropertyGet on unresolved local), resolves through `compile_time_consts` (`__platform__ === 9` → `Some(true)`), short-circuits on `||`, negates `!true`; literal-only `if` blocks drop dead branches and inline live ones without an `if` wrapper; `__platform__ === 1` (the harmonyos = 9 case) drops the then-branch and keeps the else; binding-resolved comparisons fold the same way; HStack emits `VerticalAlign.Start` (and never `HorizontalAlign`); VStack emits `HorizontalAlign.Start` (regression-pin); unary `!` over a binding-resolved comparison parenthesizes correctly (`!(9 === 1)` not `!9 === 1`); the full Mango-shape `||/||/&&` chain has no precedence-inverted substrings; runtime conditions (PropertyGet on unresolved) still emit a real `if (...) { ... } else { ... }` block (sanity check that dead-branch elim only fires on literal-only conditions). Three pre-existing tests updated for the new behavior: `issue_408_conditional_widget_add_child_emits_if_else` flipped from `Bool(true)` (now folds) to a PropertyGet so it still exercises the runtime if/else path; `issue_408_stack_distribution_and_alignment_emit_flexalign_modifiers` (which uses HStack) flipped its assertion from `HorizontalAlign.Center` to `VerticalAlign.Center`; `issue_410_conditional_addchild_emits_valid_arkts_if_block` now asserts the dead-branch elimination behavior — pre-#413 it emitted `if (9 === 9) { Button('phone') } else { Button('desktop') }`, post-#413 it emits only `Button('phone')` unconditionally. **Verified**: `cargo build --release -p perry-codegen-arkts` clean; `cargo test -p perry-codegen-arkts --release` 92 unit + 2 integration = **94 tests, all passing** (was 77 + 2 = 79); `cargo fmt -p perry-codegen-arkts --check` clean. End-to-end Mango render verification through `hvigorw assembleHap` is the maintainer's separate manual splice step. v0.5.483 (this commit) lost a patch-version race with the v0.5.482 Windows stdlib hotfix — initial commit was v0.5.482 but origin/main shipped a v0.5.482 simultaneously, so this rebased onto v0.5.483.

## v0.5.482 — Windows source-build hotfix. v0.5.467's `#347 Phase 2 readline` PR added `[target.'cfg(unix)'.dependencies] / libc = "0.2"` to `crates/perry-stdlib/Cargo.toml` but inserted the section header at line 101, *inside* the existing `[dependencies]` table — TOML treats every key after a section header as belonging to that section, so on Windows every optional dep declared after it (`tokio`, `hex`, `image`, `uuid`, `dashmap`, `lru`, `bytes`, `hyper`, `reqwest`, `sqlx`, `rusqlite`, `aes-gcm`, `sha2`, `clap`, `rust_decimal`, etc.) silently became Unix-only. `cargo metadata` confirms: pre-fix `target=cfg(unix)` for all of them on Windows, post-fix `target=` (cross-platform). Effect on Windows: `cargo build -p perry-stdlib` failed with 292 errors (`use of unresolved module / unlinked crate`), and the latest `Tests` workflow run on `main` had `doc-tests (windows-2022)` failing with the exact same `unresolved module 'tokio' / 'hex' / 'dashmap' / ...` errors. Pre-built v0.5.465 binaries (the last successful Windows release-packages run, ~6h before v0.5.467 landed) and Scoop installs were unaffected — the bug only blocked Windows source builds + the Tests CI lane across the v0.5.467-481 window. **Fix in one place** (`crates/perry-stdlib/Cargo.toml`): move the `[target.'cfg(unix)'.dependencies] / libc = "0.2"` block from line 101 (mid-`[dependencies]`) to the bottom of the file, after `rust_decimal`. Net diff +6/-5. **Verified locally on a Windows host**: `cargo build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc -p perry-stdlib` clean (4m04s, 53 warnings, 0 errors, 257 MB lib); `-p perry -p perry-runtime -p perry-ui-windows` clean (2m24s); `cargo test --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc -p perry-runtime --lib` = **210/210 passed**; `cargo test ... -p perry-codegen-arkts --lib` = **70/70 passed**; v0.5.481 `perry.exe doctor` clean; `test_issue_388_optional_chain_loose_eq.ts` + `test_issue_392_cross_module_method_dispatch.ts` + `test_issue_347_readline_phase1.ts` + `test_issue_340_axios_response_props.ts` + `test_issue_341_typed_field_native.ts` all compile cleanly. The bug class is "TOML inline target-section header dropped into a non-targeted table" — Cargo accepts it silently and resolves features the same way (so `default = ["full"]` still flips `feature="ids"` etc), then leaves the `dep:foo` references dangling on platforms where the dep didn't get declared. Followup mitigation candidate: a CI lint that asserts `[target.*]` blocks come after all top-level `[dependencies]` entries, or simpler — make `release-packages.yml::build (windows-latest)` also gate the workflow on a `cargo build -p perry-stdlib` step (which would have caught this at v0.5.467 release rather than letting it sit broken across v0.5.467-481). v0.5.482 (this commit) lost a patch-version race with #410 — initial commit was v0.5.481 but origin/main shipped a v0.5.481 simultaneously, so this rebased onto v0.5.482.

## v0.5.481 — Closes #410: v0.5.480's #408 widget-mutation tracking emitted ArkUI Index.ets that didn't compile through DevEco's ArkTS compiler — three distinct emission defects in `crates/perry-codegen-arkts/src/lib.rs`. **Bug 1 (line 82 cascade)**: `serialize_condition`'s fallback returned `"true /* unsupported condition */"`; when callers wrap that string in a `/* if ((...)) */` block-comment marker (the conditional-modifier emit path in `emit_modifier_mutations`), the embedded `*/` closed the outer comment early, leaking everything after as code. Fix: fallback now returns the literal `"true"` (no diagnostic comment), and `emit_modifier_mutations` defensively routes `cond_str` through a new `sanitize_for_block_comment` helper that replaces any `*/` substring with `*\u{200b}/` (zero-width space inserted between the `*` and `/`) — belt-and-braces against future drift. **Bug 2 (lines 48/52/68 cascade)**: `serialize_condition` for `Expr::LocalGet(id)` emitted `__local_{id}` directly — synthetic identifiers that ArkTS rejects with `Cannot find name '__local_2'`. Fix: thread the existing `bindings: &HashMap<LocalId, Expr>` (already collected by `collect_const_bindings` for body-resolution) through `serialize_condition` + `collect_mutations` + `collect_mutations_in_stmt`. `LocalGet(id)` now resolves recursively via the binding's init expression (so `let mobile = (__platform__ === 1)` produces the comparison verbatim); unresolvable LocalGets (closure-captured, loop-mutated, or `declare const` without an `init`) degrade to the literal `"true"` rather than leaking a placeholder. **Bug 3 (`__platform__` references)**: post-Bug-2-fix, `__platform__ === N` would surface in emitted code where `__platform__` isn't declared on the page struct. Fix: new `collect_compile_time_constants` pre-walk discovers `Stmt::Let { name: "__platform__", init: None }` (the canonical `declare const` HIR shape from `crates/perry-codegen/src/codegen.rs:716`) and inlines them as numeric literals (9.0 for `__platform__` since this codegen path is harmonyos-only — the platform-id table at `codegen.rs:672-700` maps OHOS to 9). The map is threaded alongside `bindings` through every condition-serialization call site. Also added `Expr::Unary` arm to `serialize_condition` so `!mobile` predicates work post-resolve. **Tests**: 7 new `issue_410_*` unit tests pin the invariants — fallback returns `"true"` exactly (no `*/`); `LocalGet` resolves through bindings (no `__local_` substring); unresolvable `LocalGet` degrades to `true`; `__platform__` inlines as `9` (no `__platform__` substring); `collect_compile_time_constants` recognizes the declare-const shape; conditional `widgetAddChild` emits valid `if (9 === 9) { Button('phone') } else { Button('desktop') }` ArkTS; conditional modifier chain has zero nested block comments verified by a brace-matching helper `assert_no_nested_block_comments`. **Verified**: `cargo build --release -p perry-codegen-arkts` clean; `cargo test -p perry-codegen-arkts --release` 77 unit + 2 integration = **79 tests, all passing** (was 70 + 2 = 72); `cargo fmt -p perry-codegen-arkts --check` clean. End-to-end Mango render verification on Pura emulator is the maintainer's separate manual splice step.

## v0.5.480 — Closes #408: `crates/perry-codegen-arkts` now follows post-construction widget mutations on `--target harmonyos`. The harvest model was a literal-tree walker — `App({body: <ref>})` traced through const bindings + array literals but ignored `widgetAddChild` / `scrollviewSetChild` / `setPadding` / `widgetSetBackgroundColor` etc. after construction, so any procedurally-built UI (the dominant Perry pattern — Mango has 63 `widgetAddChild` calls) rendered as empty containers on Pura emulator. New `Mutation` enum `{ AddChild(Expr), ClearChildren, SetScrollChild(Expr), Modifier(String), Comment(String) }` with per-mutation `condition: Option<MutationCondition>` carrying a stringified condition expression + `Branch::{Then, Else}` + per-`if`-statement group id so all mutations from one `if/else` collapse into a single emitted `if (cond) { … } else { … }` block. Pre-walk `collect_mutations` runs in `emit_index_ets` after `collect_state_bindings`/`collect_const_bindings` and before `find_and_strip_app`; walks `module.init` only — closures, loops, switches are intentionally fallback per the issue's "out of scope" section. Resulting `HashMap<LocalId, Vec<MutationEntry>>` is threaded through every `emit_*` signature alongside an `Option<LocalId> local_hint` extracted by `emit_widget` from `Expr::LocalGet(id)` *before* `resolve()`. Coverage: every mutator from the #408 table — `widgetAddChild` / `widgetAddChildAt` / `widgetClearChildren` / `scrollviewSetChild` (+ `scrollViewSetChild`) / `widgetSetBackgroundColor` / `widgetSetBackgroundGradient` (placeholder + comment) / `setPadding` (and alias `widgetSetEdgeInsets`) / `setCornerRadius` / `widgetSetHidden` / `widgetMatchParentWidth` / `widgetMatchParentHeight` / `widgetSetWidth` / `widgetSetHeight` / `widgetSetHugging` / `stackSetDistribution` / `stackSetAlignment`. Conditional `widgetAddChild` becomes a real `if/else` block in the emitted body; conditional modifiers become inline `/* if(cond) */` markers since ArkUI's modifier chain can't host an `if` mid-call. AddChild + Modifier mutations fold into Stack (Column/Row), Section, ScrollView, and (Modifier-only) every other widget kind. SetScrollChild semantics: latest unconditional set wins, conditional sets become if-branches, AddChild appends after. Untraceable parents (non-LocalGet first arg) silently dropped without crashing. **Tests**: 60 → 70 unit + 2 integration = **72 tests, all passing**. Ten new `issue_408_*` tests cover AddChild ordering, ScrollView SetChild, padding+cornerRadius+backgroundColor modifier chain, conditional if/else children, ClearChildren dropping earlier siblings, untraceable parent fallback, hidden/match-parent/distribution/alignment modifiers, and a composite "Mango three-screen" shape mirroring the issue's repro. **Out of scope (follow-ups)**: (1) mutators inside loops/switches/closures/function bodies — fallback skip per issue. (2) `let x; if/else { x = HStack(...) }` reassignment — `collect_const_bindings` only tracks `let id = init` declarations. (3) `widgetAddChildAt(parent, child, index)` collapses to `widgetAddChild` (positional insert is fidelity-lost). (4) Conditional Modifier mutations emit as inline comments rather than full guarded chains — ArkUI modifier-chain syntax can't host an `if`. (5) `widgetSetBackgroundGradient` emits a placeholder + comment; full gradient mapping is a follow-up. (6) Nested `if` inside `if` inherits the outer condition only — no 2D group keying. The `Mutation::Comment` variant + `is_widget_factory` allowlist surfaces any unrecognized perry/ui method touching a widget local as a `// perry/ui mutator X not yet handled` comment in the emitted .ets so future gaps are discoverable rather than silent. **Verified**: `cargo build --release -p perry-codegen-arkts` clean; `cargo test -p perry-codegen-arkts --release` 70 unit + 2 integration = 72 passed; `cargo fmt -p perry-codegen-arkts` clean. End-to-end Mango render verification needs an emulator splice (separate manual step).

## v0.5.479 — Two follow-ups to v0.5.477's #395 HarmonyOS link cluster, surfaced end-to-end on Mango (`@honeide/editor`-using app) booting on the Pura emulator. (1) **`crates/perry-runtime/build.rs`**: the auto-stub generator iterates the four `perry-dispatch` tables, but `perry_ui_vstack_create` / `_hstack_create` / `_button_create` are NOT in `PERRY_UI_TABLE` — they're emitted directly by `crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call/native.rs` for the simple `VStack(items)` / `HStack(items)` / `Button(label, onPress)` constructor shapes that bypass the dispatch table. The auto-generator missed them and the OHOS dynamic loader rejected the .so on first dlopen. Added a small explicit `direct_call_stubs` list at the bottom of `build.rs::main` covering exactly these three; the seen-set dedup means it's idempotent if anything later moves them into the dispatch tables. (2) **`crates/perry/src/commands/compile/link.rs`**: Mango pulls in `iana-time-zone` transitively (chrono and friends), which on the OHOS Rust target calls `OH_TimeService_GetTimeZone` from `libtime_service_ndk.so`. v0.5.477 added `-lhilog_ndk.z` for `OH_LOG_Print` but didn't have the time-service one yet. Added `cmd.arg("-ltime_service_ndk")` next to the hilog line; note the lib has no `.z.` segment in its soname (`libtime_service_ndk.so`, not `.z.so`), unlike `ace_napi`/`hilog_ndk`. Plus a `cargo fmt --all` sweep on `perry-runtime/src/lib.rs` to fix mod-ordering drift introduced by v0.5.477's PR. **End-to-end verified**: `perry compile mango/src/app.ts --target harmonyos` flips `nm -D --undefined-only` from 5 problematic symbols → 0; `DT_NEEDED` lists `libtime_service_ndk.so`; the HAP signs, installs via `hdc install`, launches via `aa start`, and **Perry's `perryEntry.run()` runs to completion and returns 0** on the emulator (4.6s onCreate). UI is blank as expected — Mango builds widgets procedurally with `widgetAddChild` calls (codegen-arkts can't harvest those) and `@honeide/editor`'s harmonyos backend is a no-op stub from hone-editor v0.2.1 — but the .so loads, every symbol resolves, the app stays alive.

## v0.5.478 — perry/tui Phase 4.5: 5 widgets that close out the v1 spec from #358 — `Spinner(frame)` (animated `-\|/` cycle), `Input(value)` (single-line + trailing `_` cursor), `List(items, selected?)` (vertical list with reverse-video selection), `Select(items, selected)` (alias for List with enforced non-negative selection), `TextArea(value)` (multi-line — splits `value` on `\n` into a column-layout Box). Each widget is purely a snapshot renderer — interactivity (cursor positioning, list navigation) is the user's job via `useInput` + `state.set`, matching Ink's hooks-and-render-from-state model. **ABI fix that landed alongside**: every widget-factory FFI now returns `i64` (raw handle) instead of `f64` (NaN-boxed). The dispatch table's `NR_PTR` contract NaN-boxes the i64 result; mismatching the IR-declared return type was working accidentally for Phase 1's `Text`/`Box` because Rust's `f64::from_bits(u64)` compiles as a register-to-register move that leaves the u64 in RAX while the f64 ends up in XMM0 with the same bit pattern, and the IR's `call i64` reads RAX. That accidental coupling broke for the new `Input`/`List`/`Select`/`TextArea` fns — the value never made it back via RAX. Switched the existing `Text`/`Box`/`Spacer`/`ProgressBar` to the canonical `i64` return for consistency, and updated the special-case `Box(args)` codegen path in `lower_call/native.rs` to call/store/read the parent handle as `I64` and NaN-box once at the very end. **List/Select string-array support**: new `read_string_array(items_ptr: i64) -> Vec<String>` helper unboxes the JS array via `js_array_length` + `js_array_get_f64_unchecked`, converts each element via `js_jsvalue_to_string`, returns owned strings; List builds one Text-child-per-item inside a Box, applying `Style::REVERSE` to the selected index so the runtime's diff emitter writes `\x1b[7m` for that row. **Plumbing**: 5 new `NativeModSig` dispatch entries (Spinner / Input / List / Select / TextArea) + TS `.d.ts` extended with the new exports. **Cumulative tui FFI surface on close**: Box, Text, Spacer, ProgressBar, Spinner, Input, List, Select, TextArea + render/run/state/useInput/exit + 7 box-style setters = 21 functions. Out of scope: Table + Tabs (issue tagged as v1.5), per-cell text styling for in-line cursor positioning. New regression test `test_issue_358_perry_tui_phase4_5_widgets.ts` exercises all 5 widgets in one program — verified output emits the expected ANSI for each.

## v0.5.477 — Closes #395 + #399 + #400 (the harmonyos perry/* FFI undefined-symbol cluster). HarmonyOS uses the `perry-codegen-arkts` harvest model — `App({body: VStack([...])})` literals are destructively rewritten into ArkUI source, and there's no `perry-ui-harmonyos` crate by design. But three families of `perry/*` FFI symbols leak into the lowered `.so` because they're called from non-harvested code paths (library factory functions like Hone's `createEditorPerryWidget`, event-handler closure bodies, conditional widget builders for `perry/ui`; and *every* call to `perry/system` / `perry/updater` since those don't go through harvest at all). User @proggeramlug filed #395 reporting `perry_ui_embed_nsview: symbol not found` at app launch when Mango pulls in `@honeide/editor`'s `embedNSView(hone_editor_nsview(handle))` chain. The .so builds clean, the OHOS dynamic loader rejects the bundle on first dlopen. **Fix in three places**: (1) **`crates/perry-runtime/build.rs`** (new) — at perry-runtime build time, scan `perry-dispatch`'s four tables (`PERRY_UI_TABLE` + `PERRY_UI_INSTANCE_TABLE` + `PERRY_SYSTEM_TABLE` + `PERRY_UPDATER_TABLE`) and auto-generate no-op `#[no_mangle] pub extern "C" fn perry_X(...) -> Y { default }` stubs for every row whose runtime symbol matches `perry_ui_*` / `perry_system_*` / `perry_updater_*` (plus the `perry_get_device_idiom` outlier). Each stub returns the zero-value for its declared `ReturnKind` (handle 0 / 0.0 / no-op for void / null pointer for str). The `perry_i18n_*` and `perry_media_*` prefixes are deliberately excluded — `perry-runtime/src/i18n.rs` (always compiled) and `perry-runtime/src/media_playback.rs` (gated `cfg(feature = "ohos-napi")`) provide real implementations and stubbing them would duplicate-symbol at link time. The build.rs uses `perry-dispatch` as a `[build-dependencies]` (one-way dep, no cycle). Output is written to `<OUT_DIR>/perry_ui_harmonyos_stubs.rs`. (2) **`crates/perry-runtime/src/ui_harmonyos_stubs.rs`** (new) — small wrapper module gated `#[cfg(feature = "ohos-napi")]` in `lib.rs` that does `include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/perry_ui_harmonyos_stubs.rs"))`. Documentation explains the harvest-model rationale + which symbols are deliberately NOT stubbed. (3) **#400 cleanup**: `crates/perry/src/commands/compile.rs` flips `ctx.needs_ui = false` UNCONDITIONALLY for harmonyos targets (was previously inside the `Ok(Some(harvest))` arm only — `Ok(None)` and `Err` paths kept needs_ui as-is, which made the build try to compile a nonexistent `perry-ui-harmonyos` crate). With the runtime stubs covering every symbol regardless of harvest outcome, the flip is now always safe and required. The dead `Some("harmonyos*") => "perry-ui-harmonyos"` arm in `optimized_libs.rs:442` and the `Some("harmonyos*") => "libperry_ui_harmonyos.a"` arm in `library_search.rs::find_ui_library` are also removed (replaced with docstrings). **Verified**: OHOS aarch64 cross-compile of perry-runtime via DevEco's bundled SDK clean; libperry_runtime.a contains 217 stubs (181 perry_ui_ + 21 perry_system_ + 14 perry_updater_ + 1 perry_get_device_idiom); zero duplicates within the OHOS build. macOS host build (default features) defines ZERO of those symbols — perry-ui-macos still owns them, no collision. cargo check --workspace clean. End-to-end on Mango is verified by user iteration on a real OHOS device since the bundle is what changed; the next undefined-symbol blocker after embedNSView is unblocked too because perry/system + perry/updater leak the same way.

## v0.5.476 — Closes #392 (followup, https://github.com/PerryTS/perry/issues/392#issuecomment-4357279589): v0.5.464 closed the structural-object-parameter shape via `js_register_class_method` + `CLASS_VTABLE_REGISTRY`, but the same silent-correctness bug still hit when the consumer module **type-only-imports** the class (`import type { Changeset } from "./changeset.ts"`) and the cross-module method touches Map/Set fields (`this.adds.set(k, v)`). User @codehz reported `processCommands(commands, changeset)` running every command but `changeset.adds.size` staying at 0 — silent data loss, exit 0, no diagnostics. **Root cause**: type-only imports are stripped at HIR lowering (the consumer's `hir.imports` doesn't mention the source module), and `compile.rs::is_unresolved_name` treats the `Named("Changeset")` parameter type as resolved (it's in `all_program_type_names`), short-circuiting the `references_interface` full-visibility fallback that would have populated `imported_classes` with every program-wide class. Result: process.ts's `ctx.classes` doesn't contain "Changeset", the static idispatch tower in `lower_call.rs` finds zero implementors of `set` (no Changeset stub in `ctx.methods`), the static fallback path also misses (no `(Changeset, set)` entry), AND the `js_native_call_method` path was previously skipped by `skip_native = class_name_opt.is_some() && !is_buffer_class` — which fell through to `js_closure_call<N>(obj.set)` (visible in disassembly: `js_object_get_field_by_name_f64` then `js_closure_call2`) silently no-opping the Map mutation. **Fix in one place** (`crates/perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs`): extend `skip_native` to NOT skip when the receiver's static class name is unknown to the codegen — `class_unknown_to_codegen = class_name_opt.is_some_and(|n| !ctx.classes.contains_key(n))`. The runtime's `js_native_call_method` then dispatches via the v0.5.464-populated `CLASS_VTABLE_REGISTRY` to the real `perry_method_changeset_ts__Changeset__set`. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_392_followup_map_field.ts` + 2-file fixture under `test-files/fixtures/issue_392_followup_pkg/` exercises the user's exact 4-file shape (type-only import, helper function in cross-module file, Map + Set field mutations); matches `node --experimental-strip-types` byte-for-byte across all 5 lines. **Verified**: cargo build clean; gap tests 27/28 = baseline (lone fail is pre-existing `console_methods` ci-env quirk); parity 197/197 (100%); existing v0.5.464 test (`test_issue_392_cross_module_method_dispatch.ts`) still matches Node byte-for-byte (the structural-object shape never hit the new branch — `class_name_opt = None` for that case).

## v0.5.475 — Closes #358 (Phase 4): native TUI engine v1.0 — 2 new widgets (`Spacer`, `ProgressBar`), `flexGrow` BoxStyle prop, and the headline acceptance-criterion-2 no-flicker proof. **`Spacer()`** is an empty Box with `flex_grow: 1` — pushes siblings apart in row layouts (and up/down in column). Equivalent to `Box({ flexGrow: 1 })` but more discoverable. **`ProgressBar(value, max, width)`** renders `[====    ]`-style filled bars: `value/max → fraction of width cells filled with '='`, the rest are spaces, brackets at both ends. Returns a Text widget so it composes naturally with Box. **flexGrow** added to BoxStyle (u16) and forwarded to Taffy as `flex_grow: f32`; lower_call/native.rs's apply_box_style helper recognizes the `flexGrow:` key. **Acceptance criterion #2 verified** via `test_issue_358_perry_tui_phase4_widgets.ts`: three frames render a growing log list (3 → 4 → 5 lines). Frame 1 emits all 3 lines; **Frame 2 emits ONLY `\x1b[4;1Hlog\x1b[4;5H4`** (just the appended row); **Frame 3 emits ONLY `\x1b[5;1Hlog\x1b[5;5H5`**. Zero re-emission of unchanged rows — the cell-level diff is doing exactly what the issue's anti-flicker design called for. The streaming-log shape from acceptance-#2 (1000 lines/sec) is bounded by stdout throughput, not by re-rendering work, on this architecture. Plumbing: 4 new NativeModSig dispatch entries (Spacer / ProgressBar / boxSetFlexGrow / the existing apply_box_style flexGrow path). New regression test exercises all 3 frames + a row-layout demo with Spacer pushing ProgressBar to the right end. **Cumulative tui test coverage on close**: 33 unit tests + 4 byte-stream regression tests across the 4 phases. **Out of scope (deliberate v1 cuts, tracked as Phase 4.5 follow-ups)**: Input (single-line text input with cursor — needs keypress-into-state plumbing), TextArea (multi-line edit), List (scrollable), Select (active-row navigation), Spinner (animated character — needs interval timer integration), Table + Tabs (issue tagged as v1.5). 24-bit truecolor + per-side padding rect + flex-shrink + percentage units (Phase 3.5 originally). React-compatibility shim (Track A — defer to ink via #348). Mouse input + sixel/kitty graphics (issue called these out as deferred). #358 closed; native TUIs (#358) and ink-as-compilePackages (#348) now both have all the primitives they need (#347 Phase 3 already shipped tty.isatty/columns/rows/SIGWINCH; this closes the Track-B side).

## v0.5.474 — #358 Phase 3: Taffy flexbox layout for perry/tui. The Box widget now accepts an optional style options object — `Box({ flexDirection: "row" | "column", justifyContent, alignItems, gap, padding, width, height }, [children])` — and the layout pass routes through Taffy 0.7's flexbox solver before paint. Defaults match the v0.1 vertical-stack behavior so existing code keeps working without supplying a style. **New module structure** in `perry-runtime/src/tui/`: `style.rs` (BoxStyle struct + parse_flex_direction / parse_justify_content / parse_align_items helpers, 4 unit tests), `layout.rs` (Taffy mirror — `compute_layout(root, term_w, term_h) -> HashMap<i64, Rect>` builds a TaffyTree<()> from the perry/tui Node tree using `taffy::prelude::{auto, length, Size, Style, NodeId, AvailableSpace}` and the `taffy::TaffyTree` plus `Display::Flex` and `taffy::Rect as TaffyRect` for padding; recursively walks the computed layout to produce absolute terminal-cell rects keyed by widget handle; 4 unit tests covering vertical default / horizontal direction / gap / padding). Tree.rs gets a new `paint_with_layout(grid, root, rects)` painter that draws each Text node at its computed (row, col) and recurses through Box children; the v0.1 paint() is kept as a fallback for unit tests. **FFI surface**: 7 new setters — `js_perry_tui_box_set_flex_direction` / `_justify_content` / `_align_items` / `_gap` / `_padding` / `_width` / `_height` — that mutate the Box's BoxStyle via a new `tree::with_node_mut(handle, fn)` helper. **Codegen**: extended the existing `Box(args)` recognizer in `lower_call/native.rs` to classify args structurally (Array → children, anything-else → style); new `apply_box_style(ctx, parent_slot, style_arg)` helper iterates the recognized style keys and emits per-field setter calls between Box construction and add_child. Added 7 NativeModSig dispatch entries for direct hand-emission. **TS surface**: `BoxStyle` interface added to `index.d.ts` with `flexDirection / justifyContent / alignItems / gap / padding / width / height`; 4 Box overloads cover (no args / children-only / style-only / style + children). **Taffy dep**: `taffy = { version = "0.7", default-features = false, features = ["std", "flexbox", "taffy_tree"] }` — the `taffy_tree` feature was the gotcha (TaffyTree is gated behind it, not in the default feature set). **New regression test** `test_issue_358_perry_tui_phase3_flexbox.ts` exercises 4 shapes: row direction (children side by side), column with gap (blank rows between), padding (offsets first child), bare Box (defaults to v0.1 column). Output ANSI escape stream confirms Taffy placed children at the expected (row, col) per shape. **Cumulative tui test coverage**: 33 unit tests (was 25, +8 for style + layout) + 3 byte-stream regression tests (Phase 1 / Phase 2 / Phase 3). Phase 4 (widget expansion: Spacer, Input, TextArea, List, Select, Spinner, ProgressBar + streaming-log-viewer no-flicker test) closes out #358.

## v0.5.473 — Closes #394 (round 2): extends the v0.5.470 sim-only iOS bottle fix to cover `--target ios` (device) too. Pre-fix, the bottle bundled only the `aarch64-apple-ios-sim`-built libs (renamed to `libperry_runtime_ios.a` / `libperry_stdlib_ios.a` / `libperry_ui_ios.a`); `--target ios` linked against those simulator-built objects, which clang accepted (warnings only) but the resulting binary wouldn't run on actual iOS devices because the slices carried `LC_BUILD_VERSION` for `PLATFORM_IOS_SIMULATOR` instead of `PLATFORM_IOS`. The collision was unavoidable at v0.5.470 because `find_ui_library` returned the same lib name `libperry_ui_ios.a` for both `Some("ios")` and `Some("ios-simulator")` and a flat lib dir can hold only one — so v0.5.470 had to pick one variant and chose sim. **Fix across two files**: (1) new `apple_class_lib_name(name, class, is_sim)` helper in `library_search.rs` composes the suffixed flat-dir name from canonical-cargo-output names. Rule: device variant uses the existing class suffix verbatim (`libperry_ui_ios.a` if stem already ends with `_ios`, else `libperry_runtime_ios.a` for generic libs); simulator variant inserts `_sim` BEFORE `.a` (`libperry_ui_ios_sim.a` / `libperry_runtime_ios_sim.a`). (2) `collect_library_candidates` flips its per-target match from `Option<&str>` (single suffix) to `Option<(&str, bool)>` (class + is_sim) and routes through the new helper. Triple-specific candidates (`target/aarch64-apple-ios-sim/release/libperry_ui_ios.a`) still fire FIRST, so in-tree dev workflows where cargo writes the canonical name into a triple subdir are unchanged — the suffix differentiation only matters when libs land in a flat dir like Homebrew's `<prefix>/lib/`. (3) `release-packages.yml` macOS legs now `rustup target add aarch64-apple-ios aarch64-apple-ios-sim` and run a `for triple in ...; do cargo build ...; done` over both targets, copying device libs into staging with the canonical `_ios` suffix and sim libs with `_ios_sim`. Bottle now ships SIX iOS libs alongside the host artifacts: `libperry_runtime_ios.a` / `_ios_sim.a`, `libperry_stdlib_ios.a` / `_ios_sim.a`, `libperry_ui_ios.a` / `_ios_sim.a`. **Migration note for users on v0.5.470/.471/.472**: the bottle's iOS lib was actually a sim-build (despite the canonical filename); v0.5.473 ships separate device + sim libs. Users who picked up v0.5.470's bottle and were targeting `--target ios` were silently building broken-on-device binaries — `--target ios` builds on v0.5.473+ now produce real iOS device-loadable binaries. **Scope still excluded**: tvOS / visionOS / watchOS (Tier-3, need nightly + `-Zbuild-std`); the `apple_class_lib_name` helper is generic enough to extend to those once they land. **Verified**: cargo build clean; the helper composition rule covers all four (class-stem, generic, device, sim) cases — verified via the four call shapes in the existing iOS arm.

## v0.5.472 — #358 Phase 2: native TUI engine v0.2 — reactive `state<T>(initial)` container with `.get()` / `.set()`, `useInput(handler)` keypress dispatch, and the `run(component)` interactive render loop. The counter program from the issue's acceptance criterion #1 (`+`/`-` to inc/dec, `q` to quit) compiles + links + runs; tested end-to-end via `printf '+++q' | binary` showing `FINAL=3` and `printf '++--q' | binary` showing `FINAL=0`. Three new perry-runtime/src/tui/ submodules + 9 new unit tests: (1) **state.rs** — `static Mutex<Vec<u64>>` slot table holding NaN-boxed JSValue bits per slot, plus a `STATE_DIRTY: AtomicBool` that flips when `.set()` writes a different value; render loop reads + clears the flag every frame to decide whether to re-render immediately or sleep. (2) **input.rs** — Unix termios raw-mode toggle (libc::tcgetattr / tcsetattr; cfmakeraw equivalent — disable ICANON/ECHO/ISIG/IXON/INLCR/ICRNL, set CS8/VMIN=1/VTIME=0; saves cooked-mode termios on first enable, restores on disable), background byte-reader thread reading stdin per-byte and queueing into `static Mutex<Vec<u8>>`, drain function that groups consecutive ESC-prefixed bytes (≤8) into single dispatch chunks so arrow keys arrive as one event rather than three, then calls the registered single-handler closure with the chunk as a string. (3) **run.rs** — render loop: enter alt screen + clear + hide cursor + enable raw mode → loop { check EXIT_FLAG; call component closure (js_closure_call0) for fresh widget tree; paint into back buffer + flush diff; clear STATE_DIRTY; drain input; if STATE_DIRTY (handler called set), continue without sleep; else sleep 16 ms (~60 fps polling cap) } → restore terminal: disable raw mode + leave alt screen + show cursor. Plumbing: 7 new NativeModSig dispatch entries (state / get / set / useInput / run / exit + the existing render/Box/Text), `("perry/tui", "state") => Some("State")` factory mapping in destructuring.rs (both the `module.method` form via `cron`-style arm AND the `import { state } from "perry/tui"` named-import form via the existing perry/ui-shaped block — the named-import path didn't exist for perry/tui pre-fix and `count.get()` fell through to dynamic dispatch with a null pointer, hitting `[NULL_PTR_METHOD_CALL] js_native_call_method` warnings). New `js_perry_tui_state_alloc/get/set/use_input/run/exit` FFI exports. TS `.d.ts` extended with State<T> interface + state/useInput/run/exit declarations. Architectural note: kept the input reader inside perry-runtime/src/tui (vs reusing the #347 readline reader from perry-stdlib) because perry-tui needs control over stdin for the duration of the render loop; the two readers don't currently coordinate (a program using both would race on stdin), but in practice TUI apps own stdin so this is fine. New regression test `test_issue_358_perry_tui_phase2_counter.ts` is the acceptance-criterion-1 counter; verified via three input sequences.

## v0.5.471 — `perry.nativeLibrary` crates now build correctly when `--target harmonyos[-simulator]`. Pre-fix, `link.rs::1535` only set the Android `max-page-size=16384` rustflag for user nativeLibrary builds; the OHOS clang + sysroot env (already exported by `library_search::harmonyos_cross_env`) was reused only for Perry's own `optimized_libs` build. Result: any user crate (e.g. `@honeide/editor`'s `native/harmonyos/`) failed at link with the host `cc` rejecting ELF flags like `--version-script`, `--no-undefined-version`. **Fix in one place** (`link.rs`, immediately after the Android arm in the nativeLibrary cargo invocation): when `is_harmonyos`, look up the OHOS SDK via `find_harmonyos_sdk()` and forward every var from `harmonyos_cross_env(&sdk, target)` (CC / CFLAGS / CARGO_TARGET_*_LINKER / CARGO_TARGET_*_RUSTFLAGS) into `cargo_cmd.env`. Same env shape `optimized_libs` uses, so any user crate built through Perry's nativeLibrary mechanism now picks up the cross-toolchain. Verified end-to-end on the Hone editor's new HarmonyOS no-op stub crate (`hone-editor v0.2.1`'s `native/harmonyos/`): `perry compile mango/src/app.ts --target harmonyos` flips from "linking with `cc` failed: ld: unknown options: --version-script" to a clean libentry.so where every `hone_editor_*` symbol resolves; remaining undefined symbols on Mango are now `perry/ui` HarmonyOS-bridge gaps, not nativeLibrary build infrastructure.

## v0.5.470 — Closes #394: Homebrew-installed `perry` couldn't compile for iOS — `perry foo.ts -o app --target ios-simulator` failed at link with `perry/ui imported but libperry_ui_ios.a not found`. Two layered defects in the release-packages workflow + library lookup: (1) the macOS legs of `release-packages.yml` only built `perry-ui-macos` (`libperry_ui_macos.a`), never `perry-ui-ios` — so the bottle shipped without `libperry_ui_ios.a` at all; (2) even when the bottle had iOS libs, the bottle layout (binary at `<prefix>/bin/perry`, libs at `<prefix>/lib/`) wasn't probed by the cross-compile branch of `library_search.rs::collect_library_candidates` — the host-build branch already had the `prefix.join("lib").join(name)` candidate, but the cross-compile branch only had `dir.join(name)` (which would be `<prefix>/bin/libperry_ui_ios.a`) and never walked back up to the lib dir. **Fix in two places**: (1) `release-packages.yml` macOS legs now `rustup target add aarch64-apple-ios-sim` and run `cargo build --release --target aarch64-apple-ios-sim -p perry-runtime -p perry-stdlib -p perry-ui-ios`; the staging step copies the resulting `.a` files into the tarball with the `_ios` suffix convention `library_search.rs`'s iOS arm already understands (`libperry_runtime_ios.a` / `libperry_stdlib_ios.a` / `libperry_ui_ios.a`). (2) `library_search.rs::collect_library_candidates` — collapsed the five duplicate per-platform arms (iOS / visionOS / watchOS / tvOS / HarmonyOS) into a single suffix-driven block that picks the right `_ios` / `_visionos` / `_watchos` / `_tvos` / `_harmonyos` rewrite, and adds `prefix.join("lib").join(suffixed)` as a candidate so Homebrew bottles resolve. Same canonical paths as the host branch (`brew --prefix`/`bin/perry` → walk up → `/lib/<name>`); zero behavioral change for in-tree dev builds (the new candidate just falls behind the existing `target/<triple>/release/` candidates that already win for in-tree builds). **Scope notes**: only iOS simulator (Tier-2 stable) is bundled in this release. iOS device (`aarch64-apple-ios`, also Tier-2) is a follow-up — same lib filename `libperry_ui_ios.a` would collide with the sim variant, so adding it requires name-differentiation in `find_ui_library` + the lookup. tvOS / visionOS / watchOS are Tier-3 (need nightly + `-Zbuild-std`) and out of scope here. **Verified**: cargo build clean; the new lookup unifies five duplicate arms (~50 LOC → ~25 LOC) with no logic change for the dev-build path. End-to-end Homebrew-bottle iOS compile is verified by the next release run since the bottle is what changed; users on v0.5.470+ should see `perry foo.ts -o app --target ios-simulator` link without the `not found` error.

## v0.5.469 — #358 Phase 1: native TUI engine v0.1 — `import { Box, Text, render } from "perry/tui"` works end-to-end. New `perry-runtime/src/tui/` module (5 submodules: cell, color, render, tree, ffi — ~875 LOC + 16 unit tests). **Cell grid**: packed `Vec<Cell>` with each cell at 8 bytes (char=4, fg=1, bg=1, style=1, pad=1) → 80×24 fits in 15 KB. **Double buffer + cell-level diff**: `flush()` walks the back buffer, emits move_to + minimal SGR + char only for cells that differ from the front, then swaps; SGR state is tracked across cells in a single flush so we don't repeat e.g. `\x1b[31m` for ten consecutive red cells; adjacent cells skip redundant move_to (one CSI per dirty run, not per cell). **No erase, no rewrite** — this is the architecture's whole point vs ink's `\e[2K\e[1A`-per-line approach. **ANSI emitter**: hand-rolled `format_u8` / `format_u16` (no itoa dep). **16-color palette** (Color enum: Default + 8 named + 8 bright); 24-bit truecolor lands in Phase 3.5 alongside Taffy. **Style flags**: bold / italic / underline / reverse (Style bitmask). **Widget tree**: global handle table indexed by atomic counter, Mutex-protected; `Node::Box { children, fg, bg }` and `Node::Text { content, fg, bg, style }`. v0.1 paints Box children as a vertical stack at the parent's row+0,1,2,…; real flexbox via Taffy is Phase 3. **FFI surface**: `js_perry_tui_text(content_ptr) -> f64` (NaN-boxed POINTER handle), `js_perry_tui_box() -> f64`, `js_perry_tui_box_add_child(parent: i64, child: i64)`, `js_perry_tui_render(root: i64)`, `js_perry_tui_enter()`. The grid singleton (Mutex<Grid>) auto-sizes to the current terminal via TIOCGWINSZ on first render. **Plumbing in 5 places**: (1) new `perry-runtime/src/tui/` module + `pub mod tui` in lib.rs; (2) `"perry/tui"` added to NATIVE_MODULES + RUNTIME_ONLY_MODULES (no stdlib needed — pure runtime FFI, no async-runtime, no tokio); (3) `perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` adds 4 NativeModSig entries (Text / Box / render / enter); (4) `perry-codegen/src/lower_call/native.rs` recognizes the `Box([children])` array shape — lowers to `js_perry_tui_box()` + `js_perry_tui_box_add_child(parent, child)`-per-element (mirrors the existing perry/ui VStack pattern); bare `Box()` falls through to the regular dispatch table; (5) `perry/src/commands/types.rs` registers the new `types/perry/tui/index.d.ts` stub so `tsc` and IDEs resolve `import { Box, Text, render } from "perry/tui"` cleanly. **Architecture decision**: kept tui logic inside perry-runtime (vs the issue's original "new crates/perry-tui" sibling-crate plan) because perry-tui needs `StringHeader` + `js_nanbox_pointer` from runtime, and runtime would need to dep perry-tui to bundle the FFI symbols into libperry_runtime.a — circular. Living inside runtime adds ~30 KB to every Perry binary post-strip but eliminates the cargo plumbing entirely (no new workspace member, no new optimized-libs branch, no new link flag). Same architectural tradeoff as `perry/thread` (also a runtime module, not a sibling crate). New regression test `test-files/test_issue_358_perry_tui_phase1.ts`: `Box([Text("Hello"), Text("World")])` then `render()`. Compiles + links + runs; output is the expected double-buffered diff stream (`\x1b[1;1HHello\x1b[2;1HWorld\x1b[0m`) followed by a "perry/tui render ok" marker — the cell-grid + ANSI emitter is working end-to-end. Phase 2 (hooks + interactive render loop + keypress integration via #347) layers on this; Phase 3 (Taffy flexbox) and Phase 4 (widget expansion + log-viewer no-flicker test) follow.

## v0.5.468 — Closes #347 fully (Phase 3): `tty.isatty(fd)`, `process.std{in,out,err}.isTTY`, `process.stdout.columns`/`.rows`, and `process.stdout.on('resize', cb)` SIGWINCH integration. New `crates/perry-runtime/src/tty.rs` (~250 LOC + 4 unit tests) handles all of these via libc on Unix (libc::isatty, libc::ioctl with TIOCGWINSZ for winsize, libc::sigaction for SIGWINCH); Windows / non-Unix paths are stubbed (return false / undefined / no-op) until the windows-rs Console API is wired. SIGWINCH handler is async-signal-safe (sets a single AtomicBool); the actual resize callback dispatch happens on the next event-loop tick via `js_tty_resize_drain`, which I hooked into `js_run_stdlib_pump` (in perry-runtime/src/lib.rs) so it ticks even when stdlib isn't linked — a pure runtime-only TUI program with `process.stdout.on('resize', cb)` still sees its callback fire on terminal resize. **Plumbing in 7 places**: (1) new `tty.rs` with all the FFI exports + SIGWINCH installer; (2) `perry-hir/src/ir.rs` adds 7 new HIR variants — `Expr::TtyIsAtty(fd)`, `ProcessStdinIsTTY` / `ProcessStdoutIsTTY` / `ProcessStderrIsTTY`, `ProcessStdoutColumns`, `ProcessStdoutRows`, `ProcessStdoutOn { event, handler }` — plus `"tty"` added to NATIVE_MODULES + RUNTIME_ONLY_MODULES; (3) walker.rs gets exhaustive arms for all 7 variants (compile-error guard against future variant drift, same pattern as Phase 2); (4) `perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs` recognizes `tty.isatty(fd)` BEFORE the `os` module dispatch (drops into the existing builtin-module-alias gate), and merges the existing `process.stdin.X` recognition into a `(stream, method)` match that also handles `process.stdout.on('resize', ...)`; (5) `perry-hir/src/lower/expr_member.rs` recognizes the double-Member shape `process.std{in,out,err}.{isTTY,columns,rows}` BEFORE the `process.X` arm, since `process.stdin.isTTY` is a 2-deep member access not a 1-deep one; (6) `perry-codegen/src/expr.rs` lowers all 7 variants to direct extern calls (`js_tty_isatty` / `js_process_*_isatty` / `js_process_stdout_columns/rows` / `js_process_stdout_on`); (7) `runtime_decls.rs` declares all 7 new externs. The runtime stubs in `perry-runtime/src/stdlib_stubs.rs` aren't needed because tty lives in runtime (always linked). Android stdlib stubs intentionally don't include the new tty symbols — they'd conflict with the runtime's real implementations on Android. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_347_readline_phase3.ts` exercises all 4 surfaces (isatty / isTTY / columns/rows / on('resize')) with stdin piped from /dev/null (CI runner shape — `tty.isatty(fd)` returns false for every fd, isTTY is false, columns/rows are undefined matching Node). Matches `node --experimental-strip-types` byte-for-byte across all 10 lines. Live SIGWINCH dispatch is covered by the 4 unit tests in `tty.rs::tests` + the runtime hookup is verified by `js_run_stdlib_pump` calling `js_tty_resize_drain` first. **Cumulative test coverage for #347**: 9 readline unit tests + 4 tty unit tests = 13 unit tests, plus 3 byte-for-byte parity regression tests covering the full surface. With #347 closed, native TUIs (#358) and ink (#348) now have all the primitives they need.

## v0.5.467 — #347 Phase 2: `process.stdin.setRawMode(bool)` + `process.stdin.on('data'|'keypress'|'end', cb)`. The Phase 1 `thread_local!` PENDING_LINES queue was a latent cross-thread bug (the reader thread and main thread had separate instances — never observed in Phase 1's test which only exercised synchronous `rl.close()`); rewrote queue state to `static Mutex<Vec<...>>` while keeping callback storage thread_local (callbacks dispatch from main only). Reader thread switched to byte-mode (one-byte reads); pivots between line-mode (accumulate to `\n`, queue line) and raw-mode (immediate single-byte chunk dispatch) based on the `RAW_MODE: AtomicBool` flag — toggling mid-stream is supported. Termios on Unix via libc::tcgetattr / tcsetattr (cfmakeraw equivalent — disable ICANON/ECHO/ISIG/IXON/INLCR/ICRNL, set CS8/VMIN=1/VTIME=0, save the cooked-mode termios on first enable so disable can restore). Windows raw-mode + iOS/Android are no-op stubs (raw mode flag still flips so the byte-mode dispatch still works at the OS-line-cooked level). New keypress parser recognizes CSI arrow keys (`\x1b[A..D` / H / F → up/down/right/left/home/end), Ctrl+letter (`0x01..0x1a` → letter + ctrl=true), Enter (`\r`/`\n` → "return"), Tab, Escape, Backspace (0x7f or 0x08), Space, plain letters (uppercase sets shift=true) — emits `(seq, { name, ctrl, shift, meta, sequence })` to the keypress callback. **Plumbing in 7 places**: (1) new HIR variants `Expr::ProcessStdinSetRawMode(arg)` + `Expr::ProcessStdinOn { event, handler }` in `perry-hir/src/ir.rs`; (2) walker.rs gets exhaustive arms for both (compile-error guard against future variant drift); (3) `perry-hir/src/lower/expr_call.rs` recognizes `process.stdin.setRawMode(...)` and `process.stdin.on(...)` patterns BEFORE the generic `module.Class.staticMethod` arm — falls through for any other method name (e.g. `process.stdin.write` keeps using the existing closure-on-stub-object path); (4) `perry-codegen/src/expr.rs` lowers both variants to direct extern calls (`js_readline_set_raw_mode` / `js_readline_stdin_on`); event string goes through `unbox_str_handle` for SSO safety per #214; (5) `perry-codegen/src/runtime_decls.rs` adds the two extern declarations; (6) `perry-stdlib/src/readline.rs` rewritten ~530 LOC + 9 unit tests covering parse_keypress arrow keys / Ctrl+letter / special keys / shift flag, raw_mode_toggle, the chunk-injection drain — exposes `js_readline_set_raw_mode` / `js_readline_stdin_on` / re-shaped `js_readline_process_pending` that drains BOTH line and data queues; new `try_register_pump` shim wrapping the (async-runtime-gated) `ensure_pump_registered` call so readline imports don't hard-require tokio; (7) `perry/src/commands/compile/collect_modules.rs` HIR-debug-grep detects `ProcessStdinSetRawMode`/`ProcessStdinOn` usage and flips `needs_stdlib=true` + adds `"readline"` to native_module_imports — without this, a TUI program using `process.stdin.X` but no explicit `import readline` would link in runtime-only mode and fail with `_js_readline_set_raw_mode undefined`. `stdlib_features.rs` maps `"readline" => &["async-runtime"]` so the pump drains live event-loop ticks. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_347_readline_phase2.ts` (typeof guard for Node compatibility — Node throws TypeError on setRawMode for non-TTY stdin) matches `node --experimental-strip-types` byte-for-byte. `cfg(unix)` libc target dep added to perry-stdlib. Phase 3 (tty.isatty + columns/rows + SIGWINCH) layers on top of this; #347 stays open until Phase 3 lands.

## v0.5.466 — #347 Phase 1: line-buffered `readline.createInterface` / `rl.question` / `rl.on('line'|'close')` / `rl.close()`. New `crates/perry-stdlib/src/readline.rs` mirrors `worker_threads.rs::start_stdin_reader` — a single background thread reads lines from stdin and queues them; the main event-loop pump drains the queue on every tick via `js_readline_process_pending`, dispatching to the registered question (one-shot, takes precedence) / line / close callbacks. `rl.close()` fires the close callback synchronously to match Node's `Interface.close()` semantics (program-visible: `rl.on('close', cb); rl.close(); log('after')` prints "closed" before "after"). Plumbing in 6 places: (1) new `readline.rs` (~190 LOC + 3 unit tests); (2) `lib.rs` exports the module; (3) `async_bridge.rs` calls `js_readline_process_pending` on every tick + `js_readline_has_active` in the has-active-handles check + flips `ensure_pump_registered` from `fn` to `pub fn` so non-await stdlib callers can register the runtime pump (without this, a program with only `readline` and no `await` exits before `main` returns can fire any callbacks); (4) `perry-hir/src/ir.rs` adds `"readline"` to `NATIVE_MODULES` so `import * as readline from 'readline'` resolves to the native stdlib path (NOT in `RUNTIME_ONLY_MODULES` — readline lives in stdlib); (5) `perry-hir/src/destructuring.rs` registers `("readline", "createInterface") => Some("Interface")` so subsequent `rl.question/.on/.close` method calls dispatch via the new `("readline", true, METHOD)` entries; (6) `perry-codegen/src/lower_call.rs` adds 4 `NativeModSig` entries (createInterface returns `NR_PTR`, instance methods take `NA_STR` for event name + `NA_PTR` for callback closure, NA_F64 for opts). Android stdlib stub. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_347_readline_phase1.ts` exercises createInterface + on('close') + close(); matches `node --experimental-strip-types` byte-for-byte. Live stdin paths (`rl.question`, `rl.on('line', ...)`) are covered by the 3 unit tests in `readline.rs::tests` (the parity runner doesn't currently pipe stdin into compiled binaries). Phase 2 (raw mode + keypress) and Phase 3 (isatty + columns/rows + resize) layer on this; #347 stays open until they land.

## v0.5.465 — Release-night eighth-round fix: `cargo fmt --check` failed on the v0.5.460 Tests run with diffs in 4 files added/touched by v0.5.457-v0.5.464 — `crates/perry-hir/src/js_transform.rs` (the largest, 938-line addition from v0.5.458's #341 fix), `crates/perry-runtime/src/gc.rs`, `crates/perry-runtime/src/object.rs` (both touched by v0.5.457's Windows runtime cluster fix), and `crates/perry-stdlib/src/common/dispatch.rs`. None of those commits ran `cargo fmt --all` before commit. Same fix-pattern as v0.5.452's media_playback / ohos_napi fmt sweep — ran `cargo fmt --all` mechanically; 23 insertions / 40 deletions across the four files (mostly use-ordering, single-line `if`/`else` collapses, and signature reflow). v0.5.461-v0.5.464 all inherit the same lint failure since they descend from v0.5.458; v0.5.463's release-packages would have hit it on Tests too. v0.5.465 patches forward.

## v0.5.464 — Closes #392: when a class instance was passed as an argument to a function defined in another module (with a structural-object parameter type like `{ set(k, v): void }`), method calls on the instance inside that function silently failed. Same-module calls worked because the codegen's static idispatch tower in `lower_call.rs` enumerates `ctx.classes` to find implementors of the called method and emits a class_id check that dispatches to `perry_method_<class>__<method>` directly. Cross-module calls fell through to `js_native_call_method`, which DOES support vtable dispatch via `CLASS_VTABLE_REGISTRY` — but the codegen NEVER emitted any `js_register_class_method(...)` calls. The FFI was declared but no caller invoked it; method registration was effectively dead code. **Fix in `crates/perry-codegen/src/codegen.rs::emit_string_pool`**: after the existing `js_register_class_parent` block, emit one `js_register_class_method(class_id, name_ptr, name_len, func_ptr, param_count)` call per (class, method) pair for every class DEFINED in the current module (skipping imported class stubs via the `method.body.is_empty()` check — stubs have empty bodies, real local methods don't). Pre-intern every method name into the string pool BEFORE `emit_string_pool` runs (since it takes `&strings`, not `&mut strings`) so the bytes globals exist when the registration call references them. New regression test `test-files/test_issue_392_cross_module_method_dispatch.ts` + 2-file fixture under `test-files/fixtures/issue_392_pkg/` covers the issue's exact 3-file shape; matches `node --experimental-strip-types` byte-for-byte. Verified: parity 190/190 (100%).

## v0.5.463 — Release-night seventh-round fix: v0.5.456's Tests passed end-to-end and `await-tests` cleared, but `release-packages.yml::build` then failed on the `ubuntu-22.04 x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` target with `libgstreamer1.0-dev : Depends: libunwind-dev / E: Unable to correct problems, you have held broken packages.` `apt-get install -y libgtk-4-dev libgstreamer1.0-dev` was not enough on jammy because the runner image holds `libunwind-dev` back, breaking the dep chain. Fix in one place: extend the apt-get install with `libunwind-dev` explicitly so it's named on the install line and apt's resolver can satisfy the chain. ubuntu-24.04-arm doesn't have this issue but the explicit name is harmless there. Same workflow file as v0.5.456's gstreamer fix, same step.

## v0.5.457 — Closes #385 + #386 + #387 (the entire Windows runtime divergence cluster from the v0.5.452-455 release-night skip-listings) at root cause: a Darwin-tight `HEAP_MIN = 0x200_0000_0000` (2 TB) floor in `crates/perry-runtime/src/array.rs::clean_arr_ptr`, `value.rs` (inline length reader, twice), and `object.rs::is_valid_obj_ptr` was rejecting every legitimate heap pointer on Windows because Windows mimalloc allocates well below 2 TB (typically in the GB-to-tens-of-GB range — verified via TEB.StackBase reads and direct heap-pointer probes during repro). Effect: `clean_arr_ptr` returned NULL for valid `*const ArrayHeader`, so `js_array_map(arr, cb)` went through the `if arr.is_null() { return js_array_alloc(0); }` path and returned an empty array — `[1,2,3,4,5].map(n => n*2).join(",")` produced `""` instead of `"2,4,6,8,10"` (#385). `is_valid_obj_ptr` rejected JSON.parse'd object pointers, so `JSON.parse('{"name":"perry"}').name` returned `"undefined"` instead of `"perry"` (#386). The same filter applied across the runtime caused cascading garbage in `language/supported_features.ts` — `greet=90836720631611930000000000000000, Perry!` (a NaN-boxed string handle decoded as a number) and `sum=[object Object]` (a number decoded as an object pointer) — culminating in a 0xC0000005 ACCESS_VIOLATION when downstream code dereferenced the resulting bogus pointers (#387). Behavior was non-deterministic because mimalloc's allocation address depends on heap layout — some runs landed above 2 TB by luck and worked; most landed below and the floor silently null-ed everything. **Fix in 4 places** (1 line per file): array.rs::clean_arr_ptr, value.rs (POINTER_TAG handle path + raw bitcast path), object.rs::is_valid_obj_ptr — add `target_os = "windows"` to the existing `(android, linux)` low-floor cfg branch so Windows uses the 4 KB classic user-space floor. Also added a Windows `get_stack_bottom()` impl in `crates/perry-runtime/src/gc.rs` reading TEB.StackBase via inline asm (`gs:[0x08]` on x86_64, `fs:[0x04]` on x86, `[x18 + 0x08]` on aarch64) — a separate latent bug (the `#[cfg(not(any(macos, linux)))]` fallback returned 0, which made the conservative stack scan early-return on Windows, so any GC fired during a closure callback would sweep live arrays held only on the stack). The stack-bottom fix is belt-and-braces — repros didn't surface a GC-trigger on the small test cases, but the gap would have hit larger programs as soon as the heap-floor fix made enough pointers valid to start triggering allocation pressure. **Verified locally on Windows host**: 3/3 doc-tests that were skip-listed in v0.5.452-455 (`runtime/array_methods.ts`, `runtime/json_parse.ts`, `language/supported_features.ts`) flip from skipped/STDOUT_DIFF to PASS once `windows` is re-added to their `// platforms:` banners (which this commit does); 5/5 language doc-tests + 3/3 runtime doc-tests all PASS; 173/173 perry-runtime unit tests pass. The umbrella Windows runtime divergence concern from the v0.5.455 changelog ("3 known-skipped tests under a single follow-up... they all share the symptom-cluster of compile cleanly, link cleanly, then either segfault, return undefined for valid lookups, or return empty for valid array results which suggests a single underlying root cause") was dead-on: the single root cause is this 2 TB heap floor.

## v0.5.456 — Release-night sixth-round fix: v0.5.455's Tests + Simulator Tests both passed and `await-tests` cleared, but the `release-packages.yml::build` matrix then failed on the `ubuntu-24.04-arm aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu` target (and would have failed on `ubuntu-22.04 x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` too — `fail-fast` cancelled the rest).
